content:
Survey Stack

BI Helpfile - Segment Trend

Survey Stack Introduction Survey Stacks is a data management feature that empowers users to seamlessly integrate data from multiple surveys into a single, cohesive dataset. This centralized repository of information facilitates streamlined reporting, in-depth analysis, and the generation of actionable insights. By merging data from various surveys, Survey Stacks eliminates the need for manual data consolidation and enables a holistic view of respondent feedback and behavior. This feature is particularly valuable for clients that conduct multiple surveys over time, across different departments, or for varying target audiences. With Survey Stacks, users can identify trends, compare results, and track changes in respondent sentiment over time. This comprehensive understanding of data can inform strategic decision-making, drive process improvements, and enhance overall organizational performance. Purpose and Benefits: Data Consolidation: This involves merging responses from multiple surveys into a unified dataset, allowing for a comprehensive analysis of collected information. Efficiency and Automation: Streamlining the data cleaning and merging process saves valuable time and reduces the need for manual intervention, minimizing the risk of errors and improving overall efficiency. Standardization: Ensuring consistency across different reporting systems enables seamless integration and facilitates accurate and reliable comparisons of data, leading to more informed decision-making. Core Functionality Browse and Select: Allows you to navigate through and choose the specific survey(s) that you want to include in your data merging process. It may display survey titles, creation dates, or other relevant information to help you identify the correct survey. Auto-Match Identical Fields: The system can automatically recognize and match fields that have the same name in different surveys, streamlining the merging process. Manual Field Name Override: If the automatic matching is incorrect or if you need to combine fields with different names, you can manually adjust and specify how the fields should be mapped. Getting Started Coming Soon Survey Stacks BI -> Survey Stacks BI -> Survey Stacks BI -> Survey Stacks ->Add Your Survey BI -> Survey Stacks ->Add Your Survey -> Select Survey BI -> Survey Stacks ->Add Your Survey -> Select Survey -> Add Survey BI -> Survey Stacks ->Add Your Survey -> Select Survey -> Add Survey-> Autopopulated combined fields from survey BI -> Survey Stacks ->Add Your Survey -> Select Survey -> Add Survey-> Add New combined fields (optional) BI -> Dashboard BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard -> Add Widget BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard -> Add Widget -> Question based BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard -> Add Widget -> Question based -> Data Source BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard -> Add Widget -> Question based -> Data Source -> Stacked Survey BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard -> Add Widget -> Question based -> Data Source -> Select Question BI -> Dashboard-> Create Dashboard -> Add Widget -> Question based -> Data Source -> Select Question -> Select Widget -> Add Widget Glossary Stack: A stack is a dataset that merges data from multiple surveys, allowing you to combine and analyze responses from different sources as if they were part of a single, unified dataset. This is particularly useful when you're conducting similar surveys over time, across different regions, or with different demographic groups, and want to compare or track trends across these variations. Field Mapping:Field mapping is a crucial aspect of creating a stack. Since different surveys might use different names or labels for similar questions or variables, field mapping involves aligning these fields so that they can be combined and compared accurately. This ensures that data from different surveys is organized consistently and that you're not comparing apples to oranges. Auto-Stack:Auto-Stack is an automated feature that streamlines the process of stacking new data as it comes in. Based on pre-saved rules and field mappings that you define, Auto-Stack can automatically merge new survey data into your existing stack, saving you time and effort on manual data integration. This allows you to keep your stacked dataset up-to-date and analyze trends in real time as new responses are collected. summary: Guide to using BI Variables for importing external datasets into QuestionPro.
body: BI Variable Guide to using BI Variables for importing external datasets into QuestionPro. BI Variables BI Variables allow you to import variables from external datasets into QuestionPro. These variables can be mapped to existing survey data or used to create a new dataset. To create BI variables, please follow the following steps: BI » BI Variable BI » BI Variable » New BI Variable BI » BI Variable » New BI Variable » Map to Survey BI » BI Variable » New BI Variable » Map to Survey » Select Survey BI » BI Variable » New BI Variable » Map to Survey » Select Survey » Download template file BI » BI Variable » BI New Variable » Map to Survey » Select Survey » Download template file » Add Data (Numerical, Categorical OR Long Text) BI » BI Variable » New BI Variable » Map to Survey » Select Survey » Download template file » Add Data (Numerical, Categorical OR Long Text) » Upload template file to BI » Create Multi-Select Data Support (Released: August 2025) The platform now supports multi-select data (Select all that apply). To add this type of data, follow these steps: Step 1: In the import template, use row 1 to define variables and their options: Select the variable number and enter the option name after it. Example: Variable 1 - Master Each option should be placed in its own column. The variable number remains the same for all options, while only the option name changes. Step 2: For every column in the import template, set row 2 to Multi select categorical. Step 3: The data in each column should be entered as either 1 (selected) or 0 (not selected). How do I create a dashboard from or use the BI variables in an existing dashboard? You will simply see BI variables as a new data source while creating a dashboard or even while adding widgets to an existing dashboard. Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? title: BI Variable Builder
kw: variable composite add variables step bi data create new dataset name feature survey click select values derived value maximum number upto builder existing directly video customer generated ranges define age group 50 defined default 400 research edition experience help documentation
summary: Help documentation for the BI Variable Builder feature
body: BI Variable Builder Help documentation for the BI Variable Builder feature Composite variables Composite variables are a powerful feature that allows users to create new variables by combining two or more data points from an existing survey. It is designed to eliminate manual, offline workarounds and bring composite metric creation directly into the platform. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video This feature is expected to be released in Q4 2025. How do I create a Composite Variable? Step 1: Navigate to: BI Variables Click on New BI Variable to begin. Step 2: Select Map to Survey. This links the composite variable directly to your survey dataset, allowing it to be used like a survey question in analysis, dashboards, or filters. Select the data source Step 3: Name the dataset and choose Derived. Give your dataset a clear name such as Demographics Derived Data or Customer Segments. Then select the Derived option, indicating that this dataset will be generated using existing data fields. Step 4: Create the BI Variable. Click Create BI Variable to proceed with defining the new variable. You’ll now specify its name and value ranges. Step 5: Define the variable. Enter a descriptive name for your new variable — for example, Age Group or Income Band. This name will appear in reports and visualizations. Step 6: Add the variable values and the conditions associated with it. Define your value ranges. For instance, for an Age Group variable, you might add: 18 to 29 30 to 39 40 to 49 50 or older Each range represents a segment that respondents will be grouped into based on their original data. If there are any respondent that fall outside the boundaries of defined composite variable, select the default category and assign an appropriate default value. Step 7: Save and sync. Once you’ve defined all values, click Create. The new variable will be generated automatically and synced within minutes. The dataset also auto-syncs every 24 hours to keep your data current. Key information What is the maximum number of values I can add in a single composite variable? You can add upto 50 values. What is the maximum number of composite variable I can add? You can add upto 400 variables. What is the maximum number of composite variable I can add? You can add upto 400 variables. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Research Edition Communities Customer Experience Employee Experience University - Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Dashboard sharing
kw: dashboard sharing viewers filters filter data enable base share dashboards controls dynamic curated interactive experience interact shared bi viewer apply internal see insights comments enabled video only access securely feature stakeholders using shareable link whether version sensitive once tailor available
summary: Learn how to share dashboards in QuestionPro BI Dashboard, enable viewer access controls, apply base and dynamic filters, and securely share curated data views.
body: Dashboard sharing Learn how to share dashboards in QuestionPro BI Dashboard, enable viewer access controls, apply base and dynamic filters, and securely share curated data views. Dashboard sharing Introduction The dashboard Sharing feature lets you securely distribute your BI dashboards to internal or external stakeholders using a shareable link. With flexible sharing controls, you can decide exactly what viewers can see, whether it’s insights, comments, or interactive filters. You also have the option to apply a base filter before sharing, giving viewers a curated version of the dashboard without exposing sensitive or internal data. Once sharing is enabled, you can further tailor the viewer experience by choosing which filters are available and whether viewers can interact with them. This ensures you maintain full control over the data while still allowing stakeholders to explore it meaningfully. Watch this video for a quick overview of dashboard sharing in BI: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video How do I share a dashboard externally? Inside the dashboard, click the “Share dashboard” button at the top-right. This opens the sharing configuration panel. How do I enable sharing for my dashboard? Turn on the dashboard sharing toggle. Once enabled, a unique shareable link appears, which you can copy and share with your viewers. What interaction options can I enable for shared dashboards? After enabling sharing, you can choose what viewers can access, including insights, comments, a base filter, and dynamic filters. These settings help tailor the viewer experience and control what data they can interact with. How does the base filter work when sharing a dashboard? When you enable the base filter, you can select a saved filter that defines the default data context for the shared dashboard. This ensures viewers see only a curated and predefined subset of the data. The base filter you apply remains hidden from viewers and is applied silently in the background to prevent exposure of sensitive or internal information. How do dynamic filters work for shared dashboards? Dynamic filters give viewers the ability to interact with the data. You can choose to allow only saved filters or enable fully interactive filters where viewers can adjust filter values themselves. What additional filter controls can I enable for interactive filters? When using interactive filters, you can enable additional controls such as date filtering, response status filtering, and filter value editing to give viewers more flexibility in exploring data. What does the final shared dashboard look like for viewers? Viewers will see a clean, curated version of your dashboard with only the options you’ve enabled. They may interact with filters, view insights, or leave comments, depending on your configuration. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Team Edition Research Edition Communities Customer Experience Employee Experience Wa

title: Dynamic Public Sharing
kw: public dashboard sharing filters shared link step dynamic filter links interactive create configure controls access language configurations viewers open settings permissions support bi click base predefined select experience use allows experiences video allow values option enable customized configurable multilingual dashboards
summary: Dynamic Public Sharing allows you to create customized public dashboard links with configurable filters, permissions, interactive controls, and multilingual support.
body: Dynamic Public Sharing Dynamic Public Sharing allows you to create customized public dashboard links with configurable filters, permissions, interactive controls, and multilingual support. Dynamic Public Sharing Introduction Dynamic Public Sharing in BI allows you to create customized public dashboard experiences using unique shareable links. Instead of duplicating dashboards for different audiences, you can configure individual shared links with their own permissions, filters, interactive controls, commenting access, and language preferences. This makes it easier to securely share tailored dashboard views with executives, clients, partners, or internal teams while maintaining a single source dashboard. Watch this video for a quick overview of Dynamic Public Sharing in BI: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video Core Functionality Custom Public Dashboard Experiences Create multiple public sharing links from a single dashboard with independent configurations and viewing experiences. Dynamic and Interactive Filters Allow viewers to interact with dashboard filters, date ranges, response statuses, and editable filter values. Configurable Access Controls Control whether viewers can access insights, comments, and filter interactions within the shared dashboard. Base Filter Support Apply predefined filters so dashboards always open with specific data conditions. Multilingual Dashboard Support Select the language in which the shared dashboard experience should appear. Step By Step Guide to creating a public sharing link: Step 1: Open the dashboard for which you want to create a public sharing link. Navigate to BI and select the dashboard you want to share publicly. Step 2: Open dashboard settings. Click the Settings option from the dashboard toolbar. Step 3: Navigate to Shared links. Open the Shared links tab to manage public sharing configurations. Step 4: Start creating a new public sharing link. Click the Create button to open the public sharing configuration window. Step 5: Configure basic sharing settings. Provide a link name and configure optional settings such as Insights and Comments access. Step 6: Configure base filters. Enable Base Filters to apply a predefined filter whenever the shared dashboard is opened. Step 7: Configure dynamic filters. Enable Dynamic Filters to allow viewers to interact with dashboard filters in the shared view. Enable Show only saved filters to restrict the shared dashboard to predefined saved filters only. Step 8: Configure interactive filters. Select which interactive filter should be available, along with Date Filters, Response Status, and editable filter values. Step 9: Select dashboard language. Choose the language in which the shared dashboard experience should appear. Step 10: Create the public sharing link. After configuring all required settings, click the Create button to generate the public sharing link. Managing public sharing links: How to edit an existing public sharing li

Use the Edit option from the Actions column to modify existing configurations such as filters, permissions, language preferences, and interactive controls. How to disable a public sharing link? Use the status toggle to temporarily disable public access without deleting the shared link. How to delete a public sharing link? Use the Delete option from the Actions column and confirm the action from the popup to permanently remove the shared link. Glossary Dynamic Public Sharing: A BI capability that allows creation of customizable public dashboard links with independent configurations. Base Filter: A predefined filter automatically applied when a shared dashboard is opened. Dynamic Filters: Interactive filters that viewers can use while exploring the shared dashboard. Interactive Filters: Advanced filter controls that allow viewers to modify filter selections and values directly. Shared link: A publicly accessible dashboard link with customizable permissions and configurations. body: Segment trend BI Helpfile - Segment Trend Segment Trends Help Documentation Segment Trends A robust tool that empowers businesses to monitor and analyze the ever-changing landscape of consumer behavior and preferences over time. This analysis can be focused on specific consumer segments or broader population cohorts. By understanding these dynamic shifts, companies can proactively adapt their products, services, and marketing strategies to better align with evolving consumer expectations and maintain a competitive edge. Segment Trend allows users to draw correlations between specific events and shifts in consumer behavior or perception. These correlations can be further hypothesized and tested using business intelligence (BI) statistical methods to establish causation. By identifying causal relationships, businesses gain a competitive advantage in understanding and connecting with their core customers. Key Features and Benefits Tracks Consumer Behavior and Preferences: Segment Trend allows businesses to monitor how consumer behavior and preferences shift over time. Identifies Trends and Patterns: By analyzing historical data and current market conditions, Segment Trend can identify emerging trends and patterns in consumer behavior. Focuses on Specific Consumer Segments: Businesses can use Segment Trend to track the behavior and preferences of specific consumer segments. Correlates Events with Behavior Shifts: Segment Trend enables users to identify correlations between specific events and shifts in consumer behavior or perception. Supports Hypothesis Testing and Causation: Businesses can test hypotheses and establish causal relationships between events and consumer behavior. Facilitates Competitive Advantage: Understanding and anticipating customer needs helps businesses develop targeted strategies. How to create Segment Trend in BI? Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend > Select Survey > Select Question > Create Segment > Save. Step 1: Login to BI > Add Widget Step 2: Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend Step 3: Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend > Select Survey Step 4: Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend > Select Survey > Select Question > Create Segment > Save. Advanced Segmentation Segment formation is based on the hypothesis you would like to test through your analysis. QP’s BI platform allows you to build complex consumer segments by combining demographics, preferences, psychographics, and usage questions to track patterns among critical user personas. By leveraging the data and insights provided by QuestionPro's BI platform, companies can develop a deeper understanding of their customers and create strategies that drive growth and success. Segment trend

BI Helpfile - Segment Trend

Segment Trends Help Documentation Segment Trends A robust tool that empowers businesses to monitor and analyze the ever-changing landscape of consumer behavior and preferences over time. This analysis can be focused on specific consumer segments or broader population cohorts. By understanding these dynamic shifts, companies can proactively adapt their products, services, and marketing strategies to better align with evolving consumer expectations and maintain a competitive edge. Segment Trend allows users to draw correlations between specific events and shifts in consumer behavior or perception. These correlations can be further hypothesized and tested using business intelligence (BI) statistical methods to establish causation. By identifying causal relationships, businesses gain a competitive advantage in understanding and connecting with their core customers. Key Features and Benefits Tracks Consumer Behavior and Preferences: Segment Trend allows businesses to monitor how consumer behavior and preferences shift over time. Identifies Trends and Patterns: By analyzing historical data and current market conditions, Segment Trend can identify emerging trends and patterns in consumer behavior. Focuses on Specific Consumer Segments: Businesses can use Segment Trend to track the behavior and preferences of specific consumer segments. Correlates Events with Behavior Shifts: Segment Trend enables users to identify correlations between specific events and shifts in consumer behavior or perception. Supports Hypothesis Testing and Causation: Businesses can test hypotheses and establish causal relationships between events and consumer behavior. Facilitates Competitive Advantage: Understanding and anticipating customer needs helps businesses develop targeted strategies. How to create Segment Trend in BI? Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend > Select Survey > Select Question > Create Segment > Save. Step 1: Login to BI > Add Widget Step 2: Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend Step 3: Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend > Select Survey Step 4: Login to BI > Add Widget > Segment Trend > Select Survey > Select Question > Create Segment > Save. Advanced Segmentation Segment formation is based on the hypothesis you would like to test through your analysis. QP’s BI platform allows you to build complex consumer segments by combining demographics, preferences, psychographics, and usage questions to track patterns among critical user personas. By leveraging the data and insights provided by QuestionPro's BI platform, companies can develop a deeper understanding of their customers and create strategies that drive growth and success. Step 1: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings Step 2: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Segment > Create Segment Step 3: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Segment > Create Segment > Save Aggregate Overview and Drill Down Capabilities While analysing trends it becomes essential to zoom out to have an overall understanding of changing trends or zoom in to a specific time period to see if there is any correlation between an event and consumer behavior. The following capability is available in the Segment Trends → Analytics section where the user could either choose if they would zoom on to yearly / quarterly trends or zoom in to monthly and weekly trends. Step 1: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings Step 2: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Analytics > Weekly (Interval) Moving Averages in Segment Trend Moving averages are used to smooth time-series data by calculating a continuously updated average price, which helps to mitigate temporary fluctuations. Time-series data can sometimes show sharp increases or decreases in trends that aren't caused by a specific event or factor. In these cases, using moving averages can help to reduce these abnormal variations. The system's default configuration includes the calculation of two moving averages i.e. the current period and the preceding two periods, within the time-series data. The duration for calculating the moving average is configurable to align with the specific parameters of the research. Step 1: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings Step 2: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Analytics Step 3: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Analytics > Moving Average Exclude Responses from Trend Certain time intervals might have low responses for no specific reason, potentially affecting moving average computations. If the objective is to maintain a minimum base per period and mitigate this impact, select the 'Exclude Responses from Trend' feature. BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings> Analytics > Exclude Responses from Trend Custom Segment in Segment Trend By default the system calculates proportions on the total number of completed responses. However with Custom Segment, we could further carve the segment by adding the denominator to any variable. The default calculation for proportions within the system is based on the total number of completed responses. However, the Custom Segment feature offers more flexibility. With Custom Segment, you can create a more specific segment by defining a denominator based on any variable to all the segments shown in the widget. The following allows you to focus on specific groups within your data and gain more targeted insights. Step 1: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings> Segment Step 2: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Segment > Add denominator Step 3: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Segment > Add denominator > Step 4: BI Segment Trend Widget > Settings > Segment > Add denominator > Save Glossary Interval: Measured along a numerical scale that has equal distances between adjacent values, mostly periodical scale in Segment Trends. title: Create RIM / raked weighting scheme
kw: weighting scheme rim step raked data select survey population iterations create weights add name next efficiency raking sample represent individuals due research samples first iteration dimension totals second weightings new weight questions filter proportions save click required minutes see status
summary: Raking or RIM weighting
body: Create RIM / raked weighting scheme Raking or RIM weighting Raked / RIM weighting In a survey we design a sample with the objective to represent the population and provide us insights about the population which would help researchers to draw inferences and conclusive findings for business or public agencies. When we sample for a survey assign equal probability for individuals to be selected who would represent the population. However due to availability of individuals OR due to sensitivity of the research subjects OR accessibility of samples sometimes our samples could get skewed and would have the right representation of the population. In RIM weighting we consider the an iterative proportional fitting procedure estimates the individual weights. The first iteration computes weights to match the first dimension (weighting variable) totals, the second iteration matches the second dimension totals, and so on. These steps for all the dimensions are performed repeatedly unless convergence is achieved within an acceptable margin of error. How to create a raking / RIM weighting scheme for your data? Login to BI » Weightings » New weight scheme » Raked weighting » Select survey » Select the questions » Select data filter » Add a name » Add the proportions » Save Click to download video Step 1: Weightings » New weight scheme Step 2: Raked weighting » Next Step 3: Select survey Step 4: Select questions » Next Step 5 (Optional): Create the required filter » Next Step 5: Give a name » Add the proportions » Save After the weighting scheme is created Processing can take anywhere from 1 minutes to 60 minutes. You can see the status here: You can click on the weighting scheme name to see the stats for that particular scheme: Important information: Efficiency: At what percentage of data efficiency did the weights converge.. Iterations: The number of iterations required Status: Success (The data converged successfully) | Max stuck (More iterations could not improve the efficiency) | max iter (Reached the maximum number of iterations before data could converge) License This feature is available with the following licenses : Research Edition Communities Customer Experience Employee Experience Was the article helpful? content:
Create RIM / raked weighting scheme

Raking or RIM weighting

Raked / RIM weighting In a survey we design a sample with the objective to represent the population and provide us insights about the population which would help researchers to draw inferences and conclusive findings for business or public agencies. When we sample for a survey assign equal probability for individuals to be selected who would represent the population. However due to availability of individuals OR due to sensitivity of the research subjects OR accessibility of samples sometimes our samples could get skewed and would have the right representation of the population. In RIM weighting we consider the an iterative proportional fitting procedure estimates the individual weights. The first iteration computes weights to match the first dimension (weighting variable) totals, the second iteration matches the second dimension totals, and so on. These steps for all the dimensions are performed repeatedly unless convergence is achieved within an acceptable margin of error. How to create a raking / RIM weighting scheme for your data? title: Advanced cross-tabulation
kw: cross report tab bi advanced across row column tabulation variables proportion add select columns responses customize analytics click my rows questionpro reports data download within total all create questions tests configure each mean average test confidence labels dashboards chi square
summary: Learn how to create and customize an advanced cross-tabulation report in QuestionPro BI. This tutorial covers setup, analytics options, labeling, and exporting reports for seamless integration into yo
body: Advanced cross-tabulation Learn how to create and customize an advanced cross-tabulation report in QuestionPro BI. This tutorial covers setup, analytics options, labeling, and exporting reports for seamless integration into your BI dashboards. Advanced Cross-Tabulation in QuestionPro BI The Advanced Cross-Tabulation Report in QuestionPro BI is designed to help you uncover deeper insights from your survey data. With just a few clicks, you can run cross-tabulations across multiple questions or custom variables, apply advanced analytics like Chi-Square and proportion tests, and customize how results are displayed. Whether you’re preparing a quick snapshot or a detailed analysis, this feature makes it easy to explore relationships in your data and integrate reports seamlessly into your BI dashboards. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video How do I add an advanced cross tabulation report? To add an advanced cross-tabulation report, follow these steps: Navigate to Reports. Select Create report Select crosstab report and give report name Select a data source for the cross-tab Confirm data source » Create Once created, a default report will be genarated with first two variables from the selected dataset How do I configure my cross-tab report? To configure the cross-tab: Select settings (top-right hand corner) Drag variables / questions from question list and drop them in rows (stubs) and columns (banner). Variables in cross-tab reports could be further drilled down by selecting questions or BI variables from the drop-down. BI variables could also be added to the cross-tab as Stubs (rows) or Banners (columns). How do I configure the analytics? To add advanced analytics to the cross-tab: In the Analytics tab, choose from the following analysis: Count: Number of responses (or respondents) in each cell. Row percentage: Proportion of responses within a row distributed across columns (100% per row). Column percentage: Proportion of responses within each column relative to that column’s total. Row overall: Total responses or percentages across all columns for that specific row. Column overall: Distribution of responses across all rows within each column. Row total and Column total: Sum of all responses across rows/columns. Mean and Average: Arithmetic mean (sum/count) or average shown across categories to compare groups. Row and Column Mean: Average of values across all columns/rows. Row and Column heatmap: Highlights patterns and intensity with shading for quick trend spotting. Fisher's Exact Test: Tests non-random associations between two categorical variables (small sample sizes). Chi-Square: Tests whether differences between observed and expected frequencies across categories are statistically significant. Confidence Interval for proportion test: A confidence interval for a proportion test in cross-tabs gives the range of values within which the true population proportion is likely to fall, with a chosen level of confidence

body: BI dashboard limits bi dashboard limits BI dashboard limits We need to have certain limits on BI dashboard, mainly to maintain our strict UX standards. We want all dashboards and widgets to load within seconds, and for that, we have put in certain limitations / guard rails. Following are the key limitations: Description Limit Number of widgets in a tab 40 Number of answers in a widget No limit. Pie/Donut/Semi-Circle/Donut:max answers 10 Number of tabs 10 Data slicer creation 100 Apply data slicers to a widget, tab or dashboard 10 Number of colours in a custom theme 50 Surveys in a dataset 10 Questions in a basic cross tab 10 Questions in an advanced cross tab Coming soon Height of a widget. (In pixels) 960 AI insights (char limit) 400 Insight comments in a dashboard 1,000 Comment reply for a single AI insight or widget 100 Questions in a comparative bar chart 10 Questions in a heatmap widget 50 Segments in a heatmap widget 50 Widget level filters 100 Number of rules in a filter 10 Can any of these limits be increased Please reach out to your customer success manager. In rare cases, depending on the requirement, we can make extensions. There will be a cost assoicated for any such requests. summary: Learn how to create an Advanced Text Analytics dashboard in QuestionPro TextAI, select a data source, configure codebooks, choose questions, add context, and track dashboard creation status.
body: Advanced Text Analytics Dashboard Learn how to create an Advanced Text Analytics dashboard in QuestionPro TextAI, select a data source, configure codebooks, choose questions, add context, and track dashboard creation status. Advanced Text Analytics in TextAI Introduction Advanced Text Analytics in QuestionPro TextAI helps teams turn open-ended feedback into structured, actionable themes. By selecting a source such as a survey, Customer Experience project, Employee Experience project, or dataset, you can create a TextAI dashboard that applies topic modeling to qualitative responses and highlights the patterns that matter most. The setup flow lets you define the dashboard goal, choose an output language, generate or upload a codebook, select the questions to analyze, and provide question-level context for more accurate results. This helps product, research, customer experience, and employee experience teams organize large volumes of text feedback without manually reading and tagging every response. Watch this video for a quick overview of Advanced Text Analytics in TextAI: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video How do I create an Advanced Text Analytics dashboard in TextAI? Step 1: Go to bi.questionpro.com, and open TextAI from the left navigation menu. Step 2: On the TextAI dashboards page, click Create Dashboard to start a new dashboard. Step 3: To get started, choose the type of data you want to analyze, such as Surveys, Customer Experience, Employee Experience, or Datasets. Step 3.1: If you choose Datasets as the data source, select one of the available datasets to use as the source. Step 4: Enter a clear dashboard name so the report is easy to identify later. Step 5: Choose the preferred output language for the TextAI dashboard. Step 6: Add the goal for topic modeling to guide how TextAI should interpret and organize the responses. Step 7: Choose from the available methods for creating or selecting a codebook. Step 7.1: To let QuestionPro AI generate the codebook for you, select Generate with QuestionPro AI. Step 7.2: To upload your own codebook, select Upload from template file. Step 7.3: To use a codebook from an existing dashboard, select Upload from report codebook. Step 8: Click Next to continue to the question selection step. Step 9: Select the questions for which you want to run Advanced Text Analytics. Use the checkboxes to include or exclude individual questions from the analysis. Step 10: Add question-level context that explains what each question is intended to capture. Step 11: If the selected questions cover different topics or contexts, enable separate dashboard creation to create multiple dashboards—one for each group of related questions. Step 12: Click Create Dashboard to start generating the Advanced Text Analytics dashboard. Step 13: Wait while the dashboard creation process starts. Step 14: Return to the dashboard list to view the new dashboard and monitor its status. Step 15: When multiple

Step 3: To get started, choose the type of data you want to analyze, such as Surveys, Customer Experience, Employee Experience, or Datasets. body: Sub-topic stacked bar Sub-topic stacked bar Sub-topic stacked bar What is a sub-topic stacked bar? A Stacked Bar enables users to visualize data through stacked bar charts. The Sub-topic Stacked Bar builds on this by automatically incorporating all sub-topics generated by QuestionPro AI as drill-downs under their corresponding main topics. Users can view these sub-topics by clicking the arrow to the left of each topic. This functionality streamlines the exploration and comparison of hundreds of sub-topics within a single, intuitive interface. This widget is scheduled for beta release on August 15, 2025, and will be made available to all clients by October 15, 2025. How do I add a sub-topic stacked bar? Click to download video Step 1: Click on add widget Step 2: Select the required question. Step 3: Give it a name (optional) >> Select sub-topic stacked bar How do I actually see the sub-topics? To see the sub-topics, you need to drill-down into one of the topics. To do this, simple click on the arrow on the left of the topic. title: Use your own codebook

summary: Use your own codebook
body: Use your own codebook Use your own codebook Use your own codebook for topic analysis. QuestionPro AI automatically analyzes all open-ended text data to generate relevant topics and sub-topics. However, if you upload your own codebook, the system will bypass auto-generation and instead tag all responses based on the topics and sub-topics defined in your uploaded file.This feature is especially useful when you want to maintain consistency in your topic structure, such as when tracking trends over time. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video This feature will be available to beta clients by the end of August 2025, and will be rolled out to all clients by the end of October 2025. How do I use my codebook for topic generation? To use your own codebook, please follow the following steps: Step 1: Navigate to the TextAI » Click on Create dashboard » Select advanced analytics » Click on continue to proceed Step 2: Select the source and survey. Step 3: Select an existing industry or create a new one Step 4: Add the goal for the topic modeling » Enable Upload codebook » Download Step 5: Add the topics » Add the sub-topics Sub-topic need to be comma separated. Step 6: Upload the Excel file back in the system and and click on create. How long does it take to process the data? Once you upload the codebook, QuestionPro AI will begin processing the responses based on the provided instructions. The processing time typically ranges from 1 to 8 hours, depending on the volume of responses. You can proceed to add the desired widgets to your dashboard immediately. The data will begin populating in real-time as the processing progresses. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Research Edition Communities Customer Experience Employee Experience University - Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Learn about Workspaces in QuestionPro BI, how they help organize dashboards and enable collaboration across teams.
body: Introduction to workspaces Learn about Workspaces in QuestionPro BI, how they help organize dashboards and enable collaboration across teams. Introduction to Workspaces Introduction Workspaces in QuestionPro Business Intelligence help you organize your dashboards, reports, and analysis in a structured and meaningful way. Instead of managing all content in a single place, workspaces allow you to group related dashboards and reports based on projects, teams, or use cases. This makes it easier to navigate, manage, and maintain your analytical assets as your work grows. In addition to organization, workspaces also support collaboration by allowing you to share access with other users in your organization. Workspaces are designed to: Organize dashboards and reports into dedicated environments. Simplify navigation and content management. Enable collaboration through shared access. How Workspaces Work A workspace contains dashboards, reports, and other analytical assets created by users. These assets are grouped together to provide a focused view of a specific project or area of analysis. Users can share access to their workspace with others in the organization. Depending on the level of access provided: Read Access: Users can view dashboards and reports within the workspace. Write Access: Users can create, edit, and manage content within the workspace. This ensures that teams can work together while maintaining control over content changes. Create a new Workspace This feature is expected to be released in July 2026. Key Benefits of Workspaces Better Organization: Keep related dashboards and reports grouped together. Improved Navigation: Quickly find and manage content within a structured environment. Collaboration: Share workspaces with team members to collaboratively create, edit, and manage dashboards and reports. Access Control: Define who can view or edit content. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Team Edition Research Edition Communities Customer Experience Employee Experience

body: Organization workspace Learn about Organization Workspace in QuestionPro BI, a centralized workspace that enables organization-wide access and collaboration on dashboards and reports. Organization Workspace This feature is expected to be released in June 2026. Introduction The Organization Workspace is a centralized workspace designed for organization-wide access and collaboration in QuestionPro BI. Unlike individual workspaces, which are typically used for specific projects or teams, the Organization Workspace serves as a common environment where all users within the organization can access and contribute to shared dashboards and reports. This makes it easier to maintain a single source of truth and ensures that important insights are accessible to everyone in the organization. How Organization Workspace Works The Organization Workspace is accessible to all users within the organization, providing a shared environment for viewing and contributing to dashboards and reports. Users can: Access shared dashboards and reports created across the organization. Contribute by creating or updating content within the workspace. Collaborate in a single, unified environment. This setup helps reduce duplication of work and ensures consistency in reporting and analysis. Key Benefits of Organization Workspace Centralized Access: All users can access shared dashboards and reports in one place. Improved Collaboration: Teams can contribute to and build on shared work without duplication. Consistency: Maintain a single source of truth for reporting and insights. Visibility: Ensure important insights are easily accessible across the organization. title: Manage permissions
kw: access workspace permissions edit users read step manage workspaces shared organization level click bi content assign levels view dashboards team user sharing control ensure create reports analytical assets maintaining specific video within need open only questionpro including share contribute modify
summary: Learn how to manage workspace permissions in QuestionPro BI, including how to share read and edit access and access workspaces shared with you.
body: Manage permissions Learn how to manage workspace permissions in QuestionPro BI, including how to share read and edit access and access workspaces shared with you. Manage Permissions Introduction Workspace permissions in QuestionPro BI allow you to control who can access and contribute to your workspace. By assigning appropriate access levels, you can ensure that the right users can view, create, and modify dashboards, reports, and analytical assets while maintaining control over your content. Permissions can be assigned at multiple levels, including organization-wide access, team-level access, and individual user access. This flexibility allows you to manage collaboration at scale while still maintaining granular control when needed. Whether you are sharing a workspace broadly across your organization or with a few specific users, permissions help ensure that access is both controlled and aligned with user responsibilities. Watch this video for a quick overview of how to manage permissions in the BI workspace: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video Permission Levels When sharing a workspace, you can assign either Read Access or Edit Access depending on the level of interaction required. Users with Read Access can view dashboards, reports, and analytical assets within the workspace, but they cannot make any changes. This level is suitable for stakeholders who need visibility into insights without modifying content. Users with Edit Access can create, update, and manage dashboards, reports, and analytical assets within the workspace. This level is intended for users who actively contribute to building and maintaining content. Sharing a Workspace Follow these steps to share your workspace with other users: Step 1: Open Workspaces. From the BI navigation panel, click Workspaces. Step 2: Open sharing settings. Hover over the workspace and click the padlock icon. Step 3: Configure general access. In the popup, use General Access to assign Read or Edit access to everyone in your organization. Step 4: Add specific permissions. Click Add Permissions to grant access to specific users or teams within your organization. Step 5: Assign access levels. Select Users or Teams and assign them the desired Read or Edit access. Individual permissions override team and organization-level permissions. For example, if organization or team access is set to Read, but a user is granted Edit access individually, the user will have Edit access. Accessing a Shared Workspace When a workspace is shared with you, it will appear in the Shared Workspaces section. Step 1: Navigate to Workspaces. Click Workspaces from the BI navigation panel. Step 2: Locate the shared workspace. Find the workspace under the Shared Workspaces section. Step 3: Open the workspace. Click on a workspace to jump in and start working. Your level of access (read or edit) will determine whether you can only view content or also make changes. Best Practices Assign Edit Access

title: Login
kw: login attempt attempts unsuccessful if portal consecutive user case first second then communities member number account either 24 hours reset password able see error message stating enter valid credentials happen third successful again license platform ve added security layer side
summary: Login, Communities platform, member portal
body: Login Login, Communities platform, member portal Portal Login We've added a security layer on the member portal login side, where, the number of unsuccessful tries plays a major role. It means that, on the portal, we'll be providing the maximum number of 6 attempts where the user has to login successfully to the account. If the user has consecutively 6 login fail attempts, their account will either be blocked for 24 hours or they will have to reset the password. Click to download video What will be the case, if I have unsuccessful login for the first or second attempt? If it's the first or second attempt, you will be able to see an error message stating "Enter valid credentials." What will happen if I have an unsuccessful login for the third/fourth/fifth attempt? If it's a consecutive failure attempt after the first and second, you will be able to see an error message stating "Enter valid credentials. N Attempts Remaining." where N = Total attempts (6) - Current attempt When it's a third consecutive unsuccessful attempt - What will happen if I have an unsuccessful login for the sixth attempt? You will either need to wait 24 hours to regain the new 6 attempts or reset the password. What will be the case, if I have an unsuccessful login for the consecutive 4 attempts and then one successful and then again an unsuccessful attempt? In the case mentioned above, as the chain of consecutive 6 failed attempts is broken, then post the successful attempt, the user will again receive 6 attempts for the retry. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Update Community Status
body: Update community status Update Community Status Update Community Status You can change your community status to active, inactive or in Draft mode. If you would like to make any changes to your community, you can change the status to draft and work on it, once done with the changes, you can make the community active. Similarly, if you wish to make any community inactive, you can change the status to Inactive. How to change the status of the Community? Step 1: Click on Community. Step 2: Click on status dropdown. Step 3: Select the status you want to change it to. Active: Your community will be accessible to all members. They can login and use all the options available. Inactive: Community users won’t be able to login to the community if the status of the community is set to inactive. Draft: In this mode, the community is neither active nor inactive. It means that the admin/moderator is still designing and in the process of making a community live. From November 2, 2023, onwards, Communities product (both member and admin portal) will not be fully supported on IE Browser. title: Delete community
kw: community deleted communities delete permanently any click list days see yes page once all recycle bin seven retrieve back warning message marked restored panel urls no if option participants admin member available license point deletion gets grace period active go
summary: Delete Community
body: Delete community Delete Community Delete Community The community can be deleted at any point. After deletion, we have the Community in the list for seven days and it gets deleted permanently after 7 days. In the grace period of seven days, you can retrieve the community back to the active list. To delete a Community, go To: Login >> Communities >> Select/Hover over the Community Click on the Delete icon. You will see a warning message, click on Yes to delete the Community. When a Community is deleted from the listing page,the community is marked as deleted. The community remains in the deleted list and can be restored by the panel administrator. Once marked as deleted, the community landing page and any direct URLs are no longer accessible to panel members. Where can I see all my deleted communities? The deleted community will be moved to another folder - Recycle Bin. Recycle Bin will be seen only if there are any deleted communities/community in it. Can the deleted community/communities be retrieved? You can retrieve the community/communities after clicking on the Restore option. Can the community/communities be permanently deleted? If you want to delete the community permanently, you can click on the Empty Recycle Bin option and then on the warning message, click on Yes. Communities once permanently deleted cannot be restored back. Once the Community is permanently deleted: All pages and URLs become inaccessible to participants. Participants visiting the old URL will see: “The community page is unavailable. Please contact your admin for more details.” All member data, collected research, discussions, and configurations are permanently erased. The associated domain becomes available for reuse by any Community. From November 2, 2023, onwards, Communities product (both member and admin portal) will not be fully supported on IE Browser. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Members wil be able to reset passwords using their registered phone number or the associated email address in the community.
body: Password Reset Members wil be able to reset passwords using their registered phone number or the associated email address in the community. Password Reset Why do members need to reset passwords? As a member of the community, you should be able to reset your password. You can use this using your registered phone number or the email address associated with your account. Click to download video How does reset password using registered phone numbers work? Members will be able to reset their passwords using their registered number, on the login page click on the forgot password button. A pop up with options to reset password using email address or phone number will appear. Enter your email address and select the Using Phone Number option and hit recover password to reset your password. If you do not have a phone numer registered to your profile, you will get an error message. However, if your profile has a registered number, you will get a success message saying "A temporary password has been sent to your phone number" You can use the temporary password received as an sms on your phone to login into your community Once you've entered the temporary password, you will be asked to create a new password for your profile. How does reset password using email work? Members will also be able to reset their passwords using their registered email. Enter your email address and select the Using Email Address option and hit recover password to reset your password. If the entered email address is incorrect, you will get an error message. However, if your enterd email address is correct, you will get instructions on how to reset the password on the entered email address. Follow the steps, and then you will be asked to create a new password for your profile. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Best Practices
kw: best community survey points practices email 100 many research minutes guidelines activity 50 brand name my there no example need incentives good response rates here some general under 150 na get variables important take about so text qualitative activities edition
summary: Community best practices
body: Best Practices Community best practices Community Best Practices How many points should I give my members? There is no fixed rule, every community can be different. Example a pupose based community does not need very high incentives, but a research community does need good incentives for good response rates. That being said, here are some general guidelines - Activity Time needed to complete Recommended points Verify phone and email Under 2 minutes 50 to 100 Qualifying survey 5 to 8 minutes 80 to 150 Survey Upto 8 minutes 20 to 40 Survey Over 8 minutes 100 to 150 Signin to the mobile app NA 50 to 100 Refer a friend NA 50 to 100 Answer a poll Under a minute 5 to 10 Points conversion: 100 points = USD 1.00 How get get the best engagement from my email invitaiotns? There are many variables to boost your email open / response rates. Here are some important factors - Subject: Should be standard, to help recognise your brand. The best way to do it is your panel name followed by the survey name. (Example: QuestionPro idealabs: Beverage research survey) Body: This follows all the guidelines of general email best practices. However, for a community activity invitation, how long it would take and how many points would be awarded for the activity. Please be careful about what information you share, sharing the survey name for instance could lead to your responses getting skewed. Look and feel: The most important element is consistancy, so that your email represents your community brand. You should have a logo, and follow your brand guidelines in terms of text size, color, etc. Link text: It is reccomended to keep it simple. So something like "Start survey" or "Take survey" is the best. What about qualitative activities? Points for qualitative activities are extremely hard to decipher and involve too many variables to provide a points guide. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Research Edition Communities University - Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

Community Best Practices How many points should I give my members? There is no fixed rule, every community can be different. Example a pupose based community does not need very high incentives, but a research community does need good incentives for good response rates. That being said, here are some general guidelines - Activity Time needed to complete Recommended points Verify phone and email Under 2 minutes 50 to 100 Qualifying survey 5 to 8 minutes 80 to 150 Survey Upto 8 minutes 20 to 40 Survey Over 8 minutes 100 to 150 Signin to the mobile app NA 50 to 100 Refer a friend NA 50 to 100 Answer a poll Under a minute 5 to 10 Points conversion: 100 points = USD 1.00 How get get the best engagement from my email invitaiotns? There are many variables to boost your email open / response rates. Here are some important factors - Subject: Should be standard, to help recognise your brand. The best way to do it is your panel name followed by the survey name. (Example: QuestionPro idealabs: Beverage research survey) Body: This follows all the guidelines of general email best practices. However, for a community activity invitation, how long it would take and how many points would be awarded for the activity. Please be careful about what information you share, sharing the survey name for instance could lead to your responses getting skewed. Look and feel: The most important element is consistancy, so that your email represents your community brand. You should have a logo, and follow your brand guidelines in terms of text size, color, etc. Link text: It is reccomended to keep it simple. So something like "Start survey" or "Take survey" is the best. What about qualitative activities? Points for qualitative activities are extremely hard to decipher and involve too many variables to provide a points guide. summary: Portal Link and Recruitment URL - Communities
body: Preview community Portal Link and Recruitment URL - Communities Portal Link and Recruitment URL - Communities In communities platform, we offer 2 links to the members - Portal Link Recruitment Link Click to download video A portal link allows you to direct your members to your Community's home page where they can further Login/Signup based on their preferences. What is a Recruitment URL Link? The Recruitment URL allows you to direct your recruitment traffic to a URL where they have to first fill in the Profiling form and answer all profile related questions and then can proceed with further signing up. This is solely done to filter the members per need. Can I use any variable I want (including symbols like . _ - / etc)? The tracking variable is stored in a cookie. In general, you can use whatever symbols that browser cookies can handle. However, keep in mind that the link is in the URL - so if you have characters like "&" etc. they need to be HTML Escaped. Our recommendation is to not put any special characters in the value of the source field. Values like "&" and "?" interfere with the HTML URL and may not be captured correctly. Since November 2, 2023, the member portal and admin portal is not supported on Internet Explorer. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Community Settings
body: Community Settings Community Settings Community Settings What is this community settings section? This section allows the Moderator or Admin to apply certain settings to the community. You can go to the settings page via - Go to Community >> Settings. For more details refer to the below sections. What is this Default Mode? The default mode is the selection of the page where the admin or moderator would like the community users to land by default when they log in to the community. By default in a community, the users will be landed on the "Member Dashboard" page, however, this can be changed from the drop-down selection. If the Default Mode selected is Member Dashboard, then the user (on the portal) when logs in for the first time, will NOT see the welcome message. However, if the Default Mode selection is anything else apart from Member Dashbaord, then the user when refreshes the page or logs in for the 1st time, will see the welcome message. What is a Private Community? A private community is a community for which you will never be able to sign up i.e. never will you see a Signup button on the landing page. You could either login to the community by entering the username or email address and password or via the recruitment link. If your Private community is toggled on, please DO NOT toggle on the Social Only Community from the Security settings. What is a New Landing Page? In the Communities platform, we offer 2 kinds of landing pages i.e. Old and New. In order to provide the new landing page experience to the users, toggle this functionality. The only difference is the level of customisation possible for both types of landing pages. What is this logo field? While setting up the community, we let the admin/moderator choose the logo that they would like to get displayed on the landing pages. However, if they wish to edit in future, they can upload a new logo by clicking on the current image and then updating the desired image. What is the functionality of the Portal Dashboard Image toggle? A moderator/admin should toggle this on if they want the users to see cards/tiles on the member dashboard page. So, if you toggle this on, Portal Images open subsequently. These images are the images that get pre-populated on the cards like- Topics/Idea/Discussion/Surveys etc on the user portal. These tiles are just for informational purposes that indicate to a user upfront what they can expect in the community and then if there are new surveys/new topics added, they'll get a hint of it upfront from the dashboard. What is the functionality of the Carousel Banner toggle? Carousels are the banners present on the top of the community. So, if the admin/moderator feels to add banners on a seasonality basis or if they wish to communicate something to the users, they can toggle this on. The admin can now add a hyperlink to the banners, which means if the end users click on those banners, they'll be landing on that page in the same window though. When this Carousel Ban
content:
Community Settings

Community Settings

Community Settings What is this community settings section? This section allows the Moderator or Admin to apply certain settings to the community. You can go to the settings page via - Go to Community >> Settings. For more details refer to the below sections. What is this Default Mode? The default mode is the selection of the page where the admin or moderator would like the community users to land by default when they log in to the community. By default in a community, the users will be landed on the "Member Dashboard" page, however, this can be changed from the drop-down selection. If the Default Mode selected is Member Dashboard, then the user (on the portal) when logs in for the first time, will NOT see the welcome message. However, if the Default Mode selection is anything else apart from Member Dashbaord, then the user when refreshes the page or logs in for the 1st time, will see the welcome message. What is a Private Community? A private community is a community for which you will never be able to sign up i.e. never will you see a Signup button on the landing page. You could either login to the community by entering the username or email address and password or via the recruitment link. If your Private community is toggled on, please DO NOT toggle on the Social Only Community from the Security settings. What is a New Landing Page? In the Communities platform, we offer 2 kinds of landing pages i.e. Old and New. In order to provide the new landing page experience to the users, toggle this functionality. The only difference is the level of customisation possible for both types of landing pages. What is this logo field? While setting up the community, we let the admin/moderator choose the logo that they would like to get displayed on the landing pages. However, if they wish to edit in future, they can upload a new logo by clicking on the current image and then updating the desired image. What is the functionality of the Portal Dashboard Image toggle? A moderator/admin should toggle this on if they want the users to see cards/tiles on the member dashboard page. So, if you toggle this on, Portal Images open subsequently. These images are the images that get pre-populated on the cards like- Topics/Idea/Discussion/Surveys etc on the user portal. These tiles are just for informational purposes that indicate to a user upfront what they can expect in the community and then if there are new surveys/new topics added, they'll get a hint of it upfront from the dashboard. What is the functionality of the Carousel Banner toggle? Carousels are the banners present on the top of the community. So, if the admin/moderator feels to add banners on a seasonality basis or if they wish to communicate something to the users, they can toggle this on. The admin can now add a hyperlink to the banners, which means if the end users click on those banners, they'll be landing on that page in the same window though. When this Carousel Banner is toggled on, by default you will see other 2 settings too - Auto Play and Slide Speed. Auto Play allows the banners to be automatically slid and the speed informs that after X seconds the banner will slide. If you do not wish to have the carousels auto-played, you can toggle that off and consequently, the slide speed box will also disappear. Please note that the links to be added in the carousel boxes needs to be have https format. Links like www.google.com won't work as we'd need https links in the text box. title: Survey Opt-Out for Members
kw: opt survey surveys member opted members see setting tab settings page send option off letting if not however enable let community feature clicking once toggled points no license now wish participate only done admin moderator click download video going toggle
summary: Member opt-out of surveys
body: Survey Opt-Out for Members Member opt-out of surveys Letting members opt-out of surveys Members can now opt out of surveys if they do not wish to participate, however, this setting can only be done by the admin/moderator. Click to download video How to enable the setting to let members opt-out of surveys for your community? You can enable this feature by going to the community settings page and toggle on the Survey Opt-Out setting and then clicking on the "Save" button. Once this setting is toggled on, in the send screen, you will see an option Opt Out which will by default be toggled on. Why are there 2 Opt-out toggles - One in Send and another in Settings? Let's consider the cases below for better understanding - Survey Opt-Out (Settings Page) Survey Opt-Out (Send Page) Result ON ON Member can opt out of the survey as they will see an option of Opt-out and will also see a tab named as "Opted Out" where they can view all the surveys from which they have opted out of. ON OFF Member can not opt out of the survey, however, they can still see the "Opted Out" tab. OFF Automatically OFF Member can neither opt out from the survey and nor will they be able to see the Opted out tab. How can a member opt out from the survey and where will the opted out surveys be visible? The member can opt out by clicking on the Opt Out option present besides the survey. Once the member has opted out from the surveys, those surveys will appear in the Opted Out tab. If a survey has points allocated to it and members opts out from that survey, they will no longer get those points. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Security Settings
body: Security Security Settings Security Settings What is Username setting? This setting is provided to ensure anonymity is maintained across the member portal platform. There are multiple modules in Communities where the members interact in open forums. Enabling this setting would ensure the username of the community members is displayed all across the platform, and hence, the privacy of the community members is protected. What is Code Based Sign Up ? To enable code based signup so that only authentic members can join the panel, you have to enable the "Code Based Signup" option under Security settings option for the panel. Go to: Communities » Set Up » Security How does the code based sign up work on the panel? Once you have enabled the code based sign up setting for your panel the "Passcode" field will be asked on the sign up form. Only passcode that matches to the one that you set for the panel is accepted for registration. This way your panel is not a public panel and only members whom you share your passcode with can access/ register to the panel. What is SSL Security (https)? When the SSL Option is checked, the links for the surveys are automatically encoded using SSL (Secure Socket Layer) the industry standard encryption technology. This makes sure that no one else has access to the data and adds another added layer of security. The SSL technology encrypts data that gets passed between our servers and the end-user survey respondent. What is https? Secure Socket Layer (SSL) is a cryptographic protocol that provides communication security over the Internet. Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) is a widely-used communications protocol for secure communication over a computer network, with especially wide deployment on the Internet. Technically, it is not a protocol in itself; rather, it is the result of simply layering the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) on top of the SSL protocol, thus adding the security capabilities of SSL/TLS to standard HTTP communications. HTTPS provides authentication of the web site and associated web server that one is communicating with, which protects against Man-in-the-middle attacks. Additionally, it provides bidirectional encryption of communications between a client and server, which protects against eavesdropping and tampering with and/or forging the contents of the communication. In practice, this provides a reasonable guarantee that one is communicating with precisely the web site that one intended to communicate with (as opposed to an impostor), as well as ensuring that the contents of communications between the user and site cannot be read or forged by any third party. How can I enable SSL security on the community? To enable SSL go to: Communities » Set Up » Security What is Verification To View Profile? When a new member signs up for community and enter their email address, an email is sent to the member. The email contains the email verification link. The new member will have to click on the link to
content:
Security

Security Settings

Security Settings What is Username setting? This setting is provided to ensure anonymity is maintained across the member portal platform. There are multiple modules in Communities where the members interact in open forums. Enabling this setting would ensure the username of the community members is displayed all across the platform, and hence, the privacy of the community members is protected. What is Code Based Sign Up ? To enable code based signup so that only authentic members can join the panel, you have to enable the "Code Based Signup" option under Security settings option for the panel. Go to: Communities » Set Up » Security How does the code based sign up work on the panel? Once you have enabled the code based sign up setting for your panel the "Passcode" field will be asked on the sign up form. Only passcode that matches to the one that you set for the panel is accepted for registration. This way your panel is not a public panel and only members whom you share your passcode with can access/ register to the panel. What is SSL Security (https)? When the SSL Option is checked, the links for the surveys are automatically encoded using SSL (Secure Socket Layer) the industry standard encryption technology. This makes sure that no one else has access to the data and adds another added layer of security. The SSL technology encrypts data that gets passed between our servers and the end-user -- survey respondent. What is https? Secure Socket Layer (SSL) is a cryptographic protocol that provides communication security over the Internet. Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) is a widely-used communications protocol for secure communication over a computer network, with especially wide deployment on the Internet. Technically, it is not a protocol in itself; rather, it is the result of simply layering the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) on top of the SSL protocol, thus adding the security capabilities of SSL/TLS to standard HTTP communications. HTTPS provides authentication of the web site and associated web server that one is communicating with, which protects against Man-in-the-middle attacks. Additionally, it provides bidirectional encryption of communications between a client and server, which protects against eavesdropping and tampering with and/or forging the contents of the communication. In practice, this provides a reasonable guarantee that one is communicating with precisely the web site that one intended to communicate with (as opposed to an impostor), as well as ensuring that the contents of communications between the user and site cannot be read or forged by any third party. How can I enable SSL security on the community? To enable SSL go to: Communities » Set Up » Security What is Verification To View Profile? When a new member signs up for community and enter their email address, an email is sent to the member. The email contains the email verification link. The new member will have to click on the link to verify their profile. What will happen if the new member does not click in the link sent via email? If the new member does not click on the link sent via email, the member would not be able to login because its a verification process to verify a genuine email address for security reasons. What is Captcha Verification? Once this option is enabled, a new member would be able to see captcha verification while sign up. What is User Sign-Up Control? When a new member registers, an email notification is sent to the community admin containing approval and rejection links. The admin can use these links to allow or deny access for the member. Available since August 2025Previously, email notifications were triggered only when a member signed up directly through the portal. With this update, notifications will also be sent when a member signs up using the qualifying survey. What is Social Only Community? When this option is enabled, the member will be allowed to login only using social medias, i.e facebook, linkedin, google etc. The sign up option would be disabled once Social Only Community mode is enabled. What is Enable Terms and Conditions? This setting provides access to community terms and conditions that members need to opt-in while signing up. The community manager need to provide the following in community Terms and Conditions: safeguarding login credentials, using the platform responsibly, consequence of unauthorized access, misuse of information, or violation of these terms may resulting in account suspension. Community > Settings > Security > Enable Terms And Conditions (Enable) Community > Sign-up Page > Terms And Conditions (Opt-in) What is Strong Passwords? This setting is provided to ensure standard guidelines are applied while setting up a login password, to enhance security and have a more robust community. When this option is enabled, the new members will have to sign up with a strong password that meets all the guidelines and the existing members will be asked to reset their password when they try to log into the portal. Below are the guidelines to be followed while creating a strong password: Be a minimum of eight (8) or twelve (12)characters in length Contain both uppercase and lowercase alphabetic characters (A-Z, a-z) Have at least one numeric character (0-9) Have at least one special character (~!@#$%^*()_+=-) What is Minimum Password Length? In order to comply with GDPR Regulations and company standards, we are providing an option to the Moderators/Admin where they can select the length of the password to categorise that as strong password. Currently, we provide 2 options: 8 or 12 characters. Based on the length of the characters selected from dropwdown, the character limit in the guidelines gets updated. What changes will be seen on the portal when the Strong Passwords setting is enabled? A tooltip will be displayed in the Password input field of the sign up page. Hovering over it will show all the guidelines to be met while
