title: Global unsubscribe
kw: unsubscribe email list community global suppression address add communities members mail addresses used senders spam emails recipients future messages unsubscribed users go no themselves example globalsuppression url license suppressed comply act 2003 requires commercial provide functioning opt mechanism placed suppress
summary: Global Unsubscribe | Suppression List - Community
body: Global unsubscribe Global Unsubscribe | Suppression List - Community Global Unsubscribe | Suppression List - Community A Suppression List is a list of suppressed e-mail addresses used by e-mail senders to comply with the CAN-SPAM Act of 2003. CAN-SPAM requires that senders of commercial emails provide a functioning opt-out mechanism by which email recipients can unsubscribe their email address from future email messages. The unsubscribed email addresses are placed into a "suppression list" which is used to "suppress" future email messages to that email address. How can I add users to Global Unsubscribe? Go To: Communities >> Members >> Make Members Inactive Once you add an email address to the Global Unsubscribe list it is globally unsubscribed. No emails will be sent to the email address for any of the communities or projects. How can community members unsubscribe themselves from community? Go to: Login to community >> My account >> Unsubscribe How can email recipients unsubscribe themselves? Example https://qpcommunities.questionpro.com/a/globalSuppression.do In the example above, if /a/globalSuppression.do is appended to your community url, and the url is visited, users can add their email address to the Unsubscribe list there. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Export Data | Community Member Data Download
body: Export members Export Data | Community Member Data Download Export Data | Community Member Data Download To Export Community Data: Go To: Communities >> Members >> Search Click on the Export Users button. Community members can also be exported using the filters like Profile,Invitations & Surveys,Sign-Up & Access and Custom Variables. The excel file will be generated as follows: Can I export the "Codes" associated with the Profile data instead of the values? Yes - Please check the box Export Answer Codes/Index when downloading the file. The generated file will have codes instead of profile data values. Codes/Index values are available only for "Multiple Choice" type profile fields. What data/variables are available with the export data option? The exported data file will containing all the default system variables along with community member profile fields and custom fields. Following is the list of default system variables: Column Field Description A ID Community member ID. B Timestamp Timestamp when the user signed up. C Email Address Community member email address. D First Name Community member first name. E Last Name Community member last name. F Zip code Zip code for panel member location G State / City Community member state / city. H Birthday Date of birth for the Community member. I Device UID Community member device UID if the member signed up from a registered device (iPhone/iPad, etc). J Status Community member status (New, UnVerified, Verified, Blocked, etc.). K Source Community member source from where they signed up (Web, Upload, etc.). L Referral Source Referral source for the Community member. M Tracking Source Tracking source for the Community member from where they signed up. N Points Total points allocated to the panel member. summary: Schedulers, Import Schedulers, Export Schedulers
body: Import and Export Schedulers Schedulers, Import Schedulers, Export Schedulers Import and Export Schedulers Scheduler is another way to automate the members import and export process. You need to provide the FTP details while scheduling the Import/Export feature. With the scheduler in place, Communities will read the excel files to be imported. You can even get the members' data exported at your FTP location periodically. Set the scheduler and wait for it to run. Click to download video What does Auto Scheduler do? The Auto scheduler allows you to do the following:- Automate the members import process. Automate the members exporting in the Excel format. How can I set up the FTP for importing members to the Communities platform? To set up the import, go to – Login >> Communities >> Community >> Members >> Schedulers Click on Add Auto-scheduler button Name: Give the name for the import event. Import Mode: How would you wish to import the members i.e using FTP or WebDav Secure FTP: Toggle on if you wish to secure the FTP import. Host: Enter the host details. Language: Select the language in which you want the survey to open for the end user. Location: Enter the location from where the files is supposed to be imported in to the Communities system. File Password: This is an optional field where if the file has some password, it'll be required here to provide that. Username: Enter the Username. Password: Enter the Password if any. Click on the Test Connection button. If all the details provided are valid and correct, you will be able to save the scheduler else not. Now, once the connection is verified, you may add other details like - Setting up the Frequency, Day and Time. Click on "Save" to create the Import Scheduler. Once the FTP is created, you need to create the members' file and upload it in the FTP location. You can download the members' import template by clicking on the "Download Template" option. It will give you a sample file in which you need to fill the memebrs' details and put in the FTP location. How do I test if FTP is successful or not? After creating the FTP scheduler, you need to upload the sample member file in the FTP location.After uploading the sample member file in the FTP location, you can click on 'Run Now' to test the connection. If the connection is successful, the system will import the members' from the sample contact file and delete the file from the FTP location. Once the FTP is run successfully, go to Members List and check the imported members. Can I edit/suspend the scheduler? Yes, you can edit and suspend the scheduler.To edit the scheduler, hover on the row, click on 3 dots, and then click on Edit and make the changes you want in the scheduler. Similarly, if you want to suspend the schedular, click on the Suspend button. Can I delete the schedular too? Yes, if you do not want to suspend but delete the schedular, you can do so by hovering over the row, click on the Bin icon and the schedular will be deleted. Is there
content:
Import and Export Schedulers

Schedulers, Import Schedulers, Export Schedulers

Import and Export Schedulers Scheduler is another way to automate the members import and export process. You need to provide the FTP details while scheduling the Import/Export feature. With the scheduler in place, Communities will read the excel files to be imported. You can even get the members' data exported at your FTP location periodically. Set the scheduler and wait for it to run. Click to download video What does Auto Scheduler do? The Auto scheduler allows you to do the following:- Automate the members import process. Automate the members exporting in the Excel format. How can I set up the FTP for importing members to the Communities platform? To set up the import, go to – Login >> Communities >> Community >> Members >> Schedulers Click on Add Auto-scheduler button Name: Give the name for the import event. Import Mode: How would you wish to import the members i.e using FTP or WebDav Secure FTP: Toggle on if you wish to secure the FTP import. Host: Enter the host details. Language: Select the language in which you want the survey to open for the end user. Location: Enter the location from where the files is supposed to be imported in to the Communities system. File Password: This is an optional field where if the file has some password, it'll be required here to provide that. Username: Enter the Username. Password: Enter the Password if any. Click on the Test Connection button. If all the details provided are valid and correct, you will be able to save the scheduler else not. Now, once the connection is verified, you may add other details like - Setting up the Frequency, Day and Time. Click on "Save" to create the Import Scheduler. Once the FTP is created, you need to create the members' file and upload it in the FTP location. You can download the members' import template by clicking on the "Download Template" option. It will give you a sample file in which you need to fill the memebrs' details and put in the FTP location. How do I test if FTP is successful or not? After creating the FTP scheduler, you need to upload the sample member file in the FTP location.After uploading the sample member file in the FTP location, you can click on 'Run Now' to test the connection. If the connection is successful, the system will import the members' from the sample contact file and delete the file from the FTP location. Once the FTP is run successfully, go to Members List and check the imported members. Can I edit/suspend the scheduler? Yes, you can edit and suspend the scheduler.To edit the scheduler, hover on the row, click on 3 dots, and then click on Edit and make the changes you want in the scheduler. Similarly, if you want to suspend the schedular, click on the Suspend button. Can I delete the schedular too? Yes, if you do not want to suspend but delete the schedular, you can do so by hovering over the row, click on the Bin icon and the schedular will be deleted. Is there a naming convention for the members' file uploaded in FTP? No, there is no specific naming convention in the the members' file needs to be uploaded.Only make sure the data in the file is in the same format as of the sample file. How many files can I upload at a time in the FTP location? You can upload n number of files in the FTP location at a time. The scheduler will read each file one by one and import the contact from the file. For better and faster execution, we prefer to upload one file at a time in the FTP location. Does QuestionPro keeps a copy of the files uploaded in the system? No, QuestionPro doesn't keep a copy of your files. Once the system processes the file(s) from the FTP location and upload the contact details, it deletes the file from the location. How can I figure out if some transactions failed the import process? After the transaction file is processed, the bad/invalid transactions are shared on the same FTP location in an Excel file. You can pick the file from there. Also, if any such file remains on FTP location, it will not get imported back in the system. How to set up Export Report Scheduler? Go to the "Scheduler Export" section. Click on the Add Auto-Scheduler Name: Give the name for the export event. Secure FTP: Toggle on if you wish to secure the FTP import. Host: Enter the host details. Location: Enter the location from where the files is supposed to be imported in to the Communities system. Username: Enter the Username. Password: Enter the Password if any. Click on the Test Connection button. If all the details provided are valid and correct, you will be able to save the scheduler else not. Now, once the connection is verified, you may add other details like - Setting up the Frequency, Day, Time, End Date and Filters. Click on "Save" to create the Export Scheduler. Once the above-mentioned steps are done, you will get an excel report of members in that location. How to test/edit/suspend/delete the export schedular? You can follow the same steps as for the Import Schedular. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Ways to Search Members to the Community
kw: click user yes delete search selected icon members pop clicking checkbox order users row once deleted filters export profile ways community present confirm all any applied hover hand side then number filter reset edit details data codes member over bin
summary: Communities - Member Search
body: Ways to Search Members to the Community Communities - Member Search Ways to Search Members to the Community How can I search the member? The basic search that can be carried out by the user is the Search by Email Address. You can enter any word that is the part of email address and the results will be displayed accordingly. In order to have advanced search, Click on the Funnel Icon. You can now add any filter as per the requirement and the results will be based on the filters applied. How can I reset the filter? When the user choses to use advanced filter, the number of filters applied will be displayed on the funnel. So, in order to reset all the filters applied at once, the user needs to click on the reset icon. All the filters applied will be set to null. How can I edit the user details? In order to edit the user's details, hover on the row, you will see a pencil icon. Click on the icon and then you can edit the details. Can I delete any user? Yes, you can delete any user. There are different ways by which a user can be deleted. Hover over the row, click on the bin icon. Once done, a confirmation pop-up will open. Click on "Yes" and delete the user. The other way is, clicking on the checkbox present on the left-hand side of the row. Click on the checkbox and then choose "Delete Selected". Confirm the pop-up by clicking on Yes and the user will be deleted. In order to bulk delete the users, click on the checkbox present on the header. Once checkboxed, it will show the number of users that have been selected for deleting. Click on the "Delete Selected" option and confirm the pop-up by clicking on "Yes". All the selected users will be deleted. How can I export/download the members' data> In order to export the data, click on the export icon (located on the right-hand side of the Add Members button). Community members can also be exported using the filters like Profile,Invitations & Surveys,Sign-Up & Access and Custom Variables. Can I export the "Codes" associated with the Profile data instead of the values? Yes - Please toggle on the functionality Export Answer Codes/Index when downloading the file. Codes/Index values are available only for "Multiple Choice" type profile fields. Can I skip the Global Profile Information fields from the download? Yes - Please toggle on the functionality Skip Global Profile Information when downloading the file. Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

Community - Referral Settings

Community - Referral Settings The referral setting allows the community members to invite others into the community. If "Invite A Friend" is enabled, the administrator has the ability to set a limit on how many referrals can be allowed per community member. How can I add a referral limit? To add referral limit, go to: Communities » Community » Recruitment » Referral Setting Enter the referral limit in the box next to Maximum Referral Limit Click on Save Settings License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Communities - Referral Tracking

Communities - Referral Tracking

Communities - Referral Tracking Referral Tracking Links are used by the Admin/Moderators to monitor and measure the effectiveness of different channels or sources that drive respondents to participate in a survey. These referral links are of 3 types - Static Referral Links Dynamic Referral Links Unique Referral Links Click to download video What is a Static Referral Link? A static referral link refers to a single, unchanging URL that is used to direct respondents to login/signup using that URL. A static referral link is the same for all respondents, regardless of how they login/signup. How to setup this static referral link? By following certain steps, the admin/moderator can setup the static referral URL - Go to Communities >> Recruitment >> Static Referral Tracking section. Click on the button "Add Referral Tracking Link". Add the details in the pop-up like: Title - This is the name/title of the Static URL Code - This code will be displayed in the URL Point to Home/SignUp - This will allow the members to either signup/login using that link. Mobile Tracker - If the admin/moderator wants the member to signup/login via mobile using the link. URL (App Store) - Only required if admin/moderator makes the choice wrt mobile signups/logins) Identifier - In case you want to keep separate identifiers for each link, select the image from this drop-down. Once all this information is added, click on "Save" button and the Static Referral URL will be created. How to use this URL and what all information will be available for the moderators? The URL that will be auto-generated can be passed on to the respondents or members to signup/login using this link. Once the members signs-up or gets themseleves verified, this information will be captured and displayed under "Signup" and "Verified" counts. This allows moderator to keep an eye on how many members have signed up or have got themeselves verified using this link. For Signup/Login, there are 2 forms and on clicking the links, you can see those forms too. You can also view the analytics - i.e. how many people have updated their profile information. Lastly, you can easily delete this URL by clicking on the "Delete" button. How will the admin/moderator check that which member has signed-up using this static referral URL? The moderator/admin can check by - Go to Communities >> Members Click on the email address of the member and look for the Source. The source will appear as "Web . (Title Name). This Title Name will be same as provided during configuration time. What is a Dynamic Referral Link? A Dynamic Referral link is a URL, whose value can be changed during run time. Here, in this section, you will see 2 URLs: Home - Use this URL when the toggle for "Qualifying Survey is OFF". Recruitment - Use this URL when there is a Qualifying Survey associated. When the moderator/admin provides this link to the members, the members can add any value they like in the URL parameter - source=INSERT_CUSTOM_CODE_HERE How to insert this value in runtime? Just replace the "INSERT_CUSTOM_CODE_HERE" with the value that you want and hit enter. Further, the user will land on the landing page and then they can choose to signup or login. How will the admin/moderator check that which member has signed-up using this dynamic referral URL? The moderator/admin can check by - Go to Communities >> Members Click on the email address of the member and look for the Source. The source will appear as "Web . (Name provided during run time). What is a Unique Referral Link? A Unique Referral link is a URL which is unique to each community member. This can be found by in member pop-up against field name "Unique Referral Link". How is this used by other members? Basically, the member can pass on their unique referral link to peers and then peers can use that link and sign up for the community. This further leads to engagement as there might be rewards linked to inviting friends. How will the admin/moderator check that which member has signed-up using this unique referral URL? The moderator/admin can check by - Go to Communities >> Members Click on the email address of the member and look for the Source. The source will appear as "Web . (referrer's email address). summary: Share Profile Analytics: DataShare™ - Community
body: Data share Share Profile Analytics: DataShare™ - Community Share Profile Analytics: DataShare™ - Community DataShare™ lets you share profile analytics with your panel member. You can enable DataShare™ when adding or editing a profile field. Communities >> Profile >> Global Community Member Profile Click on Add New Profile Field From the DataShare™ Visual drop menu, select the chart type and click on Save Changes button. title: Send new invitation

summary: Communities - Send New Invitation
body: Send new invitation Communities - Send New Invitation Communities - Send New Invitation QuestionPro Community's send option allows the administrator to send a new survey invite to the member in the community to get the responses. Here Community administrator has an option to send the survey of QuestionPro survey platform or of any third-party survey provider. This survey invitation notification can be sent as an Email, SMS and push notification to mobile when the mobile app is installed. To send a survey invite in Communities > Select Community > Send > Send New survey invitation can be sent into three simple steps: Step 1: Survey Options and Compose Email/SMS In the first step of sending survey invitation, select the survey to be sent and compose the content of email and SMS, depending on the modes you want to use to notify the community members of the new survey invitation. By default, members will be able to view the survey on the community portal. Note: For one SMS the allowed length is 160 characters; this also includes the values to be replaced by the replacement variables used in the template. If the SMS length is more, then it will be sent in parts and this will increase the cost of SMS for Campaign. The GSM-7 is the standard alphabet set for SMS encoding. However, for few special characters, the UCS-2 is used which changes the SMS length. To know the approximate length and number of SMS that will be sent for your template, visit this link. Survey Type: Select the Survey type. The options available are: QuestionPro Survey Third Party Survey UI Test Select Survey: Select the survey you wish to distribute. Survey Length:Enter the length of the survey in minutes. Multiple Response Options - Allows members to take the survey multiple times: You may enable the respective option if you want the members to take the survey multiple times from comunity portal or using Email link. Select Recipients In the second step select the recipients for the survey invite. Select the recipients from Existing Filter saved, Custom filter member by applying filters based on profile fields and previous invitations sent. Review and Send In the third step review the selected member's sample. View the sample or download the members in excel. On this screen, you can send an invite or schedule the invitation to be sent later. On this step you can also choose the modes of notifying the members about the new Survey Invitation. You can do following on this step. Schedule the Survey Send the Survey Immediately Apply Sub-Sample Selection and Resting Period Rules Notify member by Email Notify member by Push Notification for members who have installed Communities' mobile application. Notify member by SMS: If you toggle this ON, system will display the available balance and approximate cost of sending the SMS for the campaign. You will also be able to view the total number of SMS recipients for the campaign, this count will include only those members who have updated their mob
content:
Send new invitation

Communities - Send New Invitation

Communities - Send New Invitation QuestionPro Community's send option allows the administrator to send a new survey invite to the member in the community to get the responses. Here Community administrator has an option to send the survey of QuestionPro survey platform or of any third-party survey provider. This survey invitation notification can be sent as an Email, SMS and push notification to mobile when the mobile app is installed. To send a survey invite in Communities > Select Community > Send > Send New survey invitation can be sent into three simple steps: Step 1: Survey Options and Compose Email/SMS In the first step of sending survey invitation, select the survey to be sent and compose the content of email and SMS, depending on the modes you want to use to notify the community members of the new survey invitation. By default, members will be able to view the survey on the community portal. Note: For one SMS the allowed length is 160 characters; this also includes the values to be replaced by the replacement variables used in the template. If the SMS length is more, then it will be sent in parts and this will increase the cost of SMS for Campaign. The GSM-7 is the standard alphabet set for SMS encoding. However, for few special characters, the UCS-2 is used which changes the SMS length. To know the approximate length and number of SMS that will be sent for your template, visit this link. Survey Type: Select the Survey type. The options available are: QuestionPro Survey Third Party Survey UI Test Select Survey: Select the survey you wish to distribute. Survey Length:Enter the length of the survey in minutes. Multiple Response Options - Allows members to take the survey multiple times: You may enable the respective option if you want the members to take the survey multiple times from comunity portal or using Email link. Select Recipients In the second step select the recipients for the survey invite. Select the recipients from Existing Filter saved, Custom filter member by applying filters based on profile fields and previous invitations sent. Review and Send In the third step review the selected member's sample. View the sample or download the members in excel. On this screen, you can send an invite or schedule the invitation to be sent later. On this step you can also choose the modes of notifying the members about the new Survey Invitation. You can do following on this step. Schedule the Survey Send the Survey Immediately Apply Sub-Sample Selection and Resting Period Rules Notify member by Email Notify member by Push Notification for members who have installed Communities' mobile application. Notify member by SMS: If you toggle this ON, system will display the available balance and approximate cost of sending the SMS for the campaign. You will also be able to view the total number of SMS recipients for the campaign, this count will include only those members who have updated their mobile number. Minimum balance of $5 in SMS credits is required to send out SMS invites to the members. What are Replacement Variables? Replacement variables are similar to "mail merge" tags. You can insert the replacement variable tags in your email and they will be dynamically replaced with appropriate data for each panel member when sending email invitations. Replacement Variable Tag Panel Name {PANEL_NAME} Survey Length {SURVEY_LENGTH} Points {POINTS} Survey Link {SURVEY_LINK} Panel Member's First Name {FIRST_NAME} Panel Member's Last Name {LAST_NAME} Panel Member's Email Address {EMAIL_ADDRESS} Custom Variable 1 {CUSTOM1} Custom Variable 2 {CUSTOM2} Custom Variable 3 {CUSTOM3} Custom Variable 4 {CUSTOM4} Custom Variable 5 {CUSTOM5} Also you can use Global Member Profile Fields as replacement variables. Replacement Variable Tag Field Name {PROFILE_ID} for example : Birthday {PROFILE_100} How to replace Member Profile fields in New Invitation? Go to: Communities >> Send >> Send Select Global member profile field which you want to add in New Invitation from Include Replacement Variables drop down menu. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Multiple Responses (Email/SMS)
kw: survey multiple responses communities email portal setting distributing community only taken once members times allow using response license sms project default completed cannot complete again however some research projects need enable sticky mode allows answer login send enabling member provide
summary: Communities - Multiple Survey Responses
body: Multiple Responses (Email/SMS) Communities - Multiple Survey Responses Communities - Multiple Survey Responses Multiple Responses (Portal) When distributing a survey project to your community, by default the survey can only be taken once. Once the survey is completed, members cannot complete the survey again. However, for some research projects, the survey may need to be taken multiple times. To allow this, you can enable 'Multiple Responses (Portal)' when distributing the survey. Sticky mode allows community members to answer the survey multiple times from the Portal. Login >> Communities >> Send Multiple Responses(Email) Enabling 'Multiple Responses(Email)' setting would allow member to provide multiple responses to the survey by using the survey link received in the email notification for the campaign. Every response collected using this setting will be individual response. This setting should only be used for the surveys where the responses are expected to change over the time. There are chances of data loss in this case. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.
content:
Multiple Responses (Email/SMS)

Communities - Multiple Survey Responses 

Communities - Multiple Survey Responses Multiple Responses (Portal) When distributing a survey project to your community, by default the survey can only be taken once. Once the survey is completed, members cannot complete the survey again. However, for some research projects, the survey may need to be taken multiple times. To allow this, you can enable 'Multiple Responses (Portal)' when distributing the survey. Sticky mode allows community members to answer the survey multiple times from the Portal. Login >> Communities >> Send Multiple Responses(Email) Enabling 'Multiple Responses(Email)' setting would allow member to provide multiple responses to the survey by using the survey link received in the email notification for the campaign. Every response collected using this setting will be individual response. This setting should only be used for the surveys where the responses are expected to change over the time. There are chances of data loss in this case. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? summary: Communities - Survey Authentication: Communities Invites Only
body: Survey authentication Communities - Survey Authentication: Communities Invites Only Communities - Survey Authentication: Communities Invites Only Communities Invites Only authentication restricts non-community members from accessing a survey link. Once this survey authentication is enabled, a shared survey link by the community member cannot be used to take the survey by a non-community member. How to enable communities invites only authentication? Go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit Click on the Settings menu under Edit. Select Security on the left navigation bar. Select the Communities Invites Only option and save. Communities Invites Only will be visible under survey authentication only if there’s a community created in the account. How does communities invites only authentication work? Survey should be sent using communities send option. Respondent panel members can click open and respond to the survey when they are logged in via their community. If the survey link is shared and opened externally, respondents will see the following error message: License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: New invitation profile exclusion
kw: members include completed select profile survey invitation previous yes all selected new exclusion possible sent status sample number invitations count points inclusion exclude batch minimum maximum section options criteria just need surveys whose fall range past received many multiple selecting
summary: New Invitation Profile Exclusion / Inclusion
body: New invitation profile exclusion New Invitation Profile Exclusion / Inclusion New Invitation Profile Exclusion / Inclusion Is it possible to include / exclude members who have completed the previous survey that was sent to them? Yes. You can do that. You just need to select the previous invite and the status you would like to include in the sample. Status - Default. This would include the previous batch of members as it is. Both completes and incompletes. You dont need to re sample the profile all over again. Status - Complete. This would include the members who have completed the batch that you have sampled on. Which means the survey will only go out to members who have completed the previous survey that you sent (Selected batch). Status - Pending. This would include the members who have not completed the survey that was sent in the previous invitation you selected. Is it possible to include members who have completed n number of surveys? Yes. You can select the minimum and maximum surveys completed in the Survey Completed section. This will include all the members whose completed survey number fall in the range. Is it possible to include / exclude members on the number of Invitations sent in the past? Yes. You can select the minimum and maximum Invitations count in the Invitations Received section. This will include all the members who have received that many invitations in the past. Is it possible to include / exclude members on the number of points? Yes. You can select the minimum and maximum Points in the Reward Points section. This will include all the members whose Points fall in the range. When I select multiple options for single select/ multi select profile fields, what should I expect in the result? If you are selecting multiple options for field to sample the members, the query will fetch you all the members who have selected at least one of the options for that field/ profiling question. How do I know how many members fit into my selected sample ? Once you are done selecting the criteria, just hit the update count button and that will give you the member count that fit into the selected criteria. After you select the criteria, please make sure you hit the update count button before going to the next step to execute delivery. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Profile field mapping
kw: question profile field communities community surveys survey type select options recruitment order mapping link must text member used answer drop then same add once linked mapped gender values any fields get done following multi choice one many row information misc
summary: Surveys to Communities - Profile Field Mapping
body: Profile field mapping Surveys to Communities - Profile Field Mapping Surveys to Communities - Profile Field Mapping Do you know that your Survey question can be used to link the answer values from the survey to any community profile field.The answer values used in the survey will be updated to the ‘profile fields’ in the community. However, there are a few pre-requisites to get this done- The survey must have a recruitment type of question. The question type must be on of the following- Multi-Choice : Select one, select many, drop-down. Text : Comment box, single row text, numeric input, contact information. Image chooser : Select one, select many. Misc : Date & time, calendar. Mobile : QR code-Like/Dislike. The profile fields must also be present on the Communities platform. If the question type is Multi-Choice, then the order of options must be same in survey's question and in the communities profile field. Click to download video How to add a Recruitment type question? Go to Surveys >> Edit >> Workspace >> Add Question >> Advanced >> Misc >> Community Recruitment. You can add any kind of validation by clicking on the validation link. In order to link this question with the community, select - "Settings" and then in the Community drop-down, select the target community. Once, these steps are done, the Recruitment type question will be added and linked to the particular community. It is advised to keep the recruitment type question at the end of the survey for better user experience. How to link the Survey Question to the Communities Profile Field? Foremost thing to remember is that the only selected Question type can be used for this linkage. The same has been provided above. Next step is, once you have identified the question, click on "Settings" >> Communities In the drop-down, select the community where you want this information to be mapped to. Then in the next row, you will see the question text and the options from the Communities profile field. Select the profile field where you would like this value to get inserted. Once all these steps are performed, you will be able to see that the Question will have a sub-text mentioning that this question is linked to the community. It also specifies to which field in that community it is linked to. Now, whenever a member takes the survey and answers this question, the answer will automatically be mapped to the profile field. Can you explain the whole working? Taking the example: Here in the Surveys, I've selected "What is your Gender" question type. Mapped this to "Gender" profile field under Communities. Made sure that order of the options is consistent across both places - In the Surveys In the Communities >> Profile Field options Now, whenever a member takes the response from the surveys platform, the same will be captured in communities for that particular field. Order of options in Surveys Order of options in Communities >> Profile >> Global Community Member Profile Member's gender field gets a

Go to Surveys >> Edit >> Workspace >> Add Question >> Advanced >> Misc >> Community Recruitment. You can add any kind of validation by clicking on the validation link. In order to link this question with the community, select - "Settings" and then in the Community drop-down, select the target community. Once, these steps are done, the Recruitment type question will be added and linked to the particular community. It is advised to keep the recruitment type question at the end of the survey for better user experience. How to link the Survey Question to the Communities Profile Field? Foremost thing to remember is that the only selected Question type can be used for this linkage. The same has been provided above. Next step is, once you have identified the question, click on "Settings" >> Communities In the drop-down, select the community where you want this information to be mapped to. Then in the next row, you will see the question text and the options from the Communities profile field. Select the profile field where you would like this value to get inserted. Once all these steps are performed, you will be able to see that the Question will have a sub-text mentioning that this question is linked to the community. It also specifies to which field in that community it is linked to. Now, whenever a member takes the survey and answers this question, the answer will automatically be mapped to the profile field. Can you explain the whole working? Taking the example: Here in the Surveys, I've selected "What is your Gender" question type. Mapped this to "Gender" profile field under Communities. summary: Automatic Closure of Projects
body: Automatic Closure Automatic Closure of Projects Automatic Closure of Projects Automatic Closure is a functionality that helps you to define criteria while sending out a survey from the Communities platform to close the survey on the defined close date and time or when the quota limit is reached. With this feature, you can automatically close the project and the survey will not be visible on the member portal once the criteria are met. How to set an Automatic Closure criteria for a project/campaign? When sending out a survey from the Communities platform, under Delivery Options, You need to set the Automatic Closure toggle on. You can set a closure criteria either by defining a Response Quota limit or by providing a Close date and time How can I update an existing Closure criteria or set a new criteria later? Go to: Send » History From the listing page, click on the gear icon (settings) for your respective project A pop up window will appear where we can set Automatic Closure criteria on/off and can also edit the criteria if it is already defined License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

Survey Alias Survey Alias allows a community admin/moderator to rename a survey before sending it to the community. This prevents members from guessing the survey's purpose, reducing potential bias and ensuring more accurate responses. Click to download video How does survey alias work? Administrator will be able to create an alias for an existing survey, while sending the survey out in the community. The members will not see the original survey name, instead they will see the alias set by the administrator. summary: AI-generated email feature for panel management that helps users draft professional emails to panelists using simple prompts.
body: AI create AI-generated email feature for panel management that helps users draft professional emails to panelists using simple prompts. Write Smarter: Use AI to Create Your Survey Invitations This feature enables users to quickly create professional and personalized survey invitations. Instead of drafting each email from scratch, simply provide a short prompt describing what you want to communicate, and QuestionPro AI will generate the invitation for you. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video This feature is scheduled for beta release in mid-September and will be available to all users by the end of Q3, 2025. How does the AI work? The AI generates invite in simple steps: Step 1: Select community Step 2: Select send from community home page Step 3: Select survey >> Select 'Help me write' Step 4: Provide prompt to 'Help me write'. Here you can either write your own prompt or select prompts provided by QuestionPro. Help me write >> Add Description (include survey objective, category, length of survey and incentive for survey completion) >> Click Create to generate AI suggestion Step 5: Review suggestion provided by AI >> Refine suggestion by making the invite shorter, longer, more formal, or more casual >> Select recreate to generate new suggestion Review AI-generated invite and send Users can refine or regenerate suggestions until satisfied before sending the final invite. Glossary AI (Artificial Intelligence): Technology that mimics human intelligence to generate content. Panel Management: Communication and engagement with a group of panelists participating in surveys. Prompt: A short instruction entered by you to guide AI in generating emails. Generated Email: A draft email automatically created by AI that the user can edit and send. License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Community History
body: Invitation history Community History Community History Project History stores all sent batch details / statistics. You can view the entire history under Project History. Communities >> Send >> History How can I check the status of the project ? Once you pull the individual project history by clicking on the link. You will see the status of the project just below the project header. What are the different Status's that I can expect when I execute the project? The project can be in below mentioned status Completed Successfully: This would mean that the project is executed successfully Pending Processing: The project is not completed and still executing. Close: You can turn the status to Closed once you are done with the data collection What is the impact of closing the project? To close the project you will need click on the status drop down and select the close project option to close the project. Closing the project will not display the sent survey on the portal and the members will not be able to see/ answer the survey for that project. This is usually done when you are done with your data collection and ready to send out new project. I dont want members to see the previous project surveys in their Active Surveys list. What can I do? All you need to do in that case is close the project and the survey will not be visible to any member License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? summary: The administrator of the community will be able to remove access to a survey for a particular member or a group of members, who might have fallen out of the study criteria.
body: Remove Access to a Survey The administrator of the community will be able to remove access to a survey for a particular member or a group of members, who might have fallen out of the study criteria. Remove survey access for a member or a group of members. Why do you need to remove access to surveys for a particular member? As the administrator of the community, you should be able to remove survey access for a member or a group of members using existing filters as the member/members might have fallen out of the specific criteria you had set for the study. How does remove access to surveys work? The administrator of the community will be able to remove survey access for a member or use existing filters in the community to remove survey access for a group of members. You can remove survey access for a group of people by going to Invitation History under the send tab and hover over the more icon, you will see the Remove Access option. On the remove access pop-up, select the filter from the dropdown, and hit save button. Count of members whose access will be removed is displayed. After hitting the save button, you will see success message saying survey access has been removed. If you want to remove access for a single member, you can go on the survey invitations tab on the member pop-up and remove the access by clicking on the option. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Communities Health - Email Delivery and Access Rules
body: Community health Communities Health - Email Delivery and Access Rules Communities Health - Email Delivery and Access Rules To set up Email Delivery Rules: Go To: Communities >> Send >> Preferences >> Community Health - Email Delivery & Access Rules Resting Period: With the Resting Period setting, all email invitations and sample selection will be validated to make sure members are not emailed too frequently. Resting Period defines the minimum number of days the system will wait before sending another email invitation. Sample Limit: The Sample Limit setting allows the admin to setup a maximim limit PER SAMPLE PULL that anyone can request. If members request a higher invitation count, then the system will warn the member about the limit. Set this up if you have sub-accounts with access to your community. Member Project Limit: The Member Project Limit is the maximum number of projects a member can complete in a given month. This prevents members from abusing the community by running too many projects in a given month. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? summary: Communities - System Email Templates
body: System email notification Communities - System Email Templates System Email Templates System email templates are predefined formats used for all automated communications triggered by specific events within the system. Examples include a birthday greeting sent to a member, a welcome email upon signup, and other event-based notifications. Go to: Community >> Send >> Templates >> System Notifications New Member Verification: When a user signs up for the community, a verification email is sent to confirm that the provided email address is valid. Once the user clicks the verification link in the email, their account is marked as verified. Member Welcome: Once a community member’s account is verified, a welcome email can be automatically triggered. This email typically expresses gratitude for joining and provides key information about the community to help them get started. Refer-A-Friend: Here, you can configure the email that will be sent to the referred individual (referee). Community Invitation (Private Community): Here, you can configure the email that will be triggered as soon as users are invited to the community. Reward Rejection:When manual approval for rewards is enabled, rejecting a reward will trigger an email notification to the community member informing them of the rejection. You can configure the content of this email in this section. Birthday Wish: You can collect and store a member’s date of birth in your community. If this information is available, a birthday greeting email can be automatically sent to the member on their birthday. The content of this email can be configured in this section. Password Reset: This email template is triggred every time you manually send the password reset link to your members. Topic Approval: Here you can set the template for the email that is sent to the member when the topic posted by the him has been approved. Topic Rejection: Here you can set the template for the email that is sent to the member when the topic posted by the him has been rejected. New Membership Approval: When manual approval of signups is enabled, an email is sent to the community administrator to approve or reject the new membership request. You can configure the content of this email in this section. Does the language dropdown auto-translate portal notifications? The language dropdown does not trigger translation in the system. It is only a language selector that decides which pre-written content version to display. This means the client must add notification content per language. If the dropdown is set to English and the notification template is written in English, then members whose preferred language is English will see an English portal and English portal notifications. If a member’s preferred language is German, Spanish, or another supported language, the portal UI and system notifications displayed to them will be the versions created for that specific language. The system displays the matching language variant, it does not

Once the user clicks the verification link in the email, their account is marked as verified. Member Welcome: Once a community member’s account is verified, a welcome email can be automatically triggered. Reward Rejection:When manual approval for rewards is enabled, rejecting a reward will trigger an email notification to the community member informing them of the rejection. You can configure the content of this email in this section. Birthday Wish: You can collect and store a member’s date of birth in your community. If this information is available, a birthday greeting email can be automatically sent to the member on their birthday. The content of this email can be configured in this section. Password Reset: This email template is triggred every time you manually send the password reset link to your members. You can configure the content of this email in this section. Does the language dropdown auto-translate portal notifications? The language dropdown does not trigger translation in the system. It is only a language selector that decides which pre-written content version to display. This means the client must add notification content per language. If the dropdown is set to English and the notification template is written in English, then members whose preferred language is English will see an English portal and English portal notifications. summary: Communities Notifications
body: Portal notifications Communities Notifications Communities Notifications What is community notification? When there is any activity in the community like a survey is deployed etc. then the panel member gets a notification when they log into their portal. This is community notification. Where can I see the communities notifications? All the notifications will be visible on the bell icon located at the top right corner once you log into your portal: Are these notifications customizable? Communities Notifications Templates can be found under Send>>Templates>>Portal Notifications. Admin can edit the templates and add customized notification messages. Does the language dropdown auto-translate portal notifications? The language dropdown does not trigger translation in the system. It is only a language selector that decides which pre-written content version to display. This means the client must add notification content per language. If the dropdown is set to English and the notification template is written in English, then members whose preferred language is English will see an English portal and English portal notifications. If a member’s preferred language is German, Spanish, or another supported language, the portal UI and system notifications displayed to them will be the versions created for that specific language. The system displays the matching language variant, it does not auto-translate the message. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? summary: Communities - Qualifying Survey
body: Rewards Communities - Qualifying Survey Communities - Qualifying Survey A Qualifying Survey enables you to recruit members for your community, which meet your specific criteria. For example, if you want to recruit members belonging to a specific age group to join your community, you can include a question asking the age of respondents in the Qualifying Survey. You can screen the respondents using this question based on your criteria. How to set up a Qualifying Survey? Create a survey using your QuestionPro account. Add the required questions to the survey. Make sure that you add the Community Recruitment question at the end of the survey. Community Recruitment question is mandatory. Then go to Communities >> Recruitment >> Qualifying Survey Enable the Qualifying Survey option and then select the required survey from the drop-down. Once you save the changes, the selected survey will be set as the Qualifying Survey for your community. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Communities - Rewards Setup
body: Communities Rewards Communities - Rewards Setup Communities - Rewards SetUp Rewards are incentives given to encourage specific behaviors or actions of the community members. These incentives can be in any form that could be allocated to the members. In Communities, we offer points i.e if a member earns "X" points, then they will get gift cards/incentives which they can redeem for their own use. Click to download video How to enable Rewards in the communities? Go To: Communities >> Incentive >> Rewards In order for members to see the earned/available rewards, you need to toggle on the "Rewards" settings. Approve Redemption Request- If as an admin/moderator, you would like to approve the rewards redemption request raised by the members, toggle on this setting. Community Reward Redemption Amount Limit (Daily)- It allows to set a threshold limit on the total amount of rewards that can be redeemed by the community members. Max. Rewards Redemption count Per Member (Daily) - It allows to limit the maximum number of rewards that the members can redeem on daily basis. Once, all the settings are done for this section, click on the "Save Settings" button. When the Rewards toggle is on, then Panel side- When the Rewards toggle is on, then Portal side- What is this Point System? It allows admin to set the points which a member can earn when they meet the certain criteria. New User Signup: That is how many points to be allocated to a user who signs up on the community. Profile Update: How many point to alloacte to a user/member when they update their profile. Refer-A-Friend: If a member refers to other person and the secondary person signsup using the Refer a friend link, then the primary member will get the points. Mobile Download Incentive: When a member downloads the mobile app, they will receive the certain points. Once all the values are added, click on the "Save Settings" button. All these measures are taken to increase the user engagement and adoption on the platform and as well as on app. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Communities - Reward Approval Log
body: Reward approval log Communities - Reward Approval Log Communities - Reward Approval Log You can determine whether you would like to approve or reject a reward on your community. To enable this feature, you will have to get in touch with your account manager. Once this feature is enabled, when a member tries to redeem their reward, they will receive a notification via email that their reward is pending with the admin. These email addresses will be recorded under Reward Approval Log. Communities >> Incentive >> Rewards >> Reward Approval Log You may click on Bulk Approve to approve all the rewards, or approve rewards one at a time. You may click on Bulk Reject to reject all the rewards. The approved or rejected email addresses will be tracked in the Rewards Approved/Rejected field. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Communities - Reward Inventory
body: Reward inventory Communities - Reward Inventory Communities - Reward Inventory Go to: Communities >> Incentive >> Inventory Click on + Create Inventory. Select the Reward Type, Status, Title, Content, Image (if any), Points, Fulfillment Email, Card Value, and Currency. Click on Check Availability. Click on Save Changes. What does this Download Options dropdown helps in? It provides you with 2 options: Reward Stats: Once selected, it will ask for the date range under which the rewards were created. Hence, when you download it, all the rewards that were created in-between the date range will be reflected in the excel. Member Point Stats: This will also ask for selecting a date range or year, to provide you the results of the members with their total points earned during that time period and total points redeemed during that time frame. What is Custom Reward? With Custom Reward, you can give out personalized rewards. Status: Select the status of the reward as Active, Suspended or Hidden. Title: Add a Title to your reward. Description: Write the content for your reward. Reward URL: You may specify the URL if any. Brand Disclaimer Text: Enter the Brand Disclaimer Text. Image URL: You may upload the image of the URL. Fulfilment Email: Add the fulfilment email here. Filter: Add a filter to make the reward available to specific members Points to redeem: Mention the points which would be required to claim the reward. Card Value: Enter the card value here. Currency: Specify the currency. Notify Members: Turn this option On if you wish to notify the members. Show Card Value: Displays the value of the card to members. Minimum Item Limit: Enter minimum quantity limit which when hit will trigger an email to the admin. Save Changes: After filling all the sections, click here. How to set up Tango Card's Rewards? Select, the reward under Tango Card's Rewards Status: Set the status as Active, Suspended or Hidden Fulfilment Email: Enter the fulfilment email. Filter: Add a filter to make the reward available to specific members Points to redeem: Enter the points. Card Value: Enter the card value between the range given. Notify Members: Turn this option On if you wish to notify the members. Minimum Item Limit: Enter minimum quantity limit which when hit will trigger an email to the admin. Check Availability:Click on this option to check if the reward's availability. Click on Save Changes What are the reward types available under Tango Card's Rewards? title: PayPal Integration
kw: paypal rewards integration gift community client card amount account go communities settings dollar redeem members 500 redemption manager configure click id secret key configuration api admin save under add option inventory email license benefit using integrate support transfer amounts wallet
summary: PayPal Integration
body: PayPal Integration PayPal Integration PayPal Integration - Rewards What is the benefit of using PayPal integration? Integrate with PayPal to support transfer of gift card amounts to the members PayPal wallet directly. With this members will have flexibility in spending the gift amount. Maximum of $500 redemption amount will be allowed per day and this cannot be changed by the Community Manager. Community manager will be able to configure a lesser amount than $500 to set a cap on daily transactions. Click to download video What are the prerequisites for PayPal integration? You should have : A PayPal business account PayPal App credentials (Client ID and Secret Key) Sufficient balance in PayPal account What are the steps for PayPal configuration? A REST API KEY has to be generated in the Community Go to: Communities » Modules » Mobile » API Keys To configure the PayPal account, go to Communities >> Settings >> Admin Settings >> PayPal Configuration . Now, here we need to save the PayPal Client ID and PayPal Client Secret, provided by the client, under Admin Settings How do I add PayPal option to Rewards? To add PayPal Go to: Incentive » Inventory » Create Inventory Provide PayPal as a Reward Type in the Rewards pop up and save the changes. What currencies can be applied as a payout option for Paypal rewards? American dollar Australian dollar Canadian dollar Euro Mexican peso Pound sterling How do I redeem the PayPal Rewards? Once you login your community, under rewards you will see the PayPal gift card. Click on redeem to redeem the gift card. An email will be sent to the fulfillment email address after redemption. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Badges
kw: badges badge more click communities community add member members admin feature view points if once edit color deleted apply button name yes image background awarded default search allow all active page message rule sample receive set whole toggle criteria time
summary: Communities - Badges
body: Badges Communities - Badges Communities - Badges Badges are silent rewards that the members receive upon completion of a milestone set by the organisation of theirs. It enables the users to be engaged with the platform more. Click to download video How to apply badges? Badges can be applied for the whole community by going to Communities >> Incentive >> Badges >> Toggle on What does this Apply Badges button do? When admin trigger Apply Badges the members who satisfy the criteria at that time will be awarded the badges. The badges won't be applicable to the panelists/members who would have met the criteria after Apply Badges is run. By default, the admin/moderator will see the 5 badges. They can add more badges as per the requirement. What is this search feature for? Search feature will allow the user to serach for the particular badge that will match the words entered in the serach box. The suggested results will be either the batch name or the batch description. You can now switch view from the grid view to list view by clicking on the icon present besides the search. What are the default Badges supported for panel? Currently the following badges are by default supported on community member panel: Points Bandit: This badge will be awarded to the member that has achieved X points. Life Time Achievement: This badge will be awarded to the member who have been the part of community for X number of days. Community Leader: The member who has Participated in all the active modules of Community. Profile Verified: If this badge is one, then once the member had verified their profile, they will get this badge. Wifly: The member who has downloaded the QuestionPro Communities Mobile Application. List View: Can we edit the badges? Yes, surely you can! Click on the 3 dots of any badge, click on edit option. Once you land to new page, you can edit the badge name, message, image, background or frame color. Click on the Save button for the changes to be saved. Currently, we are allowing the admin/moderators to edit the rule for Points Bandit and Lifetime achievment badges. In future, we'll not allow to edit the rule of any badge. Can we add more badges? Yes, you can do now! We've provided the capability where the admin/moderators can add more badges to the existing set of badges. How can we add more badges? Click on the "Add Badge" button. Once you click on the hutton, you will be landed on a screen, where you would need to enter all the details required for creation of a badge. Badge name: Provide the name of the badge. Sample filter: Select the sample filter as a rule for creation of a badge. In order to create more rules, you need to create sample filters from Sampling page. Points allocated: Optional field. You can enter the number of points a user will receive on earning this badge. Message: Message serves as the description of the badge. Upload Image: Upload an image for the badge. Background Color: If the image uploaded has a transparent background, you c
content:
Badges

Communities - Badges 

Communities - Badges Badges are silent rewards that the members receive upon completion of a milestone set by the organisation of theirs. It enables the users to be engaged with the platform more. Click to download video How to apply badges? Badges can be applied for the whole community by going to Communities >> Incentive >> Badges >> Toggle on What does this Apply Badges button do? When admin trigger Apply Badges the members who satisfy the criteria at that time will be awarded the badges. The badges won't be applicable to the panelists/members who would have met the criteria after Apply Badges is run. By default, the admin/moderator will see the 5 badges. They can add more badges as per the requirement. What is this search feature for? Search feature will allow the user to serach for the particular badge that will match the words entered in the serach box. The suggested results will be either the batch name or the batch description. You can now switch view from the grid view to list view by clicking on the icon present besides the search. What are the default Badges supported for panel? Currently the following badges are by default supported on community member panel: Points Bandit: This badge will be awarded to the member that has achieved X points. Life Time Achievement: This badge will be awarded to the member who have been the part of community for X number of days. Community Leader: The member who has Participated in all the active modules of Community. Profile Verified: If this badge is one, then once the member had verified their profile, they will get this badge. Wifly: The member who has downloaded the QuestionPro Communities Mobile Application. List View: Can we edit the badges? Yes, surely you can! Click on the 3 dots of any badge, click on edit option. Once you land to new page, you can edit the badge name, message, image, background or frame color. Click on the Save button for the changes to be saved. Currently, we are allowing the admin/moderators to edit the rule for Points Bandit and Lifetime achievment badges. In future, we'll not allow to edit the rule of any badge. Can we add more badges? Yes, you can do now! We've provided the capability where the admin/moderators can add more badges to the existing set of badges. How can we add more badges? Click on the "Add Badge" button. Once you click on the hutton, you will be landed on a screen, where you would need to enter all the details required for creation of a badge. Badge name: Provide the name of the badge. Sample filter: Select the sample filter as a rule for creation of a badge. In order to create more rules, you need to create sample filters from Sampling page. Points allocated: Optional field. You can enter the number of points a user will receive on earning this badge. Message: Message serves as the description of the badge. Upload Image: Upload an image for the badge. Background Color: If the image uploaded has a transparent background, you can always give a background color to that. Frame Color: Serves as the kind of outer circle boundary color. Once all the steps are done, click on the "Save" button and your badge will be created. How will the badge look like once added? What is the function of Active toggle in badges? The toggling will allow the admin/moderator to make a badge active/inactive for the whole community. What if I add more badges? How will they appear? We've added a pagination feature. As soon as you have more than 11 badges, the pagination feature will be seen on the top right side of the page. Can the badges be deleted and restored also? Yes, you can delete the badge only if it's in inactive state and also, you can retrieve the badges if it has got deleted by mistake. The deleted badges will appear in Deleted tab. When hovered over on deleted badge, you will see an option to restore. Click on the icon and the badge would be restored. Where can my panels members can view the Badges? Members can check their badges on My Account under the Badges tab. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Communities - Ideas Points Set Up
body: Idea points setup Communities - Ideas Points Set Up Communities - Ideas Points Set Up Using this Points setup you can assign points to members for submitting new ideas and posting comments on ideas. Daily, Weekly, Monthly points limit can be setup. Go to Communities >> Modules >> Idea Board >> Points New Idea: Assign the points you wish to award to the members for posting an Idea. New comments Assign the points you wish to award to the members for posting a comment. Maximum Points Represents the points a member can earn per month, week, day. By default, the total maximum points per month is 100; however, this may be modified by the admin. Click on Save. Will members receive points for every idea or comment they posted? If the limit for day/week/month is not set then members will receive points for all the idea or comments that they post. If the daily/weekly/monthly limit is set then members will receive points for the assigned number of points per day/week/month. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Campaigns
kw: idea ideas enabled board campaign member get communities email members posted new comment notification community ideaboard click moderate status delete any campaigns modules enter sent approval comments them activity portal daily weekly yes also license post enable please allows against
summary: Communities - Idea Board
body: Campaigns Communities - Idea Board Communities - Idea Board Idea Board is a platform where community members can post their ideas and discuss the ideas that are posted. To enable Idea Board on your community, please these steps: Communities >> Modules >> Idea Board >> Enable What is an Idea campaign? Idea campaign allows community members to post ideas against a particular campaign that is being run. Communities >> Modules >> Idea Board >> Campaigns Click on + New Idea campaign Enter the Name of the Idea Campaign Enter a Description Click on Save Ideaboard settings Below are the Ideaboard settings: New idea approvalOnce enabled, any idea posted by the member would be sent to the moderate section for moderation / approval. New comments approvalOnce enabled, any comment posted by the member would be sent to the moderate section for moderation / approval. Social sharingOnce enabled, the member will see the option to share the idea on social media. (Twitter) Notification Below are the different options uner notification. Idea status changeWhen enabled, the member would get an email notifying them of any change of status of their submitted idea. When an idea is approvedWhen enabled, the member would get an email informing them that their idea has been approved. When a new comment is approvedWhen enabled, the member would get an email informing them that the comment they posted has been approved. To members regarding Ideas that they have createdWhen enabled, the member would get an email notification every time there is any activity on the ideas that they have posted. Portal notificationWhen enabled, all members would see an in-app portal notification everytime there is a new comment or idea posted in the ideaboard DigestWhen enabled, you will get a daily or weekly summary of all ideas / comments that have been posted on the ideaboard via email. Below are the different options for digest notification: Daily: Get daily summaries of the ideaboard. Weekly: Get weekly summaries of the ideaboard. Recipients: Choose whether notifications should be sent to the administrator, the moderator, or both. The ability to send notifications to both the roles will be available in Q3 2025. Idea Activity EmailWhen enabled, the member would get an email notification whenever the status of the idea changes. Comment Activity EmailWhen enabled, the member would get an email notification whenever the status of his comment changes. Can I delete/deactivate an ongoing Idea Campaign? Yes, you can delete an ongoing Idea campaign. To delete, click on the Delete option against the campaign name. A pop-up window will appear with the comment "Are you sure?". Click on Yes to delete. You can also mark a Campaign as Inactive by clicking on the Status. Inactive Campaigns will be hidden from the Portal. Moderate idea Moderate idea Allows the community manager or moderator to view the ideas / comments that are queued for approval. Communities >> Modules >> Idea Board >> Moderate idea How do

Members can also share their ideas on their Facebook and Twitter account. title: Moderate ideas
kw: ideas idea delete moderate members view comments communities click posted status filter name order hyperlink yes allows all moderation portal admin them under other details following added member source statuses change if no approval then default set new select available
summary: Communities - Moderate Ideas
body: Moderate ideas Communities - Moderate Ideas Communities - Moderate Ideas Moderate Ideas typically refers to the process of reviewing, managing, and controlling the ideas submitted by the participants. This moderation ensures that the content aligns with the goals of the survey or platform, filters out inappropriate or irrelevant submissions, and maintains the quality of the data collected. Click to download video Where can I view and moderate the ideas posted by members? Once members start creating ideas in the portal, the community admin can view them under Moderate Ideas. Communities >> Modules >> Idea Board >> Moderate Ideas What are the other details I can get from this page? You can view the following details- Name of the idea which was added by the participant. The description of the idea. Number of downvotes, upvotes, comments are received on that particular idea Name of the member who has posted the idea along with date What is the campaing name under which the idea was posted What is the source of it. What are the moderation statuses and how can I change them? When a member adds the idea and if there is no approval required, then by default the idea status is set to New. In order to change the status of the idea, click on the "New" hyperlink and select the status from the dropdown. The available statuses are- In-Active In Review In Progress Completed Pending Approval Deleted Can I view the comments which were added by members on the idea? Yes, you can view the comments by clicking on the "comments" hyperlink. What is the usecase of this Filter function? This "Filter" function allows you to filter the ideas which are having comments, or allows you to filter the ideas which doesn't have any comment or allows you to view all the ideas which have comments or not. By default, "All" ideas are displayed. Can I delete the ideas? Yes, you can delete a single idea or you can perform bulk deletion too. In order to delete a single idea, click on "Delete" hyperlink present next to the source. In order to bulk delete the ideas, either select the checkbox present infront of the "Delete Selected Ideas" to delete all ideas or checkbox specific ideas and then click on the "Delete Selected Ideas" to delete those specific ideas. How does it looks on members portal? By clicking on the "Add Idea" button, members can add the idea. Members cannot see the Idea posted by other members if the status is set to In-Active by the admin/moderator. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.
