summary: This page describes how to use sentiment analysis
body: Automatic categories This page describes how to use sentiment analysis Sentiment Analysis How to use sentiment analysis? In order to Semantics you also have to ask the administrator to enable that feature. Go to Sentiment Analysis tab Click on the Automatic categories dropdown to change which categories you want to enable. By default it is None. After clicking the dropdown, the options will appear and if you hover on that option, on the right side you will see the description of it. Enable Automatic categories. After enabling the automatic categories, you will be asked if you are sure. By clicking the Yes button you confirm that you are sure. Automatic categories are active now and you will be notified that the Semantic domain is changed. Please note that it may take some time for sentiment analysis of all the reviews. The time required may vary depending on the number of reviews. By default the sentiment analysis starts with the next sync operation but if you want it to start immediately, please click on the sync manually button available in Settings->Locations How to use sentiment analysis? From the left navigation bar, goto Sentiment Analysis page.Here you can check Sentiment Analysis. Here you can see a table. The first column specifies the predefined sentiment categories. The categories can be further expanded to check the topics. The second column specifies the total number of occurences of a particular category/topic in the reviews synced into the CX Reputation platform. Clicking on the count, takes you to the reviews screen filtering the reviews mentioning the categories/topics. The third column specifies the total number of occurences of a particular category/topic with the positive sentiment in the reviews synced into the CX Reputation platform. Clicking on the count, takes you to the reviews screen filtering the reviews mentioning the categories/topics. The fourth column specifies the total number of occurences of a particular category/topic with the negative sentiment in the reviews synced into the CX Reputation platform. Clicking on the count, takes you to the reviews screen filtering the reviews mentioning the categories/topics. Another way to check Sentiment Analysis is by going to the Reviews page using the left navigation bar. If the text is highlighted in green color that indicates the positive sentiments and if it is highlighted in red color that indicates negative sentiments. Hovering over the highlighted text also shows you the category and the topic that sentiment belongs to. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Getting started with Closed Loop
kw: feedback loop clf closed tickets customer resolution ticket auto overdue customers survey process users identification views winback experience feature satisfaction communication management enabling escalation incorporates ensuring root causes actions settings three different email templates questionpro getting started help based issues
summary: This page will help you understand what Closed Loop Feedback is all about and why it is an important component of the customer experience management
body: Getting started with Closed Loop This page will help you understand what Closed Loop Feedback is all about and why it is an important component of the customer experience management Getting started with Closed Loop What is Closed Loop Feedback? Closed Loop Feedback is a response-based ticketing system. It is a vital part of Customer Experience. Closed loop feedback is when a business responds to or acts on customer feedback through an online or offline survey. This system is designed to help a user to manage automatic tickets and manual tickets in one convenient place. Why is closed-loop feedback important for customer experience? Closed-loop feedback is a customer-centric process that involves gathering feedback, analyzing it, taking action, and closing the loop with customers. It starts by collecting feedback through surveys, reviews, or direct communication, followed by analyzing the data to identify areas for improvement. Actionable steps are then taken to address the feedback, such as resolving issues or refining processes. Closing the loop entails communicating with customers, providing updates or solutions. Continuous improvement is integral, with ongoing monitoring, evaluation, and incorporation of feedback into decision-making. This iterative process enhances customer satisfaction, trust, and loyalty, while driving organizational improvement. How does CLF work? Closed-loop feedback improves products and services. It is the process of contacting customers who have provided feedback. Closed loop feedback begins with a business surveying with the customer in mind. Once the survey is made and sent to customers, they give their feedback, and the business collects and looks at the data. With closed-loop feedback from QuestionPro CX, businesses can do the following: Customer identification Collecting information for action Follow-ups can be automated Case management that is efficient Identification of the internal problem What are the key features of CLF? CLF is an innovative ticket management solution that offers a range of features to help users manage their tickets efficiently and effectively. The key features of CLF include: Auto Overdue: CLF includes an auto overdue feature that displays alerts next to ticket ID's when they become overdue, enabling quick identification and resolution of overdue tickets. Auto Escalation: CLF incorporates an auto-escalation process for assign tickets automatically to assignees based on predefined rules, ensuring timely resolution and minimizing delays. Root Causes and Actions: CLF empowers users to set root causes and corresponding actions in the settings, enabling the identification and resolution of underlying issues for tickets, enhancing efficiency, and elevating customer satisfaction. Three Different Views: CLF offers three different views of tickets including list, feed, and kanban views, providing users with the flexibility to view their tickets in the way that best suits their needs. Email Templates:

title: Enhanced Closed Loop Access Control

summary: enhanced closed loop
body: Enhanced Closed Loop Access Control enhanced closed loop Enhanced Closed Loop How can one control Enhanced Closed Loop access to Managers? By default, Managers would not have access to the Enhanced Closed Loop system. The access can be given via the manager settings. Goto Admin->Managers You will see a list of managers. Click on the ellipses besides each manager, select View Manager Details, a pop-up will open. The pop-up has an option for Role. You can select the Enhanced Closed Loop role for providing the access. How can one control Enhanced Closed Loop access to sub-accounts? Once Enhanced Closed Loop is enabled in the product switcher, it is accessible to all the accounts and sub-accounts. If an access is to be revoked, that can be done via organization settings. Goto Account Settings, Select Organisation. Select Role. A list of roles will appear. Select the role, select product as Customer Experience. Goto to Action tab and uncheck Enhanced Closed Loop License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? There are two ways in which tickets get created in Enhanced Closed Loop Action Alerts in CX Manual Tickets in Enhanced Closed Loop All the tickets that get generated based on the action alerts set in your CX survey will be shown under their respective Workspace. To check the tickets created in your Workspace Go to Enhanced Closed Loop >>Select your Workspace Once you open the Workspace, all the tickets generated from the surveys in the workspace will be displayed here: For creating manual tickets, there is a "+" sign available. You click on it and create tickets with the details How does a ticketing system work in CLF? The closed-loop feedback system, as used in the context of customer experience management, is designed to continuously monitor and improve the interactions between a company and its customers. Basically, feedback needs to be created from CX where the responses of customers will show against a detailed survey. There could be several surveys in one feedback, and the feedback name will appear on the closed loop dashboard containing tickets for each. The integration of automatic and manual ticket creation in this system ensures that all customer feedback is recorded and acted upon, no matter how it is received. An automatic ticket is generated when a customer provides a rating of 0-6 on a survey. This provides a quick and efficient way to respond to negative feedback and resolve any issues that may have arisen. The automatic ticket includes all relevant information, such as the customer's contact information, the specific feedback received, and any other relevant data. In addition to automatic tickets, manual tickets can be created by manually entering information into the required fields. This manual ticket creation provides an additional layer of control and customization, allowing companies to address any customer feedback that may not be captured through the automatic ticketing process. By combining automatic and manual ticket creation, the closed-loop feedback system provides a comprehensive and flexible approach to managing the customer experience. It allows companies to respond quickly and effectively to negative feedback, while also providing the ability to address more complex issues in a more personalized manner. The end result is improved customer satisfaction, increased customer loyalty, and a stronger overall relationship between a company and its customers. Ticket

this page will help you to understand about the ticketing system, how it works and which features it contains.

Tickets How does a ticket get created in Enhanced Closed Loop? There are two ways in which tickets get created in Enhanced Closed Loop Action Alerts in CX Manual Tickets in Enhanced Closed Loop All the tickets that get generated based on the action alerts set in your CX survey will be shown under their respective Workspace. To check the tickets created in your Workspace Go to Enhanced Closed Loop >>Select your Workspace Once you open the Workspace, all the tickets generated from the surveys in the workspace will be displayed here: For creating manual tickets, there is a "+" sign available. You click on it and create tickets with the details How does a ticketing system work in CLF? The closed-loop feedback system, as used in the context of customer experience management, is designed to continuously monitor and improve the interactions between a company and its customers. Basically, feedback needs to be created from CX where the responses of customers will show against a detailed survey. There could be several surveys in one feedback, and the feedback name will appear on the closed loop dashboard containing tickets for each. The integration of automatic and manual ticket creation in this system ensures that all customer feedback is recorded and acted upon, no matter how it is received. An automatic ticket is generated when a customer provides a rating of 0-6 on a survey. This provides a quick and efficient way to respond to negative feedback and resolve any issues that may have arisen. The automatic ticket includes all relevant information, such as the customer's contact information, the specific feedback received, and any other relevant data. In addition to automatic tickets, manual tickets can be created by manually entering information into the required fields. This manual ticket creation provides an additional layer of control and customization, allowing companies to address any customer feedback that may not be captured through the automatic ticketing process. By combining automatic and manual ticket creation, the closed-loop feedback system provides a comprehensive and flexible approach to managing the customer experience. It allows companies to respond quickly and effectively to negative feedback, while also providing the ability to address more complex issues in a more personalized manner. The end result is improved customer satisfaction, increased customer loyalty, and a stronger overall relationship between a company and its customers. What is the advantage of different types of views in CLF? The Closed Loop Feedback (CLF) ticketing system offers three types of views to manage customer feedback and tickets: the list view, the feed view, and the kanban view. Each of these views has its own advantages, allowing users to choose the view that best suits their needs and work style. List View:This view provides a detailed, organized list of all tickets. It includes all relevant information about each ticket, such as the customer's name, feedback type, status, and segment. The list view is ideal for those who need a comprehensive, at-a-glance overview of all tickets. Feed View:The feed view provides a more conversational and real-time view of the tickets. It displays tickets in chronological order, making it easier to track the progression of each ticket and to see how feedback is being addressed in real-time. Kanban View:The kanban view organizes tickets into columns based on their status (e.g., new, open, escalated, resolved). This view is useful for those who need to visualize the progress of tickets and prioritize tasks. It provides a quick and visual way to understand the current status of each ticket and the overall workload. Can the tickets be edited? In the CLF ticketing system, editing tickets is possible, but with certain limitations. The following elements can be changed. Segments: The current segment of the ticket can be modified to better align with the company's internal processes and workflows. Priority: The level of urgency of the ticket can be changed based on new information or updated circumstances. Status: The progress of the ticket can be updated to reflect the current stage of resolution. Assignee: The person responsible for resolving the ticket can be changed to better align with the company's internal resources and expertise, though it is dependent on the current segments. In the CLF ticketing system, certain elements like customer name, date, and email are non-editable to maintain accurate and consistent record-keeping. While some ticket details can be edited, it's important to understand and respect these restrictions for the resolution processes and to get accurate also efficient feedback from customers. Is it possible to assign tickets automatically? Yes, it is possible only when the assignee is selected from the time being of the ticket has been created. In CLF, tickets can be automatically escalated by adding escalation rules in the settings section. To enable this feature, the user needs to specify the assignee and time for escalation. Once the rule is added, all the tickets for the selected feedback and segment will follow the escalation process. However, it is important to note that tickets that have already been resolved, escalated manually, or not assigned will not be part of the auto-escalation process. body: Ticket Details In this page we will discuss on the ticket detail, what features we get inside a ticket Ticket Details Is it possible to change any information from the ticket details? Yes, it is possible to change informations like- assignee, current segment, status and priority How the action tab is working in the ticket details? The Action tab in the ticket details page facilitates direct communication with the customer, enabling efficient and professional interactions to address their needs and provide timely support. What is the purpose of the comment tab in the ticket details section? The comment tab in the ticket details section serves as a communication channel for internal team members or stakeholders to provide updates, notes, or additional information related to the ticket. It allows for seamless collaboration and ensures that all relevant information and discussions are captured within the ticketing system for reference and transparency. How does the root causes tab benefit users in the ticketing system? The root causes tab in the ticketing system benefit users by enabling them to identify underlying issues, implement targeted solutions, and proactively prevent future problems. It ensures efficient and consistent issue resolution, facilitates collaboration and knowledge sharing, and supports data-driven decision-making. summary: Configure and understand the AI Response Assist feature in QuestionPro's enhanced closed loop feedback, including setup, usage, and capabilities
body: AI Response Assist Configure and understand the AI Response Assist feature in QuestionPro's enhanced closed loop feedback, including setup, usage, and capabilities AI response assist AI response assist overview Watch this video for a quick overview of the AI Response Assist feature in QuestionPro’s enhanced closed loop feedback system. Your browser does not support the video tag. What is AI response assist? AI Response Assist is a powerful feature in QuestionPro's enhanced closed loop system that automates customer response management. It provides intelligent, personalized replies to customer feedback, enabling organizations to respond quickly and improve support quality. How does AI response assist work? The feature automates feedback handling through the following steps: Analyzes incoming customer feedback and survey responses Identifies sentiment and assigns priority levels Generates contextually appropriate response drafts Tracks response effectiveness to enable future improvement Automates the delivery of personalized communication Where to find AI response assist Navigate to the following location: Customer Experience → Closedloop → Workspace → Ticket → Actions How to use AI response assist? Steps to use the AI response assist feature: Go to Closedloop and select a workspace Click on the Ticket ID you want to respond to Review the Customer Details and feedback context Navigate to the Action tab on the ticket overview page Click the QuestionPro AI button to generate a response draft If needed, click Refine to choose from editing actions You can also, click Intent to choose from email intent options Click Insert to add the draft response to the ticket License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: CLF Ticket Resolution Checklist
kw: ticket resolution step checklist mandatory steps workspace closed loop status clf feature resolved settings go requirements enhanced feedback customer experience video questionpro overview key system not click general any now see all try updating required completed no edit change license
summary: Understand and implement the CLF Ticket Resolution Checklist feature in QuestionPro’s enhanced Closed Loop Feedback, including an overview, key functionality, and step-by-step workflow.
body: CLF Ticket Resolution Checklist Understand and implement the CLF Ticket Resolution Checklist feature in QuestionPro’s enhanced Closed Loop Feedback, including an overview, key functionality, and step-by-step workflow. Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist What is the Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist? The CLF Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist is a key feature within the eCLF (Enhanced Closed-Loop Feedback) system that enforces the completion of critical validation steps before a ticket can be marked as resolved. By making these steps mandatory, the checklist ensures that every ticket resolution is reliable, consistent, and auditable, leading to improved internal accountability and customer experience. Watch this video for a quick overview of the CLF Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist feature in QuestionPro’s enhanced Closed Loop Feedback system. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video Where do I configure the Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist? Customer Experience » Closed Loop » Workspace » Workspace Settings » General Settings How do I enable the Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist? Step 1: Navigate to Closed Loop and open any workspace you’d like to explore. Step 2: Inside the workspace, go to Workspace Settings. Step 3: Under General Settings, you will now see Ticket Resolution Requirements. Step 4: Go to All Tickets and click a Ticket ID to view its details. Step 5: Go to Ticket Status and try updating the status to "Resolved". Step 6: A new modal appears with a list of required ticket resolution steps, with "Complete" as the CTA. Step 7: Clicking on any of the CTAs takes you to the relevant requirement tab, where you must perform the action in order to mark the step as completed. Step 8: Finish the remaining required actions by repeating the previous steps. Step 9: Go to Ticket Status again and try updating the status to "Resolved". Step 10: You will now see the ticket status update to "Resolved" once all mandatory resolution requirements are completed. Can I customize the steps Mandatory Ticket Resolution Checklist? No, customizing the mandatory ticket resolution checklist steps in the current version is not allowed. Can anyone using the workspace edit or change the ticket resolution requirements? No, only managers or admins can edit or change the ticket resolution requirements. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience

summary: Learn about the intuitive ticket status board drag and drop feature in QuestionPro’s closed loop feedback system, including overview, key functionality, and benefits.
body: Intuitive Ticket Status Board Learn about the intuitive ticket status board drag and drop feature in QuestionPro’s closed loop feedback system, including overview, key functionality, and benefits. Intuitive ticket status board Intuitive ticket status board overview Watch this video for a quick overview of the intuitive ticket status board drag and drop feature in QuestionPro’s closed loop feedback ticketing system. Your browser does not support the video tag. What is the intuitive ticket status board? The intuitive ticket status board is a feature within the closed loop feedback (CLF) system that allows support agents to update ticket statuses easily by dragging and dropping tickets between status columns such as new, open, escalated, and resolved. This visual and interactive interface streamlines ticket status management and improves workflow efficiency. What does the feature do? Enables users to move tickets between different status columns via drag-and-drop action. Automatically preserves the order of tickets within each column using position values for accurate placement. Prompts additional input when escalating tickets, such as segment selection and assignment to a user. Improves workflow by reducing clicks and the need to navigate into ticket details for status changes. Benefits of the intuitive ticket status board Enhances efficiency by allowing rapid ticket status changes with drag-and-drop. Provides a user-friendly, visual way of managing ticket workflows. Reduces unnecessary navigation by eliminating the need to open the ticket details page for status updates. Supports escalation workflows with required selection and assignment steps. Maintains clear, ordered organization of tickets within each status column. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: CLF AI-generated Tags

summary: Ai-generated tags overview and usage
body: CLF AI-generated Tags Ai-generated tags overview and usage AI-generated tags What are AI-generated tags? AI-generated tags automatically classify new support tickets based on their content using advanced language models. This feature significantly reduces manual effort, ensures consistency, and improves the speed and accuracy of ticket triage, trend identification, and reporting. Watch this video for a quick overview of the AI-generated tags feature in QuestionPro’s enhanced Closedloop feedback system. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video Where can I find AI-generated tags? Customer experience » Closedloop » Workspace » Ticket » Customer details How do I use AI-generated tags? Step 1: Navigate to Closedloop and open any workspace you’d like to explore. Step 2: Inside the workspace, click the plus (+) icon to create a new ticket. Step 3: Enter customer details, including feedback in the ticket description, then hit save. Step 4: Go to all tickets and click a ticket ID to view its details. Step 5: Go to the customer details tab and review the ticket description for feedback and context. Step 6: Scroll to the AI-generated tags section to see automatically suggested tags based on ticket content. Step 7: Click any tag to instantly filter and view other tickets with the same theme or topic. Step 8: Click the filter icon to select or search for more tags of your choice. Step 9: Click Apply Filter to see all tickets filtered by the tags you selected in one view. Can I edit tags? No, tags are autogenerated using AI and cannot be manually edited. Can I manually add or remove tags? Manual addition or removal of tags is not supported in the current version of AI-generated tagging. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience

summary: Learn how to use the Bulk Action Toolbar in QuestionPro’s Closed Loop Feedback system to quickly update, assign, or manage multiple tickets at once. Includes overview, feature details, benefits, and s
body: Bulk Action Toolbar Learn how to use the Bulk Action Toolbar in QuestionPro’s Closed Loop Feedback system to quickly update, assign, or manage multiple tickets at once. Includes overview, feature details, benefits, and step-by-step workflow. Bulk Action Toolbar What is the Bulk Action Toolbar? The Bulk Action Toolbar is a feature in QuestionPro’s Closed Loop Feedback (CLF) system that appears at the top of the ticket table when one or more tickets are selected using checkboxes. It enables agents to make batch updates—such as changing the assignee, status, or priority—efficiently, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and auditability across multiple tickets at once. Watch this video for a quick overview of the Bulk Action Toolbar in QuestionPro’s Closed Loop Feedback system, demonstrating how to update, assign, and manage multiple tickets at once. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video Where do I find the Bulk Action Toolbar? The toolbar automatically appears at the top of the ticket table whenever one or more tickets are selected in the workspace ticket view. How do I use the Bulk Action Toolbar? Step 1: Go to your workspace and open the ticket table view. Step 2: Select the tickets you want to update using the checkboxes beside each ticket row. Step 3: The Bulk Action Toolbar will appear at the top of the table with options for Status, Priority, and Delete. Step 4: Choose the desired action (e.g., change status to "Open") from the dropdown menu in the toolbar. Step 5: Confirm the bulk action in the pop-up dialog to apply changes to all selected tickets. Step 6: Once confirmed, the updates will reflect instantly in the ticket table, and all changes will be logged in the ticket activity history for audit purposes. Step 7: All changes will be logged in the ticket activity history for audit purposes. What actions can I perform using the Bulk Action Toolbar? Select multiple tickets at once using checkboxes beside each row. Change the ticket Status or Priority in bulk. Delete multiple tickets at once using the selection checkboxes. Review and confirm bulk updates in a confirmation dialog to prevent mistakes. Track changes instantly in the ticket table with full activity logs. Benefits of the Bulk Action Toolbar Boosts agent productivity by reducing repetitive single-ticket edits. Minimizes errors and rework through confirmation dialogs and clear feedback. Ensures auditability by logging all bulk changes with detailed history. Provides a seamless, responsive experience across desktop and laptop devices. Limitations of the Bulk Action Toolbar Cannot perform actions on tickets outside the current workspace view. Some fields or custom ticket attributes may not be supported for bulk updates. Bulk actions require proper permissions; only agents with sufficient rights can execute changes. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Th

title: Root cause actions

summary: Learn about the revamped Root Cause Actions in QuestionPro’s Closedloop (CLF 3.0), including mandatory linkage to root causes, structured workflows, and new analytics widgets.
body: Root cause actions Learn about the revamped Root Cause Actions in QuestionPro’s Closedloop (CLF 3.0), including mandatory linkage to root causes, structured workflows, and new analytics widgets. Root Cause Actions What are Root Cause Actions? Root Cause Actions are a revamped management system within the Closedloop (CLF) platform designed to ensure that corrective steps taken during ticket resolution are meaningful, traceable, and actionable. By moving away from unstructured free-text notes, the system now enforces a mandatory linkage model where every action is explicitly tagged to a specific root cause. This structure drives accountability and provides deep insights into which actions effectively reduce ticket recurrence. Watch this video for a quick overview of the Ticket Actions revamp and the new centralized root cause integration. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How to Configure Root Cause Actions in Settings? Administrators can manage the library of available actions through Workspace Settings. Please note that the "Root causes" and "Centralized root causes" tabs have been removed from this view to simplify management. Navigate to Customer Experience → Closedloop → Workspace Settings → Root cause actions If no actions exist, you will see a new empty state; click “Create new root cause action”. Enter the Action title (e.g., Provide refund, Escalate to manager) Select the Root Causes that should be linked to this new action. You must select at least one root cause to activate the create button. Click Create. How to edit the root cause actions once created? In the list view, you can hover over an action to edit, copy or delete it. Deleting or editing an action will reflect across all tickets and activity logs. How to Use Root Cause Actions in a Ticket? Agents must now document specific actions tied to the identified issue before closing a ticket. Open a Ticket and navigate to the dedicated Root cause tab. Identify and select the Root Cause for the ticket first. This is required before actions become available. Go to the Root Cause Actions tab to see the list of available actions. Select the action taken by clicking the checkbox. A confirmation appears on top of the page to confirm action tagging. If a necessary action is missing, Managers can click “+ Add root cause action” directly within the ticket to create a new association. Frequently Asked Questions Can I resolve a ticket without selecting a Root Cause Action? Yes. Hpwever, if the mandatory checklist feature is enabled, tickets cannot be resolved until a root cause action has been tagged to the ticket . What happened to my old Root Cause data? During the feature release, old ticket root cause data is automatically migrated to the new root cause action linked data format. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

body: Ticket Import/Export This page will provide information for export and import system in closed loop feedback Export Tickets How can I download the tickets data from Closedloop? The closed loop feedback system provides a seamless export feature, allowing users to directly export tickets in an Excel file (.xlsx). Users can download tickets from each Workspacr separately. To download data, go to the Manage tab from the left navigation menu and in the Export section youwill find the option to select the Workspace. Does the ticketing system allow users to export tickets based on specific criteria or filters? Yes, users can use the Advance opiton to apply filters based on Stats, Priority, Type, Segment, Assignee and date. Users can also download ticket data for a particular survey. Also, we provide optoin to customzie the columns in the excel report. You can pick and chose which column will be a part of the excel download License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? summary: Learn about the improved activity history feature in QuestionPro’s closed loop feedback system, including overview, design enhancements, and benefits.
body: CLF Activity History Learn about the improved activity history feature in QuestionPro’s closed loop feedback system, including overview, design enhancements, and benefits. Activity history Activity history overview Watch this video for a quick overview of the activity history feature in QuestionPro’s closed loop feedback ticketing system. Your browser does not support the video tag. What is the activity history? The activity history is a redesigned visual feature within the closed loop feedback (CLF) system that presents a clear, structured record of all activities related to a ticket. It organizes events with improved readability to enhance support agents' ability to track ticket progress efficiently. Coming soon, ETA October 2025 What does the feature do? Introduces a vertical timeline with a clean line connecting events, visually linking them in chronological order. Groups events by date with bold date headers (e.g., “Monday 7 Jul 2025”), providing clear temporal structure. Aligns events to the right of the timeline using a compact, readable layout with effective styling for hierarchy. Displays dots for each event on the timeline to indicate discrete activity points. Enhances visual hierarchy to distinguish different types of activities clearly. Benefits of the activity history Speeds up scanning by enabling users to easily follow the flow of ticket activities in chronological order. Improves user experience by providing a cleaner, more organized, and less cluttered interface. Reduces cognitive load and user fatigue by clearly grouping related activities and using visual hierarchy. Aligns with modern design standards seen in productivity and ticketing tools, adding professional appearance. Increases productivity for support agents by making it faster to find, interpret, and act on activity history. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

body: Analytics In this page we will discuss on the analytics part of Closed Loop Feedback as how it will work Analytics How can the analytics dashboard help the user? In the Analytics section of CLF, users are presented with a comprehensive overview of their performance and the performance of their team. The following graphs and charts are included: Ticket Timeline Graph: A visual representation of the number of new and resolved tickets over a given period of time. Ticket Priority Resolved Ratio Graph: This graph displays the ratio of resolved tickets based on their priority in both percentage and count. Tickets by Survey Graph:This graph provides a visual representation of the feedback received from customers regarding the ticket resolution process in both percentage and count. Top Ticket Solver Graph:This graph showcases the top assignee names in terms of resolved tickets. Root Cause Frequency:This graph displays the frequency of the root causes of tickets in terms of both percentage and count. Action Frequency:This graph shows the frequency of actions taken to resolve tickets in terms of both percentage and count. Priority Trend Graph:This graph represents the trend of ticket priority over a given period of time. Support Staff Analysis Graph:This graph displays an analysis of the performance of support staff in terms of ticket resolution. Basically for this term, the user needs to select the feedback very first, from the filter option. Experience the power of our analytics dashboard, offering a captivating glimpse into key insights, trends, and data visualizations that unravel the story behind your ticketing system. Uncover valuable metrics, track performance, and make informed decisions with ease, elevating your understanding and optimizing your support processes for exceptional results- These graphs and charts help the user to identify strengths and weaknesses, track progress, and make informed decisions to improve the ticket resolution process and overall customer satisfaction. How can a user get the best report from the analytics dashboard? To get the best report from the CLF analytics dashboard, a user can utilize the following steps: Choose the specific feedback and segment from the filtering option, based on the requirement. Select the desired date range or assignee to narrow down the report. Utilize the available graphs, such as the Ticket Timeline, Ticket Priority Resolved Ratio, Tickets by Survey, Top Ticket Solver, Root Cause Frequency, Action Frequency, Priority Trend, and Support Stuff Analysis, to understand the performance of the team and the tickets. Analyze the data presented in the graphs to identify any trends or patterns. Use the information to make informed decisions and take action to improve the ticket-handling process. By following these steps, a user can effectively use the analytics dashboard to get the best report. Is it possible to access data from the analytics dashboard without applying any filters? title: Sentiment Analysis-Tickets

summary: Detailed help documentation for PathosAI provided by QuestionPro
body: Sentiment Analysis-Tickets Detailed help documentation for PathosAI provided by QuestionPro PathosAI What is Sentiment Analysis? Sentiment Analysis refers to the use of natural language processing, text analysis, computational linguistics, and biometrics to systematically identify, extract, quantify, and study affective states and subjective information. Sentiment analysis is widely applied to the voice of the customer data. We use our in house solution for sentiment analysis- PathosAI. Using PathosAI you can analyze your multi-line ticket description and understand the customers’ opinion and sentiment behind every keyword in the response. The tool performs following tasks: Categorize by theme and topic Implicit and Explicit Sentiment detection - at an overall level as well as by topic and theme How do I set up PathosAI in Enhanced Closed loop? Go to: Login >> Closedloop>> Analytics. . Click on the Sentiment Analysis icon in the left navigation menu How can I set up the sentiment industry? Setting up sentiment industry has to be configured from the Global Settings of your QuestionPro account To set up sentiment industry: Sentiment Analysis dashboard displays Default Closedloop Filter Overall sentiment score Sentiment score by theme Ticket sentiment distribution Details Overall sentiment score The overall sentiment score is presented on a scale from 1 to 5. The sentiment bar is distributed into 6 sentiments Very Negative, Negative, Mixed, Positive, Very Positive and Neutral Sentiments. The PathosAI model analyzes customer expressions to unearth the ‘intensity’ of sentiment. Intensity of sentiment expressed by customers show how important the issue is to them as well as the extent to which it is likely to affect their future decisions. We measure and report sentiments on the following range: very positive positive—-neutral mixed negative very negative The categorization between the Very Positive to Positive and Very Negative to Negative is done based on the language used by the customer, number of issues or positive areas, willingness to recommend, intention to spend, likelihood to switch, level of satisfaction. Based on this, the model predicts the correct sentiment. Very Positive sentiments refer to superior experience and potentially higher levels of loyalty and intention to spend, while Very Negative sentiment means higher levels of negativity, which could mean higher retention and revenue at risk. This categorization will help our clients prioritize the most critical areas to focus on first. Sentiment score by theme Within these charts, there are multiple themes available, yet the top 10 themes are exhibited alongside their associated sentiment scores. When the breakdown feature is enabled, it will provide sentiment scores categorized by themes for the chosen responses. For instance, if you choose responses related to "How often do you travel via airline ?" The chart will exhibit sentiment scores grouped by themes for the selected responses. W

Sentiment Analysis-Tickets

Detailed help documentation for PathosAI provided by QuestionPro

PathosAI What is Sentiment Analysis? Sentiment Analysis refers to the use of natural language processing, text analysis, computational linguistics, and biometrics to systematically identify, extract, quantify, and study affective states and subjective information. Sentiment analysis is widely applied to the voice of the customer data. We use our in house solution for sentiment analysis- PathosAI. Using PathosAI you can analyze your multi-line ticket description and understand the customers’ opinion and sentiment behind every keyword in the response. The tool performs following tasks: Categorize by theme and topic Implicit and Explicit Sentiment detection - at an overall level as well as by topic and theme How do I set up PathosAI in Enhanced Closed loop? Go to: Login >> Closedloop>> Analytics. . Click on the Sentiment Analysis icon in the left navigation menu How can I set up the sentiment industry? Setting up sentiment industry has to be configured from the Global Settings of your QuestionPro account To set up sentiment industry: Sentiment Analysis dashboard displays Default Closedloop Filter Overall sentiment score Sentiment score by theme Ticket sentiment distribution Details Overall sentiment score The overall sentiment score is presented on a scale from 1 to 5. The sentiment bar is distributed into 6 sentiments Very Negative, Negative, Mixed, Positive, Very Positive and Neutral Sentiments. The PathosAI model analyzes customer expressions to unearth the ‘intensity’ of sentiment. Intensity of sentiment expressed by customers show how important the issue is to them as well as the extent to which it is likely to affect their future decisions. We measure and report sentiments on the following range: very positive-----positive—-neutral----mixed----negative-----very negative The categorization between the Very Positive to Positive and Very Negative to Negative is done based on the language used by the customer, number of issues or positive areas, willingness to recommend, intention to spend, likelihood to switch, level of satisfaction. Based on this, the model predicts the correct sentiment. Very Positive sentiments refer to superior experience and potentially higher levels of loyalty and intention to spend, while Very Negative sentiment means higher levels of negativity, which could mean higher retention and revenue at risk. This categorization will help our clients prioritize the most critical areas to focus on first. Sentiment score by theme Within these charts, there are multiple themes available, yet the top 10 themes are exhibited alongside their associated sentiment scores. When the breakdown feature is enabled, it will provide sentiment scores categorized by themes for the chosen responses. For instance, if you choose responses related to "How often do you travel via airline ?" The chart will exhibit sentiment scores grouped by themes for the selected responses. When you click on any of the bars in the charts, it will prompt a pop-up window to appear, presenting all comments and details regarding the current selection. body: Settings This page will help to get the knowledge about the settings of closed loop feedback Workspace Settings What configuration options are available in the settings? The CLF settings encompass various key features, including General settings, Email Template, Root Causes, Rootcause Actions, Escalation, Email Settings, Segment Controller, Winback Rule and Notification. These settings offer comprehensive control and customization options, allowing users to configure general system preferences, define action templates, manage actions and root causes, synchronize temporary data, set up escalation rules, configure email settings, and administer the CLF system. With these powerful features, users can tailor the CLF settings to align with their unique requirements, ensuring optimal functionality and efficiency in their feedback management process. General Settings How does the ticket become overdue? In CLF, there is a system for getting the tickets overdue and the system will get in the general settings. The main manager can set the overdue time from the settings. If the manager set 1 day then the ticket will be overdue after 1 day and it will show an alert symbol beside the ticket id in the ticket listing against feedback. What is Escalation Status? In order mark to have access for the Esaclate status, users need to enable to escalte toggle. Action Email Template How to create a Action email template? Click the + New template>>Name the Template>> Select the Type based on your content>>Type in your content>> Save the template Once created, action email templates are available to all users and managers within the workspace where the templates are created. Root Causes Can the Root Causes be customized according to the specific business needs in the CLF system? In CLF, it is possible to customize by adding Root Causes and for different business purposes, but only the main manager has the ability to create or customize the Root Causes. Other managers can select multiple root causes from the root cause tab of the ticket details if they want and are able to change them as well. Rootcause Actions Can the Root Cause Actions be customized according to the specific business needs in the CLF system? In CLF, it is possible to customize by adding Root Causes action for different business purposes, and only the main manager has the ability to create or customize the actions. Other managers can select multiple actions from the root cause tab of the ticket details page if they want and are able to change them as well. With these powerful features, users can tailor the CLF settings to align with their unique requirements, ensuring optimal functionality and efficiency in their feedback management process. General Settings How does the ticket become overdue? In CLF, there is a system for getting the tickets overdue and the system will get in the general settings. The main manager can set the overdue time from the settings. If the manager set 1 day then the ticket will be overdue after 1 day and it will show an alert symbol beside the ticket id in the ticket listing against feedback. What is Escalation Status? In order mark to have access for the Esaclate status, users need to enable to escalte toggle. Action Email Template How to create a Action email template? Click the + New template>>Name the Template>> Select the Type based on your content>>Type in your content>> Save the template Once created, action email templates are available to all users and managers within the workspace where the templates are created. Root Causes Can the Root Causes be customized according to the specific business needs in the CLF system? In CLF, it is possible to customize by adding Root Causes and for different business purposes, but only the main manager has the ability to create or customize the Root Causes. Other managers can select multiple root causes from the root cause tab of the ticket details if they want and are able to change them as well. Rootcause Actions Can the Root Cause Actions be customized according to the specific business needs in the CLF system? In CLF, it is possible to customize by adding Root Causes action for different business purposes, and only the main manager has the ability to create or customize the actions. Other managers can select multiple actions from the root cause tab of the ticket details page if they want and are able to change them as well. Escalation How does the escalation process function within the settings of the ticketing system? To activate auto-escalation, users enable the feature, select feedback and specify the segment. This ensures that tickets meeting the criteria are automatically escalated for prompt resolution, streamlining ticket management and addressing critical issues efficiently. Within CLF, users have the capability to establish multiple rules that operate to escalate based on the level numbers. Email Settings How does the email settings function in the CLF system, enabling effective communication with users? Users can enhance customer communication by enabling the action alert feature with a simple click of the checkbox. This empowers them to send timely and personalized email notifications to customers, ensuring effective and proactive engagement throughout the ticket resolution process. The signature section in CLF allows users to define and customize their email signature, which will be appended to outgoing emails sent to customers. This feature adds a touch of professionalism and personalization to the communication, allowing users to showcase their brand identity and provide essential contact information. By utilizing the signature feature, users can ensure consistent and impactful email communication that leaves a lasting impression on their customers. Winback Rules What is a winback survey? Winback Surveys is an exclusive feature available in QuestionPro that is specifically designed to check the satisfaction of the customers upon ticket resolution. Once a detractor ticket is created in our closed-loop feedback system, the assigned manager can work on the ticket resolution process. A solution is provided for the issue raised and after effective communication with the customer, the ticket is marked resolved. Currently, there is no way to rectify the negative NPS score associated with the detractor ticket. Even after ticket resolution, the NPS score associated with the ticket is a detractor. Winback survey allows users to send a new survey once the ticket is resolved and captures the updated NPS score. Notifications What are ticket notifications? Notifications help you set up alerts for any activity taken on your tickets. These alerts can be sent to the Admin or the Manager added in your CX Workspace. Alerts can be set for various actions like ticket creation, ticket deletion, comments added by other users. All the notifications are workspace specific. body: Email Settings This page will help to get the knowledge about the email settings of closed loop feedback Email Settings Action Alerts Users can enhance customer communication by enabling the action alert feature with a simple click of the checkbox. This empowers them to send timely and personalized email notifications to customers, ensuring effective and proactive engagement throughout the ticket resolution process. SMTP Configuration Users have the flexibility to set up their own SMTP server, allowing them to send and receive emails from a dedicated server. To configure the SMTP relay enter all details for the SMTP connection. MTP relay uses your corporate SMTP server settings for sending emails and provides authenticity and thereby avoids the email invite being flagged off as SPAM/Junk mail. Email Content The email content section within the eCLF system allows users to customize the email body and subject that will be sent to customers. This feature provides a personalized and professional communication channel, enabling users to craft engaging and impactful email messages tailored to the specific needs of their customers. Signature Users can create custom signatures to append to the email replies they send to their customers from the closed loop portal. Here tey can set up multiple signatures and use it while drafting the action emails. We also have a option to set a default signature. title: Winback Survey
kw: winback survey ticket nps score once select created resolved create template rules resolution detractor marked no new tab feature available check loop feedback customer there associated want go email set rule add workspace triggered original yes response license exclusive questionpro
summary: winback-survey
body: Winback Survey winback-survey Winback Survey Winback survey is an exclusive feature available in QuestionPro that is specifically designed to check the satisfaction of the customers upon ticket resolution. Once a detractor ticket is created in our closed-loop feedback system, the assigned manager can work on the ticket resolution process. A solution is provided for the issue raised and after effective communication with the customer, the ticket is marked resolved. Currently, there is no way to rectify the negative NPS score associated with the detractor ticket. Even after ticket resolution, the NPS score associated with the ticket is a detractor. Winback survey allows users to send a new survey once the ticket is resolved and captures the updated NPS score. How can you create a Winback Survey? Create a survey in your CX account that you want to use as your Winback survey Once the survey is created, go to Enhanced Cloosed loop and create Winback email template While creating the template for your Winback survey, select the template type as Winback Next, we have to set up the Winback rules. To set winback rules: Go to Settings> Winback Rules. Create a winback rule by clicking on the + Add Rules button Select the Workspace in which you have created the Winback survey You can also add the Segment for which you want to trigger the Winback survey. Select the survey from the drop-down Select the NPS question Select the Winback email template Save the rule Now, for the tickets created under the selected workspace winback survey will be triggered for every ticket that is marked Resolved Once the Winback survey is triggered, a new tab will appear where you can check all the winback details. Once the ticket is marked resolved and the Winback survey is sent you can't change the ticket status In the analytics tab, the Winback NPS trend graph will show the data for the number of Detractors converted to Promoters Does Winback NPS score replace the original survey NPS score? No, the Winback NPS score is captured separately and the existing NPS score of the survey is not affected by the Winback NPS score. Is there an identifying factor between the responses collected on the original survey and the Winback survey? Yes, a new response ID is generated for the Winback survey response which is displayed in the Winback tab of the ticket. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Notifications for Tickets
kw: ticket notification notifications email manager users sent selected alerts tickets status priority set assignee creation segment delete criteria get if everytime updated admin well added inactivity select type send default deletion comments provided no taken cx workspace content allows soon
summary: notifications
body: Notifications for Tickets notifications Notifications for Tickets Notifications help you set up alerts for any activity taken on your tickets. These alerts can be sent to the Admin or the Manager added in your CX Workspace. Alerts can be set for various actions like ticket creation, ticket deletion, comments added by other users. All the notifications are workspace specific. Here is a glimpse of the notification alerts: How do I set up a new notification? Go to Settings » Notification Click on the +New Notification button and name your notification Select the workspace From the list, select the Notification type Select the Manager from the list to whom the notification needs to be sent. You can select the admin as well Add the email content and the subject line and click on the Create button What are the different types of notifications you can set up? The different types of notifications are- Ticket Creation This notification type allows you to send alerts as soon as the ticket is created in eCLF. This notification can be sent to the default ticket manager, the ticket segment manager or the parent segment manager Ticket Delete - Upon ticket deletion, the selected users in the notification criteria get the delete notification Ticket assignee - If the ticket assignee is changed, the users selected in the notification criteria get an email notification Ticket Status - Everytime the ticket status is updated, the selected manager or admin gets the email notification Ticket Priority - Similarly for priority change as well, everytime there is an updated in the ticket priority the users are notified Ticket Comment - For the comments added on the tickets, we have provided an option for sending notifications. Ticket Inactivity Status - If no action has been taken on the ticket, this notification will be sent to the selected users. You can set the threshold for the number of days defining inactivity All notifications except the Ticket Creation notification will be sent to the ticket assignee by default Customization for Notifications While creating the notifications we have provided multiple options to customize the alerts, here are the details: Additional email addresses: Users can now send email notifications to email addresses which are not a part of their CX organization. Email Content: You can customize the email content and include the survey response as well the custom variable values. The email subject line can also be customized now The From Email Address cannot be edited. Notifications will be sent from cxtickets@cx-email.com License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Escalation settings
kw: escalation settings create rules configure button segment ticket click selecting levels set rule time feature feedback closedloop workspace see system next once sets questionpro enhanced closed loop go page enter necessary details configuring range wait escalating unresolved choosing manager whom
summary: Configure and understand the ticket Escalation feature in QuestionPro's enhanced closed loop feedback, including setup, usage, and capabilities
body: Escalation settings Configure and understand the ticket Escalation feature in QuestionPro's enhanced closed loop feedback, including setup, usage, and capabilities Escalation settings Escalation settings overview Watch this video for a quick overview of the Escalation settings in QuestionPro’s enhanced closed loop feedback system. Your browser does not support the video tag. What does Escalation feature do? Escalation settings allow you to configure time bound rules to automatically escalate tickets once the set time is elapsed. It allows you to create multi-tiered escalation setups. Where to find Escalation settings Navigate to the following location: Customer Experience → Closedloop → Workspace → Settings → Escalation How to configure escalation rules? Steps to configure escalation rules: Go to Closedloop → Workspace → Settings → Escalation and click on Create escalation rule button You will see a page to enter necessary details for configuring the escalation rules Configure the rule by selecting a segment, selecting a time range the system should wait before escalating an unresolved ticket, and choosing the manager to whom the ticket should be escalated You can add upto 5 levels of escalations by clicking on + Next level button and once you're done setting up the levels, click on Create escalation workflow button to save the escalation settings What actions can I perform for the existing escalation sets? You can edit, duplicate, activate/deactivate and delete the escalation sets To edit a set for a particular segment, click on the segment name name Copy and Delete options are shown on the right side when you hover on any escalation set You can also perform bulk actions by selecting multiple sets How many escalation rules can I create? You can create only 1 escalation set for each Segment. Each esclation set can have a maximum of 5 levels Is there a way to see the rules that have been deleted? Yes, you can use the Activity logs icon next to the Segment filter to access the activity logs. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Custom variables general
kw: custom variables variable survey ext ref use email characters url data using questionpro information link used additional also please pass external reference following xxxx management each https com special passed respondents upload if character more visit example yes tags license
summary: Customer Experience - How to set up custom variables while programming a survey using Survey Analytics.
body: Custom variables general Customer Experience - How to set up custom variables while programming a survey using Survey Analytics. CX - Custom Variables Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information that is to be passed to the Survey. Custom Variables when passed to the Survey are stored with the Respondents Response. You can view the Custom Variables online using the Response Viewer. Custom Variables are also available in the Excel and CSV Raw Data downloads. You can also analyze your data based on Custom Variables. To track e-mail addresses / Custom Variables please use the Respondent Tracking link when sending emails via QuestionPro Email Management. How can I pass in additional information to the Survey? You can pass in additional information in the Survey using Custom Variables. What are the limits on Custom Variables. You can upload up to 100 custom variables. Each Custom Variable can store up to 512 characters. You cannot use a Comma (,) or the Pound/Hash (#) sign in the Custom Variables. Note: If you are passing the Custom Variables / External Reference Variable via the URL please limit your use to Alphanumeric Characters (A to Z, a to z, 0 to 9). Avoid use of Special Characters: Space Character (" "), Pound/Hash character ("#"), Ampersand ("&"), Percent character ("%"), Question Mark ("?"), etc. How to pass in the Custom Variables. When using QuestionPro Email Management tool you can upload the Custom Variables with each respondents email address. For more information please visit the Upload Custom Variables link below. If you are Not using QuestionPro Email Management tool for distribution then you can pass in Custom Variables along with the Survey URL. Following is the format for passing in the Custom Variables: After the URL (page name) we use question mark ( ? ) to start the variable data pair and we separate each variable data pair with one ampersand ( & ) mark. Example Survey URL: https://questionpro.com/t/AIZBjs6 Custom Variables are passed as follows: https://questionpro.com/t/AIZBjs6?custom1=John&custom2=Wayne&custom3=Sales&custom4=23456&custom5=Manager Do Custom Variable support multi-lingual characters? Yes, you can upload Multi-lingual characters as Custom Variables. Limited special characters are also supported. Where can I use the Custom Variables? Custom Variables can be used in Questions / Answer Options on the Survey. You can also use Custom Variables in the Email Invitation. For more information please visit the Personalizing Email Invitations link under Additional References. How to use Custom Variables? To display the Custom Variables on the Survey / Email Invitation / Thank You page you can use the following tags for the corresponding Custom Variables: Custom Variable 1: ${custom1} Custom Variable 2: ${custom2} Custom Variable 3: ${custom3} Custom Variable 4: ${custom4} Custom Variable 5: ${custom5} Custom Variable N: ${customN} You can also prepopulate the Open Ended text type question on
content:
Custom variables general

Customer Experience - How to set up custom variables while programming a survey using Survey Analytics.

CX - Custom Variables Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information that is to be passed to the Survey. Custom Variables when passed to the Survey are stored with the Respondents Response. You can view the Custom Variables online using the Response Viewer. Custom Variables are also available in the Excel and CSV Raw Data downloads. You can also analyze your data based on Custom Variables. To track e-mail addresses / Custom Variables please use the Respondent Tracking link when sending emails via QuestionPro Email Management. How can I pass in additional information to the Survey? You can pass in additional information in the Survey using Custom Variables. What are the limits on Custom Variables. You can upload up to 100 custom variables. Each Custom Variable can store up to 512 characters. You cannot use a Comma (,) or the Pound/Hash (#) sign in the Custom Variables. Note: If you are passing the Custom Variables / External Reference Variable via the URL please limit your use to Alphanumeric Characters (A to Z, a to z, 0 to 9). Avoid use of Special Characters: Space Character (" "), Pound/Hash character ("#"), Ampersand ("&"), Percent character ("%"), Question Mark ("?"), etc. How to pass in the Custom Variables. When using QuestionPro Email Management tool you can upload the Custom Variables with each respondents email address. For more information please visit the Upload Custom Variables link below. If you are Not using QuestionPro Email Management tool for distribution then you can pass in Custom Variables along with the Survey URL. Following is the format for passing in the Custom Variables: After the URL (page name) we use question mark ( ? ) to start the variable data pair and we separate each variable data pair with one ampersand ( & ) mark. Example Survey URL: https://questionpro.com/t/AIZBjs6 Custom Variables are passed as follows: https://questionpro.com/t/AIZBjs6?custom1=John&custom2=Wayne&custom3=Sales&custom4=23456&custom5=Manager Do Custom Variable support multi-lingual characters? Yes, you can upload Multi-lingual characters as Custom Variables. Limited special characters are also supported. Where can I use the Custom Variables? Custom Variables can be used in Questions / Answer Options on the Survey. You can also use Custom Variables in the Email Invitation. For more information please visit the Personalizing Email Invitations link under Additional References. How to use Custom Variables? To display the Custom Variables on the Survey / Email Invitation / Thank You page you can use the following tags for the corresponding Custom Variables: Custom Variable 1: ${custom1} Custom Variable 2: ${custom2} Custom Variable 3: ${custom3} Custom Variable 4: ${custom4} Custom Variable 5: ${custom5} Custom Variable N: ${customN} You can also prepopulate the Open Ended text type question on the Survey. For more information please visit the Advanced Dynamic Replacement of Custom Variables link under Additional References. Also visit the Personalizing Email Invitations link under Additional References. Can the Custom Variables be used for Analysis? Yes. You can set up Grouping based on the Custom Variables. For example if you have uploaded the Department as Custom Variables. Then you can setup Groups for each department and run analysis separately for each Department. What is the External Reference Variable? External Reference Variable is an another variable which can be used to pass in data to the Survey. The External Reference variable (i.e. ext_ref) is used to associate responses to their respondents. You can assign an ID number or anything else that will uniquely identify a respondent to the ext_ref variable. When the results are collected the ext_ref variable is captured and stored with the other data. How can I use the ext_ref variable with my own email program/provider? Every survey that you create will have a unique URL. To include other data to be captured along with the survey data you just need to add the ext_ref parameter to the URL. For example: https://questionpro.com/t/AIZBjs6?ext_ref=xxxx Replace xxxx with your unique id for the respondent. I am using Customized URL. How to pass the ext_ref variable with customized URL? The ext_ref parameter can be passed along with the URL in the following format: For Example: The URL for the survey is: https://test.questionpro.com The ext_ref tag is appended as follows: https://test.questionpro.com/?ext_ref=xxxx Are there any limitations? The External Reference Variable can store up to 64 characters. You can use Alphanumeric Characters (A to Z, a to z, 0 to 9). Accented characters or characters from languages other than English may not get saved. Please limit the character use to English. You cannot use the Pound/Hash [#] in the External Reference Variable We recommend you not to use Comma and Quotes in the ext_ref/custom variable. How can I use the ext_ref with the Email/List Manager? If you are using the Email/List Management interface to send out email add the following tags to your survey invitation: <SURVEY_LINK>&ext_ref=xxxx <ANONYMOUS_SURVEY_LINK>&ext_ref=xxxx The &ext_ref variable is automatically captured in the raw response data. Can I rename my custom variables? Yes, you can rename custom variables using our variable mapping feature. Click here for more information Note: The mail merge tags are special variables that are used in Email List Management in tracking the email addresses of respondents. Simply append the &ext_ref=xxxx to the end of the mail merge tags. License This feature/tools described here are available with the following license(s) : Customer Experience (Annual) License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Variable mapping
kw: custom mapping variable fields variables add segment display used click name section additional customer experience survey button if code save field information names syntax multiple enter type page edit delete special store use assign appropriate passed stored response helps using
summary: Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information about your customers. You can use variable mapping to assign appropriate names to the variables.
body: Variable mapping Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information about your customers. You can use variable mapping to assign appropriate names to the variables. Customer Experience - Custom Variable Mapping Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information which can be passed to the survey. The additional info passed via the variables are stored with the respondents response collected. Variable mapping helps dramatically when you have a lot of variables used in your survey. It helps to work with your own codes instead of using a system syntax (custom1, custom2, etc.,). You can use variable mapping to assign appropriate names to the variables which also appears as the column headers in the reports downloaded. To add variable mapping go to: Customer Experience » Admin » Mapping Click on the Add button on the top right side to map variables one by one. If you wish to bulk map multiple variables, click on the "Bulk Add" option Select the Custom variables from the drop down. Enter the display name in the corresponding Display Name section (this name will be used as the header in the downlaoded reports variable section). Enter the Code for the custom variable and click the Save button (this will be used in the syntax to be applied in the survey or wherever you wish to display the variable value). Toggle on the "Analytics Enable" option if you want a filter for that Custom variable on the CX Dashboard, and if not required, leave it disabled and click on Save. If the toggle is on, select the type of variable - Categorical or Numerical For Numerical type, put range of integers as the value For Categorical type, put the relevant categories (text) as values Segment Custom Fields Mapping Segment custom fields are same like custom variables where additional information related to segments can be stored. You can add multiple custom fields for a segment. These segment custom fields are workspace specific and no duplicate names are allowed. How to add segment custom fields? Once you are on the Mapping page under the Admin section, scroll to the Segment Custom Fields Mapping section and click on the "+Add" button to add the custom fields. In the Add Field Mapping pop-up enter the Display Name and Code for the custom field you want to add. You can add multiple custom fields for a segment. Once done, click and save the custom field mapping by clicking on the "Save" button. How to edit or delete the segment custom fields? On the Mapping page under the Segment Custom Fields Mapping section, you can see all the custom fields created for the respective workspace. Edit and delete buttons are provided in front of each of the custom field using which you can edit and delete the intended custom fields. To learn how to access these segment custom fields, refer to JavaScript Logic Syntax Reference Custom variable "Code" does not accept space. The "Display Name" accepts space. The Code is used to display the custom variab
