summary: How to setup custom variable mapping.
body: Feedback custom variables How to setup custom variable mapping. CX - Custom Variable Mapping Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information that is to be passed to the Survey. Custom Variables, when passed to the Survey, are stored with the Respondents Response. You can view the Custom Variables online using the Response Viewer. Custom Variables are also available in the Excel and CSV Raw Data downloads. You can also analyze your data based on Custom Variables. To add variable mapping for your survey go to: Customer Experience » Admin » Mapping Click on the Add button on the right side. From the Custom variables column select the Custom variables from the drop down. Enter the display name in the corresponding Display Column. Enter the Code for the custom variable and click the Save button. There is a Bulk Add button too near the Add button. This will enable a user to add whole bunch of custom variables at once. Spaces are not allowed for the Code but the display name can have spaces.The Code is used to refer to the custom variable on the Survey, Email Invitation, Thank You page or on Email Notifications. The display name will appear as a column header in response viewer and in raw data. How to display the values of Custom Variable on the Dashboard? Click on the Edit hyperlink In the pop-up window, turn on the Is Analytics Enabled Add the values in the Category dropdown and then click on the Save button. In the Category box, as per the selected option, the values needs to be provided which will then be displayed on the dashboard. How to delete a Custom Variable? Click on the Delete hyperlink. Once the pop-up opens, click Yes The value assigned to that custom variable will be deleted. How to use custom variables in the survey? The custom variables can be used in Questions/Answer Options of the Survey.To set the custom variables in the survey just follow the below mentioned steps: Login >> Customer Experience >> (Select Survey) >> Edit Survey 1. Click on the Question/Answer text. 2. Add this tag ${Code} where you want the actual text to appear.The Code is the name given by you to the custom variable at the time of creation. Example: Department custom variable's Code is "dpt" So to refer to this custom variable we are going to use the tag like this ${dpt} How to pass custom variables in a survey URL? The values of the custom variables can be passed into a survey by appending custom variables values in the survey link. To append custom variable to a survey link: 1. Copy the survey link and paste it into notepad. 2. Add a question mark (?) to the end of the link, now type Code for the custom variable = Actual value (it is the value that you want to pass for that custom variable). Example: https://www.questionpro.com/t/AMBJzZbDfd?id=43&dpt=Sales 3. If you want to pass the value for multiple custom variables then, we have to separate each variable data pair with an ampersand & (). Example: https://www.questionpro.com/t/AMBJzZ
content:
Feedback custom variables

How to setup custom variable mapping.

CX - Custom Variable Mapping Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information that is to be passed to the Survey. Custom Variables, when passed to the Survey, are stored with the Respondents Response. You can view the Custom Variables online using the Response Viewer. Custom Variables are also available in the Excel and CSV Raw Data downloads. You can also analyze your data based on Custom Variables. To add variable mapping for your survey go to: Customer Experience » Admin » Mapping Click on the Add button on the right side. From the Custom variables column select the Custom variables from the drop down. Enter the display name in the corresponding Display Column. Enter the Code for the custom variable and click the Save button. There is a Bulk Add button too near the Add button. This will enable a user to add whole bunch of custom variables at once. Spaces are not allowed for the Code but the display name can have spaces.The Code is used to refer to the custom variable on the Survey, Email Invitation, Thank You page or on Email Notifications. The display name will appear as a column header in response viewer and in raw data. How to display the values of Custom Variable on the Dashboard? Click on the Edit hyperlink In the pop-up window, turn on the Is Analytics Enabled Add the values in the Category dropdown and then click on the Save button. In the Category box, as per the selected option, the values needs to be provided which will then be displayed on the dashboard. How to delete a Custom Variable? Click on the Delete hyperlink. Once the pop-up opens, click Yes The value assigned to that custom variable will be deleted. How to use custom variables in the survey? The custom variables can be used in Questions/Answer Options of the Survey.To set the custom variables in the survey just follow the below mentioned steps: Login >> Customer Experience >> (Select Survey) >> Edit Survey 1. Click on the Question/Answer text. 2. Add this tag ${Code} where you want the actual text to appear.The Code is the name given by you to the custom variable at the time of creation. Example: Department custom variable's Code is "dpt" So to refer to this custom variable we are going to use the tag like this ${dpt} How to pass custom variables in a survey URL? The values of the custom variables can be passed into a survey by appending custom variables values in the survey link. To append custom variable to a survey link: 1. Copy the survey link and paste it into notepad. 2. Add a question mark (?) to the end of the link, now type Code for the custom variable = Actual value (it is the value that you want to pass for that custom variable). Example: https://www.questionpro.com/t/AMBJzZbDfd?id=43&dpt=Sales 3. If you want to pass the value for multiple custom variables then, we have to separate each variable data pair with an ampersand & (). Example: https://www.questionpro.com/t/AMBJzZbDfd?id=43dpt=Sales&dpt=Sales&EmpID=12345 You can also view these values in the Response viewer and in the downloaded report. body: CX Workflow CXWorkflow CX Workflow Powered by AI The AI-powered CX Workflow streamlines survey assignments and reminders with tailored rules, ensuring timely delivery to targeted customers. Utilize it for seamless data import, survey assignment, scheduling, reminder triggering, and transaction exclusion as required. This document outlines the process of setting up a CX Workflow in QuestionPro CX. How does CX Workflow Powered by AI work? There are different rule engines in the workflow to enable flexible data import and survey distribution. Below are the 4 types of rule engine and their functions: Import Rules: Import transactions and assign surveys and Email/SMS templates to each transaction. Send Rules: Decide the time of survey delivery for the imported transactions. You can choose to deliver the survey immediately after import or schedule a delivery for later. Reminder Rules: Decide whether a reminder needs to be sent or not. If yes, when should it be triggered? Exclude rules: Used to segregate the transactions that should not receive a survey and keep them in a separate list. All the above rules combined process the lifecycle of a transaction i.e. Import - Send - Reminder Let's take an example to understand the chronology of these rules: We have an import file with 3 transactions greg.perry@gmail.com: Should receive a survey for iPhone 11 because he purchased iPhone 11 product mathew07@gmail.com: Should receive a survey for iPhone SE because he purchased iPhone SE product bill196483@gmail.com: Should not receive any survey because the product name is not mentioned in the file When the file is imported into the system, these transactions will pass all the rule engines in the below order: Exclude Rules will check if there are any transactions to be suppressed from receiving a survey. In our example, the 3rd transaction - bill196483@gmail.com will be separated from the list and the rest of the two transactions will be passed as input to the next rule engine Import Rules will receive the first two transactions. It will assign the surveys based on the product greg.perry@gmail.com :: iPhone 11 Survey mathew07@gmail.com :: iPhone SE Survey Send rules will send the survey invites to both of the above transactions based on the selected email templates in the rules. Reminder rules will act as per the configuration. How to configure workflow rules? Go to: Login » Customer Experience » Deploy » Workflow We will add the rules for the example used in the above section. Adding Import rules: You just have to specify the criteria to assign survey and Email/SMS templates. Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product group, and custom variables for setting the criteria Select the Survey you want to send out for the above criteria Select the Email and SMS template Click on Save The rule will be added to the import rule list. There is a default rule which will assign surveys to the transactions which do not meet any other rules. You can se
content:
CX Workflow

CXWorkflow

CX Workflow Powered by AI The AI-powered CX Workflow streamlines survey assignments and reminders with tailored rules, ensuring timely delivery to targeted customers. Utilize it for seamless data import, survey assignment, scheduling, reminder triggering, and transaction exclusion as required. This document outlines the process of setting up a CX Workflow in QuestionPro CX. How does CX Workflow Powered by AI work? There are different rule engines in the workflow to enable flexible data import and survey distribution. Below are the 4 types of rule engine and their functions: Import Rules: Import transactions and assign surveys and Email/SMS templates to each transaction. Send Rules: Decide the time of survey delivery for the imported transactions. You can choose to deliver the survey immediately after import or schedule a delivery for later. Reminder Rules: Decide whether a reminder needs to be sent or not. If yes, when should it be triggered? Exclude rules: Used to segregate the transactions that should not receive a survey and keep them in a separate list. All the above rules combined process the lifecycle of a transaction i.e. Import - Send - Reminder Let's take an example to understand the chronology of these rules: We have an import file with 3 transactions greg.perry@gmail.com: Should receive a survey for iPhone 11 because he purchased iPhone 11 product mathew07@gmail.com: Should receive a survey for iPhone SE because he purchased iPhone SE product bill196483@gmail.com: Should not receive any survey because the product name is not mentioned in the file When the file is imported into the system, these transactions will pass all the rule engines in the below order: Exclude Rules will check if there are any transactions to be suppressed from receiving a survey. In our example, the 3rd transaction - bill196483@gmail.com will be separated from the list and the rest of the two transactions will be passed as input to the next rule engine Import Rules will receive the first two transactions. It will assign the surveys based on the product greg.perry@gmail.com :: iPhone 11 Survey mathew07@gmail.com :: iPhone SE Survey Send rules will send the survey invites to both of the above transactions based on the selected email templates in the rules. Reminder rules will act as per the configuration. How to configure workflow rules? Go to: Login » Customer Experience » Deploy » Workflow We will add the rules for the example used in the above section. Adding Import rules: You just have to specify the criteria to assign survey and Email/SMS templates. Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product group, and custom variables for setting the criteria Select the Survey you want to send out for the above criteria Select the Email and SMS template Click on Save The rule will be added to the import rule list. There is a default rule which will assign surveys to the transactions which do not meet any other rules. You can select the survey to be sent for the default rule. Send Rules: Adding Send rules: You have to specify the criteria to set the delivery mode (Email, SMS, or Both) and time of survey delivery. Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product group, and custom variables for setting the criteria Select the delivery mode - Email/SMS/Both Select type of delivery - Immediate/Schedule for later/Specific date. If you choose to schedule, specify the number of days after which the survey should be sent with respect to Import/Transaction date Click on Save There is a default send rule which will be used to send surveys to the transactions which do not meet any criteria. By default, an email will be triggered immediately unless you choose to update the default rule. You can change the type and mode of delivery for the defualt rule as well. Note: If you want to immediate delivery or schedule for the same time for all the transactions, you can use the default rule itself. Specific Date With this delivery mode, communications (email and SMS) can be scheduled for a specific date and time. It is ideal for targeted messaging, such as promotional events and marketing campaigns. The Specific Date send rule executes only once and becomes absolute. To schedule communications for another date, a new rule must be created. Reminders: Adding Reminder rules: You have to specify the criteria to set the delivery mode (Email, SMS, or Both) and Email/SMS templates for reminders along with the time of reminder delivery. You can also set a recurring reminder here. Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product group, and custom variables for setting the criteria Select the reminder delivery mode - Email/SMS/Both Select the Email and SMS reminder templates. Mention the number of days after which the reminder should be sent If you want to set a recurring reminder, choose Repeat Every from the dropdown. Else choose Does not Repeat option Mention the number of days after which the following reminders should be sent along with the number of times (Occurrences) you want to repeat the reminder Click on Save There is a default reminder rule that will determine the reminder process if no other rules are set or no other criteria are met. The default rule has an option Don't send reminder to avoid sending any reminders. Note: If you want to schedule reminders for all the transactions at the same time, you can use the default rule itself. Exclude Rules: Adding Exclude rules: You can specify the criteria to suppress the survey distribution to some transactions. Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product group, and custom variables for setting the criteria. If the criteria matches, no survey will be sent to that transaction. Click on Save There is no default Exclude rule. If you do not want to exclude any transactions from receiving a survey, you do not need to add any rule in this section. You can only add/update a new rule i

title: Resting period and Link Expiry
kw: period resting link expiry feedback days system settings number email survey if customer not cx customers invitation set helps emails sending reaching right another same yes sent user answer default 30 configure license application keep check collection amount important them
summary: CX - Settings
body: Resting period and Link Expiry CX - Settings Resting Period and Link Expiry Resting period and its application helps you keep the number of emails in check that you are sending to your customers for feedback collection. Reaching out to the customers in right amount is as important as reaching out to them at right time for getting their feedback. Resting period in QuestionPro CX helps you avoid overwhelming your customers with the feedback email requests. This document outlines the use and configuation of resting period as well as Link expiry settings. How does Resting Period works? Resting Period defines the number of days the system will wait before sending another email invitation to the same customer for any survey in a feedback system. The system checks if the customer has received an email invitation in last "x" number of days. If yes, it will not send another email to the same customer, but if not, the email invitation will be sent. The resting period can be configured for a feedback system and it will apply to all the surveys in that feedback system. Resting period is not specific to a survey. What is Link expiry and how does it work? Link Expiry period is the number of days after which the user will not be able to answer the survey. If the user tries to answer the survey after the link is expired, they will see a page with the message - "This survey has been deactivated" By default the link is active for 30 days which can be updated for the feedback system. How can I configure resting period and link expiry in CX? Go to Admin >> Workflow >> Settings Set the resting period in number of days Update the link expiry period if required as it is set to 30 days by default Click on "Save" These settings will be applied to the emails being sent after the configuration. It is recommended to configure these settings during implementation set up phase. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Survey Resting Period
kw: survey period resting feedback days e1 if surveys then same transaction imported sent level apply wish case not functionality different email id go day all whole name feature enter corresponding applied next import eligible both yes receive customer invite reminders
summary: Survey Resting Period
body: Survey Resting Period Survey Resting Period Resting Period in Surveys What is the Resting Period Functionality? CX platform provides an ability to the users for applying the Resting Period timeframe to the different sets of Surveys or on the whole Feedback. What is the Feedback Level Resting Period? As the name says, this feature will apply the Resting Period to the whole feedback. What is Survey Level Resting Period? In this section, we allow a user to enter the Resting Period days that they wish to apply on the corresponding survey. Let's look at the cases to understand the functionality in a better way: Case 1: If the Feedback level resting period is applied and the survey was sent out to E1, then the next survey (same/different) for the same email ID E1 will go out after the feedback period days are over. Example: Feedback Level Resting Period = 5 days, Transaction is imported and sent out to E1 on Day 1. Then if we import the same email ID for the same/different survey, the survey will not go out on the second/third/fourth day, but fifth. Case 2: If ONLY Survey Specific Resting period is applied, And E1 is eligible for both Survey 1 and Survey 2. Survey 1 has a Resting period of 2 days and Survey 2 has a Resting Period of 3 days. Then, in this case, when the transaction is imported, assume that E1 receives Survey 1. The next day, we again import the transaction for E1. The system will now check if for the same email ID, was the transaction imported in the past 2 days. If yes, Survey 1 and 2 both will not go out. As per the scenario mentioned above, the transaction was imported corresponding to E1 and Survey 1 was sent out so the new transaction imported though would be eligible for Survey 2, but will not receive that. However, if you wish that the E1 was imported earlier for Survey 1 but should receive Survey 2 as well, we’ve built this as a BETA version. You may reach out to our customer support team and we’ll turn on the functionality for you! Case 3: If you wish to apply the same Resting Period for all Surveys in a particular Feedback, then select the checkbox present alongside the heading “Survey Name”, all Surveys will be auto-selected, and then click on “Set Resting Period days”. Enter the number of days you wish to apply for all surveys and then hit Save. Survey resting period should be more than Feedback resting period. The resting period is calculated from the **first survey invite sent to the contact**, not from any subsequent reminders. This means that the countdown for the resting period starts when the original invite is triggered, regardless of how many reminders are sent afterward. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Date Exclusions
kw: dates day exclusion exclude workflow days weekdays date exclusions cx survey sent holidays surveys rules section add multiple weekends response rates experience use set rule needed reminders yes communications specific like calendar public ensures more improving customer sending only feature
summary: CXWorkflow Day and Dates Exclusion
body: Date Exclusions CXWorkflow Day and Dates Exclusion Day and Dates Exclusion in CX Workflow What is the Day and Dates Exclusion Workflow? The Day and Dates Exclusion Workflow allows CX users to control when survey communications (Email and SMS) are sent by excluding specific weekdays (like weekends) or calendar dates (like public holidays). This ensures surveys are delivered when recipients are more likely to engage, improving survey response rates and overall customer experience. Why should I use Day and Dates Exclusion? By default, surveys are sent based on workflow timings, but responses tend to drop on weekends and holidays. With Day and Dates Exclusions, you can: Avoid sending surveys on low-engagement days. Ensure communications are sent only on optimal days. Boost survey response and completion rates. How does Day and Dates Exclusion work? The feature is divided into two parts: Exclude Dates: Skip sending on specific calendar dates (e.g. July 4th, New Year’s Day). Exclude Days: Skip recurring weekdays (e.g. Saturday, Sunday). How to set Exclude Dates? Go to Send Rules section in Workflow. Click on + Add Date button under the Exclusions section. Name your exclusion rule (e.g. "Christmas"). Select Start Date and End Date using the date picker. Click Apply to confirm your selection. Save the rule. All added exclusion dates will appear in the list view below with options to Edit or Delete the rule as needed. How to set Exclude Days? In the same Day and Dates Exclusion section, use the Day Exclusions dropdown. Select one or more weekdays to exclude (e.g. Sat, Sun). Selected days will be displayed in a summary list. Will reminders respect Day and Dates Exclusions? Yes — Reminders will also follow Exclusion rules. No reminders will be sent on excluded days or dates. They will be deferred to the next valid day. Can I add multiple Exclude Days or Dates? Yes — you can add multiple exclude dates and multiple weekdays in the respective lists. You may also delete or edit them as needed. Example Use Case Alex Martinez, Senior CX Manager, wants to pause all survey sends on weekends and public holidays. Using Day and Dates Exclusion Workflow, she configures: Exclude Days: Saturday and Sunday Exclude Dates: July 4, December 25, January 1 This ensures customers receive surveys only on weekdays, outside of holidays, maximizing response rates and improving experience. Important Notes Exclusion dates cannot include today or past dates. You can add multiple exclude dates or weekdays as needed. Exclusion rules are set per workflow under the Send Rules section. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: CX-Reminders
kw: reminder day time reminders two rule three invitation sent case user cx workflow if 00 occurrences use pm one select number example configured times rules response not survey want repeat scheduled new next first specific rates follow feedback create customer
summary: Learn how to configure specific execution times for reminder rules in CX Workflow to optimize engagement and response rates.
body: CX-Reminders Learn how to configure specific execution times for reminder rules in CX Workflow to optimize engagement and response rates. Reminder in CX Workflow Rules Reminders in CX Workflow Reminders are automated follow-up emails sent to respondents who have not completed a survey. They help increase response rates and ensure timely feedback collection. How to Create a Reminder Follow these steps to create a reminder for your survey: Go to: Login » Customer Experience » Deploy » Workflow » Reminders Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product Group, or custom variables for setting the criteria Select the reminder delivery mode - Email / SMS Select the Email and SMS reminder templates Mention the number of days after which the reminder should be sent Select the time of day you want the reminder to be sent. If no time is selected, the default is 9:00 AM in the user’s time zone. If you want to set a recurring reminder, choose Repeat Every from the dropdown. Else choose Does not Repeat Mention the number of days after which the following reminders should be sent along with the number of times (Occurrences) you want to repeat the reminder Click on Save What are some example use cases for this feature? Use Case 1 – Editing Reminder Time Suppose a reminder rule is configured to send reminders three times after a survey invitation — on Day 2, Day 4, and Day 6 — all scheduled for 6:00 PM in the user’s local time zone. Case A: The user updates the reminder time before the second reminder is executed (e.g., changes 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM). The new time applies to the remaining reminders (Reminder Two and Reminder Three). Case B: The user changes the time after the second reminder has already been sent but before the third one. The new time applies only to the next pending reminder (Reminder Three). Case C: The user updates the time to an earlier value after the scheduled time for that day has already passed (e.g., from 6:00 PM to 5:30 PM on the same day). The system skips that day’s reminder and moves it to the next day at the updated time. Use Case 2 – Excluding a Reminder Occurrence You have a reminder rule with multiple occurrences (e.g., Reminder One, Reminder Two, Reminder Three). If a specific reminder (for example, Reminder Two) falls on a day that should be skipped, such as a holiday or weekend, you can exclude it. Example: Reminder Rule set for 3 occurrences: Reminder One - Day 2 after invitation Reminder Two - Day 4 after invitation Reminder Three - Day 6 after invitation Reminder Two falls on a excluded date On Day 4: Reminder Two is skipped. The system sends Reminder Two on the next valid, non-excluded day at the configured time. Reminder Three is scheduled relative to the day Reminder Two was sent. Use Case 3 – Deleting a Reminder Rule If the user deletes a reminder rule (for example, to stop Reminder Two from sending), All future reminders under that rule will be cancelled. Reminder in CX Workflow Rules Reminders in CX Workflow Reminders are automated follow-up emails sent to respondents who have not completed a survey. They help increase response rates and ensure timely feedback collection. How to Create a Reminder Follow these steps to create a reminder for your survey: Go to: Login » Customer Experience » Deploy » Workflow » Reminders Click on Add Rule Name the rule Select the Segment, Product, Product Group, or custom variables for setting the criteria Select the reminder delivery mode - Email / SMS Select the Email and SMS reminder templates Mention the number of days after which the reminder should be sent Select the time of day you want the reminder to be sent. If no time is selected, the default is 9:00 AM in the user’s time zone. If you want to set a recurring reminder, choose Repeat Every from the dropdown. Else choose Does not Repeat Mention the number of days after which the following reminders should be sent along with the number of times (Occurrences) you want to repeat the reminder Click on Save What are some example use cases for this feature? Use Case 1 – Editing Reminder Time Suppose a reminder rule is configured to send reminders three times after a survey invitation — on Day 2, Day 4, and Day 6 — all scheduled for 6:00 PM in the user’s local time zone. Case A: The user updates the reminder time before the second reminder is executed (e.g., changes 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM). The new time applies to the remaining reminders (Reminder Two and Reminder Three). Case B: The user changes the time after the second reminder has already been sent but before the third one. The new time applies only to the next pending reminder (Reminder Three). Case C: The user updates the time to an earlier value after the scheduled time for that day has already passed (e.g., from 6:00 PM to 5:30 PM on the same day). The system skips that day’s reminder and moves it to the next day at the updated time. Use Case 2 – Excluding a Reminder Occurrence You have a reminder rule with multiple occurrences (e.g., Reminder One, Reminder Two, Reminder Three). If a specific reminder (for example, Reminder Two) falls on a day that should be skipped, such as a holiday or weekend, you can exclude it. Example: Reminder Rule set for 3 occurrences: Reminder One - Day 2 after invitation Reminder Two - Day 4 after invitation Reminder Three - Day 6 after invitation Reminder Two falls on a excluded date On Day 4: Reminder Two is skipped. The system sends Reminder Two on the next valid, non-excluded day at the configured time. Reminder Three is scheduled relative to the day Reminder Two was sent. Use Case 3 – Deleting a Reminder Rule If the user deletes a reminder rule (for example, to stop Reminder Two from sending), All future reminders under that rule will be cancelled. Use Case 4 – Adding a New Reminder If reminders were configured for three occurrences (e.g., Day 2, Day 4, and Day 6), the maximum number of reminders can be modified until the first reminder is sent. After the first reminder has been deployed, adding additional occurrences (e.g., Day 7), is not supported, as the reminder count is locked once the first reminder is triggered. Each reminder rule can be configured with a unique time, allowing reminders to align with regional peak response hours. Segment overview

CX - Segment Overview

CX - Segment Overview What is a segment? A segment represents your business entities as mapped within the system. By default, a Main Segment is created when your account is set up. You can add Child Segments under this main segment to represent branches or sub-units. If your business structure requires additional levels, you can continue to map them accordingly. For example: If you own McDonald’s, McDonald’s US could be your Main Segment, and its various branches across the US would be added as Child Segments. How to access the Segments page: Navigate to Login >> CX >> Admin >> Company Structure >> Segments What does various columns on the segment page represents? When the user visits segment page, there are various actions which can be performed on this page. Following are the columns displayed on the page: Name: The column displays the name of segment Code: The column consists of segment code. This code has to be unique for each segment added. It is important to have the code as all transactions and data are mapped to specific segment through segment code Managers: The column shows the count of supervisors added to the specific segment Location: It shows the information about where a segment is located Parent: The column represents the name of parent segment under which the respective segment is added Username/Key: This column is only visible if the Offline Survey option in Mobile tab under Settings is turned On. From this column user can map the username/key for offline data collection. Actions: From the Action column user can Manage Supervisor, View Detractor Tickets, Add Child segment, Edit and Delete a segment In addition to the columns, there are search, filter, Import and change view options too. On the top right of segment list, there are 2 icons: Change View - Clicking the toggle switches the display between Tree View and List View. Search - Clicking this enables the search bar, allowing the user to search for a segment. Besides the above 2 icons, there is a segment filter dropdown At the corner, there is a button - Import segments How to Add a segment? Initially there is one segment added to the account. This segment is referred as ‘Main segment’. If you want to add more BU to the system, it can be done through 2 ways: Import segments button: Enables you to import segments in bulk Add Child option: Add single segment at a time as a child to an already existing segment Import segments: There are two options in importing segments Manually Adding Bulk segments Importing through excel file Through Add Child option Click Add Child under the Action column Add the details of child segment and click Save button The new child segment will be added to the list To learn how to add bulk segments, refer to Import segments How can I Edit, Delete, Search, and Filter segments? Edit a segment On the segment list, click More drop down under the action column Click Edit option Add the details you want to the segment and click Save button Delete a segment On the segment list, click More drop down under the action column Click Delete option If there is any child segment added, then delete option will not be visible to its parent segment Search a segment On the segment page, there is a search icon above the segment columns Click Search icon The search bar will be activated. You and type and press enter to search the results The search is based on segment name, code, parent segment To reset the search you can click x icon inside search bar, which will reset the segment list and close the search bar Filter a segment On the segment page, there is a Filter drop down above the segment columns The filter drop down will show only those segments, which have at least one child segment If there are no child segments added, the filter will say No data to display What is the role of Segment Managers? Segment managers are the owners of all the activities happening for that segment. They can perform below operations: View Dashboard using Dashboard Portal for their own segment's data You can access the list of managers under CX >> Admin >> Company Structure >> Managers Manage closed loop tickets assigned to their segment. They can check the closed loop ticket under Action >> Closed loop. They are also entitled to receive an email notification when a detractor response is received, provided the proper notification alerts are configured. How can I view the tree view of Segments? You can click on the Tree viewbutton and switch to the tree mode display Listing the Child Segments? You can click on the Segment Name and expand the view of the child segments How can I search a segment? You can search the segment by the name in the search bar on the top Behavior for Segment Mapping Exceptions: Missing Segment in Transaction: If a transaction doesn’t include a CX segment, it automatically gets assigned to the Main Segment. Incorrect Segment Code: If a transaction is imported with a segment code that doesn’t match any defined segment in the workspace, it gets assigned to the Main Segment. Deleted Segment: If a segment is deleted, all past transactions associated with that segment are left without any segment assigned. These transactions won’t be visible when using segment filters. Custom Variables: Custom Variables are special variables used to store additional information which can be passed to the survey. The additional info passed via the variables are stored with the respondents response collected. Variable mapping helps dramatically when you have a lot of variables used in your survey. It helps to work with your own codes instead of using a system syntax (custom1, custom2, etc.,). You can use variable mapping to assign appropriate names to the variables which also appears as the column headers in the reports downloaded. To do so, you need to Login >> CX >> Admin >> Company Structure >> Segments Click Add Child under the Actions column Add the below mentioned details of child segment and click Submit button. Segment Name - Mandatory Segment Code - Mandatory and needs to be unique Location Address City ZIP Code State Country External Reference Manager Email Address The new child segment will be added to the list How can I bulk add segments? To add multiple segments at once, click the Import Segments button. To do so, you need to Login >> CX >> Admin >> Company Structure >> Segments >> Import segments Manual Bulk Import Enter the details in the table columns for Manual Bulk Import Click on Import segments button to import the data Excel Bulk Import Click the ‘Import segments’ button on the Segments page Click on ‘Excel Import’ tab to import segments through excel sheet Click the ‘Segment - Import Template’ for system to prepare the sample template. Click again on the link to download the template Click on ‘Choose file’ button to open file explorer and choose the correct excel file for importing transactions Click ‘Import’ button to process the selected excel file Example of Adding a segment? Refer the below example: McDonald USA: Let’s assume McDonald’s operates in three U.S. states: Boston, Atlanta, and New York. While setting up their segment hierarchy, the following steps need to be taken: McD USA, being the headquarters, will serve as the main segment with the segment code S1. This is the default segment created in the user's CX account. You can rename it using the Edit option in the Segments section. McD Boston, McD Atlanta, and McD New York needs to be added under the Main segment as their Child segments. In the table format, you can enter their details as follows: The segment codes for Boston, Atlanta, and New York are assigned as S2, S3, and S4, respectively. You can assign segment codes based on your organization's standards; however, each segment code must be unique. If McD Boston includes two cities—Canon and Medway—and McD Atlanta includes Dallas and Sunny Side, these city-level segments can be added after Boston and Atlanta have been created. To do this, click on Import Segment and enter the following details in a tabular format: The segment codes for the cities are assigned as follows: Canon – S5 Medway – S6 Dallas – S7 Sunny Side – S8 Make sure each segment code is unique and aligns with your segment hierarchy. For Canon and Medway, the parent segment should be set as Boston. Similarly, Dallas and Sunny Side should have Atlanta as their parent segment. The segment hierarchy will look as follows: The above example was for a 3-Tier business hierarchy. Through manual bulk import, you can only import one level of segments. Hence, to create the above hierarchy we imported segments twice through manual import. If you want to import all segment at one time, you can create an Excel file similar to the one shown below and import it. According to the image, Washington will be imported under McD USA. The segment’s McD Buffalo and McD Albany will be added under McD New York. McD Seattle and McD Olympia will be imported under McD Washington. The updated hierarchy will look like: If McD USA opens a new branch in Chicago, you simply click ‘Add Child’ besides McD USA and fill the details as shown below - If zip code is mentioned while adding the business units, then it will appear in the dashboard. The Segment code is Case Sensitive. While importing transactions, make sure correct segment codes are passed. summary: Managers
body: Segment managers Managers Managers Details Who is a Manager? A Manager can refer to a Store Manager, Branch Manager, Regional Manager, or any other executive responsible for overseeing activities within a specific segment of your organization. A segment may have multiple managers assigned to it, or a single manager may oversee multiple segments. Managers do not have access to the main account. They can only view dashboards via the Dashboard Portal URL. Who is an Admin? An Admin or Super Admin is a user with full access to the QuestionPro account. Admins can log in to the account, view detailed information about all managers, and access all associated data. They have complete control and visibility across the platform. Can I add multiple managers to a Segment? Yes, you can assign multiple managers to a segment. This can be done by using the "Supervise Manager" option on the Segments page or by importing managers through the Managers tab. Click on Admin >> Company Structure >> Segments >> Supervise Manager The pop up will show list of added managers. If no manager has been added, the pop up will be empty Enter the First Name, Last name and email address of the Manager Click on ‘Add’ to add the Manager Manager can also be added through: Adding/Importing Segment Through Manager Tab - Manual Add and Excel Import How can I view the logs or activities performed by Managers? You can view manager activity logs using the View Logs option. Click on Admin >> Company Structure >> Managers >> View Logs >> Activity Logs A pop-up will display all activities performed by the selected manager within a specified time frame. Admin users can adjust the date range as needed. The pop-up also shows the total visit count, indicating how often the manager accessed the portal. Additionally, an activity filter allows Admin or Super Admin users to narrow down and view specific types of activities from the log. How to edit the managers? Managers can be edited through ‘Supervise Manager’ pop-up or through ‘Managers’ tab. Click on Admin >> Company Structure >> Managers >> Click on the 3 dots >> View Manager Details The pop up will show manager details. Make the changes to the desired fields and then click on the "Save" button to save your changes. How to delete a manager? To delete a Manager, Go to Admin >> Company Structure >> Managers >> Click on the Actions menu and select the delete option. How to Invite a Manager to the Portal/CX App? Managers can be invited to Portal or the CX App by the folllowing method Click on Admin >> Company Structure >> Managers >> Click on the 3 dots >> Invite to Portal or Invite to CX Mobile App How to Reset the password for the Manager? Managers will receive the email for resetting of the password Click on Admin >> Company Structure >> Managers >> Click on the 3 dots for the manager for which you want to reset the password >> Reset Password Is there a limit to how many managers a user can create? No, there is no limit to add managers in the syst

No, there is no limit to add managers in the system. If names are present in the "transaction users" column while uploading transactions those many managers are automatically created in the system. title: Enable Dashboard on Portal
kw: dashboard shared portal share manager delete managers click tab only all there no admin user portals one option then yes enable cx view segment overall least any select hover over if case edit unshare able data relevant feature need create
summary: Share dashboard on Portal
body: Enable Dashboard on Portal Share dashboard on Portal CX - Enable a dashboard on Manager's Portal Being a CX admin user, you can share a customized dashboard tab with your portal users/managers. The managers will be able to view data relevant only to their segment and its sub segments. With this feature, all managers can refer to a similar kind of analysis for their own segment and need not create an entirely new dashboard. By default, the "Overall" tab of the admin user is shared with all the manager portals. The managers can always create a new dashboard and those will be shown under the "Dashboards" section. It is mandatory for an admin user to share at least one dashboard on the portal. How do I share a dashboard? For sharing any dashboard, you need to follow below steps: Go to: Customer Experience » Analytics Select the dashboard tab you want to share and hover over it. You will see the Visibilty Option. Select that and then toggle on if you wish to share with the managers and vice-versa. There will be a small desktop icon in besides all the shared dashboards. Once you share a dashboard, all the widgets on that dashboard will be visible to all the managers with data relevant to their segment. The admin user has to share at least one dashboard with the manager portals. Also, if it's the Overall Dashboard, then in that case, there is no option of Visibility or Delete when hovered over. Can the portal manager edit/delete a shared dashboard? No. The portal manager only has view access to the shared dashboard. They cannot edit or delete a shared tab. Neither can they edit any widget. The portal manager can only view the shared dashboard and can apply/reset the global filters if required. They won't be provided with any widget level access. Can I unshare an already shared dashboard? Yes, you can easily unshare an already shared dashboard. Click on the dashbaord name, hover over, click on the 3 dots, select Visibility as an option and then toggle it off. In case, there is no other dashboard shared, you will be asked to share at least one dashboard. Only then you will be able to unshare the existing one. Can I delete a shared dashboard? Yes, you can delete a shared dashboard. Once you click on the dashboard that you wish to delete, hover again and click on the 3 dots. Post that, click on the Delete option. There will be a confirmation box that will open. Please read the confirmation message and click on "Yes" to delete the shared dashboard. Note: In case there is no other dashboard shared with the manager portals and you attempt to delete the only shared dashboard, the "Overall" tab will automatically be shared with the manager portals. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: CX - Adding Products
body: Product and product group CX - Adding Products CX - Adding Products Products represent business entities for which you want to collect feedback. With QuestionPro CX, you can gather feedback for one or more products within a single feedback system. Simply define your products and import the relevant transactions along with product details. This data enables you to analyze and compare the performance of different products using the CX Dashboard. How do you add a Product? To add a Product, follow the following: Go to Admin >> Company Structure >> Products Click on 'Add Product' Enter product specification according to format- (Product Name,Product Code,Product Group Name,Description) Example: Lenovo Laptop,L1050,Laptop,Laptop model no. L1050 Click on Submit There are 2 options next to "+Add Product" button: Product Group filter: Use this filter to view all products that belong to the same product group. Switcher: Use this option to view all the product groups and their details. Similarly, you can use this to view the product list again. How can I analyse data for different products? To analyze the feedback collected on your surveys, go to Analytics >> Dashboard. You can create widgets on the dashboard based on the questionnaire, using Product as a base for certain widgets such as NPS List and NPS Trend Comparison. For all other widgets, Product can be applied as a filter. Below are some examples of how you can use Product in your analysis: Product based widgets: Add a new widget (NPS List or NPS Trend Comparison) to compare the NPS of different products in your feedback system Product filter: Apply a filter for the desired product and analyse the data on a specific widget for that particular product. title: Promoter amplification
kw: amplification social options click survey promoter facebook twitter cx yelp go link save configure url page settings button trip advisor media channels customer experience work only if 10 enable toggle besides message visible completion need tripadvisor segment tab see edit
summary: CX - Social Amplification
body: Promoter amplification CX - Social Amplification CX - Social Amplification What is Social Amplification? There are four social media channels in customer experience through which you can promote your business. These channels work only if the survey response is a ‘Promoter’ i.e 9 or 10. You can configure your company URL or page in the settings and user will have an option to post it on their wall or tweet it on their handle. To access the Social Amplification, go to: Login » CX » Settings » Promoter Amplification How can I enable Social Amplification options? The options under Social Amplification are as follows: Trip Advisor Yelp Facebook Twitter All the options have a toggle button besides them You can click on the toggle button to ENABLE these options For Facebook and Twitter, you will get additional options asking for Link and Message. You can give Facebook or Twitter link respectively and the message you want to display. Scroll to the bottom and click Save Social Amplification is ON, but the options are not visible at Survey completion? Once the Social Amplification options are turned on, you need to configure theSegments and Survey to make the promoter amplifications work. For Yelp and TripAdvisor you need to configure the URL in Segment. Click on Admin Go toSegment tab Besides each Segment, you will see a Edit link. Click on the edit link. Insert the TripAdvisor or Yelp URL of your organization Click Save Settings button For Twitter and Facebook, go to the survey that needs to be sent out. Click on the Survey Go to Completion Tab In the Selection Finish Options drop down, select Thanks You page with Image Click Save Changes to save the changes After setting these, on the last page of Survey you will see the following icons NOTE - The social media icons will only be visible to the Promoter i.e. if NPS rating is 9 or 10 License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Add sub accounts
kw: sub account cx workspace accounts users shared user survey add share access click customer login email no experience license annual permissions admin any modules deploy analytics action go password sharing options select if features only pricing primary file consists details
summary: The file consists of details on how to add multiple users and share the CX Workspaces with them.
body: Add sub accounts The file consists of details on how to add multiple users and share the CX Workspaces with them. CX Sub-Accounts and Permissions Being a CX customer, you can have one or more sub-accounts. The admin will have to create the sub-accounts from the application and then the CX Workspace can be shared with any sub-account user. Once Workspace is shared with a sub-account user, it can access below modules: Survey Deploy Analytics Action How to add sub-accounts in CX? To add Sub-Accounts go to: Login » Surveys » My Account (Top Right Corner) Click on Organization Click on Add User under Users Add a new User: Fill the below fields: Email Address Password Password (Confirm) Security/Sharing Options: Select from the below two options:Separate Account - No Sharing/Shared Account This option will not affect the accessibility of CX modules How to share a Workspace? To share Workspace login to the Customer Experience Management platform. Click on Access. Select the email addresses in the pop-up and click Share. If the Workspace is already shared with any sub-account user, it will be shown as selected. Click on Save. What access rights do the sub-account users have? The sub-account will have below access on a shared Workspace survey: Edit Survey Copy Survey (Email templates will also be copied along with the survey) Deploy Survey Analytics Action/Closed loop Share Workspace with other sub account users Restricted features for sub-account users: Admin features - settings, segments, managers, products, devices How will sub-account users access shared Workspace? When sub-account users will login they will land on Survey tab Go to Customer Experience The shared Workspace will be visible in the Workspace list. I only have a single user account - does this apply to me? No. This is only for Global Multi-User Accounts. What license is required for sub-accounts and pricing for the same? The CX sub-account works with the CX Annual primary account holders. If the primary account is CX Annual, you need CX sub-account annual.Contact Sales Team for CX Sub-account pricing. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Root Causes Overview
kw: causes root tag area sub tags new add all each workspace actions create cx platform across specific askwhy customer adding click feature set journey survey key question rootcauses analysis experience utilized feedback creating no button system automatically clicking options menu
summary: Root Causes help to create a centralized repository of all the causes in the CX platform, which can be used by CX and all its associated products.
body: Root Causes Overview Root Causes help to create a centralized repository of all the causes in the CX platform, which can be used by CX and all its associated products. Root Causes The Root Causes feature enables you to create a customized set of root causes for each workspace. These root causes can be broadly defined to apply across all journey touchpoints, ensuring consistency in your analytics. You can also target specific root causes for individual AskWhy questions within your surveys, giving you precise insights tailored to each context. By offering survey respondents the opportunity to select the one key issue they believe is most significant, the AskWhy question along with rootcauses enhances survey engagement and allows for deeper analysis of customer experience and churn risk. These root causes are utilized across the broader CX ecosystem, including Enhanced Closed-loop Feedback, Journey Management platform, and Outerloop, creating a unified approach to identifying and addressing key factors affecting customer satisfaction. Adding Root Causes to a Workspace: To begin adding root causes in your chosen workspace, navigate to Admin >> Root Causes If no root causes have been set up yet, click the "+ New Area" button to create a new area. The system will automatically add a new area titled "New Area" to the root causes list. After adding the area, you can assign relevant tags by clicking "+ Add Tag" under the area’s heading. To further refine the tagging structure, add sub-tags specific to each tag by selecting "+ Add Sub-tag" from the tag’s options menu. Repeat this process to add all necessary areas, tags, and sub-tags that are specific to the workspace. These will be utilized for reporting and analysis across the platform. Area Actions: You can either Rename, Copy, or Delete the area by clicking on the area settings button in the right corner of the area. Tags Actions: Creating a new sub-tag, editing the tag (rename), and deleting a tag are the actions that can be performed on a tag. This can be done using the options menu provided on each tag. Sub-tags Actions: Deleting a sub-tag - Hover over a sub-tag to reveal the delete icon, then click it to remove the sub-tag. Renaming a sub-tag - Click on a sub-tag to enter edit mode. Any changes made will be automatically saved by the system. Additional resources: Download demo video of Rootcauses and AskWhy question License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

CX On The Go is a mobile application designed specifically for CX managers, providing them with a comprehensive set of tools and features to enhance their customer experience management. Overview

The QuestionPro CX On The Go is the ultimate closed-loop solution to all your customer experience management needs. Combined with a QuestionPro CX License, you can easily monitor and manage your company's customer experience from anywhere, at any time.

Getting started with CX On The Go What is CX On The Go? The QuestionPro "CX On The Go" is the ultimate closed-loop solution to all your customer experience management needs. Combined with a QuestionPro CX License, you can easily monitor and manage your company's customer experience from anywhere, at any time. How does CX On The Go work? CX On The Go is a mobile application designed specifically for CX managers, providing them with a comprehensive set of tools and features to enhance their customer experience management. With CX On The Go, managers can conveniently access various functionalities on their mobile devices, enabling them to stay connected and take immediate actions to drive customer satisfaction. With QuestionPro CX On The Go Mobile App, businesses can do the following: Analytics: CX On The Go provides insightful analytics capabilities, allowing managers to gain a deeper understanding of customer interactions and trends. They can access real-time data, track performance metrics, and identify areas for improvement. NPS Score: With CX On The Go, managers can monitor the Net Promoter Score (NPS) of their organization. They can view NPS trends over time, analyze feedback from customers, and evaluate the overall sentiment towards their brand. Ticket Management: The app enables managers to efficiently handle customer issues by managing and creating tickets on the go. They can view customer response, track ticket status, priority and ensure timely resolution of customer concerns. Customer Outreach: Managers can identify the root causes of customer issues. In addition to identifying root causes, managers can take swift action. They can collaborate with relevant teams, assign tasks, and initiate corrective measures directly through the app. What are the key features of CX On The Go? The key features of CX On The Go include: Analytics: CX On The Go provides analytics data, allowing CX managers to access real-time data, track performance metrics, and gain valuable insights into customer interactions and trends. NPS Score Monitoring: The app allows managers to monitor the Net Promoter Score (NPS) of their organization, enabling them to gauge customer loyalty and satisfaction levels over time. Ticket Management: CX On The Go streamlines ticket management by allowing managers to create, update and track customer tickets. This feature ensures that customer issues are promptly addressed and resolved. Customer Outreach: The app facilitates email communication with customers, enabling managers to respond to queries and address concerns. Customer Response Viewing: CX On The Go enables managers to view and analyze customer responses directly from the app. This feature provides insights into customer feedback, allowing managers to understand sentiment, identify pain points, and make informed decisions to improve the overall customer experience. These are some of the key features of CX On The Go, offering a comprehensive and user-friendly CX ticket management solution to meet the needs of businesses of all sizes. Who can use CX On The Go? CX On The Go is specifically designed for CX managers responsible for enhancing customer experience. It is tailored to meet the needs of professionals, who are actively involved in managing customer interactions, analyzing data, monitoring NPS scores, handling customer tickets, and engaging in customer outreach. Whether you're in a small business or a large enterprise, CX On The Go helps you streamline workflows, make data-driven decisions, and enhance customer satisfaction. Access codes are unique codes for each Workspace System provided by the account owner. With the help of this code the Workspace system can be accessed by supervisors and CX managers. Users then can login on the CX On The Go mobile application through their email, password and this Access Code/ Company Code. How to add the Access Code? To add the Access Code Go to Login >> Customer Experience >> Admin >> Mobile and Devices Now, in the access code field, input your desired ‘access code’ and click on ‘Save’. How to Invite a CX manager? To invite a CX Manager go to: Login » Customer Experience » Admin » Company Structure >> Managers From the manager list, click on the three dotted menu, then select “Invite to CX Mobile App”. The system will send an email with the login credentials for the app to the manager. How to login to the CX On The Go app? After sending invitations, CX manager will recieve an email with credentials to login to the 'CX On The Go' app. Then, CX manager can use these credentials to login to the app from app’s login screen. after entering the credentials, just click on 'Sign in'. summary: The QuestionPro CX On The Go is the ultimate closed-loop solution to all your customer experience management needs. Combined with a QuestionPro CX License, you can easily monitor and manage your compa
body: Closed Loop The QuestionPro CX On The Go is the ultimate closed-loop solution to all your customer experience management needs. Combined with a QuestionPro CX License, you can easily monitor and manage your company's customer experience from anywhere, at any time. Closed Loop What is Closed Loop Feedback? Closed Loop Feedback is a response-based ticketing system. It is a vital part of Customer Experience. Closed loop feedback is when a business responds to or acts on customer feedback through an online or offline survey. This system is designed to help a user to manage automatic tickets and manual tickets in one convenient place. How to navigate to Closed Loop? To see Closed Loop tickets, From Dashboard >> Drawer >> Closed Loop How to filter tickets? In Closed Loop, tickets are shown by Business Unit. you can also filter tickets by date filter, and status and priority filter. How to create Ticket? From the app's dashboard or closed loop ‘Tap’ on the ‘(+)’ icon. A Ticket creation form will appear. After filling up the form ‘Tap’ on ‘Create Ticket’. After filling up the form, tap on 'Create Ticket' to create a manual ticket. How to manage tickets? From Closed Loop select any tickets. Overview: From here user can update ticket priority, status and take action. If this was an automatic ticket, then you can see customer response from here, aswell. content:
Closed Loop

The QuestionPro CX On The Go is the ultimate closed-loop solution to all your customer experience management needs. Combined with a QuestionPro CX License, you can easily monitor and manage your company's customer experience from anywhere, at any time.

Closed Loop What is Closed Loop Feedback? Closed Loop Feedback is a response-based ticketing system. It is a vital part of Customer Experience. Closed loop feedback is when a business responds to or acts on customer feedback through an online or offline survey. This system is designed to help a user to manage automatic tickets and manual tickets in one convenient place. How to navigate to Closed Loop? To see Closed Loop tickets, From Dashboard >> Drawer >> Closed Loop How to filter tickets? In Closed Loop, tickets are shown by Business Unit. you can also filter tickets by date filter, and status and priority filter. How to create Ticket? From the app's dashboard or closed loop ‘Tap’ on the ‘(+)’ icon. A Ticket creation form will appear. After filling up the form ‘Tap’ on ‘Create Ticket’. After filling up the form, tap on 'Create Ticket' to create a manual ticket. How to manage tickets? From Closed Loop select any tickets. Overview: From here user can update ticket priority, status and take action. If this was an automatic ticket, then you can see customer response from here, aswell. Comment: From here user can comment and reply for better internal communication. Activity: user can see the entire ticket’s activity from this tab. Root Cause and Action: Users can select the root cause of the ticket and select action taken for it. How can I communicate with the Customer? From Ticket details: Take Action >> Respond via Email From here, users can send email to communicate with the customer. There are email templates available for the cx manager for swift emailing. After writing the email, just hit the blue ‘Send’ icon from the top. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? summary: CX Transactions- Manual Import
body: Manual CX Transactions- Manual Import CX Transactions- Manual Import When a customer interacts with your product/services to make a purchase, it is termed as Transaction. A customer can have one or more transactions. A Customer Experience survey is sent to a customer for each of their transaction to understand their experience during that transaction. Hence, to send out a survey, the transactions have to be imported in CX which mandatorily should have the customer's contact details. The most simple and effective way to import transactions in the feedback system is using Excel file. This document will help you to understand how you can import a transaction using an Excel file in QuestionPro CX. How do I send the email surveys to the customers? To send the email or SMS Surveys, transactions needs to be imported in the feedback system. The transactions can be imported using below 3 options: Manual Import Excel Import Automated import using FTP or API For importing transactions using excel sheet, these are the simple steps to be followed: Go to: Deploy >> Distribute >> Send Select the format for importing transactions. Enter the details for all the transactions - Email address, Phone number, First and Last name, Segment, Product and other custom variables(if required). Email Address is only mandatory field. Click on Send Invite once you have entered all the details. For any invalid details, the system will display a validation message and will not import the transactions. How do I send a survey once the transactions are imported? Once you imported the file, the survey invitations will be sent to all the customers with valid email address and phone numbers. Please Note: You will not be able to import an excel file until you have the workflow rules set up. Please click here to know more about workflow rules. body: Excel CX Transactions- Excel Import CX Transactions- Excel Import When a customer interacts with your product/services to make a purchase, it is termed as Transaction. title: Survey design
kw: survey color look feel surveys click colors change themes use hex license css html my customer experience select picker value following design cx customizing brand exactly match professionally designed standard so all point interface like text if get not actual
summary: How to change the look and feel of your survey?
body: Survey design How to change the look and feel of your survey? CX- Customizing the survey look and feel - How to? When designing surveys, it is important for your respondents to instantly recognize your brand. You can customize your surveys to exactly match your brands look and feel. We provide professionally designed standard themes. Each theme is carefully designed so the look and feel is uniform irrespective of the platform, browser, or device. Our themes use the latest technology so answering surveys is a lot easier, simple and fun. All of our themes and surveys are fully mobile-friendly. QuestionPro offers point and click interface to change almost all the elements in your survey. You can change things like question text, answer text styling, background colours, logs, headers etc. If you are a geek and would like to get at the css level and override the default css completely, you can do that as well. If you are not comfortable with html/ css it is advisable to use the point and click interface only. How can I change the look and feel for my survey? Go to: Customer Experience (Select Survey) » Edit Click on the Design tab. Under Standard Themes, you can select from our professionally chosen predefined colors. The survey on the page is an example survey and not your actual survey. To view the actual survey, please click on the Preview button. Old UI - For surveys created before September 1st, 2018: How can I exactly match colors with my brand colors? You can either select the color using the color picker or you can enter the HEX value for the color. For more information on color codes, please visit: https://www.w3schools.com/html/html_colors.asp Once you have copied the color code. Click on the color picker and paste it and save. How can I find the HEX value for the colors on my site? Easiest way is to ask your designer/developer or the IT team for the HEX code. Or you can use the following steps: Using Print Screen, take a screenshot of your screen/desktop. Open/paste it in any image editing software that you have. Use the eyedropper (color picker) utility to get the HEX value for the color required. License & Access Options This feature/tools described here are available with the following license(s) : Customer Experience (Annual) License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: CX- Multilingual Surveys | Language options - How to?
body: Add language CX- Multilingual Surveys | Language options - How to? CX- Multilingual Surveys | Language options - How to? Our platform supports multilingual surveys, offering a selection of over 100+ languages. If your preferred language is not available, you can request its addition, and we will accommodate your needs. How do I create multilingual surveys? First create the Survey in English/Main Language Go to: Login >> Customer Experience >> Survey >> Settings >> Languages >> Add Languages Select the language version and click on the Add Language Version button. Selected Language versions will get added. You can add multiple languages in one go. Change language within survey: This option enables respondents to change the survey language while taking the survey. They can seamlessly switch between any available languages for which translations have been provided. Add the survey languages that you want to make available for respondents to choose from while answering the survey. Turn on the "Change language within survey" toggle. From where the respondent can change the survey language? Respondents can change the survey language by clicking the language icon located in the top right corner of the screen. This will display a list of all available languages added for that survey. A page break must be added to the survey for the "Change Language Within Survey" option to function properly. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: How to copy a survey from one Workspace system to another in CX
body: Copy CX survey How to copy a survey from one Workspace system to another in CX Copy a CX Survey How to create a copy of the survey? To copy a survey from one CX Workspace to another Workspace system, follow the below steps: Click on the Copy link against the name of the survey you want to copy Specify the name of survey and select the Workspace system where you want to copy the survey Click the Copy Survey button to finish the copying Your will be identically copied to the new Workspace system. Identically refers to the questions and formatting you applied to the survey will remain as is in the copied survey Will the copied survey work like the original one? Although most of the branching logic and settings are copied over, you may have to reset the logic for some questions. When you copy a survey, do test it and update the branching logic wherever necessary. The following logic options are not copied and will need to be reimplemented: Randomizer logic Dynamic Comment Box Quota Control Also, you need to set the NPS configuration under Settings tab and Action alerts under the Notification tab. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Import a new survey
kw: column data survey import excel external question time customer experience fill sheet optional existing same template segment need download questions format first values if respondents name responses mentioned pass row text feature system click id other fields actual not change
summary: Customer Experience - Import External Data into Existing Survey
body: Import a new survey Customer Experience - Import External Data into Existing Survey Customer Experience- Import External Data into Existing Survey With the Import External Data in existing survey feature, you can import your historical data into the survey created in CX system To achieve the same, you need to download the Import Template How to download the data import template? Login >> Customer Experience >> Analytics Click on Manage Data >> Import Under the ‘Import external data into Current Survey’, click on Download Import Template The template will have your survey questions with the question ID and other fields that you can fill to import your data. What should be the data format and how to fill excel sheet? The first twenty-four columns in the excel sheet are system variables. Actual survey questions start from column Y onwards. Do Not change the values in first three rows of the excel sheet. Making the structural changes in these rows, will not allow you to import the data. Column A: External Reference (optional) – This is an optional column. However, if you have track from what source your respondents responded to the survey, you can put the source name in this column. Example – If the survey was taken via email or SMS, you have enter the value EMAIL_RESPONSES. Column B: Time Stamp – This is a mandatory column and the time needs to be passed in format ‘MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss’ which is also mentioned in excel. Make sure while adding the time stamp in this column you change the input to ‘General’ in Excel sheet. The excel sheet by default changes the date and time format, which can give issues in imported data. Column C: Time Taken (optional) – Fill in the time that respondents took to give the survey. Column D: Weight Column (optional) – Leave this column blank. The weight is defined if you want to pass a scoring logic to a question. Column E – Column X: Custom Variable 1 to 20 (Optional) – You can pass any additional details about the respondents in these fields. It can be First name, last name, age, location, product, product group and so on. Column Y: Segment Code – This field is mandatory. You need to pass the segment code in this column to make sure all the data is mapped to correct segment. In case, you leave the column blank then all the data will be mapped to Main Segment. Column Z to Last Column – Actual survey question starts from this column. It consist of question ID on the second row and question text with instructions to fill data are in third row. The values of data should be as per the instruction mentioned in third row. For the comment/text based questions, you will have to type the text in excel. However, for other question types you can fill the numeric values, which will range from 1 to n, where n is the number of answer options. I have imported data. content:
Import a new survey

Customer Experience - Import External Data into Existing Survey

Customer Experience- Import External Data into Existing Survey With the Import External Data in existing survey feature, you can import your historical data into the survey created in CX system To achieve the same, you need to download the Import Template How to download the data import template? Login >> Customer Experience >> Analytics Click on Manage Data >> Import Under the ‘Import external data into Current Survey’, click on Download Import Template The template will have your survey questions with the question ID and other fields that you can fill to import your data. What should be the data format and how to fill excel sheet? The first twenty-four columns in the excel sheet are system variables. Actual survey questions start from column Y onwards. Do Not change the values in first three rows of the excel sheet. Making the structural changes in these rows, will not allow you to import the data. Column A: External Reference (optional) – This is an optional column. However, if you have track from what source your respondents responded to the survey, you can put the source name in this column. Example – If the survey was taken via email or SMS, you have enter the value EMAIL_RESPONSES. Column B: Time Stamp – This is a mandatory column and the time needs to be passed in format ‘MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss’ which is also mentioned in excel. Make sure while adding the time stamp in this column you change the input to ‘General’ in Excel sheet. The excel sheet by default changes the date and time format, which can give issues in imported data. Column C: Time Taken (optional) – Fill in the time that respondents took to give the survey. Column D: Weight Column (optional) – Leave this column blank. The weight is defined if you want to pass a scoring logic to a question. Column E – Column X: Custom Variable 1 to 20 (Optional) – You can pass any additional details about the respondents in these fields. It can be First name, last name, age, location, product, product group and so on. Column Y: Segment Code – This field is mandatory. You need to pass the segment code in this column to make sure all the data is mapped to correct segment. In case, you leave the column blank then all the data will be mapped to Main Segment. Column Z to Last Column – Actual survey question starts from this column. It consist of question ID on the second row and question text with instructions to fill data are in third row. The values of data should be as per the instruction mentioned in third row. For the comment/text based questions, you will have to type the text in excel. However, for other question types you can fill the numeric values, which will range from 1 to n, where n is the number of answer options. I have imported data. I need to upload more responses/data to the same survey. Yes, it can done. Follow the same process as mentioned above to import more responses to the same survey.
