content:
HTML Element Rule

Learn how to use the HTML Selector targeting rule to trigger surveys based on specific page elements.

HTML Selector Targeting Rule The HTML Selector Targeting Rule allows you to trigger Intercept surveys based on specific elements on your webpage using HTML selectors such as IDs or classes. This enables more precise, contextual, and behavior-driven survey delivery. Why use the HTML Selector rule? Traditional targeting rules like URL, time on page, or device type do not always capture moments tied to specific page elements. Many important user interactions such as viewing pricing, encountering an error message, or reaching a content block occur within specific HTML elements. Using the HTML Selector rule helps you trigger surveys exactly when these elements appear on page load, making surveys more relevant and improving response rates. How to add an HTML Selector rule? To add an HTML Selector rule: Navigate to Intercept » Rule Group Select HTML Selector from the rule dropdown Enter your HTML selector (e.g., #task, .product-title) Select an operand (Equals, Not Equals, Includes, Excludes, Exists) Enter a text value to compare Save the rule The selector supports valid querySelector(). The maximum input length for selectors and values is 255 characters. What operands are available? Equals: Matches the element’s text exactly. Not Equals: Works when the element’s text does not exactly match the value. Includes: True if the element’s text contains the value (partial match). Excludes: True if the element’s text does not contain the value. Exists: Works when the element exists in the DOM. No value needed. Technical limitations First Match Priority: Only the first matching element in the DOM is evaluated. One-Time Scan on Page Load: The rule is checked once when the page loads. It does not re-check dynamically added or updated content. Plain Text Only: The targeted element must contain plain text only. Nested HTML elements are not supported. Supported Selectors: IDs (#task) and classes (.product-title) are supported. Use cases The HTML Selector rule helps you target surveys at precise moments tied to specific on-page content. Some common scenarios include: Triggering a survey when a user reaches a Pricing section identified by a unique ID. Showing a survey on specific product pages by detecting product title or detail elements via class selectors. Displaying a survey when an error message or alert banner appears, allowing real-time feedback after user friction. Triggering feedback when a promotional or feature section loads, helping measure responses to new content. title: Project Settings
kw: project settings cookie cookies if user rule allows expiry visitor browser expire one no license section configure all level including setting define lifespan stored specify number days between 365 cleared default left blank year note type relies present classified returning
summary: This section allows you to configure all project-level settings, including cookie expiry.
body: Project Settings This section allows you to configure all project-level settings, including cookie expiry. Project Settings Project Settings - Cookies Expiry This setting allows you to define the lifespan of cookies stored in a visitor's browser for the project. You can specify the number of days (between 1 and 365) after which the cookie will expire or be cleared. Default: If left blank, the cookie will expire after one year. Note: The User Type rule in the Rule Settings relies on cookies. If a cookie is present in the visitor’s browser, they will be classified as a returning user. If no cookie is found, they will be considered a new user. Project Settings - Randomization When visitors meet the criteria for multiple rule sets in your project, one intercept will be randomly selected and displayed to them. title: Mobile Intercept

summary: Mobile Intercept What is a Mobile Intercept? Mobile Intercept lets you create, manage, and trigger intercepts within your mobile apps using the Intercept platform in QuestionPro. Whether you’re lookin
body: Mobile Intercept Mobile Intercept What is a Mobile Intercept? Mobile Intercept lets you create, manage, and trigger intercepts within your mobile apps using the Intercept platform in QuestionPro. Whether you’re looking to gather feedback, promote a new feature, or check user sentiment at key app moments, this feature makes it possible natively within your mobile environment. How to create a Mobile Intercept Creating an intercept for your mobile app via the Intercept Tool is quick and intuitive. Follow these steps: Log in to your QuestionPro account. Navigate to Customer Experience » Intercepts. Select the Workspace in which you want to create the intercept. Create a New Project or use any existing project you have created. From the toggle, select the Mobile option. Once the Mobile option is enabled, create a new intercept. Name the Intercept and choose the Interaction you wish to deploy Embed:Trigger an intercept when a user taps a specific button, icon, or logo in your app. Perfect for contextual feedback or feature-specific surveys. Prompt: Automatically display a pop-up based on your configured rules. Great for capturing quick feedback or delivering key messages without disrupting the user flow. Survey URL: Retrieve a survey link from QuestionPro and implement a fully custom intercept experience based on your app’s unique design and interaction patterns. Once the Interaction type is selected, choose the survey you want to display in the intercept and create the Intercept. Once the intercept is created, you can configure your intercept’s display rules. These are the rules you can customize: Time Spent in App: Launch intercepts based on total session duration, ideal for capturing feedback after prolonged usage or significant engagement. View Count: Trigger an intercept based on the number of times a specific app screen is viewed. Date: Choose one or multiple calendar dates for your intercept to display. Day: Select specific days of the week for intercept activation. Supports multiple day selections for maximum scheduling flexibility. Once the display rules are added, your mobile intercept set up is complete, and you can move to integrating with your mobile application Refer to our Mobile SDK Technical Documentation for setup and technical integration. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Mobile Intercept Settings
kw: intercept settings language mobile survey click save changes delay rate sdk specific intercepts sample go tab display users experience enable multiple responses trigger setting toggle allow field enter if translation configure targeting seconds percentage eligible see response set user available
summary: Mobile Intercept SDK - Configure custom variables and enable language-specific intercepts for personalized and localized survey targeting.
body: Mobile Intercept Settings Mobile Intercept SDK - Configure custom variables and enable language-specific intercepts for personalized and localized survey targeting. Mobile Intercept Settings Configure intercept settings to deliver a seamless experience for mobile users. These settings help boost engagement, improve response accuracy, and create a more personalized survey experience. Intercept Settings: Allow Multiple Responses The Allow Multiple Responses setting lets you control whether visitors can respond to the intercept more than once. How to Enable Multiple Responses: Go to the Intercept Settings tab for your intercept Toggle the Allow Multiple Responses switch to ON Click Save Changes Intercept Settings: Trigger Delay The Trigger Delay setting allows you to set a time delay (in seconds) before the intercept appears after a visitor lands on your website. How to Set a Trigger Delay: Go to the Intercept Settings tab for your intercept In the Trigger Delay field, enter the number of seconds you’d like to delay the intercept popup Click Save Changes Intercept Settings: Display Sample Rate The Display Sample Rate setting lets you control how often a mobile intercept is shown to eligible users. It defines the percentage of users who meet your targeting rules and will actually see the intercept. This helps prevent over-surveying, protects the mobile experience, and allows you to manage response volume or run controlled experiments. How to Configure Display Sample Rate: Go to the Intercept Settings tab for your intercept In the Display Sample Rate field, enter the percentage of eligible users who should see the intercept Click Save Changes Best Practices: Start with a lower sample rate (for example, 10–20%) during testing phases. Gradually increase the rate after validating performance and user experience. Combine Display Sample Rate with targeting rules for precise survey distribution. Use sampling to avoid interrupting too many user sessions in high-traffic areas of your app. What are Language-Specific Intercepts in the SDK? The Mobile Intercept SDK supports language-specific intercepts that automatically localize the survey experience based on the mobile device’s language settings. If a translation for the detected language is available in the survey, it will be used. Otherwise, the intercept defaults to English. This ensures that users receive surveys in their preferred language, enhancing engagement and response quality. How Language Detection Works in the SDK The SDK reads the mobile device's default language (e.g., fr for French). If the corresponding translation is present in the survey, the intercept will be shown in that language. If no translation is available, the SDK displays the intercept in English. Example: If a user's phone is set to Spanish and the survey has a Spanish translation, the intercept will be presented in Spanish. How to Enable Language-Specific Intercepts Log in to your admin dashboard and navigate to the Intercept Set

title: Introduction to Journey Management
kw: journey management questionpro maps customer data insights journeys experience ai initiatives teams introduction design metrics pain points not using steps track progress outcomes roi driven real traditional experiences tools feedback represent structure stages lanes connect sources elements use surface themes
summary: Introduction to QuestionPro Journey Management
body: Introduction to Journey Management Introduction to QuestionPro Journey Management Introduction to QuestionPro Journey Management Welcome to QuestionPro Journey Management. This guide introduces the concept of Journey Management, explains why it matters, and shows how QuestionPro Journey Management helps you move from static journey maps to continuous, data-driven experience management. What is Journey Management? Journey Management is the practice of designing, monitoring, and continuously improving customer journeys using real data, insights, and operational signals. Unlike traditional journey mapping - where journeys are created as one-time visual diagrams - QuestionPro Journey Management treats journeys as living, evolving systems that reflect how customers actually move through experiences. Visualize end-to-end customer experiences Measure what is happening at each stage and step Identify friction, pain points, and moments of truth Prioritize improvements based on impact Track outcomes and business results Why do traditional journey maps fall short? Many organizations invest in journey maps, but those maps often: Become outdated quickly Live in slide decks or whiteboards Are disconnected from real customer data Do not drive action Traditional mapping tools focus on visualization, while feedback tools focus on measurement. Because these tools are separate, teams can see feedback but not where it fits in the journey, and see journeys but not how they are performing. What is QuestionPro Journey Management? QuestionPro Journey Management is a purpose-built platform for designing and managing complex journey frameworks while natively integrating: Voice of Customer (VoC) data Experience metrics such as NPS, CSAT, and CES Research insights AI-powered analysis Actions and initiatives It transforms static journey maps into interactive, data-driven management systems that support continuous improvement. What makes QuestionPro Journey Management different? QuestionPro Journey Management unifies journey design, measurement, insights, and action in a single system. Instead of only answering: What does the journey look like? You can also answer: Which steps are underperforming? What pain points drive dissatisfaction or churn? Which personas are impacted? What initiatives are in progress? Are improvements delivering results? How does QuestionPro Journey Management work? Design journey maps that represent your customer experience Structure journeys using stages, steps, and lanes Connect data sources, metrics, and insights to journey elements Use AI to surface themes, pain points, and root causes Create initiatives to address issues Track progress, outcomes, and ROI Key capabilities Scalable journey frameworks Native metrics integration AI-powered insights AI-driven personas Native insight dashboards Actionable outer loop (initiatives) Backlog and ROI tracking Real-time collaboration Who is QuestionPro Journey Management for? CX and UX teams Product managers

title: Creating your first Journey Map
kw: journey map customer board create step creating first stages across pain points journeys click experiences touchpoints opportunities experience teams new start maps metrics identify visual end helps understand emotions video shared goals use case button created improve update yes insights
summary: Create your first Journey Map to visualize customer experiences across touchpoints, identify pain points, and uncover opportunities for improvement.
body: Creating your first Journey Map Create your first Journey Map to visualize customer experiences across touchpoints, identify pain points, and uncover opportunities for improvement. Create your first journey map What is a journey map? A journey map is a visual representation of the end-to-end experience a customer goes through while interacting with your brand. It helps teams understand customer actions, emotions, pain points, and opportunities across different stages and touchpoints. Watch this video for a quick walkthrough on how to create your first journey map. Your browser does not support the video tag. What is a journey board? A journey board is a way to organize multiple related customer journeys into a single collection based on shared goals, behaviors, or experience patterns. Instead of analyzing journeys in isolation, a journey board helps teams understand how similar journeys align, differ, and evolve across the same product, service, or use case. How do I create my first journey map? Step 1: Go to Journey Management and select the workspace where you want to create the journey map. Step 2: Click on + New Board button to start creating a board Step 3: Give your board a name and click on Save to create a new board. Step 4: Once a board is created, click anywhere on it to access the board details and create journey maps Step 5: Click on + New Journey button to start creating a journey map Step 6: A journey map with the basic building blocks like Stages, Steps, Lanes(Sentiment, Image, Metrics, Action etc.) will be created Step 7: You can enrich each touchpoint (stage/step) by adding customer goals, emotions, pain points, and metrics. What can I do after creating a Journey Map? Analyze customer pain points and friction areas. Identify opportunities to improve experiences across stages. Collaborate with stakeholders using a shared visual view. Continuously update the journey as customer behavior evolves. Can I edit a Journey Map after creating it? Yes. Journey maps are fully editable. You can update stages, touchpoints, insights, and metrics at any time to keep the journey aligned with real customer experiences. Tips for creating effective Journey Maps Start with a clear persona and use case. Keep stages simple and focused on customer intent. Base insights on real data whenever possible. Review and refine journeys regularly with cross-functional teams. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Creating a Persona
kw: persona personas step customer create group first needs creating journey click behaviors teams goals new better understand based real data insights align experience design video not shared context section button name created details basic expectations yes any license learn motivations
summary: Learn how to create your first customer persona to better understand customer needs, behaviors, and motivations.
body: Creating a Persona Learn how to create your first customer persona to better understand customer needs, behaviors, and motivations. Create your first persona What is a Persona? A Persona is a detailed representation of a key customer segment based on real data and insights. Personas help teams align on who the customer is, what they need, and how they behave, enabling better journey mapping and experience design. Watch this video for a step-by-step walkthrough on how to create your first persona. Your browser does not support the video tag. What is a Persona group? A persona group is a way to organize multiple related personas into a single collection based on shared characteristics, goals, or context. Instead of viewing personas in isolation, a persona group helps teams understand how different customer types relate to one another within the same product, market, or journey. How do I create my first Persona? Step 1: Go to Journey Management and select the workspace where you want to create the persona. Step 2: Navigate to the Personas section from the left navigation menu. Step 3: Click on + New persona group button to begin creating a persona group. Step 4: Enter persona group name and click on Save Step 5: Once a group is created, click anyhwere on it to access it's details and create a Persona Step 6: Click on + New persona button to create a persona. Step 7: A persona with the basic building blocks like Background, Needs, Frustrations, Goals will be created Step 8: Enrich your persona by entering the basic persona details such as persona name, demographics, needs, goals, expectations etc. How can I use a Persona? Associate personas with journey maps to provide context. Align teams around a shared understanding of the customer. Identify unmet needs and expectations. Design more relevant experiences and touchpoints. Can I edit a Persona after creating it? Yes. Personas are fully editable. You can update any section at any time to reflect new insights or changing customer behaviors. Tips for creating effective Personas Base personas on real customer data whenever possible. Focus on behaviors and needs, not assumptions. Keep personas concise and actionable. Review and refine personas regularly. title: Building Blocks of Journey Maps
kw: journey step lanes map add stages steps building blocks stage customer name button new maps while actions hover journeys organize experience phases improve end video editing support such capture across information open edit existing appears click between any lane yes
summary: Understand the core building blocks of journey maps and learn how to add stages, steps, and lanes while designing customer journeys.
body: Building Blocks of Journey Maps Understand the core building blocks of journey maps and learn how to add stages, steps, and lanes while designing customer journeys. Journey Map Building Blocks: Stages, Steps, and Lanes What are the building blocks of a journey map? Journey maps are structured using three key building blocks: Stages, Steps, and Lanes. These components help you organize the customer experience into clear phases, actions, and perspectives, making it easier to analyze and improve journeys end to end. Watch this video for a quick overview of journey map building blocks and how to add stages, steps, and lanes while editing a journey map. Your browser does not support the video tag. What are stages, steps, and lanes? Stages represent the high-level phases of the journey, such as onboarding, usage, or support. Steps capture the individual actions, touchpoints, or moments that occur within each stage. Lanes run horizontally across the journey map and are used to organize different types of information, such as customer actions, emotions, pain points, or internal ownership. How do I add stages and steps to a journey map? Step 1: Open the journey map you want to edit. Step 2: Hover over an existing stage or step name in the journey map. Step 3: When you hover, a + (add) button appears next to the stage or step name. Step 4: Click the + button to add a new stage or step. Step 5: Enter a name for the new stage or step to continue building your journey. How do I add lanes to a journey map? Step 1: Open the journey map in edit mode. Step 2: Hover your cursor between any two existing lanes. Step 3: A + (add) button appears between the lanes. Step 4: Click the + button to add a new lane. Step 5: Name the lane and use it to capture relevant information across the journey. Can I add or modify stages, steps, and lanes later? Yes. You can add, update, or reorganize stages, steps, and lanes at any time while editing a journey map. This flexibility allows you to refine your journey as new insights and requirements emerge. title: Layers
kw: layers layer journey map click maps create manage button name dropdown share all custom easy apply delete sections columns screenshot desired add open select selected view saved under save specific lanes want link visible focus each access base selecting navigation
summary: Layers in journey maps – create, save, and manage custom sections of journey maps by selecting specific rows and columns for easy navigation and screenshotting.
body: Layers Layers in journey maps – create, save, and manage custom sections of journey maps by selecting specific rows and columns for easy navigation and screenshotting. Layers for journey maps – create, save, and screenshot custom sections of the journey map What are Layers in journey maps? The Layers feature allows users to create a custom view of their journey maps by selecting specific lanes and columns. These Layers help users focus on relevant portions of complex journey maps without having to view the entire map each time. Each Layer can be named, saved, and viewed independently. Layers are optimized for screenshot capture, making it easy to insert journey insights into presentations or reports. Watch this video to learn how to use Layers in QuestionPro Journey Management to customize journey maps for different perspectives and focus on the insights that matter most to each team. Your browser does not support the video tag. How do you create a Layer in a journey map? Navigate to your desired journey map. Click on the Layers button. To create your first Layer, click on Manage Layers. In the pop-up window, click the Add Layer button. Choose the lanes and columns you want included in the Layer. You can also add tags to the Layer. Enter a name for the Layer and click Apply. Your new Layer will now appear under the Layers dropdown for quick access and reuse. How do you access and switch between Layers? All created Layers are available under the Layers dropdown, and you can switch between any of these Layers. Select any saved Layer to load its custom view instantly. The active Layer’s name is displayed for easy reference while viewing. What is the base Layer? The base Layer is the default view of the entire journey map. It cannot be edited or renamed. All user-created Layers are nested under the base Layer for easy reference and access. How do you manage saved Layers? Saved Layers are always accessible under the selected journey map. Click the Manage Layers option and select the Layer you want to edit. Make the required updates and click the Apply button. How do you delete a Layer? Open the Layers dropdown and click on Manage Layers. Hover over the Layer name you wish to delete. Click the three-dot menu (⋮) next to the Layer name. Select Delete from the dropdown. What are the benefits of using Layers? Simplifies navigation across large and complex journey maps. Enables focus on specific stages of the customer journey. Improves team collaboration by creating personalized views. Makes it easy to screenshot key sections for reports and stakeholder communication. How do you share a map with Layers applied? You can now share your journey map with all applied Layers using a shareable link. Recipients will see the map exactly as you configured it, with all selected Layers visible. Apply the desired Layers to your journey map. Click the Share button in the map settings. The sharable link for the Layer will be copied and you can share it with others. The m

title: AI Persona

summary: AI Persona Generator – Automatically create rich, data-driven customer personas based on real behavioral, feedback, and demographic data to power journey mapping and personalization strategies.
body: AI Persona AI Persona Generator – Automatically create rich, data-driven customer personas based on real behavioral, feedback, and demographic data to power journey mapping and personalization strategies. AI Persona Creator – Instantly Create Actionable Customer Personas What is the QuestionPro AI Persona Creator? The QuestionPro AI Persona Creator is an AI feature within the Journey Management platform that enables users to generate real-time, dynamic personas using customer feedback, behavioral patterns, and demographic data. These personas help you better understand customer motivations, pain points, and preferences — without manual effort. How to Access and Use the AI Persona Creator? Select the Workspace for which you want to create the personas Navigate to the Persona section from the left navigation menu Click on the “QuestionPro AI” button next to Add Persona button. Enter a brief context or description of the segment you're targeting. Select the sections you want to include in the persona (e.g., Goals, Frustrations, Tools). Enable or disable the Demographics Toggle based on your need for age, location, job role, etc. Click Generate to automatically create the persona. Review and edit the generated persona to fit your unique use case. What Are the Benefits of Using the AI Persona Creator? Reduces time spent on manual persona creation. Produces data-driven, real-time personas based on actual customer interactions. Enhances journey mapping and CX strategy with up-to-date insights. Improves personalization across marketing, UX, and product efforts. Can I Edit a Generated Persona? Yes. Once a persona is generated, it opens in an editable template. You can revise content in any card to better suit your needs or company context. Tips for Best Results Provide clear and specific context when generating personas. Only select sections relevant to your goal to keep personas concise. Use generated personas alongside journey maps for better visualization and decision-making. title: Journey AI - Interviews
kw: journey interviews ai interview customer click created map generated experience automatically transcripts select board card research page want full qualitative stages insights design across use maps using open any workspace tab left side screen new enter name choose type paste
summary: This page explains how to automatically generate customer journey maps from interview transcripts using Journey AI.
body: Journey AI - Interviews This page explains how to automatically generate customer journey maps from interview transcripts using Journey AI. Journey AI - Interviews Watch this video for a quick overview of the Journey AI - Interviews feature Your browser does not support the video tag. What is Journey AI - Interviews? Journey AI - Interviews is an AI-powered capability that transforms qualitative customer research into structured journey maps automatically. Instead of manually analyzing interview notes, Journey AI reads interview transcripts, identifies key themes, emotions, and experience signals, and converts them into a visual customer journey with stages, touchpoints, and insights. This helps teams move from raw research to actionable journey design in minutes, dramatically reducing the time traditionally required for manual synthesis. Why are interviews important for mapping customer journeys? Customer interviews provide rich qualitative insight that cannot be captured through surveys or analytics alone. They reveal motivations, expectations, frustrations, and emotional context across the customer experience. By analyzing interview data, Journey AI uncovers patterns across conversations and connects them directly to journey stages, enabling teams to design experiences grounded in real customer voices rather than assumptions. When should you use Journey AI - Interviews? Journey AI - Interviews is especially useful when you want to: Convert discovery or research interviews into a first draft journey map Scale synthesis across multiple interviews quickly Validate journey assumptions using real Voice-of-Customer (VoC) evidence Accelerate journey creation during product discovery or CX transformation Operationalize qualitative insights into structured experience design How to use Journey AI - Interviews? Open any Workspace and click on the Interviews tab on the left side of the screen. On the Interviews page, click New Interview. Enter a name for the interview, select the Board where you want the journey to be created, choose the journey type, and paste the full interview transcript. Then click Save. An interview card will be created. Hover over the card, click the three-dot menu, and select Map. The AI-generated journey map is automatically created and added to the selected Board. What happens after the journey is generated? The generated journey is fully editable. You can refine stages, update touchpoints, add metrics, validate insights with stakeholders, and enhance the map with additional research or operational data. Journey AI provides a strong starting point, while your team applies expertise to finalize and operationalize the experience strategy. Best practices for better AI-generated journeys To get the most accurate and meaningful results: Use full verbatim transcripts instead of summaries Focus interviews on a single persona or scenario when possible Ensure transcripts include context, emotions, and customer language Review and refine t

title: Import Map

summary: AI-Assisted Journey Map Import
body: Import Map AI-Assisted Journey Map Import AI-Assisted Journey Map Import AI-Assisted Journey Map Import enables teams to quickly transform existing documentation into structured journey maps. This feature is designed to reduce manual setup, accelerate migration from legacy formats, and operationalize journey insights. What is AI-Assisted Journey Map Import? AI-Assisted Journey Map Import allows you to upload one or more files containing journey information and automatically create a journey map by extracting stages, steps, and lanes. You can import structured files such as Excel as well as supporting documentation in PDF format. The system analyzes the uploaded content, shows a preview of the detected structure, and lets you adjust the mappings before creating the journey. Watch this video to learn how AI-Assisted Import Maps automatically convert your files into journey maps using AI. Your browser does not support the video tag. How do I import a journey map using AI? To import a journey map using AI, follow these steps: Go to Journey Management. Open Settings » Import. Choose the Workspace and Board where the journey should be created. Select one or more files using the Choose file button. You can upload Excel (.xlsx) files for structured journey data and PDF (.pdf) files. Click Import. The system analyzes all selected files together and generates a unified journey map. What happens after import? After import, the journey map is configured automatically: A new journey map is created in the selected board. Stages appear as the main lifecycle sections identified across the uploaded files. Steps appear within their respective stages. Lanes appear as swimlanes based on detected roles, themes, or categories. Information from multiple files is consolidated into a single visualization. You can edit, rename, or extend the journey like any other journey map. Frequently Asked Questions The following questions address common usage scenarios, limitations, and best practices for using AI-Assisted Journey Map Import. What file formats can I upload for AI-Assisted Journey Map Import? You can currently upload Excel and PDF files to generate journey maps. Support for PowerPoint files and ZIP uploads is planned for an upcoming release. Do I need to follow a specific template before uploading my file? No strict template is required. The AI reads and interprets your structure automatically, identifying stages, steps, and lanes. However, cleaner formatting such as clear headers and consistent columns will improve mapping accuracy. How accurate is QuestionProAI when converting my file into a journey map? QuestionProAI is designed to preserve your structure and context with high accuracy by automatically identifying stages, steps, and lanes from your file. After generation, you can review and refine the journey directly in the editor to make any adjustments needed. Can I edit the journey after it has been generated? Yes. Once imported, the journey behaves like any ot
content:
Import Map

AI-Assisted Journey Map Import

AI-Assisted Journey Map Import AI-Assisted Journey Map Import enables teams to quickly transform existing documentation into structured journey maps. This feature is designed to reduce manual setup, accelerate migration from legacy formats, and operationalize journey insights. What is AI-Assisted Journey Map Import? AI-Assisted Journey Map Import allows you to upload one or more files containing journey information and automatically create a journey map by extracting stages, steps, and lanes. You can import structured files such as Excel as well as supporting documentation in PDF format. The system analyzes the uploaded content, shows a preview of the detected structure, and lets you adjust the mappings before creating the journey. Watch this video to learn how AI-Assisted Import Maps automatically convert your files into journey maps using AI. Your browser does not support the video tag. How do I import a journey map using AI? To import a journey map using AI, follow these steps: Go to Journey Management. Open Settings » Import. Choose the Workspace and Board where the journey should be created. Select one or more files using the Choose file button. You can upload Excel (.xlsx) files for structured journey data and PDF (.pdf) files. Click Import. The system analyzes all selected files together and generates a unified journey map. What happens after import? After import, the journey map is configured automatically: A new journey map is created in the selected board. Stages appear as the main lifecycle sections identified across the uploaded files. Steps appear within their respective stages. Lanes appear as swimlanes based on detected roles, themes, or categories. Information from multiple files is consolidated into a single visualization. You can edit, rename, or extend the journey like any other journey map. Frequently Asked Questions The following questions address common usage scenarios, limitations, and best practices for using AI-Assisted Journey Map Import. What file formats can I upload for AI-Assisted Journey Map Import? You can currently upload Excel and PDF files to generate journey maps. Support for PowerPoint files and ZIP uploads is planned for an upcoming release. Do I need to follow a specific template before uploading my file? No strict template is required. The AI reads and interprets your structure automatically, identifying stages, steps, and lanes. However, cleaner formatting such as clear headers and consistent columns will improve mapping accuracy. How accurate is QuestionProAI when converting my file into a journey map? QuestionProAI is designed to preserve your structure and context with high accuracy by automatically identifying stages, steps, and lanes from your file. After generation, you can review and refine the journey directly in the editor to make any adjustments needed. Can I edit the journey after it has been generated? Yes. Once imported, the journey behaves like any other journey in Journey Management: Add or remove stages Modify steps or lanes Enhance with insights, ownership, or metrics Will this overwrite any existing journeys? No. Imports always create new journey maps inside your selected workspace and board. Your existing journeys remain unchanged. Is this feature included in my subscription, or does it cost extra? AI-Assisted Journey Map Import is included with Journey Management at no additional cost. When should I use AI-Assisted Import instead of building a journey manually? Use AI-Assisted Import when: You already have journeys documented in Excel or PDFs You are migrating journeys from another platform You want to accelerate onboarding, audits, or workshops Build manually when designing a brand-new journey from scratch. Can the feature handle large or complex journey files? Yes. It is designed for enterprise-scale journeys with multiple stages, steps, and lanes. Expanded bulk-import capabilities are on the roadmap to further support large transformation programs. What happens if my file contains unstructured notes or narrative text? The feature works best with structured journey content. If the document is primarily free-form narrative, the AI may not reliably convert it into stages and steps, and manual creation may be recommended. How does this help my team operationally? AI-Assisted Import reduces manual effort and accelerates time-to-value by: Eliminating duplicate journey documentation Standardizing structures across teams Making existing research immediately actionable Turning static files into live, manageable journeys License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? title: Journey Frameworks
kw: journey frameworks framework create across teams organization maps use save map reuse customer structures created new library available questionpro standardize mapping select click if edit manage management templates business units best practices approved accelerate creation governance departments users yes existing
summary: Learn how to create, manage, and reuse Journey Frameworks in Journey Management. Use organization and QuestionPro frameworks to create journey maps faster and standardize journey mapping across teams.
body: Journey Frameworks Learn how to create, manage, and reuse Journey Frameworks in Journey Management. Use organization and QuestionPro frameworks to create journey maps faster and standardize journey mapping across teams. Journey Frameworks Introduction Journey Frameworks help organizations create customer journey maps faster by allowing teams to start with reusable templates instead of building every journey from scratch. Frameworks provide predefined journey structures, phases, and touchpoints that can be reused across projects, teams, and business units. Organizations can also save their own journey maps as frameworks, creating a centralized repository of best practices and approved journey structures. What are Journey Frameworks? Journey Frameworks are reusable journey map templates that help teams standardize journey mapping and accelerate journey creation. Frameworks can be used to: Reduce the time required to create journey maps. Reuse proven journey structures and methodologies. Standardize journey mapping across teams. Preserve organizational best practices. Support journey governance initiatives. Ensure consistency across departments and business units. Journey Management supports two types of frameworks: QuestionPro Frameworks – Frameworks provided by QuestionPro. My Frameworks – Frameworks created by users within your organization. How do I create a journey using a Framework? Navigate to Journey Management. Select the Workspace that contains the board where you want to create the journey. Open the desired Board. Click New Journey. Select one of the following options: Organization Frameworks QuestionPro Frameworks Select a framework. Click Create Journey. The new journey map will be created using the selected framework structure. If you select the Default Framework, you can build your journey from scratch. How do I save a journey as a Framework? If you have created a journey map that you would like to reuse in the future, you can save it as an Organization Framework. Open the journey map you want to save. Navigate to Journey Settings. Click Save as Framework. The framework will be added to the Framework Library and will be available for future journey creation. How do I access the Framework Library? From the left navigation menu, click Frameworks. The Framework Library displays all available frameworks How do I manage Frameworks? Users can manage frameworks directly from the Framework Library. Available actions include: View available frameworks. Edit framework names. Edit framework descriptions. Delete frameworks. Reuse frameworks across multiple journey maps. Common Use Cases Create organization-approved journey templates for governance and consistency. Standardize customer lifecycle journeys across teams. Create repeatable onboarding and retention journeys. Scale journey mapping programs across departments. Reuse successful journey structures across regions, products, and customer segments. Accelerate journey creation for consultants

Journey Management supports two types of frameworks: QuestionPro Frameworks – Frameworks provided by QuestionPro. My Frameworks – Frameworks created by users within your organization. How do I create a journey using a Framework? Navigate to Journey Management. Select the Workspace that contains the board where you want to create the journey. Open the desired Board. Click New Journey. Select one of the following options: Organization Frameworks QuestionPro Frameworks Select a framework. Click Create Journey. The new journey map will be created using the selected framework structure. If you select the Default Framework, you can build your journey from scratch. How do I save a journey as a Framework? If you have created a journey map that you would like to reuse in the future, you can save it as an Organization Framework. Open the journey map you want to save. Navigate to Journey Settings. Click Save as Framework. The framework will be added to the Framework Library and will be available for future journey creation. How do I access the Framework Library? From the left navigation menu, click Frameworks. The Framework Library displays all available frameworks How do I manage Frameworks? Users can manage frameworks directly from the Framework Library. Available actions include: View available frameworks. Edit framework names. Edit framework descriptions. Delete frameworks. Reuse frameworks across multiple journey maps. Common Use Cases Create organization-approved journey templates for governance and consistency. Standardize customer lifecycle journeys across teams. Create repeatable onboarding and retention journeys. Scale journey mapping programs across departments. Reuse successful journey structures across regions, products, and customer segments. Accelerate journey creation for consultants and agency teams. Frequently Asked Questions Can I modify a journey after creating it from a framework? Yes. Once a journey map is created from a framework, it becomes an independent journey and can be modified as needed. Can I create my own frameworks? Yes. Any existing journey map can be saved as an Organization Framework. Can multiple teams use the same framework? Yes. Frameworks are designed to be reused across teams, departments, and business units. Can I edit the contents of a framework? No. Currently, users can only edit the framework name and description. To change the framework structure, create a new journey with the desired changes and save it as a new framework. What happens if I update a framework? Changes apply only to the framework metadata (name and description). Existing journey maps are not affected. What happens if I delete a framework? Deleting a framework removes it from the Framework Library but does not impact any existing journey maps that were created from it. Best Practices Create frameworks for commonly used journey structures. Establish organization-approved frameworks to support governance. Use clear framework names and descriptions. Maintain separate frameworks for different customer segments or use cases. Use frameworks to accelerate onboarding for new team members. License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience

Import Tasks via Excel lets you upload external task data in bulk and automatically create tasks within your initiative. You can include details such as Task Name, Owner, Contributors, Due Date, and Status. Existing users in your organization are automatically assigned, while new users are added as sub-accounts under your organization, ensuring smooth integration of external data. How do I import external tasks using Excel? To import tasks from an external Excel file, follow these steps: Go to Initiatives under the Planning and Outcomes section in the Journey Management Platform. Go to the Initiative where you want to add tasks. Click the Import Tasks button. In the pop-up window: Download the sample template first. Fill in your task details (Task Name, Owner, Contributors, Due Date, Status) in the template. Once complete, upload the filled template to import your tasks. Click Import to add all tasks from the Excel file into your initiative automatically. What columns are required in the Excel template? Your Excel file should include the following columns: Task Name – Name of the task. Owner – Person responsible for the task. Contributors – Team members involved in the task. Due Date – Task deadline Status – Current status of the task (e.g., Pending, In progress, Completed). What happens after import? After import: All external tasks are added to the selected initiative. Owners and Contributors are assigned automatically if they exist in your organization. New sub-accounts are created for any external users not in your organization. You can edit tasks, update owners, contributors, due dates, and status just like any other task in the platform. content:
Import Tasks

Import tasks from external Excel files into the Journey Management Platform for faster task creation and management.

Import Tasks via Excel The Import Tasks via Excel feature allows you to bring in tasks from external sources directly into your initiatives in the Journey Management Platform. This helps you quickly migrate existing task data, assign owners and contributors, and maintain continuity across projects without manual entry. What is Import Tasks via Excel? Import Tasks via Excel lets you upload external task data in bulk and automatically create tasks within your initiative. You can include details such as Task Name, Owner, Contributors, Due Date, and Status. Existing users in your organization are automatically assigned, while new users are added as sub-accounts under your organization, ensuring smooth integration of external data. How do I import external tasks using Excel? To import tasks from an external Excel file, follow these steps: Go to Initiatives under the Planning and Outcomes section in the Journey Management Platform. Go to the Initiative where you want to add tasks. Click the Import Tasks button. In the pop-up window: Download the sample template first. Fill in your task details (Task Name, Owner, Contributors, Due Date, Status) in the template. Once complete, upload the filled template to import your tasks. Click Import to add all tasks from the Excel file into your initiative automatically. What columns are required in the Excel template? Your Excel file should include the following columns: Task Name – Name of the task. Owner – Person responsible for the task. Contributors – Team members involved in the task. Due Date – Task deadline Status – Current status of the task (e.g., Pending, In progress, Completed). What happens after import? After import: All external tasks are added to the selected initiative. Owners and Contributors are assigned automatically if they exist in your organization. New sub-accounts are created for any external users not in your organization. You can edit tasks, update owners, contributors, due dates, and status just like any other task in the platform. Frequently Asked Questions The following questions cover common usage scenarios, best practices, and troubleshooting for importing tasks from external sources. Do I need to use a specific template? Yes. Using the provided sample template ensures all required columns are included and properly formatted. This minimizes import errors and guarantees correct task assignment. Can I import tasks for users not in my organization? Yes. External users specified as Owners or Contributors will be automatically added as sub-accounts under your organization. Will importing tasks overwrite existing tasks? No. Imported tasks are added as new tasks in the selected initiative. Existing tasks remain unchanged. Can I edit tasks after import? Yes. Once tasks are imported, you can edit task names, assign new owners or contributors, update statuses, and adjust due dates. What should I do if the import fails? Check that your Excel file follows the template format, all required columns are filled, and user names match existing organization members. Correct any errors and try importing again. summary: The QuestionPro CX SDK allows seamless integration of surveys directly into your mobile applications.
body: CX SDK - Overview The QuestionPro CX SDK allows seamless integration of surveys directly into your mobile applications. CX SDK Overview Introduction The QuestionPro CX SDK allows seamless integration of surveys directly into your mobile applications. With support for iOS, Android, and React Native, this SDK empowers you to gather valuable customer feedback without disrupting the in-app experience, ensuring smooth engagement and improved response rates. Key Features In-App Surveys: Trigger surveys directly within your mobile app to capture user feedback in real time. Cross-Platform Support: Compatible with iOS, Android, and React Native applications. Secure Data Storage: All feedback is securely saved in the QuestionPro CX platform for easy analysis and reporting. Customizable Triggers: Define specific app events or user actions that will launch the survey automatically. User-Centric Experience: Surveys are displayed within the app, ensuring a seamless, uninterrupted experience for users. Benefits Fast and Easy Integration: The SDK is designed for quick and simple integration across supported platforms. Enhanced Feedback Collection: Keep users engaged by conducting surveys directly within your app. Seamless Connectivity: Effortlessly link your app to the QuestionPro CX platform for reliable data management. Actionable Insights: Use collected feedback to improve user satisfaction and drive meaningful improvements. Getting Started Download the SDK: Access the QuestionPro CX SDK for iOS, Android, or React Native from the developer portal. Integrate with Your App: Follow the platform-specific integration guide to incorporate the SDK into your app's codebase. Set Up Triggers: Define the actions or conditions in your app that will initiate the survey. Test Your Integration: Verify the integration on your chosen platform(s) to ensure everything functions as expected. Monitor Feedback: Log in to the QuestionPro CX platform to analyze the feedback collected through your app. Technical Details for Integration Android SDK iOS SKD React Native License This feature is available with the following license : Customer Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Advanced Features Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 119 ## Media library URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/image-library.html Summary: Media Library - How to upload images and files Media Library - How to upload images and files The media li
body: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Advanced Features Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 119 ## Media library URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/image-library.html Summary: Media Library - How to upload images and files Media Library - How to upload images and files The media library is a repository of uploaded images, documents and any other media files that can be accessed and used anywhere across the QuestionPro platform. How to upload or add files to the media library? To upload images or files go to: Login » Survey » Edit Under the Edit tab, click on Media Library You can select a folder to upload your image to or also add a new folder Once you have selected or created a folder, you can click to add or drag and drop files anywhere on the page. The files will be uploaded and can now be used across the platform. Files uploaded to media library are publicly accessible. Please do not upload any private or sensitive files to the media library. Click here to learn more about encrypted media URLs. The following file types can be uploaded. Types Extension License Level Images GIF, JPG, JPEG, PNG, BMP, WEBP All Paid Audio MP3, WAV Advanced and above Video M4V, M4A, MP4, MOV, AVI, WEBM Advanced and above Documents / Other TXT, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX,PPT, PPTX, PDF Advanced and above Fonts TTF, OTF, WOFF, WOFF2 Research Edition The following are the storage limits License Level Storage Limit Essentials 100 MB Advanced 1 GB Team Edition* 5 GB Research Edition* 5 GB * Shared across all users within the organization. Please contact your account manager to add more storage space. What is the maximum size limit for an individual file that can be uploaded? 100MB. We allow individual files up to 100MB in size. For larger files, you can upload them on any external platform like google drive, dropbox, etc and use the public link for the file on your surveys if needed. Smaller the file size, faster it will load! How to add an image on a survey question? You can add an image on your survey questions or answer options. Click on the text and you can click on the image icon. On the popup, you can either upload a new image or select from an image from your media library. The image will be added to your survey question or answer option as selected. What options are available for the images and other files? Image/file options can be accessed by clicking on the more (vertical ellipsis) icon. The following options are available: View Details: This will open the image preview with all file details. Get Link: Click on the get link option and the image/file URL will be copied to the clipboard. Get HTML: Clicking on get html will copy the HTML for the image/file to clipboard. You can use this on the email invitation or on the survey to display or link files. Set as Logo: Click on this option to use the image as logo for the survey. Rename: Use this option to rename the file. Download: Download the file by clicking on this option. Delete: Delete the file. C

body: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Audience And Panels Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 6 ## Communities Recruitment URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/community-recruitment.html Summary: Community recruitment Community Recruitment With QuestionPro you can use surveys to recruit and create a community of members for continious discovery, and faster insights. How to add a community recruitment question? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit » Workspace Click on the Add Question button on the last question in the survey. From under Advanced questions, click on the Community Recruitment question under misc question types. The community recruitment question will be added to your survey. Click on Settings and select the community you wish to link the survey with. You can toggle on/off the fields you want to collect data for. Community Recruitment question type should be the last question in the survey. How does community recruitment work? When respondents answer your survey, they will also join the selected community and will be added as members to your community. Respondent will be added to your community as a member. License This feature is available with the following license : Communities Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback. ## Allowing multiple respondents from the same device URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/multiple-respondents-from-same-machine.html Summary: Allowing multiple respondents from the same machine? Allowing multiple respondents from the same machine? Allowing respondents to take a survey multiple times from the same machine: By default, a survey can be taken multiple times from the same machine. You can have your respondents take the survey as many times as they want. Every time the respondent takes the survey, the respondent needs to close the current browser window and open a new window in order to take the survey again. I have enabled Prevent Ballot Box Stuffing. How can I still allow multiple responses from the same machine? If you have enabled ABBS, respondents can take the survey more than once by deleting the browser cookies. If you have enabled Respondent Tracking along with ABBS, there is a check in place to see if the particular email address has a completed response recorded. If there is a completed response for the particular email address then another response cannot be submitted. If too many respondents are taking the survey from the same PC how to ensure that the cookies do not get cached? If too many respondents are taking the survey from the same PC, before sending the survey, you may append &responseCheck=false tag after the survey link. This will ensure that the cookies do not get cached, and many respondents can take the survey from the same PC. The responsecheck can be appended in 2 ways for Anonymous link: https://www.questionpro.com/t/AKbYYZYoPB?responseCheck=false OR https://www.questionpro.com/a/TakeSurvey?id=

summary: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Creating Surveys Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 47 ## Creating surveys URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/create-survey.html Summary: This QuestionPro help file will help you create a survey from scratch with your own set of questions or w
body: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Creating Surveys Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 47 ## Creating surveys URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/create-survey.html Summary: This QuestionPro help file will help you create a survey from scratch with your own set of questions or with a ready-to-use survey template. How to create a survey? There are 3 ways you can create a survey using QuestionPro: Create a survey from scratch in your account Use a pre designed survey template Upload a survey using a microsoft word document The goal of creating a survey is to elicit holistic information from a target audience that provides enough data points to generate actionable insights. How do I create a survey? To create a survey from scratch, follow these simple steps: Go to: Login » My Surveys. Click on the new survey button. There are two ways of creating a survey: Create a survey from scratch: Click new survey » enter the survey name of your choice » click on the create survey button. Your survey will be created and you can begin adding question types that you prefer, and customize their settings in accordance to your survey goals. Use a template to create a survey: Click new survey » use a template » select the survey template of your choice. A pop-up will appear giving a preview of the selected template. Click on use this template button to create a survey using the selected survey template. If you like, you can customize the questions or answer options within the template. Upload a survey using a word document Click new survey » enter the survey name » click on the create survey button » click on Add questions from MS word. A pop-up will appear giving you the option to upload a word file or download a sample document. Click on Sample Microsoft Word Document button to download a sample file for reference. Once your file is ready for upload, drag and drop the file and click on upload to create your survey. Note: The ‘Add from Microsoft Word’ option is available only when creating a new survey. Once questions are added manually, this option will no longer be accessible. Inline frames not supported Which is the most recommended survey creation method? When you are sure about what kind of survey you wish to create, you have a set goal and the questionnaire to gather relevant data, the ‘new survey’ method is the most recommended as it gives you the ability to design your survey exactly the way you want it to be. When you are not sure about what kind of survey you wish to create or the right questions to ask your target audience, you could always use the ‘templates’ and then edit/customize them accordingly. What's the best way of getting started with QuestionPro? Our weekly training sessions! We conduct a live weekly webinar series which is absolutely FREE!. Each webinar is about 45 minutes long. The topics covered over a four week span include: An overview of QuestionPro An overview of the smart survey logic Survey distributio

body: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Design And Branding Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 19 ## Standard theme URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/look-and-feel.html Summary: Customize the look and feel of your survey using our features and themes to create a better experience for your respondents. Customizing the survey look and feel When your respondents receive your surveys, it's important that they can easily recognize your brand. With QuestionPro you can customize the appearance of your surveys to align them with the look and feel of your brand. In addition to our customization features, you can use any of our standard themes. Each of them was designed to perfectly fit your brand regardless of browser or device. How can I change the look and feel of my survey? Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » Design Here you can select from the professionally designed themes under different survey layouts How can I exactly match colors with my brand colors? You can select the color using the color picker or you can enter the HEX value for the color. For more information on color codes, please visit: HTML Colors - w3schools.com Once you have copied the color code, click on the Custom Color theme to open the color picker and paste the hex code for your color and save. How can I change the default theme of my survey? To change the default theme of your Survey: Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » Design Click on the Wrench Manage Themes icon. Select the default theme from the dropdown. Once the default theme has been changed, any new survey that you create will have the selected theme set as default. summary: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Questions And Logic Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 78 ## Open-ended text analysis URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/open-ended-text-data-analysis.html Summary: Analyzing open-ended text data Analyzing open-ended text data QuestionPro offe
body: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Questions And Logic Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 78 ## Open-ended text analysis URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/open-ended-text-data-analysis.html Summary: Analyzing open-ended text data Analyzing open-ended text data QuestionPro offers six ways to analyze open-ended text collected in a survey. These tools will enable you to make sense of responses and strengthen the quality and value of the data for those using it to smart business decisions. The goal for reviewing open-ended text is to be able to identify word themes and patterns that offer deeper insights into the overall survey. You can categorize and calculate the open-ended text with these tools based on respondent profile, custom variables, or responses given. Open-ended text tools Text report - Looking at all the open ended text data together Data segmentation text report - Filtering the data by open ended text Search and Adhoc query - Searching your open ended data for keywords Word cloud - looking at what words were used in majorirty Text tagging and categorization - tagging data to further analysis Sentiment analysis - Understanding the sentiment of your open ended data PathosAI - Using our in-house AI model Text Highlighter - Understanding the sentiment of your open ended data Text report To access the text report, go to: Login » Surveys » Analytics » Text Report. What customization options are available? You can expand the section to Filter data or access additional Settings Following settings are available for the dashboard: Filter Data: By default, the survey status filter is set to all responses. You can add additional filters and the dashboard will be updated in real-time. You can also save frequently used filters to access directly as and when needed. Title and Logo: You can update the title for the report as well as add description and also add logo for the dashboard. Customize Theme: Here you can customize the theme of the dashboard. Share Settings: Click on the globe icon to open share settings for the dashboard. Data segmentation To discover what specific segments of respondents say, use the data segmentation tool to download the results. For example, If you want to analyze comments from males vs females, the data segmentation tool makes it very easy to do a side by side comparison. To access data segmented text, go to: Login » Surveys » Analytics » Analysis » Data Segmentation Select ‘New Data Filter’ to create filters based on questions, custom variables, etc., and click ‘Save filter.’ Review the newly created segment and apply response and time status (optional). To the right, access the text report for the segment. Click on text report for the segment to analyze, print, or share with others. body: QuestionPro Help — Surveys: Reporting And Analytics Source: QuestionPro Help (surveys.questionpro.com) Articles: 59 ## Responses - raw data URL: https://www.questionpro.com/help/response-viewer.html Summary: Responses - raw data Responses - raw data Responses that are collected for your surveys are available to view in the raw (unprocessed) data format in the response viewer. It's a real time view of your data. Use the response viewer to view, edit, or delete individual responses. You can also use the response viewer to access individual spotlight reports, email individual responses, print individual responses, or delete responses. Where can I see the responses collected for my survey? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics Select the responses option from the drop down under dashboard. All your responses are available in the Response Viewer Following options are available in the response viewer: By default, question and answer data is not displayed. To see this data, switch on the toggle switch for Display Questions. You can control which data you want to see by using the Show/Hide Columns option. You can export the raw data by clicking on the XLS icon. You can search for responses in the search bar. You can search using the response id's. You can search multiple response id's using a comma separated list. You can also search using an email address. You can search using either the external reference variable or custom variables 1-5. By default, all responses are displayed under the response viewer. To view responses by particular status, you can click open the All Responses drop-down and select from the following options to view: All responses (default selection) Completed Started but not completed Terminates Data segments - all user created data segments Can I search for specific responses? Yes. You can search the response viewer for responses using either the response ID, email address, external reference variable, or custom variables 1-5. To search for a specific response, if you know the response ID or the email address, you can search by entering this data. You can also search by response ID for multiple response ID's separated by a comma. For email address, external reference variable, or custom variable 1-5; comma separated search for multiple search terms is not possible. Please use a specific search term. Can I view, edit, download individual responses? Yes. You can click on the response id to open the response in a popup. You can edit the response or view the spotlight report here. Following download and export options are available for the individual response. PDF: You can download the individual report in PDF format. Excel: Download the individual response in Excel. Email: You can email the individual report. Print: You can print the individual report. How can I update custom variables in bulk? title: Creating a Study

summary: Learn how to create, manage, and organize studies in QuestionPro UX. Understand the hierarchy of studies, quests, and tasks for your research.
body: Creating a Study Learn how to create, manage, and organize studies in QuestionPro UX. Understand the hierarchy of studies, quests, and tasks for your research. Studies in QuestionPro UX What is a Study in QuestionPro UX? A Study is the highest level of organization in QuestionPro UX. It serves as a container for your research agenda, allowing you to group multiple Quests together. Each Quest, in turn, consists of specific tasks or questions designed to capture user insights. This hierarchy ensures that complex research projects remain structured and easy to manage for both the researcher and the participant. Where do I find my Studies? To access your research projects, navigate to the Qualitative Research section in the left sidebar and select Studies. You can organize these into specific Folders to maintain a clean workspace. Once inside a folder, you will see a list of all existing studies, their participant count, status, and modification history. How do I create a new study? To create a new study, click the + New Study button within your selected folder. A modal will appear where you can enter the following: Study name: Give your study a clear, identifiable title. Description: Provide context or internal notes about the research objectives. Click Save to initialize the study and begin adding content. How do I Copy, Move, or Delete a study? Management actions for studies are performed directly from the Studies list view. When you hover over a specific study row, three action icons will appear on the far right: Copy Study: Click the Copy icon to create a duplicate of the study. Move Study: Click the Folder icon to transfer the study to a different folder. Delete Study: Click the Trash icon to permanently remove the study and its associated data. Note: These actions are exclusively available via these hover icons and are not found within the study configuration settings.

title: Quests
kw: quest quests click banner participants logo between disclosure tasks study add title over region cover due date copy create manage standard diary managing structured set designed specific insights within types choose two one time needed each hover top upload icon
summary: Learn how to create and manage Quests in QuestionPro UX. Understand the difference between Standard and Diary quests, and how to configure disclosure and scheduling.
body: Quests Learn how to create and manage Quests in QuestionPro UX. Understand the difference between Standard and Diary quests, and how to configure disclosure and scheduling. Managing Quests What are Quests? A Quest is a structured set of tasks or questions designed to capture specific user insights within a study. Quests allow researchers to organize their research into digestible segments for participants. What types of Quests can I create? When you click + New Quest, you can choose between two types: Standard Quest: A one-time quest where participants engage in a structured sequence of tasks designed to explore specific insights and perspectives. Diary Quest: A recurring quest where participants complete a series of tasks, either at predefined intervals or on an as-needed basis. How do I add a Banner, Logo, and Title? You can customize the visual identity of each quest by hovering over the banner region: Title: Simply click on the title area in the banner to edit the quest name. Add Logo: Hover over the top-left region of the banner and click the Add Logo button to upload your brand icon. Change Cover: Hover over the top-right region of the banner and click Change cover to upload a custom background image. Suggested Dimensions: For optimal display, it is recommended to use a wide aspect ratio for the cover (approximately 1920x400 pixels) and a square, high-resolution file for the logo. What is Progressive Quest Disclosure? The Progressive quest disclosure toggle ensures that quests are revealed one at a time in the order you've defined. Each quest becomes visible to the participant only after the previous quest is completed. How do I set start and end dates for Quests? You can manage quest availability through the Quest settings panel: Start at & Due at: Define the window of activity; a quest remains active if the current date is between these two values. These can be edited later as needed. Stop accepting responses after due date: Enable this toggle to prevent further submissions once the deadline passes. Hide quests from participants after due date: This option hides the quest from view entirely. This is only enabled if you first choose to stop accepting responses. How do I Copy, Duplicate, or Delete a Quest? Click the three-dot menu icon on any individual Quest card to access the following management actions: Duplicate: Creates an identical copy of the quest within the same study. Copy to: Allows you to clone the quest into a different study. Delete: Permanently removes the quest and all associated data.

summary: Learn about Admin management in QuestionPro UX. Understand how to add team members and define permissions for Admin, Moderator, and Observer roles.
body: Access and control Learn about Admin management in QuestionPro UX. Understand how to add team members and define permissions for Admin, Moderator, and Observer roles. Admin Management and Roles Where do I find Admin Management settings? To manage your research team, open your study and navigate to the Admin section located at the bottom of the left-hand sidebar. This dashboard allows you to invite team members and configure study-wide observer permissions. How do I add a new team member? Within the Admin management dashboard, click the + Add admin user button. A modal will appear requiring the following information: Email: Enter the email address of the team member you wish to invite. Role: Select a role from the dropdown menu (Administrator, Moderator, or Observer) to define their level of access. Once the details are entered, click Add to grant them access to the study. What are the differences between roles? QuestionPro UX uses role-based access control to ensure study security and workflow efficiency. The table below outlines the specific permissions for each role: Feature Administrator Moderator Observer Workspace & Quest Setup Full Access No Access No Access Participant Management & Panel Full Access No Access No Access Moderation & Analytics Full Access Full Access View Only Media Library & Reports Full Access Full Access View Only Messages Full Access Full Access Conditional* Admin & System Logs Full Access No Access No Access *Observers can view and send participant messages only if the specific permission toggle is enabled in the Admin settings. What do the Admin configuration toggles do? On the right side of the Admin dashboard, you will find global settings that influence how Observers interact with the study data: Participants' messages visible to observers: When enabled, this allows team members with the Observer role to both read and send messages to participants. Quote saving for observers: This toggle allows Observers to highlight and save specific participant quotes within the "My Quotes" section for qualitative analysis. These settings are useful for keeping stakeholders informed and engaged without granting them full management privileges.

title: Study previews and study states
kw: study draft states preview mode participants published quest experience participant questionpro ux top right corner view live active via while state disabled cannot add new previews learn use manage my exactly appear click eye icon located dashboard allows navigate interface
summary: Learn how to use Preview mode to experience your study as a participant and manage Draft and Published study states in QuestionPro UX.
body: Study previews and study states Learn how to use Preview mode to experience your study as a participant and manage Draft and Published study states in QuestionPro UX. Preview and Study States How do I preview my study? To experience the study exactly as it will appear to your participants, click the Preview (eye) icon located in the top-right corner of the study dashboard. Preview mode allows you to navigate through the interface, view the study description, and interact with the quest list. This ensures that the branding, introductory video, and quest ordering are correctly configured before you go live. In this view, quests are organized into Active and Closed tabs, reflecting the scheduling settings you have defined. What is the difference between Draft and Published states? Every research project in QuestionPro UX exists in one of two states, which can be toggled via the dropdown menu in the top-right corner: Draft: This is the initial setup phase. While in Draft mode, you can edit all quest content and structure. Published: This state makes the study live and accessible to external respondents. What actions are restricted in Draft mode? Certain core management features are disabled while a study is in the Draft state to prevent incomplete data collection: Participant Recruitment: You cannot add or invite new participants until the study is Published. Panel Invitations: Inviting panelists from your existing database is also disabled in Draft mode. Note on Reverting to Draft: If you publish a study and add participants who are still in New or Pending status, you can revert the study to Draft. However, once reverted, those participants will be unable to sign in or open the study page via their links. Studies with Active participants who have already started responding cannot be moved back to Draft.
