summary: Learn about the UX Crowd feature in QuestionPro UX, a crowdsourced analysis tool for identifying the most important usability issues.
body: UX Crowd: Usability tests Learn about the UX Crowd feature in QuestionPro UX, a crowdsourced analysis tool for identifying the most important usability issues. UX Crowd Analysis What is the UX Crowd? The UX Crowd is a proprietary crowdsourced analysis tool in QuestionPro UX that helps you quickly identify and prioritize the most significant usability issues and positive features of your product. How does the UX Crowd work? After completing their tasks, participants are asked to list things they liked, things they disliked, and suggestions for improvement. These responses are then presented to subsequent participants, who vote on the feedback they agree with most. The result is a ranked list of insights, backed by the "crowd," allowing you to focus your development efforts on the areas that matter most to your users. This feature is typically available for studies with 10 or more participants.

title: Self-Reported Success (SRS)
kw: srs task success participant self reported test metric completion usability believes objective true questionpro ux gap perception actual performance successfully ensure comparing succeeded actually failure failed tasks learn about understand measure between captures whether completed unlike verified researcher measures own
summary: Learn about the Self-Reported Success (SRS) metric in QuestionPro UX. Understand how to measure the gap between participant perception and actual task performance.
body: Self-Reported Success (SRS) Learn about the Self-Reported Success (SRS) metric in QuestionPro UX. Understand how to measure the gap between participant perception and actual task performance. Self-Reported Success (SRS) What is the Self-Reported Success (SRS) metric? Self-Reported Success (SRS) is a usability metric in QuestionPro UX that captures whether a participant believes they successfully completed a task. Unlike objective task completion (which is verified by a researcher), SRS measures the participant's own perception of their performance. How is SRS captured during a test? After a participant finishes a specific task in a usability test, they are immediately asked a follow-up question: "Did you successfully complete this task?" with a simple "Yes" or "No" response option. This happens before any post-test surveys or psychometric questionnaires (like SUS or PSSUQ) to ensure the participant's memory of the task effort is fresh. Why is comparing SRS to actual completion important? The true value of the SRS metric lies in the Success Gap. By comparing the participant’s self-report against the moderator’s objective verification, you can identify three critical user states: True Success: The participant believes they succeeded, and they actually did. True Failure: The participant knows they failed the task. Hidden Failure: The participant believes they succeeded, but they actually failed. This often points to significant design flaws or misleading interface cues. You can view the SRS breakdown in the Analytics dashboard under the Task Completion tab. How do I enable SRS for my test? SRS is enabled by default for all Usability Tests involving tasks. During Step 3: Tasks of your test setup, ensure that the "Task Completion" toggle is active for each task where you want to collect both objective and perceived success data.

title: Participant Diaries and Study Analysis
kw: study participant analysis data view responses analytics insights all response specific overview tab completion aggregate level research participants ai powered thematic sentiment video features sidebar task card quests tasks timeline theme diaries analyze questionpro ux uncover trends individual qualitative my
summary: Analyze your QuestionPro UX data to uncover aggregate trends and individual-level insights from your longitudinal qualitative research studies.
body: Participant Diaries and Study Analysis Analyze your QuestionPro UX data to uncover aggregate trends and individual-level insights from your longitudinal qualitative research studies. Study Analysis How can I analyze my study data? Study Analytics allows you to examine your qualitative data at multiple levels. You can view aggregate insights across all participants to identify high-level trends or focus on individual participant journeys to understand their specific experiences. The dashboard includes response timelines, AI-powered thematic analysis, sentiment tracking, and word clouds to help you uncover meaningful insights from your research. Watch this video for a quick overview of the Study Analysis features in QuestionPro UX: Your browser does not support the video tag. Download Video Where do I access Study Analytics? To access your research data, open your study and click the Analytics tab in the left-hand sidebar. This opens the Analytics dashboard, which features two primary views: Overview for aggregate study insights and Responses for detailed, task-by-task data. What information is available in the Analytics Overview? The Overview tab provides study-level metrics through several key cards: Summary Card: Displays key metrics including total participants, completion rate, counts of standard and diary quests, total tasks, and days elapsed. Response Timeline: A visual representation of participant activity, which can be toggled between daily, weekly, or monthly views. Completion Progress: Shows the percentage of tasks and quests that are completed, in progress, or incomplete. Thematic Analysis: Features AI-powered theme extraction with integrated sentiment analysis across all study responses. How does the Response Timeline work? The Response Timeline helps you understand the temporal flow of your study. You can filter by specific tasks or view all responses to identify patterns in engagement and spot any significant surges or drop-offs in participant activity. How does AI-powered Thematic Analysis work? Using natural language processing, this feature extracts prominent themes from your study. Each theme card displays the theme title, a brief description, the percentage of responses it appears in, and a sentiment distribution (positive, neutral, or negative). How do I use the Responses tab? The Responses tab organizes all submissions by quest and task. Use the left-hand sidebar to navigate through your study structure and apply filters to drill down into specific participant segments or date ranges. How do I view insights for a single participant? Navigate to the Participant Diaries section in the Analytics sidebar. You can view your participants in a Card view (initials and completion rate) or a List view (names, emails, and login history). Clicking on a participant's profile provides a holistic view of their journey, including their personal Word Cloud, completion statistics, and a chronological feed of all their media and text responses.

title: Image analysis
kw: image analysis images visual questionpro ux people participant context photojournal search elements find task setting analyze ai quickly all automatically extract count photos tasks present across each environmental analytics specific uploaded hundreds insights identify patterns manual review video objects time
summary: Use Image Analysis in QuestionPro UX to automatically extract visual elements, setting, context, and people count from participant photos in photojournal tasks.
body: Image analysis Use Image Analysis in QuestionPro UX to automatically extract visual elements, setting, context, and people count from participant photos in photojournal tasks. Image Analysis – QuestionPro UX How can you analyze images in QuestionPro UX? In QuestionPro UX, you can automatically analyze participant-uploaded photos from photojournal tasks to extract visual elements, setting and context, and people count. The AI-powered analysis turns hundreds of images into searchable, structured insights—helping you identify patterns quickly without spending hours on manual review. Watch this video for a quick overview of how Image Analysis works in QuestionPro UX: Your browser does not support the video tag. Download Video What is Image Analysis in QuestionPro UX? Image Analysis uses AI to automatically analyze participant-uploaded photos in photojournal tasks. Instead of manually reviewing hundreds of images, you can extract structured insights about visual elements, setting and context, and people present—helping you identify patterns quickly and standardize your visual analysis across studies. The AI extracts three key categories from each image: Visual Elements (objects and scene descriptions), Setting & Context (time of day, location type, environmental cues), and People Present (count of individuals visible). All extracted data becomes searchable within your analytics, allowing you to find specific visual scenarios instantly. Where is Image Analysis available? Image Analysis is available for photojournal tasks in the Analytics view of your QuestionPro UX study. Navigate to Analytics, then expand any photojournal task to access the image analysis features. How do I analyze images in a photojournal task? Once you've opened a photojournal task in the Analytics view, look for the Analyze images button in the top-right corner of the task card. Click this button to trigger AI analysis for all images in that task. The analysis processes all participant-uploaded images automatically, extracting visual elements, environmental context, and people count from each photo. The button will be disabled until at least 5 new images are available for analysis. What information does Image Analysis extract? Once analysis is complete, expand any participant's response to view the extracted insights. Below each image, you'll find an Image Analysis section with three categories of information: Visual Elements: Objects and scenes detected in the image (e.g., "mountains," "city buildings," "park," "trees," "road," "race track," "marina," "baby," "computer mouse"). Setting & Context: Environmental details like time of day and location type (e.g., "evening," "outdoor," "clear," "daytime"). Some images may show "Couldn't analyse" if environmental cues are unclear. People Present: Number of individuals visible in the image (e.g., "no people present," "1," "2"). This helps you quickly filter images by whether they contain people and see exactly how many individuals appear

Image Analysis uses AI to automatically analyze participant-uploaded photos in photojournal tasks. Instead of manually reviewing hundreds of images, you can extract structured insights about visual elements, setting and context, and people present—helping you identify patterns quickly and standardize your visual analysis across studies. The AI extracts three key categories from each image: Visual Elements (objects and scene descriptions), Setting & Context (time of day, location type, environmental cues), and People Present (count of individuals visible). All extracted data becomes searchable within your analytics, allowing you to find specific visual scenarios instantly. Where is Image Analysis available? Image Analysis is available for photojournal tasks in the Analytics view of your QuestionPro UX study. Navigate to Analytics, then expand any photojournal task to access the image analysis features. How do I analyze images in a photojournal task? Once you've opened a photojournal task in the Analytics view, look for the Analyze images button in the top-right corner of the task card. Click this button to trigger AI analysis for all images in that task. The analysis processes all participant-uploaded images automatically, extracting visual elements, environmental context, and people count from each photo. The button will be disabled until at least 5 new images are available for analysis. What information does Image Analysis extract? Once analysis is complete, expand any participant's response to view the extracted insights. Below each image, you'll find an Image Analysis section with three categories of information: Visual Elements: Objects and scenes detected in the image (e.g., "mountains," "city buildings," "park," "trees," "road," "race track," "marina," "baby," "computer mouse"). Setting & Context: Environmental details like time of day and location type (e.g., "evening," "outdoor," "clear," "daytime"). Some images may show "Couldn't analyse" if environmental cues are unclear. People Present: Number of individuals visible in the image (e.g., "no people present," "1," "2"). This helps you quickly filter images by whether they contain people and see exactly how many individuals appear in each photo. How do I search for specific visual scenarios? Use the search bar at the top of the task card to find images with specific visual characteristics. The search function works on both participant text responses and AI-extracted image data, allowing you to quickly locate relevant visual evidence across all participants. For example, search for "hand" to find all images containing hands, or search for "outdoor" to find images taken outside. The search will return matching results from both the image analysis tags and participant written responses. What can I do with image analysis in QuestionPro UX? Image Analysis helps you: Find patterns quickly: Identify common visual themes across hundreds of participant photos without manual review. Segment by context: Filter images by usage scenario (solo vs. group, home vs. office, indoor vs. outdoor). Pull visual evidence: Search for specific objects, settings, or scenes to support findings in research presentations. Standardize analysis: Ensure consistent categorization across multiple researchers and studies. Save time: Reduce manual image review from 3–5 minutes per image to seconds.

title: Voting task analysis
kw: voting task agreement results participation levels statements statement metrics neutral data individual presentation analytics total proportional displays patterns rates respondents visual support context participants votes across all color scheme use visualizations questionpro ux view participant responses key overall top number
summary: Analyze voting task results with proportional visualizations, participation metrics, and neutral data presentation in your QuestionPro UX study.
body: Voting task analysis Analyze voting task results with proportional visualizations, participation metrics, and neutral data presentation in your QuestionPro UX study. Voting Task Analytics – QuestionPro UX How do I analyze voting task results? The voting task analytics view provides comprehensive insights into participant responses with proportional visualizations and key participation metrics. This interface displays voting patterns using neutral data presentation, allowing you to understand agreement levels and participation rates without bias. View total respondents, overall agreement rates, and individual statement performance through clear visual indicators that show the proportion of support for each voting item in your study. What do the summary metrics tell me? At the top of the analytics view, you'll find three key metrics that provide context for your voting results: Total Respondents - The number of participants who completed the voting task Total Votes - The aggregate number of individual votes cast across all statements Agreement Rate - The overall percentage showing how often participants agreed across all voting items These summary statistics help you quickly assess participation levels and general consensus patterns before diving into individual statement analysis. How do I interpret individual statement results? Each statement displays comprehensive voting data through multiple indicators: Vote count - Shows the number of participants who voted on this specific statement Visual proportion bar - Displays the ratio of agreement using a neutral blue color scheme Agreement percentage - The exact percentage of participants who agreed with the statement The proportional visualization makes it easy to compare support levels across different statements and identify patterns in participant responses. How can I organize the voting results? Use the sorting options in the top-right corner to organize your results based on different criteria: Most downvotes first - Shows statements with the lowest agreement rates at the top Default item order - Displays results in the original order they appeared in your voting task Most upvotes first - Prioritizes statements with the highest agreement rates These sorting options help you quickly identify consensus items, controversial statements, or maintain the original context of your voting sequence. How should I interpret the neutral color scheme? The analytics interface uses a neutral blue color scheme for all voting visualizations, avoiding traditional green/red associations that might imply positive or negative sentiment. This approach ensures: Unbiased data presentation that doesn't suggest value judgments Consistent visual treatment across all statements regardless of agreement levels Professional appearance suitable for stakeholder presentations The proportional bars show agreement levels as factual data points, allowing you to draw your own conclusions about what high or low agreement means in your

How do I assess participation quality? Monitor participation effectiveness by examining: Response consistency - Compare individual vote counts to total respondents to identify incomplete responses Engagement patterns - Look for statements with significantly different participation levels Overall completion rate - Use the total votes versus expected votes (respondents × statements) to gauge task completion Variations in individual statement participation can indicate comprehension issues, technical problems, or varying levels of participant engagement with specific content. How do I use these results for reporting? The neutral presentation and clear metrics make voting task results ideal for stakeholder communication: Reference specific agreement percentages for quantitative reporting Use the visual proportions to quickly communicate relative support levels Cite participation metrics to establish credibility and context Sort results to highlight key findings or maintain original task flow The unbiased color scheme and professional layout support objective data presentation in research reports and presentations.

title: Video tasks: Analysis
kw: video analysis participant questionpro ux responses key ai theme overview page see summary takeaways themes sentiments summaries automatically workspace sentiment quick find click topics individual response any transcript tab text save quote tasks use surface quotes videos quickly visually analyze
summary: Use QuestionPro UX’s Video Analysis to surface themes, sentiments, summaries, and quotes from participant videos—quickly and visually.
body: Video tasks: Analysis Use QuestionPro UX’s Video Analysis to surface themes, sentiments, summaries, and quotes from participant videos—quickly and visually. Video Analysis – QuestionPro UX How can you analyze Video responses in QuestionPro UX? In QuestionPro UX, you can automatically review participant video responses to uncover key themes, sentiments, and insights—helping you understand what people say and how they feel. The AI-powered workspace turns hours of footage into visual summaries and sentiment-based theme visuals, so you can focus on the "why" behind participant behavior rather than manual transcription or tagging. Watch this video for a quick overview of how Video Analysis works in QuestionPro UX: Your browser does not support the video tag. Download Video Where do I find Video Analysis? Select your study, then click the blue camera icon in the left sidebar. This opens the Video Analysis workspace. What’s on the Overview page? The Overview page gives you an at-a-glance readout of all video responses for the selected task: Theme bar-chart: See which topics are mentioned most and their sentiment colouring. AI summary panel: A concise paragraph capturing the collective story. Participant gallery: Clickable thumbnails that jump into individual responses. What do I see in an individual response? Click any thumbnail to open a participant’s page. Below the video you’ll find: Theme tags: Auto-detected topics (e.g., "Ease of cleaning"). Transcript tab: Timestamped, speaker-labelled verbatim text. Where are the key takeaways? Switch to Key Takeaways to see actionable bullet-points automatically generated from the participant’s response. Need a one-sentence recap? The Summary tab provides a tight, AI-written synopsis of that participant’s message—perfect for quick scans. How do I save a great quote? Highlight any text in Summary, Key Takeaways, or Transcript. Choose Save as quote from the pop-up menu to file it for reporting.

title: Create email invitation
kw: survey invitation email create click message specific invitations templates send re global template subject save new default use following more used surveys go workforce select tab customise edit sections name enter text html add images attach files media library ensure
summary: Create email invitation
body: Create email invitation Create email invitation Create email invitation To send a survey invitation by email, it's important to write a clear and engaging email message that asks your audience to participate in the survey. This message sets the tone and context for the survey, making it more likely that employees will respond. You can create two kinds of email invitation templates: Survey-specific invitations: These templates are made for one particular survey. They’re tailored to the content of that specific survey and can only be used for it. Global invitations: These templates are more general and can be used for any survey you create in your account. They’re visible across all your surveys and save time if you plan to send similar invitations for different surveys. Click to download video To create a new email invitation: Go to: Workforce » Select Survey » Send Click on the “Templates” tab. Here you would see three types of Invitations Survey Specific, SMS Specific and Global Invitations Under “Survey Specific Invitations” A default email template is provided for each survey. You can use the default template by clicking “Default” To customise your email invitation, click on '+ Create New Survey Invitation’. You can edit the following sections: Template name Subject: Subject for the email/survey invitation. Message: Enter the text for your invitation message. You can use HTML, add images, or attach files from the media library Ensure that the toggle button is switched to “Survey Specific” and when you're done editing, click Save." To Create a Global Email Invitation Go to: Workforce » Select Survey » Send Click on the “Templates” tab. To customise your email invitation, click on '+ Create New Survey Invitation’ You can edit the following sections: Template name Subject: Subject for the email / survey invitation. Message: Enter the text for your invitation message. You can use HTML, add images, or attach files from media library Ensure that the toggle button is switched to “Global” and when you're done editing, click Save." License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Deployment by groups
body: Deployment by groups Deployment by groups Deployment by groups Deployment by groups means sending a survey to specific groups of employees, like different departments or employee segments. This helps gather feedback from targeted employees and can provide more relevant and detailed insights for each group. Click to download video To Add a group: Go to: Login Workforce >> Select Survey >> Send >> Compose >> Add Group Set up Employee Filter pops up where you will see 2 options Create new Filter: To add a new specific group of targeted employees based on certain demographics, such as their team, location, or department. This helps you focus on particular groups for analysis or reporting. To create a new group, enter the criteria from the employee roaster that you want to filter for that group, then click 'Save. You can see that the system has created a separate group of 47 employees filtered from the employee roster. To deploy the survey invitation, just click on the 'Send' option. Use Existing filter Allows you to select and apply a group or filter that you set up earlier. This means you can reuse a filter you created before without having to set it up again. Click on “Add Group” and from the drop down select the “Use Existing” option. In the next drop-down menu, choose the pre-existing group where you want to send your survey invitation, then click "Save." You can see that the system has automatically selected the group of 17 employees that was previously created from the employee filter. To deploy the survey invitation, just click on the 'Send' option. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? title: Survey remainders
kw: survey reminders email sent send remainders sms employees delivery reminder haven yet click select deployment dropdown menu choose template time license follow emails completed usually designed encourage finish help increase response rates gently reminding input important typically few days initial
summary: Survey remainders
body: Survey remainders Survey remainders Survey remainders Survey reminders are follow-up emails/sms sent to employees who haven't completed a survey yet. These reminders are usually sent by email or sms and are designed to encourage employees to finish the survey. Reminders can help increase response rates by gently reminding employees that their input is important. They are typically sent a few days after the initial survey invitation and can be sent multiple times if needed. Click to download video To send survey reminders Go to: Login Workforce >> Select the survey >> Send >> Select the “Sent” option. You can find and send survey reminders to employees who haven't taken the survey yet under 'Deployment History' by clicking on 'Reminders. Delivery Mode: In the dropdown menu, you can choose how to send survey reminders. You have three options: Email, Email and SMS, SMS only Email Template: You can either use the default template provided by the system or customize your own email reminder by editing the text in the email message space. Delivery Time: Click the dropdown menu to choose between sending immediate survey reminders or scheduling the email delivery for a specific day and time and then press the “Send reminder” button. After you set up the survey reminder, you can see the reminders below the Deployment progress bar. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: SMS Surveys
body: SMS Surveys SMS Surveys SMS Surveys SMS surveys for workforce employees are quick and easy ways to collect feedback. Employees receive a text message with a survey link or question, allowing them to respond directly from their phones. It's a convenient way to gather opinions, check satisfaction, or get insights on company policies. Responses are fast and can help improve the workplace based on real-time feedback. Click to download video To send a survey through SMS: Go to: Workforce >> Select Survey >>Send>> Compose Switch the SMS toggle ON Enter the email addresses of the users you want to invite or use "Add Group" to send the invitation to a specific group or to everyone. Select the SMS invitation template and click on “Send” You can send email invites, SMS invites, or both by simply turning the right toggles ON or OFF. To upload employees with their phone numbers: Go to: Workforce >> Manage Employee Roster >> Import Click on Manual Employee Import Enter the employee's details like email address, first name, last name, and contact number in the given format and click on save. You can also import employees with their contact numbers using an Excel file by following these steps: Go to Workforce > Manage Employee Roster > Import. Click on Bulk Employee Import - Excel. Click on Download Import Template. Enter the employee's details, like email address, first name, last name, and contact number, in the provided format and save the file. Drag and drop or browse to select the Excel file from your system. Click on “Import Employees”. To create invitations templates for SMS: Go to: Send >> Templates >> Click on “+Create New SMS Invitation” Here, you can edit the “Template Title” and the “Invitation Message”. You can also add variables to fill in data automatically. After designing the invitation, click “Save”. summary: Sms pricing
body: Sms pricing Sms pricing Sms pricing QuestionPro SMS distribution enables you to reach your target audience via mobile devices and collect feedback on the go. The multiple factors that affect how many credits are used when sending SMS surveys will all be covered on this page. These include the recipient's country, the message length, and the characters used in the message. The characters you use in your SMS message can influence whether the message is split into multiple messages. The character encoding determines this. Encoding is classified into two types: GSM-7 and UCS-2. The default encoding standard is GSM-7, which includes the majority of commonly used characters. The characters that are part of the GSM-7 set can be found here. A message encoded with GSM-7 can have a maximum of 160 characters. If your message contains a non-GSM character, the entire message will be encoded using UCS-2. A UCS-2 message can have a maximum of 70 characters. Points to consider: The survey link within the message will count against your character limit. If your message has more than 160 GSM-7 or 70 UCS-2 characters, it will be sent in multiple messages. Each message can contain up to 153 GSM-7 or 67 UCS-2 characters and will cost credits. Note: SMS prices may change from time to time without prior notice. SMS price may take up to 24 hours to reflect in the SMS logs. title: Slack survey distribution

summary: Slack survey distribution
body: Slack survey distribution Slack survey distribution Slack survey distribution Through this feature admin can send out surveys through Slack direct messages. Reminders can also be scheduled to be sent to workers who have not yet completed the survey. The key benefit of this feature is to increase participation and response rates. To Distribute survey through Slack, go to: Workforce >> Manage Employee Roaster >> Integrations and in the search option Search for “Slack” Select “Slack Message” Enter your workspace's Slack URL. You can find the URL in the top left corner of the Slack messaging app. Click on “Continue“ Click on “Allow” The Slack messaging integration is now active and ready to use Select the survey you want to send through Slack messages and click the "Send" option. In the Compose section, enter employee emails for the Slack survey invite. For larger groups, click "Add Group" and use Employee Filters to target specific demographics. Once the employee email addresses are added, enable the “Slack” button. In the Default Template dropdown menu, you can either create a new invitation template or select the default template option. Click on “Send” to send the invitation via Slack messages. Employees will receive the survey invitation via slack messages as displayed Click on "Send Later" if you want to schedule the deployment for a future date and time. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Collectors and Anonymous Surveys
body: Collector forms Collectors and Anonymous Surveys Collectors and Anonymous Surveys The collector ensures the confidentiality of mandatory demographic data while providing an option for employees to voluntarily share additional information to enhance organizational insights. Upon successful roster inclusion, the collector seamlessly generates a unique survey link tailored to the employee, fostering a personalized engagement with the feedback process. Post-survey completion, the collected data is automatically synchronized and integrated into the corresponding member's profile, streamlining comprehensive record-keeping and analysis. Click to download video Click on send and choose “ collector forms” under it If you are visiting collectors for the first time then you see the below screen and you can click on “New collector form” to create create new collector. You have to Enter the collector name Status (by default it is active), Only surveys in the "active" stage will be collected. User can switch from active to inactive and vice versa Verify employee identity OFF: No personal identification: Employee ID and email address will not be collected. Demographic data: You can still collect demographic information using custom fields. Verify employee identity ON: Personal identification: At least one or more of the following is mandatory and both will be collected from the user: Employee ID Email address You can select all the custom field data you want to collect from the user and if you mark it as required then it will be mandatory for the user to fill that field Then click on create On the dashboard screen, you will see all the created collectors with date modified, date created, status and responses Users can perform other actions Copy the link by clicking on the “Copy link” option Download or print the QR code by clicking on the” QR code” option Edit option to edit any setting of the collector Delete option to delete the collector When the user clicks on the copied link or QR code the user will see all the fields that are asked Once the user enters the asked details, all the employee details will be added to the employee roster and followed by the survey We have the below use cases covered If the employee details are missing in the employee roaster, the admin can collect personal and demographic details followed by a survey using collector forms If the admin wants to do an anonymous survey along with demographic details If the admin has only limited details or the email ID of the employee then collector forms can be used to collect the required custom fields Update To share surveys using a standard URL and validate employee identities before they take survey Enable Validate employee toggle If the main folder is created with employee ID mandatory then employee ID and Email address will be the options to select for validation When the toggle is on and user selected employee id to validate,if the user enters a valid employee id , user is sent to takesurv
content:
Collector forms

Collectors and Anonymous Surveys

Collectors and Anonymous Surveys The collector ensures the confidentiality of mandatory demographic data while providing an option for employees to voluntarily share additional information to enhance organizational insights. Upon successful roster inclusion, the collector seamlessly generates a unique survey link tailored to the employee, fostering a personalized engagement with the feedback process. Post-survey completion, the collected data is automatically synchronized and integrated into the corresponding member's profile, streamlining comprehensive record-keeping and analysis. Click to download video Click on send and choose “ collector forms” under it If you are visiting collectors for the first time then you see the below screen and you can click on “New collector form” to create create new collector. You have to Enter the collector name Status (by default it is active), Only surveys in the "active" stage will be collected. User can switch from active to inactive and vice versa Verify employee identity OFF: No personal identification: Employee ID and email address will not be collected. Demographic data: You can still collect demographic information using custom fields. Verify employee identity ON: Personal identification: At least one or more of the following is mandatory and both will be collected from the user: Employee ID Email address You can select all the custom field data you want to collect from the user and if you mark it as required then it will be mandatory for the user to fill that field Then click on create On the dashboard screen, you will see all the created collectors with date modified, date created, status and responses Users can perform other actions Copy the link by clicking on the “Copy link” option Download or print the QR code by clicking on the” QR code” option Edit option to edit any setting of the collector Delete option to delete the collector When the user clicks on the copied link or QR code the user will see all the fields that are asked Once the user enters the asked details, all the employee details will be added to the employee roster and followed by the survey We have the below use cases covered If the employee details are missing in the employee roaster, the admin can collect personal and demographic details followed by a survey using collector forms If the admin wants to do an anonymous survey along with demographic details If the admin has only limited details or the email ID of the employee then collector forms can be used to collect the required custom fields Update To share surveys using a standard URL and validate employee identities before they take survey Enable Validate employee toggle If the main folder is created with employee ID mandatory then employee ID and Email address will be the options to select for validation When the toggle is on and user selected employee id to validate,if the user enters a valid employee id , user is sent to takesurvey. If the user enters invalid employee id , then an error message“ Enter valid employee id” is shown. You can add number of responses expected for that collector which is editable later Feature Walkthrough: Click to view License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? summary: Add widgets in a dashboard
body: Add widgets Add widgets in a dashboard Add widgets in a dashboard Once you have created a dashboard, the next step is to add widgets. You can create multiple tabs in a dashboard and each tab can have its own set of widgets independently. You can add widgets like Scorecard, response rate, heatmap, and, many more in a tab based on the analysis required. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I add a Widget inside a dashboard in the EX Portal? To create a widget, login to the EX portal >> Dashboards page >> Select a dashboard >> Select a tab >> Add Widget. After selecting a widget from the widget options lets say scorecard, enter the mandatory details step by step to create a widget. Once the information is filled, click on the Create button, to add the widget. To add more criteria to the widget, you can click on the three dots kebab menu in the widget and choose "Edit Widget." On the edit sidebar, you can perform a lot of actions to update the widget. You can add or change benchmark, enable or disable the Company overall toggle, change the analytics type, choose decimal place upto 2, show/hide markers and building blocks, change the chart colors, and many more settings based on the widget type. You can edit, duplicate, delete, or apply filters to the widget, by going to the three dots (kebab menu) at the top right of the widget. title: Survey Comparison
kw: comparison survey data side across once historical set select dashboard questionpro workforce cross multiple lifecycle surveys track trend metrics deltas compare employee sentiment over mathematically evaluate video click analyze structural waves add optional global means go matching variables list widget
summary: Learn how to configure Survey Comparisons in QuestionPro Workforce to cross-reference multiple historical, pulse, or lifecycle surveys side-by-side to track trend line metrics and cultural deltas.
body: Survey Comparison Learn how to configure Survey Comparisons in QuestionPro Workforce to cross-reference multiple historical, pulse, or lifecycle surveys side-by-side to track trend line metrics and cultural deltas. Survey Comparison Survey Comparison acts as an analytical bridge across your historical timeline, enabling you to cross-examine and compare data across multiple employee lifecycle deployments side-by-side. Instead of assessing engagement data in isolated silos, this multi-study vector framework allows leadership teams to track critical metric shifts, isolate sentiment trajectories over consecutive fiscal cycles, and mathematically evaluate the concrete impacts of localized action-planning initiatives over time. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I set up and analyze a Survey Comparison? To evaluate structural data fluctuations and correlate metrics between separate survey waves, log in to QuestionPro Workforce and follow these steps: After logging into the EX Portal, navigate to the Survey Comparison option in the navigation sidebar. Once inside, click on New Comparison button situated on the top of the page. Set a comparison name, select the base survey as the data source, add label (optional) and set it global (optional). Setting a comparison as global means that anyone who has access to creating a dashboard can use this comparison. Once done, go into the comparison and start selecting your historical or complementary Comparison Survey models. You can compare a maximum of 4 surveys at once. Important Matching Rule: While the titles and structural indices of variables may differ slightly across annual waves, mapping exact semantic matches manually ensures that your long-term trend calculations remain mathematically sound. Map the specific questions across your chosen studies by matching the corresponding data variables from the interactive list interfaces. Once the links are mapped securely, go to the dashboard and setup the widget. In the dashboard, select the Survey Comparison widget in the add widgets list, and select the information as needed. This helps you analyze the newly rendered graphical interface matrix, which dynamically isolates standard favorability scores, index means, and positive vs. negative variance columns highlighting your overall sentiment deltas. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

body: Heatmap Learn how to create, configure, and customize Workforce Heatmap Analysis widgets within your Employee Experience dashboards, including advanced column layouts like Split, Merged, and Preset configurations. Heatmap Analysis Heatmap widgets offer a comprehensive, industry-grade visual representation of performance indices across your organization. They excel at showcasing demographic comparisons, highlighting macro-level sentiment high and low points, and illustrating how different custom field variables perform against core engagement items. By mapping complex data metrics onto a color-coded matrix, leadership can instantly identify operational highlights and areas requiring targeted action planning. How do I create and add a Heatmap widget to my dashboard? To create a heatmap, log in to your Employee Experience account and execute the following steps: Navigate to the EX Portal, and select an existing reporting dashboard or create a new one. Within your active dashboards, navigate to the needed tab and click on Add Widget. From the available widgets menu, select Heat map to launch the creation flow. Step 1: Widget Parameters and Display Analytics Provide an explicit widget Title and include an optional Description. Under the core configuration settings, select your preferred analytics display options, Mean, or Favourability calculation profiles. Step 2: Data Source Mapping Select the source Survey, along with the specific historical or active Deployments and Questions . Step 3: Column Structural Architecture Define how your matrix columns are to be generated. The dashboard offers three advanced column structures to handle custom field cross-tabulations: Split Column: Automatically splits and generates distinct, isolated reporting columns for each specific custom field value combination. This allows for multiple custom fields to be structurally evaluated side by side simultaneously. Merged Column: Consolidates data fields by blending filters across completely different custom fields, aggregating the target parameters into a single unified column view. Preset Column: (Currently in Beta Mode) Allows administrators to create pre-defined column layout standards and intentionally assign them to specific stakeholders or downstream managers when securely sharing dashboard access. Click on Add Widget to render the visualization. If you need to void the operation, click Back. Important Note: If you need to adjust column configurations later, the edit workflow replicates this exact logical sequence to preserve user-experience efficiency. content:
Heatmap

Learn how to create, configure, and customize Workforce Heatmap Analysis widgets within your Employee Experience dashboards, including advanced column layouts like Split, Merged, and Preset configurations.

Heatmap Analysis Heatmap widgets offer a comprehensive, industry-grade visual representation of performance indices across your organization. They excel at showcasing demographic comparisons, highlighting macro-level sentiment high and low points, and illustrating how different custom field variables perform against core engagement items. By mapping complex data metrics onto a color-coded matrix, leadership can instantly identify operational highlights and areas requiring targeted action planning. How do I create and add a Heatmap widget to my dashboard? To create a heatmap, log in to your Employee Experience account and execute the following steps: Navigate to the EX Portal, and select an existing reporting dashboard or create a new one. Within your active dashboards, navigate to the needed tab and click on Add Widget. From the available widgets menu, select Heat map to launch the creation flow. Step 1: Widget Parameters and Display Analytics Provide an explicit widget Title and include an optional Description. Under the core configuration settings, select your preferred analytics display options, Mean, or Favourability calculation profiles. Step 2: Data Source Mapping Select the source Survey, along with the specific historical or active Deployments and Questions . Step 3: Column Structural Architecture Define how your matrix columns are to be generated. The dashboard offers three advanced column structures to handle custom field cross-tabulations: Split Column: Automatically splits and generates distinct, isolated reporting columns for each specific custom field value combination. This allows for multiple custom fields to be structurally evaluated side by side simultaneously. Merged Column: Consolidates data fields by blending filters across completely different custom fields, aggregating the target parameters into a single unified column view. Preset Column: (Currently in Beta Mode) Allows administrators to create pre-defined column layout standards and intentionally assign them to specific stakeholders or downstream managers when securely sharing dashboard access. Click on Add Widget to render the visualization. If you need to void the operation, click Back. Important Note: If you need to adjust column configurations later, the edit workflow replicates this exact logical sequence to preserve user-experience efficiency. How do I customize, filter, and manage an active Heatmap widget? Once generated, a heatmap widget can be customized or maintained to modify display thresholds or comparative baselines: To update criteria, change column types, or switch analytical calculations, click on the three-dot kebab menu icon in the upper-right corner of the widget frame and select Edit Widget. The formatting inspector panel provides access to the following presentation controls: Add or Change Benchmark: Compare real-time data against historical corporate baselines or target industry indices. Company Overall Toggle: Enable or disable an aggregated institutional overview benchmark line item. Analytics Aggregation Type: Instantly change the calculation algorithm between score Mean, Decimal precision modes, or Favorability models. N/A Value Visibility: Toggle to display or conceal items returning null, zero-respondent, or non-applicable entries. Column Visibility Filters: Dynamically pick, choose, isolate, or mask distinct columns within your active matrix. UI Toggle Frameworks: Choose whether to display or hide behavioral markers and structural building-block indicators. Color Palette Matrix: Personalize standard visualization gradients using divergent or sequential company-approved spectrum themes. Asset Replication, Maintenance, and Exports: Through the secondary management node in the kebab menu, you can duplicate the widget structure to build an alternate column view, execute full widget deletion, or perform high-fidelity exports. Selecting the Export profile lets you extract the heatmap data as an operational Microsoft Excel spreadsheet file or a print-ready PDF vector document. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Anonymity Settings

summary: Confidentiality Settings
body: Anonymity Settings Confidentiality Settings Confidentiality Settings The Confidentiality Settings is a setting that ensures individual responses remain anonymous in reports and data analysis. It sets a minimum number of responses needed before showing any data for a specific group or segment. If the number of responses is below this threshold, the data won't be displayed to protect the privacy of the employees in that group. This helps ensure that no one can figure out who said what based on the data. Click to download video How to set confidentiality threshold: Go to: Workforce » Select Survey » Click on the kebab menu (three dots) and select “Settings” Define the minimum number of responses needed, then save your settings. Once the settings are saved When the users/ managers log in to the analytics portal to check the results under "Widgets Data." They will see a confidentiality icon if the response threshold is not met, and when they hover over it, a notification will appear saying, "There is not enough data to meet the confidentiality threshold. Admins can also apply an Admin Anonymity threshold After the confidentiality settings are saved Go to Employee roaster >> portal >> Setup and toggle ‘ON’ Admin Anonymity threshold. When the admin logs into the portal to check the results under "Widgets Data." They will see a confidentiality icon if the response threshold is not met, and when they hover over it, a notification will appear saying, "There is not enough data to meet the confidentiality threshold. Similarly, if the entire dataset does not meet the threshold, then the pop-up shown to the user/admins will be like the one in the image below. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: eNPS in Workforce Analytics Portal
kw: enps widget analytics portal add workforce select chart 100 promoters detractors score click employee edit ability data more see set department also experience dashboard type created segments user criteria distribution color coding neutrals greater than 30 license revamp provides great
summary: eNPS in Workforce Analytics Portal
body: eNPS in Workforce Analytics Portal eNPS in Workforce Analytics Portal eNPS in Workforce Analytics Portal The eNPS widget revamp provides a great amount of additional functionality and ability to view data in more detail. For instance, see the eNPS by any filters you have set up, such as department, location, tenure and more. See the filtered scores broken down into promoters, passives and detractors, while also being able to benchmark them to the overall score. Here's a glimpse of how it works Click to download video How to add eNPS widget: Go to: Analytics Portal » Employee Experience » Create a Dashboard. Click on Add a widget and select eNPS. In pop-up, add widget title, select the chart type, select survey and deployments. Click on Add Widget. Your eNPS widget is created on the dashboard. In kebab option, select Edit Widget. Users can edit the widget, change the chart type and also add segments on the eNPS. Segments allow the user to analyse the data as per the set criteria, which are the custom fields from employee roster. It can be department, region, gender and so on. In the edit widget, user has the ability to hide the distribution chart. The eNPS widget can be created in guage, donut and distribution chart types. The eNPS widget can be downloaded in PDF and Excel format. You can define the eNPS score ranges according to your own criteria for what is considered a good, average, or poor final score. eNPS widget coloring Range of eNPS can be -100 - 100. Color coding is divided into promoters, neutrals and detractors. This is the standard color coding that is followed for NPS RED/DETRACTORS: -100 to 0 YELLOW/NEUTRALS: greater than 0 - 30 GREEN/PROMOTERS: greater than 30-100 License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

body: Text Analysis Widget Text Analysis Widget in Workforce Analytics Portal Text Analysis Widget in Workforce Analytics Portal Text analysis is an analytical feature allowing tagging and categorization of text into reports used to quantify open-ended text responses into common themes and patterns for an in-depth review of what respondents want you to know in their own words. How do I utilize text analysis capabilities for my workforce surveys? To set up text analysis, user logged in to their portal dashboard can follow the below steps, Your browser does not support the video tag. Click on: Add widget and select Text Analysis. Select applicable details for the fields, Title Description Survey Deployment Questions Text analysis works with open-ended questions, as it excels in deciphering the nuanced and diverse responses inherent in such queries. It effectively organizes the unstructured data from open-ended questions into actionable insights, enabling users to glean valuable information and trends from the responses received. Clicking on a keyword title within the widget, presents a pop-up that will help you locate the specific instances of a keyword and where it appeared among the responses submitted. To check out similar keyword that have been categorized together in the analysis, click on Keywords on the pop-up. summary: Response-rate
body: Response rate Response-rate Response rate A Response Rate widget is an interactive feature on a dashboard that shows the percentage of people who have responded to a survey or questionnaire. It gives you a clear, easy-to-understand overview of how many participants have taken part in the data collection process. This helps you quickly assess the level of engagement and participation in your survey. Click to download video How to use Response Rate analysis capabilities in workforce surveys: To set up Response rate analysis, admin users can log in to their portal dashboard and follow these steps: Click on "Add Widget" in the Portal. Select the “Response Rate” widget In the pop-up, enter the widget's title and description, along with the survey, deployment details, custom fields, values, and criteria and then Add Widget. To edit, duplicate, delete, or export the widget, click on the three dots (kebab menu) Users can download the widget as an Excel file by selecting the export option To add more criteria in the widget, users can click on the three dots (kebab menu) in the widget and choose "Edit Widget." In the Criteria section, you can add more criteria based on what you need. For example, we chose Location as the source and wanted to see which Departments” have completed or are still pending. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

A widget is created using the selected question, and it shows the count of responses for each answer option To edit, duplicate, delete, or filter the widget, click on the three dots (kebab menu) Once the widget is created, you can download the data as a PDF or Excel report. In the "Edit Widget" option, you can make changes to the existing widget. Bulk Add: Single Question Widget Users can select multiple questions to create a single question widget in bulk on the analytics portal. In the "Add Widget" pop-up, multi-select the questions you want to include in the widget. Then click on “Add Widget” Single-question widgets are created in bulk based on the selected questions. summary: Heatmap-default-update
body: Heatmap default Heatmap-default-update Heatmap default The heatmap color gradient visually represents your data, making it easier to identify trends and patterns. The color scale used will vary based on your dashboard's theme. The intensity of the color within each range is determined by the actual score. Below are the calculations and ranges for heatmap default: Favorability percentage How to access this: Create widget >> Heatmap >> Analytics >> select favourability% >> %score(Toggle on the heatmap) Dark Green: Scores between 93% and 100% Medium Green: Scores between 84% and 92% Light green: Scores between 76% and 83% Yellow : Scores between 51% and 75% Orange: Scores between 34% and 50% Medium Red: Scores between 18% and 33% Dark Red: Scores between 0% and 17% Favorability delta How to access this: Create widget >> Heatmap >> Analytics >> select favourability% >> Delta(Toggle on the heatmap) Dark Orange: Greater than +15.5% Medium Orange: Between +11% and +15% Light Orange: Between +6% and +10% Light Gray: Between -5% and +5% Light Purple: Between -6% and -10% Medium Purple: Between -11% and -15% Dark Purple: Less than -15.5% Actual Mean How to access this: Create widget >> Heatmap >> Analytics >> select Mean >> %score(Toogle on the heatmap) Dark Green: Mean score 4.6 and above Medium Green: Mean score between 4.1 and 4.5 Light Green: Mean score between 3.6 and 4.0 Yellow: Mean score between 3.1 to 3.5 Orange: Mean score between 2.6 to 3.0 Medium Red: Mean score between 2.1 to 2.5 Dark Red: Mean score below 2.0 and below Delta Mean How to access this: Create widget >> Heatmap >> Analytics >> select Mean >> Delta(Toggle on the heatmap) Dark Orange: Greater than +1.6 Medium Orange: Between +1.1 and +1.5 Light Orange: Between +0.6 and +1.0 Light Grey: Between -0.5 and +0.5 Light Purple: Between -0.6 and -1.0 Medium Purple: Between -1.1 and -1.5 Dark Purple: Less than -1.6 License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Sentiment analysis
kw: sentiment widget analysis theme themes responses based each not create specific response overall edit menu icon manager team click under distribution assigned one more total number positive negative concepts comment count match multiple any other duplicate delete user really difficult
summary: Sentiment analysis
body: Sentiment analysis Sentiment analysis Sentiment analysis Create Sentiment Analysis widget : Click on Create New Widget> Text analysis Enter the Title and description (optional) Enable/Disable: A toggle switch will control the visibility of sentiment analysis results. Select the respective survey, deployment and question under the source Click on Create widget Key themes distribution Responses will be categorized by theme and assigned a sentiment based on that specific theme's context. A response will be assigned to one or more relevant themes based on its content. The total number of responses for each theme will be shown, along with positive, negative, and neutral labels for each theme. Specific concepts within each theme will be identified to provide more transparency and rich analysis behind the comment categorization. Overall Sentiment Total Count will likely not match with Themes Count: As Overall Sentiment is based on the sentiment of the whole response, but the themes are based on concepts tied to that specific theme, and multiple themes can appear in one comment. It’s possible for a single response to have multiple themes or not have any theme at all. Responses that do not match any defined theme will not be included in the theme distribution. Other actions Edit Widget - For editing the sentiment analysis widget - Menu Icon on the widget > Edit widget Duplicate widget- For duplicating the sentiment analysis widget - Menu Icon on the widget > Duplicate widget Delete widget- For deleting the sentiment analysis widget - Menu Icon on the widget > Delete widget User has the ability to choose if you want to see the raw number of responses in a widget or % under edit option Here is an example for user statement: My manager is really difficult but I’m lucky that I’m a part of a great team and we help each other through everything. Output :-> Overall Sentiment - Mixed Concept -> Opinion, Sentiment - Theme manager -> really difficult, negative - Manager/Leader team -> great positive - Team Note: Please make sure you have set sentiment industry as "Workforce" License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Scorecard

summary: Scorecard
body: Scorecard Scorecard Scorecard The Scorecard widget in the EX Portal visualizes key metrics from user-defined markers, building blocks, and questions, showing favorable, neutral, and unfavorable responses as percentages or mean scores. Perfect for tracking engagement drivers like Leadership, Manager effectiveness, or Growth, it lets you compare performance across areas at a glance and spot priorities needing action. Add it to dashboards for executives or HR to provide a clear KPI overview, with drill-downs available for details. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I create a Scorecard widget in the EX Portal? To create a scorecard, login to the EX portal >> Manage Dashboards page >> Select a dashboard >> Add Widget » Scorecard. After selecting Scorecard from the widget options, enter the below details to create a scorecard: Title: This is the name of the widget, and is a mandatory field. Description: This is an optional field. After this, select the preferred analytics type, Mean or Favorability from the analytics dropdown. After you have filled the textual information and selected the analytics, it's time to set a data source for your widget. Select the survey you want to pull the data from, then choose the deployments as needed, and then select the questions you want to analyse. Once the information is filled, click on the Add Widget button, to create the widget. You will see key metrics, respondent count, split between favourable, neutral and unfavourable, mean/favourability score. To add more criteria to the widget, you can click on the three dots kebab menu in the widget and choose "Edit Widget." On the edit sidebar, you can perform a lot of actions to update the widget. You can add or change benchmark, enable or disable the Company overall toggle, change the analytics type, choose decimal place upto 2, choose to hide or show between favourable, neutral and unfavourable, show/hide columns, show/hide markers and building blocks, change the chart colors, and many more. You can edit, duplicate, delete, download or filter the widget, by going to the three dots (kebab menu) at the top right of the widget. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Driver analysis
body: Driver analysis Driver analysis Driver analysis Driver analysis in workforce employee surveys helps identify which factors have the biggest impact on employee satisfaction and performance. The purpose of driver analysis is to find the correlation between two variables, which can be markers, building blocks, or independent questions within the survey. By analysing these correlations, organisations can determine what matters most to employees, like management support or work-life balance, and focus on improving these areas to boost overall engagement and productivity. Click to download video How to Set up Driver analysis in the dashboard: To set up Driver Analysis users can navigate from the survey >> analytics >> Employee experience Click “Add Widget” Select Driver Analysis Widget In the widget pop-up, add a title, select the survey, and choose the questions. In the columns, pick the “Drivers” to find which ones have the strongest correlations affecting each other in the study. Then click on “Add widget” The Driver Analysis widget on the Dashboard displays survey questions in rows on the left, categorised into markers and building blocks. On the right, columns show the drivers you want to analyse to find the highest correlations with those survey questions. Expanding a “Marker” shows how the broader idea is broken down into detailed parts (Building Blocks), leading to the exact survey questions When you expand the building blocks, you’ll see the specific questions that were asked to collect the data. Scoring under driver analysis Scores under the driver reflect how strongly employees agree or disagree with each question related to that driver. Each score shows the level of agreement with statements about different aspects of the onboarding process. In QuestionPro “Lower” scores indicate more positive responses, while “Higher “scores suggest areas needing improvement. Analysing these scores helps identify which parts of the onboarding process are most effective and which need enhancement. How to Use Driver Analysis in Workforce Studies For example: Understanding the Key Factors Influencing Employee Engagement and Satisfaction During Onboarding This topic helps you see how different stages of onboarding affect employee satisfaction, engagement, and long-term commitment. By analysing responses from these stages, you can identify what drives negative outcomes. As I want to know key drivers impacting my employee experience from day one to the first 100 days. content:
Driver analysis

Driver analysis

Driver analysis Driver analysis in workforce employee surveys helps identify which factors have the biggest impact on employee satisfaction and performance. The purpose of driver analysis is to find the correlation between two variables, which can be markers, building blocks, or independent questions within the survey. By analysing these correlations, organisations can determine what matters most to employees, like management support or work-life balance, and focus on improving these areas to boost overall engagement and productivity. Click to download video How to Set up Driver analysis in the dashboard: To set up Driver Analysis users can navigate from the survey >> analytics >> Employee experience Click “Add Widget” Select Driver Analysis Widget In the widget pop-up, add a title, select the survey, and choose the questions. In the columns, pick the “Drivers” to find which ones have the strongest correlations affecting each other in the study. Then click on “Add widget” The Driver Analysis widget on the Dashboard displays survey questions in rows on the left, categorised into markers and building blocks. On the right, columns show the drivers you want to analyse to find the highest correlations with those survey questions. Expanding a “Marker” shows how the broader idea is broken down into detailed parts (Building Blocks), leading to the exact survey questions When you expand the building blocks, you’ll see the specific questions that were asked to collect the data. Scoring under driver analysis Scores under the driver reflect how strongly employees agree or disagree with each question related to that driver. Each score shows the level of agreement with statements about different aspects of the onboarding process. In QuestionPro “Lower” scores indicate more positive responses, while “Higher “scores suggest areas needing improvement. Analysing these scores helps identify which parts of the onboarding process are most effective and which need enhancement. How to Use Driver Analysis in Workforce Studies For example: Understanding the Key Factors Influencing Employee Engagement and Satisfaction During Onboarding This topic helps you see how different stages of onboarding affect employee satisfaction, engagement, and long-term commitment. By analysing responses from these stages, you can identify what drives negative outcomes. As I want to know key drivers impacting my employee experience from day one to the first 100 days. Main Drivers are: Day One First 100 Days Driver “Day One” Study Under Driver 'Day One,' we see that the highest score of (0.85) correlates with the 'Marker' on the right, which is “First 100 Days”. Clicking the dropdown next to 'First 100' reveals detailed information of the building block”. The highest score of (0.76) links to the “Integration” block. “Day One” Outcome By expanding the Building Block 'Integration,' we identify the exact survey question that correlates with the highest score of (0.66): 'People went out of their way to make me feel part of the team.' This score indicates that employees disagreed with this statement, suggesting they felt people at the company did not make them feel welcomed into the team. Since “Day One” Driver “First 100 days” Study (Survey Sent to same employees after the 1st 100 days of their joining.) Let’s assume the company chose not to act on the findings and decided to let things continue as they were. Under Driver 'First 100 Days' we see that the highest score of (0.85) correlates with the 'Marker' on the right, which is “Engagement” Clicking the dropdown next to “Engagement” reveals details of the building block. The highest score of (0.77) links to the “Sentiment” block. “First 100 days” Outcome: By expanding the Building Block “Sentiment”, we identify the exact survey question that correlates with the highest score of 0.73 “I’m satisfied with the on boarding process” This score indicates that employees disagreed with this statement, suggesting they felt dissatisfied with the onboarding process which directly relates to the Day one experience. Summary of Driver Analysis Outcomes: The driver analysis shows that employees felt unwelcome on their first day, leading to dissatisfaction with the entire onboarding process in their first 100 days. This suggests that a negative Day One experience affects how employees view their onboarding overall. Employers should focus on improving these areas to enhance onboarding and employee satisfaction. How can you use Driver Analysis in workforce studies to identify the key factors that affect employee Net Promoter Scores (eNPS)? Click on the kebab menu (Three-dots) >> Select “Edit Widget” Add a title. Select the survey. Choose the deployments. In the “Items” section, select all questions except the eNPS questions. In the Columns section, add only the eNPS question from the dropdown. Here the eNPS result shows us the highest score of (0.08) that correlates with the 'Marker' on the right, which is “Miscellaneous”. Click the dropdown next to the “Miscellaneous” marker to see detailed information about the building block. The highest score of (0.1) is linked to the “Familiarity” block on the right. Expanding the "Familiarity" Building Block reveals the survey question with the highest score of (0.09): "Our CEO knows who I am." This score shows employees disagreed with the statement, suggesting they felt the CEO did not know them. With these results, we now understand the key factor affecting the company’s eNPS scores. To edit, duplicate, delete, or filter the widget, click on the three dots (kebab menu) Users can export in PDF or Excel format select Export and the choose Export Excel or PDF Note: Driver analysis widget supports only matrix questions for analysis License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Scorecard: All options view
kw: view options scorecard all widget toggle switch category scale original click edit favorability data feature questions seven default quick more feedback disabled only available video sidebar off license lets between simplified unfavourable neutral favourable fewer response shown insights explore detailed
summary: Scorecard: All Options View
body: Scorecard: All options view Scorecard: All Options View Scorecard: All Options View This feature lets you switch between a simplified 3‑category view (Unfavourable, Neutral, Favourable) and the original options view in the Scorecard widget for questions with seven or fewer response options. By default, the 3‑category view is shown for quick insights, but you can toggle to the original scale to explore more detailed, nuanced feedback. For questions with more than seven options, the toggle is disabled to maintain clarity, and only the 3‑category view is available. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I switch to the All Options View in the scorecard widget? When creating a scorecard in your dashboard, the default view would be the 3-category view. To switch to the All Options View, follow the below steps: Click on the Kebab menu (the three dots) at the top of the widget and click on Edit Widget . On clicking Edit Widget, the editing sidebar will open up allowing you to edit the widget as per your needs. Scroll down the sidebar and locate the Favorability Scale toggle in the Favorability section. To switch to the All Options View , turn the Favorability Scale toggle OFF. You will see that once the toggle is switched OFF, the scorecard now displays the data in the original options scale. Important Note: On switching to the All Options View, the scorecard will display the data only at Question level, the Markers and Building blocks will be disabled. This is done to ensure easy and consitent interpretation of data. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Heatmap Custom Columns
kw: custom columns column heatmap add create widget click viewers edit clicking feature dashboard option toggle all icon users ex portal control employee data reorder drag drop go kebab menu dropdown pop open new now needed enable select delete license enables
summary: Heatmap Custom Columns
body: Heatmap Custom Columns Heatmap Custom Columns Heatmap Custom Columns This feature enables users in the EX Portal to create, customize, and control columns within the heatmap widget by combining multiple employee attributes such as Department, Location, Language, etc. This powerful feature allows deeper, multidimensional analysis by defining custom segments directly on the dashboard, enhancing data clarity and insight precision. Users can reorder these custom columns with drag-and-drop ease and tailor visibility through fine-grained access control, ensuring sensitive data is shared securely and relevance is maximized for each stakeholder. Custom columns are add-on columns to the existing heatmap, you cannot create a heatmap with only custom columns. How do I create a Custom Column in the heatmap widget in the dashboard in EX portal? Create a heatmap as you usually do in the dashboard. To add custom columns in this heatmap, follow these steps: Go and click on the Custom Columns option in the Kebab menu dropdown on the widget. The Custom Columns pop up will open. To create a new column, click on the New Column button. You can now give a name to the column by updating the column label and add the filters as needed. You also have the option to add specific viewer(s) to the column by switching on the Enable Viewers toggle. By default the Enable Viewers toggle is Off. To add viewers switch on the toggle and select the viewers from the select dropdown. After all the details are selected as needed, click on Save to add the column. To edit a custom column, go and click on the Custom Columns option in the kebab menu on the widget. The Custom Columns pop up will open up showing all the added custom columns. You can now reorder the columns by drag and drop, hide a column by clicking on the eye icon, delete a column by clicking on the delete icon or can edit a column by clicking on the edit icon. On clicking Edit, you can update all the information about the column. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.
