kw: dashboard tab create tabs new data filters employee click experience layout widgets dashboards portal within single configure add level ex multi organize across filtering video manage list page all created next 15 each menu located license learn utilize effectively allow
summary: Learn how to create a new EX dashboard and utilize the multi-tab layout to organize your widgets and data filters effectively.
body: Create new dashboard Learn how to create a new EX dashboard and utilize the multi-tab layout to organize your widgets and data filters effectively. Create New Dashboard Dashboards in the EX portal allow you to visualize, track, and analyze your employee engagement and experience data in real time. With the updated multi-tab layout functionality, you can seamlessly organize extensive datasets across separate tabs within a single dashboard interface. This allows for clean data segregation, customized filtering, and specialized widget setups tailored to different business units or tracking metrics. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I create a dashboard and manage multiple tabs? To create a new dashboard and configure its tabs, log in to QuestionPro Employee Experience and follow these steps: Navigate to Access Portal inside the employee list page. By clicking this, you will land on the Dashboards page in the Employee Experience portal which shows the list of all the created and shared dashboards. Click on Create New Dashboard. Provide a title for your dashboard in the setup configuration window, then click Create. By default, your newly created dashboard will generate with a single tab labeled Tab 1. To add more tabs to your dashboard, click on the + (Add Tab) icon next to the existing tab. You can add up to a maximum of 15 tabs within a single dashboard. Important Note: Each tab functions as an independent canvas. You can configure an entirely unique set of widgets for each individual tab without impacting the visual layout of the other tabs. To manage your tabs, click on the action menu (three dots) next to the tab name. From the drop-down options, you can choose to Rename, Duplicate, Reorder, or Delete the tab. Configure your data segmentation using dual-level filtering: 1. Dashboard-level filters: Located at the top global header, these filters apply data restrictions across all 15 tabs simultaneously. 2. Tab-level filters: Located within the specific tab's kebab menu, these filters restrict data exclusively for the active tab's widgets. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

By clicking this, you will land on the Dashboards page in the Employee Experience portal which shows the list of all the created and shared dashboards. Click on Create New Dashboard. Provide a title for your dashboard in the setup configuration window, then click Create. By default, your newly created dashboard will generate with a single tab labeled Tab 1. To add more tabs to your dashboard, click on the + (Add Tab) icon next to the existing tab. You can add up to a maximum of 15 tabs within a single dashboard. Important Note: Each tab functions as an independent canvas. You can configure an entirely unique set of widgets for each individual tab without impacting the visual layout of the other tabs. To manage your tabs, click on the action menu (three dots) next to the tab name. From the drop-down options, you can choose to Rename, Duplicate, Reorder, or Delete the tab. Configure your data segmentation using dual-level filtering: 1. Dashboard-level filters: Located at the top global header, these filters apply data restrictions across all 15 tabs simultaneously. 2. Tab-level filters: Located within the specific tab's kebab menu, these filters restrict data exclusively for the active tab's widgets. summary: Discover how to optimize and manage your EX dashboards. Learn to rename, duplicate, securely configure access controls, and utilize the recovery bin for clutter-free insight administration.
body: Manage Dashboard ( rename, copy, delete and access change) Discover how to optimize and manage your EX dashboards. Learn to rename, duplicate, securely configure access controls, and utilize the recovery bin for clutter-free insight administration. Dashboards Managing Dashboards empowers dashboard authors and administrators to seamlessly maintain data hygiene, safeguard data privacy, and streamline reporting workflows. By utilizing rapid duplication, fine-grained access parameters, and a secure operational recycling system, you can ensure that leadership teams and stakeholders interact with accurate, structured, and relevant employee experience data. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I organize, secure, and manage my dashboards? To modify settings, adjust privacy controls, or clean up obsolete visualizations, log in to your QuestionPro Employee Experience account and follow these steps: Navigate to Access Portal inside the employee list page. By clicking this, you will land on the Dashboards page in the Employee Experience portal which shows the list of all the created and shared dashboards. To update structural context, click on the Edit (pencil) icon adjacent to your target dashboard name. Type your updated, descriptive title to better align with current tracking goals, and then click Save. To scale reporting without rebuilding setups from scratch, click the Copy (duplicate) icon. This instantly generates an identical clone within your active list, allowing you to rapidly deploy template variations for separate divisions or testing sandboxes. Important Note: Duplicating a dashboard transfers all underlying page structures, visualization properties, and custom tab layouts into the new sheet automatically, saving valuable implementation time. To govern data privacy and limit corporate visibility, locate the Access column dropdown field. Switch the status from Private (restricted entirely to the primary owner during setup) to Global (publishing the interface to authorized portal viewers as a unified system asset) or Custom (to share the dashboard with a few targeted users). To clear out legacy or duplicate sheets, use individual or bulk deletion tools: 1. Single Deletion: Click the Delete (trash) icon on a specific dashboard row. 2. Bulk Deletion: Check the selection boxes next to multiple dashboard items, then click the global Delete Selected button to move items to a secondary storage buffer. To permanently purge configurations or restore critical historical reporting, click on the Recycle Bin interface option. You can check individual items and click Restore to return them to the live dashboard panel immediately, or select Empty Recycle Bin to complete a compliant, permanent extraction. title: Dashboard and Tab filters
kw: tab filters dashboard level data employee experience across all filter global specific click dashboards portal inside dual including views isolated segment raw department roster localized applied tabs within only video apply list page go entire menu select active available exclusively
summary: Learn how to utilize dual-level data filtering—including dashboard-level global views and tab-level isolated views—to segment employee experience metrics and drive targeted organizational outcomes.
body: Dashboard and Tab filters Learn how to utilize dual-level data filtering—including dashboard-level global views and tab-level isolated views—to segment employee experience metrics and drive targeted organizational outcomes. Dashboard and Tab filters Filters transform massive employee engagement datasets into clear, actionable focal points. By segmenting raw analytics against key demographic indicators—such as department, location, tenure, or custom roster fields, leadership teams can strip away macro-level noise to uncover hyper-localized workforce sentiments. We have two levels of filters in a dashboard, Dashboard filters and Tab filters. Dashboard filters are applied across all the tabs within a dashboard, while Tab filters are applicable on the specific tab only. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I configure and apply dual-level filters to segment my data? To implement filters across your dashboards in the EX portal, log in to QuestionPro Employee Experience and follow these steps: Navigate to Access Portal inside the employee list page. By clicking this, you will land on the Dashboards page in the Employee Experience portal which shows the list of all the created and shared dashboards. Go to the needed dashboard or create a new one. Once inside a dashboard, to isolate information across your entire workspace simultaneously, click on the Filter Button located at the global top-right header. Setting conditions here activates a Dashboard-level filter. In the setup configuration menu, select your criteria from your active employee roster attributes (e.g., Department = "Engineering"). Click Apply to automatically recalibrate every widget across all available tabs in that dashboard. For more granular breakdown inside a tab, go to the kebab menu of that tab and select Tab filters. Define your parameters within this tab filter. This restricts and refines the data visualization properties exclusively for the localized widgets on this specific tab, leaving all other global reporting canvases completely unaffected. Important Note on Data Exports: Data exports can be done on a Tab level only. You can export the entire tab or specific widgets in a tab. Whever filter are actively applied, any operational data downloads extracted directly from the system including automated PDF summaries or raw Excel matrix spreadsheets will exclusively contain the isolated data values matching your active filters to keep external presentations structurally accurate. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Global dashboard Read-only access
body: Global dashboard Read-only access Global dashboard Read-only access Global dashboard Read-only access Employees will have read-only access to the global dashboard widgets. Go to: Analytics Portal » Dashboards » Author and Access will be mentioned Global. Click on the Dashboard. In the dashboard, you’ll find various widgets and analyses. summary: Share survey
body: Share survey Share survey Share survey The "share survey" feature lets a user share a survey with anyone, whether they are inside or outside their organisation. This gives others access to the survey when they log into their accounts, allowing them to collaborate on the survey with "Edit" access only. Click to download video To Share Survey Go to: Workforce >> Select Survey >> Click on the kebab menu (three-dots) >> Select “Share Survey” In the pop-up under “Share Survey”, enter the email address of the user you want to share the survey with and click on “Save”. When users log into their account, the user can only see the survey that is in the folder they have “Edit only” access to. If a user already has access to a folder the same information will be displayed in the share survey pop-up. title: Share Dashboard with Custom Groups
kw: custom dashboard groups employee access create share portal click group filters only tab save users roster all sub user filter button needed select created update sharing ex allows dashboards defined employees creator saving then video view go follow steps clicking
summary: Share Dashboard with Custom Groups
body: Share Dashboard with Custom Groups Share Dashboard with Custom Groups Share Dashboard with Custom Groups Sharing dashboard with custom groups is a new enhancement in the EX Portal that allows users to share dashboards with defined sets of employees instead of just the creator or the entire company. Admins can create custom groups by applying filters (like Department, Location, Role) in the Employee Roster and saving those as groups. These groups can then be selected when granting dashboard access in the Portal, enabling targeted sharing to exactly the right users. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video Dashboard Access Levels Private: Only the dashboard creator can access it. Global (View only): The dashboard is visible to all portal users. Custom (View only): The dashboard is shared only with admin-defined employee groups. How do I create Custom Groups in Employee Roster? After logging into your QuestionPro EX account, navigate to the required folder. Then go into Employee List and follow the below steps: In the Employees tab, locate the Employee Filters sub-tab. You can create custom user groups in this sub-tab. Go into the Employee Filters sub-tab and you will the screen as shown. To create custom user groups, click on the Create Employee Filter button. On clicking the Create Employee Filter button, the Setup Employee Filter modal will open up which allows you to create and save custom groups as needed. Set a name for the group and select filters as needed. You can also see the number of members that are matched in the group. To save this custom group, click on the Save button. After saving, the custom group is created. How do I update dashboard access to Custom Groups in Portal? When you log into Employee Experience Portal, you land on the Manage dashboard page. Follow the following steps to update the dashboard access: In the Manage Dashboard page, you will see all the dashboards that you have created. To update the dashboard access, click on the access dropdown of the needed dashboard and select Custom. On clicking Custom, a pop up opens which has all the custom user groups that you created in the roster. Select the custom group you want to share the dashboard with and click on Save You will notice that the access has now been updated to Custom and a notification appears to confirm the same. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Benchmarking
kw: benchmark scores click add benchmarking benchmarks enter manually data go survey then import excel manual organisation against standards option download choice analytics dashboard settings new name want set check if once edit using sheet template range saved save tab updated
summary: Benchmarking
body: Benchmarking Benchmarking Benchmarking Benchmarks provide additional context to your data, helping you identify your organisation’s strengths and areas that need improvement. They allow you to compare your results against industry standards or other relevant metrics, giving you a clearer picture of where you stand. In QuestionPro Workforce, you can easily add your own benchmarks to tailor the analysis to your specific needs. Alternatively, you have the option to use the benchmarks we offer, making it easier to assess your organisation’s performance against proven standards. Click to download video To Add Benchmarks: Go to: Survey of your choice » Analytics » Dashboard Settings » Click on Benchmarking » + New Benchmark. Add a Benchmark Name or select a Survey that you want to set as a benchmark. To make it accessible to everyone, check "Global access." If you want to keep it private, leave it blank. Then, click "Create." Once the benchmark is created, you can add benchmark scores, edit or rename it, and delete it. To add benchmark scores, click on the benchmark name at the pop-up, you will see two options: one to “Import “scores using an Excel sheet, and another to enter scores manually. “Manual “entry is usually preferred when there is less data to input. To import benchmark scores using Excel, click on "Download Benchmark Import Template." Fill in the scores under "Mean %." Scores should be in the 0-10 range as decimals, and Agreement% should be in the 0-100 range as whole numbers, without the % sign, as shown in the image below. Drag and drop the saved Excel sheet template here, or browse to upload the file. Then click on ‘Save’ To check if the Benchmark scores have been successfully added to the questions, click on the "Manual" tab to view them. Users can also enter the scores Manually when the data set has fewer scores to enter. To Add benchmark scores manually go to: Go to: Survey of your choice » Analytics » Dashboard Settings » Click on Benchmarking » + New Benchmark. Click on the “Manual” tab and manually enter the scores in the “Add value” sections and then “Save” Once the benchmark is updated, the user can find the option to add the benchmarks to widgets in the edit menu. All saved benchmarks in the admin can be seen in the dropdown. The widget is updated with a benchmark. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: PPT export templates
kw: templates template export powerpoint add set ppt new settings color slide create default step setup alignment see click admin first setting next also font feature ex portal custom dashboard widgets button only users general adding name choosing theme image title
summary: PowerPoint Export Templates
body: PPT export templates PowerPoint Export Templates PowerPoint Export Templates PowerPoint export templates is the new feature in EX portal, that allows you to create custom templates for the PowerPoint export of dashboard widgets. How do I create custom templates for exporting dashboard widgets as Microsoft PowerPoint ? After logging into the Ex portal, go to the navigation sidebar and follow these navigations steps as Settings > PPT export templates . Once you are inside the PPT export templates tab, you will see a system default template Default , to create a new template, click on the New PPT Template button. Only admin users can create a new template, non-admin users can see only the default template and the templates created by the admin. After clicking on the New PPT template button, the first step is to setup the General settings by adding a name for the template and choosing the theme color. After setting a name and choosing a theme color, click on Nextto move further. After adding the general settings, the next step is to setup the first slide. You can add an image and set the layout for it. You can also add a title and a description and set the alignment, font, color, etc. You can also add a logo and set the alignment for it. After setting up the first slide, the next step is to setup the widget slide. You can add a logo, and set the Title text settings with needed alignment, font and color. After setting up the widget slide, the next step is to setup the last slide. You can add a background image, a thankyou message and set the alignment, font and color. After the settings are done, click on Save to add the template. After saving, the template is added with the creation date. If you hover onto the row, you will see the options to edit, delete and also to set the template as default. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Office 365 Integration
body: Office 365 integration Office 365 Integration Office 365 Integration Office 365 integration allows you to connect with your office 365 platform. You can import your employees from the office 365 platform to your workforce. Once the integration is established, employees can log into their workforce portal by their microsoft account. How can I enable Office 365 integration with my workforce? Only admin user can establish Office 365 integration. To enable Office 365 integration with your workforce, follow the steps mentioned below: Go to: Login>> WorkForce>>Employees>>Integration Click on “Connect” option given in Office 365. Enter login details and submit to establish integration. Can I import employees from once Integrated with Office 365? Yes, you can import employees once integrated with Office 365. Click on import employee to import the employees. Can I remove Office 365 integration? Yes, you can remove Office 365 integration. Click on remove integration link to remove integration. summary: Workforce FTP Integration
body: FTP Sync Workforce FTP Integration Workforce FTP Integration FTP Sync is another method to bring in the employee list along with their demographic details and status into the system. You'll need to provide FTP details when setting up the Import feature. Once scheduled, the system will read the Excel file and import the employee information. If a new employee is found, they will be added. If an existing employee's details or status have changed, the system will update their information accordingly. What does FTP Scheduler do? The Auto scheduled FTP allows you to do the following:- Automate the employee import. Automate the any changes happening to employee status and demographics. How can I set up the FTP sync for employee import? To set up the import, go to – Login >> Workforce >> Manage Employee Roster >> Integration >> FTP Click on Add Auto-scheduler button Set up the FTP details and click on Create Scheduler Event to create the employee import scheduler. A weekly FTP scheduler will be created. Download the employee import template. Update the template with employee details. Upload the updated template to the FTP location. You can run the scheduler immediately by clicking "Run Now" after uploading the Excel file. You can also pause, edit, or delete the FTP scheduler as needed. To view the logs of the employee imported, click on the Logs link given for the scheduler. title: Portal Language Translation
kw: language portal employee file translation add import translations default languages button click template login set particular go select menu save enter format excel column please make license hr admin now define so employees view data analytics preferred my update workforce
summary: Portal Language Translation
body: Portal Language Translation Portal Language Translation Portal Language Translation HR admin can now define the language of the employee portal so the employees when login into the portal can view the data/analytics in their preferred language. How can I set my employee portal in a particular language? To set up an employee portal in a particular language, update the default language. Go to: Login » Workforce » Manage employee roster » Portal » Languages The default language is the main language for your employee portal. Select a language from the drop down menu to save as Default Language button. You can also choose to add as many languages to your employee portal as you need. Click on "Add Language" button to add languages Select the language you want to add in your employee portal and click on save. Once you add a language, go to Import Translations in left navigation menu. Click to Download Import Template Enter translations to this file and import this file back by clicking on Import Translations button. What is the file format for the translation template? The template file is in Excel (.xlsx) file format. For each language, there is a pre-defined column in the excel file. Please make sure you enter your translations in the correct language column. Please do not make any changes in columns A, B, C and rows 1, 2 and 3. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Unique Identifier
kw: unique identifier employee employees survey data adding responses feedback hris other using create folder roster license distinct code assigned each participant ensuring remain anonymous while allowing accurate tracking helps maintain confidentiality encouraging honest without revealing identity addition safeguarding privacy identifiers
summary: Unique Identifier
body: Unique Identifier Unique Identifier Unique Identifier A Unique Identifier in an employee survey is a distinct code or identifier assigned to each survey participant, ensuring their responses remain anonymous while allowing for accurate data tracking. It helps maintain confidentiality, encouraging honest feedback from employees without revealing their identity. In addition to safeguarding privacy, Unique Identifiers play a crucial role in Human Resources Information Systems (HRIS) by enabling seamless integration of survey data with individual employee records. By linking survey responses to specific employees, HRIS can provide comprehensive insights into employee engagement, performance, and other vital metrics, facilitating data-driven decision-making and targeted HR interventions for organizational improvement. How to upload employees using unique identifier Go to: Create New Folder » Switch Enable Unique Identifier toggle on » Fill other details and create the folder. Click on the Employee Roster. The roster is updated with Unique Identifier as the primary option for employee ID. Adding employees manually follows the same procedure as adding them using an email address. For adding employees in bulk, user can download the import file template. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Access Rules: Permissions
kw: access rules employee rule portal permissions view click workforce employees based hierarchy roster fields data login created create specific hr manager see department admin assign add demographic option switch default delete under edit license admins dashboards custom like level location
summary: Workforce Access Rules: Permissions
body: Access Rules: Permissions Workforce Access Rules: Permissions Access Rules: Permissions Admins can create specific access rules for employees to view dashboards in the employee portal. These rules can be based on custom fields like Level, Location, and Team. The employee's view in the portal will follow the hierarchy defined in the employee roster. For instance, to allow an HR manager to see data from a specific department, the admin can select the department and assign the rule to the HR manager. Click to download video To setup access rules for the employees in the portal go to: Login >> Workforce >> Employee Roster >> Portal >> Permissions >> Click on” Add Rule “to add rules Create access rules by selecting the appropriate demographic fields. Multiple fields can be selected for a single demographic. Assign the created rule to the employee(s) and click on “save”. You can view the created rule for the employee, and this rule applies to all employees specified within it. Portal view of the access rule When an employee logs into the employee portal, they will see data according to the rules set by the admin. Employees also have the option to switch to the default view(hierarchy based rule), which displays data based on their hierarchy. Click on "Rule Filter" to view the created rules or switch to the default hierarchy-based access. Delete Access Rule Login >> Workforce >> Employee Roster >> Portal >> Permissions >> Click on the “Delete” icon under Access Rules. Edit Access Rule Login >> Workforce >> Employee Roster >> Portal >> Permissions >> Click on the “Edit” option under Access Rules License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Employee custom fields
body: Employee custom fields Employee custom fields Employee custom fields Employee custom fields are profile fields that can be updated for each employee. For example, you can define an employee's role, team, location, etc. This allows you to filter employees for various types of analysis as needed. Click to download video How to use employee custom fields? Login > Workforce > Manager Employee Roster > Custom Fields > Add New Field Add Title and select the field type from Multiple Choice (single select), Date/Time, or Free Text to define the field type. Add values to the field for single select type. Check or uncheck the custom field to be visible on the employee roster. Click the edit button to modify the field values. Important** When editing always add new variables in the custom field at the bottom, never at the top or in the middle, as this can disrupt data analytics. Click on "Reorder" to rearrange the active custom fields by dragging and dropping them, then save your changes. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Employee Bulk Import - Excel
kw: employee bulk import excel field employees upload format adding list experience status not click download custom template document country code importing demographic details video pop add information specified fields mandatory if active values license helps keep track also allows distribute
summary: Adding or importing bulk employee list in Employee Experience
body: Employee Bulk Import - Excel Adding or importing bulk employee list in Employee Experience Employee Bulk Import - Excel Adding employees to your employee list helps you keep track of their status and demographic details. It also allows you to distribute surveys to specific demographics and analyse the survey results by applying demographic filters Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video For bulk employee upload, go to: Login >> Employee Experience>> Employee List >> Import >> Bulk Import - Excel Click on Import Format, to know the details needed for bulk upload. Preview the pop-up with the custom field options. After closing the pop-up, click on Download Import Template to download the Excel file. Add your employees and their information to the Excel template as specified and save the document To upload document, you can either drag and drop the document in the space shown on the image or browse. Note: Employees should be uploaded in the format specified on the upload screen and in the Excel template. Required fields and formats: Email address of employees is a mandatory field. Country code is a mandatory field if you are adding phone number. Country code should be in the format: +country code. Employee Status will have 2 fields: "Active" and "Terminated". If the employee status field is blank then it automatically considers employee as active. Date format should be MM/DD/YYYY You can only add "." and "/" in the custom field values, "," and "-" are not allowed Header row should not be edited or deleted. Custom field unique values must be predefined before importing the information. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.
content:
Employee Bulk Import - Excel

Employee Bulk Import - Excel Adding employees to your employee list helps you keep track of their status and demographic details. It also allows you to distribute surveys to specific demographics and analyse the survey results by applying demographic filters Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video For bulk employee upload, go to: Login >> Employee Experience>> Employee List >> Import >> Bulk Import - Excel Click on Import Format, to know the details needed for bulk upload. Preview the pop-up with the custom field options. After closing the pop-up, click on Download Import Template to download the Excel file. Add your employees and their information to the Excel template as specified and save the document To upload document, you can either drag and drop the document in the space shown on the image or browse. Note: Employees should be uploaded in the format specified on the upload screen and in the Excel template. Required fields and formats: Email address of employees is a mandatory field. Country code is a mandatory field if you are adding phone number. Country code should be in the format: +country code. Employee Status will have 2 fields: "Active" and "Terminated". If the employee status field is blank then it automatically considers employee as active. Date format should be MM/DD/YYYY You can only add "." and "/" in the custom field values, "," and "-" are not allowed Header row should not be edited or deleted. Custom field unique values must be predefined before importing the information. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? summary: Workforce - Employee Filter
body: Employee filter Workforce - Employee Filter Workforce - Employee Filter Employee filters help users set multiple criteria based on roster demographics. Users can create new filters, save them, or use existing filters. To Create an Employee Filter Login>> Workforce >> Employee Roster >> Employee Filter To create a new employee filter, click on “+ Create Employee Filter” In the pop-up for Setup Employee Filter, name the filter and add the demographic details you want to use to filter your employees and then “save” the filter. Once the filter is saved, you can view it in the list of created filters. To Delete an Employee Filter Login >> Workforce >> Employee Roster >> Employee Filter. Select the filter you want to Edit on the left, then click the “Edit” icon on the right. Edit the changes and then “Save” it To Create a New Employee Filter from Send options Login >> Workforce >> Select the survey >> Send >> Click on “Add Group” In the pop-up for Setup Employee Filter Select “Create New” from the drop down , name the filter and add the demographic details you want to use to filter your employees and then “save” the filter. As you can see, we have 28 employees based on the selected filters To use a Existing Employee Filter from send options Login >> Workforce >> Select the survey >> Send >> Click on “Add Group” In the pop-up for Setup Employee Filter Select “Use Existing” from the drop down, Select the Existing filter then “save” the filter. title: Employee list
kw: employee list email address employees click reset details view password portal send admin role individual link remove all tree page changes please note once listed example name supervisor location age etc there here format set edit ways give person removed
summary: All the details of the employees in an organization are listed in the employee list. For example, Employee name, Employee email address, Supervisor email address, Role, Location, Age, etc.
body: Employee list All the details of the employees in an organization are listed in the employee list. For example, Employee name, Employee email address, Supervisor email address, Role, Location, Age, etc. Employee List All the details of the employees in an organisation are listed in the employee list. For example, the employee's name, email address, supervisor's email address, role, location, age, etc. There are two types of view in the employee list: List view and Tree view List view: Here, the employee details are in the list format, and 10 email addresses appear on the page simultaneously. Tree view: The employee details are given in the hierarchical tree view format. Set Pagination Limit: Click on the dropdown to set the number of members you want to display per page. How to edit individual employee details? To edit employee details, click on the email address in the employee list and update the necessary information. How to reset portal password or send portal password reset link to Employee? There are 2 ways to reset employee password for their portal access, click on employee email in the list. Click on “Reset password link” to send the employee to the portal page to reset their password. 2 Click on "Email reset link" to send the employee an email with a link to reset their portal password. How to give Individual admin rights? Admin role is assigned to the person who is handling the whole workforce study. This role can be transferred from one person to another. To give an individual admin right, click on email address in the employee list and toggle on the button and save the changes. When this action takes place, please note, the hierarchy changes. Please note: Saving changes here will automatically deactivate the previous admin user How can we remove the employees from the list? The employees can be removed individually from the employee list in 2 ways Click on the Kebab menu (Three Dots) > Remove Select an employee in the box and click "Remove." You can also select multiple employees at once and remove them in bulk. Please note: Once an employee is removed, all associated data for that individual is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. Therefore, it is important to be careful when removing employees from the roster. What is the use of an alternate email address? An alternate email address will be used to send out an exit survey to the terminated employees. Admin can enter employees personal email address to send out exit survey once they are terminated. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Workforce - Screener Page Settings
body: Screener page settings Workforce - Screener Page Settings Workforce - Landing page Once you login into your Workforce account, you will get a view of managing your employee roster and create new studies. In manage employee roster you can create your entire employee base along with their demographic details. After creating and deploying your survey you can view details like survey type,created date, status of the survey, deployment details, responses on your landing page. You can also go to survey distribution and analytics from the landing page Lets have a look at them individually Type: It represents which type of study it is. For example- employee experience, 360 feedback or pulse. Created: This is the creation date of the study. Status: It represents whether the study is active or inactive. Deployment: It provides the details of number of times the study was deployed. On click of the the deployment count user can view the details of each deployment. Responses: It represents the total number of responses collected so far for that specific study. Send: This option takes you to the send page where you can send out your survey Analytics: This section takes you to the dashboard tab where you can view the analytics of the collected responses. More: Under this option you can apply the rename, copy and delete the survey. Recycle Bin: All the deleted studies can be viewed here. Those surveys can either be restored or can be deleted forever. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Lifecycle Rule
kw: lifecycle survey user rule select click days add new rules employee enter medium reminder if exit admin joining options criteria selected one more required sms slack option toggle details many zero repetition clicks gets interval settings delete employees send onboarding
summary: Lifecycle Rules for On-boarding and Exit Survey
body: Lifecycle Rule Lifecycle Rules for On-boarding and Exit Survey Lifecycle Rules for On-boarding and Exit Survey HR admin will now no longer have to identify employees joining in and leaving the organization everyday to send out a survey. With the help of lifecycle rules, HR admin can create rule to send onboarding and exit survey after X days of employee joining and exit date. For example, HR admin has to send an onboarding survey to the employees after 7 days of the employees joining date than s/he can simply go to the lifecycle rule, select the survey for onboarding rule and define it to be send after 7 days. All the new employees joining in will get the onboarding survey after 7 days of joining Click to download video To setup Lifecycle rule, go to: Login » Employee roaster » Admin » Lifecycle Click on “add rule” for creating new lifecycle rules User will have the below options to enter to create new rule: Rule name Select the employee filter Select the criteria Select the survey from the selected criteria Enter schedule deployment in days Select one or more required medium from sms,survey and slack Select the respective templates for the medium selected User has an option of “Add reminder “ toggle which is optional, if the toggle is on user has to enter below details Select one or more required medium from sms,survey and slack Days- After how many days the reminder should get triggered ( Can’t be zero) Repetition - How many times should reminder repeat ( Can’t be zero) User can add multiple “days and repetition” intervals ,If the user clicks on “+” sign user gets an option to add new interval and If the user clicks on “-” then the interval gets deleted Click on save once the settings are made On the lifecycle home page you have the following options Click on “edit” to change any settings Click on “delete” to delete the lifecycle rule Click on “logs” to see the delivery details of the sent messages Feature Walkthrough: Click to view License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: 360 category header

summary: 360 category header
body: 360 category header 360 category header 360 category header Grouping questions under specific headers means organizing related questions into clear sections based on their themes or topics. This method makes feedback structured, coherent, and easy to understand, giving a detailed and nuanced view of the surveyed area. For example, in a workforce survey, questions about teamwork, communication, leadership, and job satisfaction can be grouped under their own headers. This improves the survey's clarity and makes it easier to analyse, as responses can be segmented and compared across different categories. Click to download video To Set up 360 Category Headers, Go to: Login >> Workforce >> Employee Roster >> Admin >> 360 Category Headers >> Add New Header A pop appears to add headers one per line After adding the headers click on “Create Category Headers” Here you can see that 360 Category Headers have been listed successfully To Edit a 360 Category Header, click on “Edit Header”. Change/ Edit Header name and click on “update” To Delete a 360 Category Header, click on the circled “x” mark. Delete Item pops up then click on “Yes” To Add 360 Category Header’s to Survey questions, Go to: Login workforce >> Select the survey >> Click the Kebab menu (3dots) >> Edit Section Click on Rows >> Under Category Headers >> Assign 360 Headers to each question Once you have assigned 360 Headers to all questions click on “Update” You can click "Preview" to see how the 360 Category Header looks on the survey. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Folder setup
kw: folder data click employee information setup workforce edit deleted login dropdown real survey responses select delete recycle bin available folders used create dummy helps see other id employees feedback hris important improve want then yes 90 days no license organize
summary: Workforce folder setup
body: Folder setup Workforce folder setup Folder setup Folders are used to organize Employee Information and data separately and more efficiently. They help admins to categorize and group related items together, making it easier to locate, manage, and retrieve information quickly. This organizational structure improves workflow, reduces clutter, and enhances productivity by providing a clear and systematic way to store and access data. Click to download video How to create a new folder? Login>> Workforce Click on dropdown Name the folder and click on “Create folder” Populate dummy data “On” means to fill a database, spreadsheet, or application with placeholder or mock data. This data is typically not real and is used for testing, demonstration, or development purposes. It helps Users and testers see how the system behaves with data, ensuring that the functionality works as expected before using real data. For example, dummy data can include fake names, addresses, phone numbers, and other information that mimics what real data would look like. Enable Employee ID Employee ID in an employee survey is a special code given to each participant. This ensures their responses stay anonymous while allowing accurate data tracking. It helps keep responses confidential, encouraging employees to provide honest feedback without fear of being identified. Besides protecting privacy, unique identifiers are vital in Human Resources Information Systems (HRIS), as they allow survey data to be easily matched with individual employee records. By linking survey responses to specific employees, HRIS can offer detailed insights into employee engagement, performance, and other important metrics. This supports data-driven decisions and targeted HR actions to improve the organization. How to edit folder Login >> Workforce>> Click on the dropdown >> Click on “Edit Folder” Select the folder you want to edit and then click on “Update” How to delete a folder Login >> Workforce>> Click on the dropdown >> Click on “Edit Folder” Select the folder you want to Delete. A pop up appears asking are you sure to delete folder then click on “Yes” Here, you can see that the Test 1 Folder has been completely deleted. How to use recycle bin Once the folders are deleted, they go to the recycle bin. The information will be available there for 90 days only. Select the deleted Item and click on “Restore” Important ** After 90 days, the data will be permanently deleted from the recycle bin and will no longer be available. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: New study
kw: study new survey create employee experience click custom creating questions design add logic video button clicking available license involves setting fresh project start naming adding organizing them blocks customize tailored responses test distributing target audience watch quick walkthrough understand works
summary: Creating new study in Employee Experience
body: New study Creating new study in Employee Experience Employee Experience - New Study Creating a new study in a survey involves setting up a fresh survey project. Start by naming your study, adding questions, and organizing them into blocks. Customize the design, add logic for tailored responses, and test the survey before distributing it to your target audience. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How to I create a New study? To create a new study, click on + New Study button. Login >> Employee Experience >> Click on New study After clicking on the + New Study button, you will see a modal with all the available study types. You can choose an existing template and create a survey or create a custom one. To create a custom study, hover on the custom study card and click on Survey. summary: Create Initiatives for Empower for QuestionPro Workforce
body: Create-Initiatives Create Initiatives for Empower for QuestionPro Workforce Create Initiatives The Initiative feature in Empower helps streamline the process of turning feedback collected through employee listening surveys into actionable initiatives, and driving continuous improvement within the organization. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I create an Initiative in Empower> Click the "+" button located in the bottom right corner of the page. Choose an initiative type: Select a template: A pop-over appears offering various pre-made initiative templates categorized based on type (e.g., communication). You can preview each template to see if it aligns with your needs. Create a custom initiative: If no template matches your needs, choose this option to build your initiative from scratch. Filter by type (optional): If you know the initiative type beforehand, use the filter to narrow down the displayed templates. Provide basic details: Regardless of the chosen method (template or custom), enter the following information: Name: A clear and concise title for your initiative. Description: A more detailed explanation of the initiative's goals and purpose. Goal: Is it selected as per the template theme which can be edited Assign ownership and contributors: Owner: Select the individual responsible for leading the initiative. Contributors (optional): Add additional users who will participate in the initiative. Create the initiative: Click the "Create" button. Template selection: If using a template, predefined tasks associated with that template will automatically populate the initiative. Custom creation: If creating a custom initiative, the initial task list will be empty. Status: The initiative's status will automatically be set to "New". You can later change it to "Active" to officially begin execution. You can change it to “completed” once it is done. You can change it to “closed” if you choose to close License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? summary: Create tasks for Empower for QuestionPro Workforce
body: Tasks-Initiative Create tasks for Empower for QuestionPro Workforce Tasks Tasks are basically Action items created for an initiative along with the capability to assign owners, contributors and due dates . How do I create and manage tasks under initiatives Ideation? Users can add tasks either individually or in bulk: Single Task: Click the "New Task" call to action (CTA). Bulk Tasks (optional): A future feature may allow uploading a file containing multiple tasks. Task Details: Fill in the following information for each task: Name: A clear and descriptive title for the task. Description: A more detailed explanation of the task's purpose and requirements. Due Date: The date by which the task should be completed. Contributors (optional): Assign additional users who will work on the task alongside the owner. Owner: Select the individual responsible for leading the task's completion. Create Task: Click the "Create Task" CTA. Default Status: Newly created tasks will automatically have a "Pending" status. Each task displays the following details: Name Due Date Owner (responsible individual) Contributors (supporting individuals) Status (current progress stage) Completed (displays completion date and time taken upon hovering) Users can perform the following actions on each task: Delete: Remove tasks deemed irrelevant to the initiative's goals. Edit: Modify any of the task details mentioned above. Each task can have a dedicated owner and multiple contributors can be assigned to support the owner in completing the task. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Upstream and Downstream
kw: upstream downstream causes employees burnout problems work refers management example report high levels hr response root reacting occur involves crisis damage control treating symptoms rather than implement wellness programs like meditation apps stress workshops mental health days helps cope doesn
summary: Upstream and Downstream
body: Upstream and Downstream Upstream and Downstream Upstream and Downstream The concepts of upstream and downstream in this context refer to proactive vs. reactive approaches to solving problems. Downstream work refers to reacting to problems as they occur. It involves crisis management, damage control, and treating symptoms rather than causes. Example: Employees report high levels of burnout. HR Response: Implement wellness programs like meditation apps, stress management workshops, or mental health days. This helps employees cope with burnout but doesn’t address why they’re burning out in the first place. Upstream work refers to preventing problems before they happen. It requires identifying root causes and implementing long-term solutions. Example: Employees report high levels of burnout. HR Response: Identify root causes—perhaps workload imbalance, unclear expectations, or inefficient processes—and take steps to fix them. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Ideation

summary: Ideation
body: Ideation Ideation Ideation Ideation facilitates the seamless sharing of ideas, fostering a collaborative environment where individuals can effortlessly express their thoughts on a given topic. Through this platform, participants not only contribute their insights but also engage in the process of refining concepts through voting and constructive discussions. This interactive approach not only enhances the quality of ideas but also ensures that they are meticulously organized, resulting in a more refined and impactful outcome. How do I create a new item under Ideation? To create a new item under Ideation, follow the navigation steps as Initiative > Ideation > New addition All the available ideation will be shown on the ideation home page. Click "New Ideation": This redirects you to the ideation creation page. Fill in Title and Introduction: Briefly describe your ideation topic and provide an overview. Upload a Logo (Optional): Add a visual element to represent your ideation (JPG, JPEG, SVG, PNG formats accepted). Add Your Ideas (Optional): Enable "Promoted Ideas" to begin with your own suggestions. Click "+" to add additional ideas, and "-" to remove. Crowdsource Ideas (Optional): Toggle "Crowdsource Request" to enable colleagues to contribute. Formulate a clear question within 225 characters to guide their input. Enable Comments (Optional): Allow anonymous replies and comments on each ide. Set Interaction Limit: Determine the maximum number of votes each user can cast (up to 10). Choose Language: Select the ideation language from English, Spanish, or German. Preview the Ideation: Click the "Preview" button to see how your ideation will appear to your colleagues. Publish or Save as Draft: Choose "Publish" to generate a shareable link for employee access. Drafts can be edited later. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

title: Ideation Insights
kw: ideation insights sentiment actionable emotions trending list ideations click tab analysis ai feedback clear key topics attitudes engagement patterns more following steps select see top across no improve employee experience directly task license powered analytics tool designed convert open ended
summary: Ideation Insights
body: Ideation Insights Ideation Insights Ideation Insights Ideation Insights is an AI-powered analytics tool designed to convert open-ended feedback from your ideation campaigns into clear, actionable intelligence. By automatically analyzing sentiment, detecting key emotions, and surfacing trending topics, Ideation Insights empowers Admin to quickly interpret participant attitudes, uncover engagement patterns, and make informed decisions to drive organizational improvement and foster a more responsive workplace culture. How do I Access Ideation Insights for an Ideation? Start by accessing the list of Ideations, by following these navigation steps as Initiative > Ideation , the list of all Ideations will appear. From the list of Ideations, select the relevant Ideation and click on it. After opening the Ideation, you will see the option for Insights Tab. (The Insights tab will be visible only when the number of Ideas in the Ideation are more than or equal to 5) Click on the Insights Tab, and you will see the detailed AI analysis. The Analysis includes: Summary - Provides overview of response patterns, identifying predominant attitudes and highlighting opportunities for enhanced engagement strategies. Top Emotions - Top emotions are identified and displayed with visual indicators. Sentiment Analysis - Gauge chart displaying sentiment distribution across positive, negative, mixed, and no sentiment categories with clear percentage breakdowns. Trending Topics - Identification of trending themes to help categorize and prioritize responses. Actionable Insights - Provide simple and effective steps to address key issues, improve outcomes, and enhance the employee experience. By clicking on the “+” icon across an Actionable Insight, you can directly create a Task. You can select a date, set up the owner and contributor(s) for the task directly from the pop up and click on save changes. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

body: Empower-Analytics -Analytics on Empower dashboard Empower Analytics In the Empower homepage we can view upcomping tasks, shortcut to create inititaives and analytics section What can we analyze from analytics section on empower dashboard? Active Initiatives: The initiative must be marked as "Active” and you must be involved in the initiative as a creator, owner, contributor. Tasks in progress: Tasks that are Active and are not completed count will be displayed. New Ideas: All the ideas that are active and also the logged-in user has to be the Creator/Owner/Contributor of that initiative. Top goals: Displays top goals, by grouping them on the basis of goals and calculating the count. Top Contributors: This identifies users with the highest number of In-Progress tasks, effectively highlighting top contributors. Status Summary: This shows a comparison between active and completed tasks between initiatives. You can choose to download the dashboard summary in Excel or PDF format. summary: Conversations
body: Conversations Conversations Conversations Conversations is designed for focused, real-time employee dialogue. It combines the ease of a community forum with enterprise-grade anonymity, allowing leaders to pulse the organization on specific topics and instantly pivot from raw feedback to structured action. Think of it as a secure, topic-driven space where collective feedback is transformed into measurable organizational steps. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I start a Conversation ? To start a conversation, login in Empower and follow these steps: Once inside the homepage, open the sidebar navigation and click on Conversations. After going to the Conversations page, click on New Conversation to open the setup modal. After clicking on New Conversation, this setup modal will open up. Add details like title, question, logo, cover image and allow likes, dislikes and comments as you see fit. Then click on Create. Important Note: No two conversations can have same title. The question can be same accross the conversations but the titles will always be unique. The conversation is now created. As the moderator, you are directly taken to the Moderation page. To start, you can preview the conversation by clicking on the eye icon and see how the conversation would appear to the respondents. To distribute the conversation and get responses, simply copy the link and share with the desired group. On opening the link, the respondent will get assigned a name and an avatar. This is done to ensure correct data collection and analysis while keeping the employee identities anonymous. The name and avatar can be regenerated if needed, they can then click on Continue to go to the chat forum. Users can add their response(s) to the question, comment on other users' response and like/dislike a response as they want. Once the responses start coming in, you can view the data in real time on the moderation page. You can add replies to the responses as a moderator and also have the ability to delete a response or a reply if found inappropriate for the setting. The Analytics page uses AI to analyze the collected responses and find insights. It includes an executive summary, trending topics, sentiment analysis, key metrics and most engaged with responses. This pages shows the data only after there are 5 or more responses. If at any point you want to update the details of the conversation, like title, question, logo, cover image or settings, you can do so via the setup page. After the data collection is done, you can close a conversation to move to the next step of taking action. Once the conversation is closed, you will see bolt icons next to the trending topics on the Analytics page. Click on the bolt icon to see the recommended action. content:
Conversations

Conversations

Conversations Conversations is designed for focused, real-time employee dialogue. It combines the ease of a community forum with enterprise-grade anonymity, allowing leaders to pulse the organization on specific topics and instantly pivot from raw feedback to structured action. Think of it as a secure, topic-driven space where collective feedback is transformed into measurable organizational steps. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How do I start a Conversation ? To start a conversation, login in Empower and follow these steps: Once inside the homepage, open the sidebar navigation and click on Conversations. After going to the Conversations page, click on New Conversation to open the setup modal. After clicking on New Conversation, this setup modal will open up. Add details like title, question, logo, cover image and allow likes, dislikes and comments as you see fit. Then click on Create. Important Note: No two conversations can have same title. The question can be same accross the conversations but the titles will always be unique. The conversation is now created. As the moderator, you are directly taken to the Moderation page. To start, you can preview the conversation by clicking on the eye icon and see how the conversation would appear to the respondents. To distribute the conversation and get responses, simply copy the link and share with the desired group. On opening the link, the respondent will get assigned a name and an avatar. This is done to ensure correct data collection and analysis while keeping the employee identities anonymous. The name and avatar can be regenerated if needed, they can then click on Continue to go to the chat forum. Users can add their response(s) to the question, comment on other users' response and like/dislike a response as they want. Once the responses start coming in, you can view the data in real time on the moderation page. You can add replies to the responses as a moderator and also have the ability to delete a response or a reply if found inappropriate for the setting. The Analytics page uses AI to analyze the collected responses and find insights. It includes an executive summary, trending topics, sentiment analysis, key metrics and most engaged with responses. This pages shows the data only after there are 5 or more responses. If at any point you want to update the details of the conversation, like title, question, logo, cover image or settings, you can do so via the setup page. After the data collection is done, you can close a conversation to move to the next step of taking action. Once the conversation is closed, you will see bolt icons next to the trending topics on the Analytics page. Click on the bolt icon to see the recommended action. You can select an existing initiative from the list and assign the recommended action directly as a task to the person responsible. After you successfully assign an action, the bolt icon turns into a green check showing that the action has been assigned. summary: AI Content Moderation for Conversations
body: AI-Powered Content Moderation in Conversations AI Content Moderation for Conversations AI-Powered Content Moderation in Conversations AI-Powered Content Moderation is a proactive safety feature integrated into the Conversations module, designed to maintain a secure and professional environment for employee feedback. By leveraging advanced machine learning, the system automatically detects and intercepts harmful content—such as hate speech, personal threats, sexually explicit material, or doxxing in real-time. This eliminates the need for constant, manual monitoring by moderators. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video The system operates on an asynchronous "Optimistic UI" architecture. When a participant submits a response, the platform displays it instantly to the author to ensure a fluid experience. Simultaneously, the content undergoes a background AI scan. If the content is flagged as a high-severity violation, the system automatically keeps the response hidden from all other participants. This architecture ensures that harmful content is never publicly visible and maintains the forum's integrity, while maintaining it's social nature. How does the Moderation process work from the Respondent's perspective? Transparency and safety are foundational to the participant experience. If your content triggers a moderation flag, the system provides clear feedback regarding your status: Immediate Feedback Loop: Upon clicking submit, your response is immediately visible in your view, maintaining the spontaneity of the conversation. Automatic Content Hiding: If your response is flagged by the AI for violating community guidelines, it remains hidden from all other participants to protect the community. Inline Warnings and Guidance: If your post is hidden, you will see an inline warning message. This notification explains that your post has been restricted and provides a direct link to the tool’s Community Guidelines, fostering an educational approach to workplace ethics. Participant Restrictions: In cases of persistent violations, a moderator may restrict your account from this specific conversation. If you are restricted, you will be unable to post new responses; your input interface will be disabled, though you retain the ability to read and view existing discussions. A clear error message will notify you of your restricted status. How do Moderators manage flagged content and enforce forum safety? As a moderator, you have access to a robust suite of tools designed to manage forum integrity with minimal effort. The workflow centers on proactive alerts and centralized dashboard management. Real-Time Transactional Alerts: When the AI identifies a high-severity violation, you receive an automated email notification. content:
AI-Powered Content Moderation in Conversations

AI Content Moderation for Conversations

AI-Powered Content Moderation in Conversations AI-Powered Content Moderation is a proactive safety feature integrated into the Conversations module, designed to maintain a secure and professional environment for employee feedback. By leveraging advanced machine learning, the system automatically detects and intercepts harmful content—such as hate speech, personal threats, sexually explicit material, or doxxing in real-time. This eliminates the need for constant, manual monitoring by moderators. Watch this quick walkthrough to understand how it works: Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video The system operates on an asynchronous "Optimistic UI" architecture. When a participant submits a response, the platform displays it instantly to the author to ensure a fluid experience. Simultaneously, the content undergoes a background AI scan. If the content is flagged as a high-severity violation, the system automatically keeps the response hidden from all other participants. This architecture ensures that harmful content is never publicly visible and maintains the forum's integrity, while maintaining it's social nature. How does the Moderation process work from the Respondent's perspective? Transparency and safety are foundational to the participant experience. If your content triggers a moderation flag, the system provides clear feedback regarding your status: Immediate Feedback Loop: Upon clicking submit, your response is immediately visible in your view, maintaining the spontaneity of the conversation. Automatic Content Hiding: If your response is flagged by the AI for violating community guidelines, it remains hidden from all other participants to protect the community. Inline Warnings and Guidance: If your post is hidden, you will see an inline warning message. This notification explains that your post has been restricted and provides a direct link to the tool’s Community Guidelines, fostering an educational approach to workplace ethics. Participant Restrictions: In cases of persistent violations, a moderator may restrict your account from this specific conversation. If you are restricted, you will be unable to post new responses; your input interface will be disabled, though you retain the ability to read and view existing discussions. A clear error message will notify you of your restricted status. How do Moderators manage flagged content and enforce forum safety? As a moderator, you have access to a robust suite of tools designed to manage forum integrity with minimal effort. The workflow centers on proactive alerts and centralized dashboard management. Real-Time Transactional Alerts: When the AI identifies a high-severity violation, you receive an automated email notification. This alert includes the full text of the flagged comment, the participant’s anonymized ID, the category of the violation (e.g., Hate Speech, Threats), and the system’s confidence score. Restricted Responses view: Access the centralized list of flagged responses by clicking the link provided in your email alert, or navigate to the Community Violation modal within your Empower conversation. Here, you can review a queue of all flagged activities. Moderation Actions: You can perform three distinct actions on any flagged item: Unhide: If you determine the flag was a false positive, selecting this option restores the comment to the forum and immediately lifts the visibility restriction for all participants. Delete: This permanently removes the response from the conversation. Note that the warning previously displayed to the author will remain until they refresh their browser session. Restrict Participant: This action blocks the individual from submitting further contributions to the conversation. This is an effective measure for participants who persist in violating community guidelines, resulting in an error message whenever they attempt to interact with the forum. summary: Slack Integration
body: Slack integration Slack Integration Slack Integration Slack integration allows you to connect your system with Slack. You can easily import your employees from Slack into your workforce. Once the integration is set up, employees can log into their workforce portal using their Slack account. To set up Slack integrations, Go to: Login >> Workforce >> Employee roster >> Integrations. In the search options search “Slack” Select “Slack” Integration Click on “Add another workspace” Create and name your workspace in Slack. Once it’s created, it will appear in the list, as shown in the image below. Select a Channel for the workforce Click On “Allow” Slack integration is now active when the “green check mark” is shown Under the Kebab menu (Three dots) we have 3 options Import Employees: lets you automatically import employees from Slack to your roster. Remove Integration: This lets you remove the Integration completely In ‘Settings,’ you can schedule an automatic sync of employees into the system daily, weekly, monthly, or at a specific time. Turn on Auto Sync, select the frequency from the dropdown and set the time as per US Pacific and click on “Auto Sync” License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: Google Apps Integration
body: Google apps integration Google Apps Integration Google Apps Integration Google Apps integration allows you to connect with your google platform. You can import your employees from the google platform to your workforce. Once the integration is established, employees can log into their workforce portal by their google account. How can I enable Google App integration with my workforce? Only admin user can establish Google App integration. To enable Google App integration with your workforce, follow the steps mentioned below: Go to: Login>> WorkForce>>Manage Employee roster>>Integration Click on “Connect” option given in Google App. Click on “Allow” to establish integration. Can I import employees from once Integrated with Google App? Yes, you can import employees once integrated with Google App. Click on import employee to import the employees. Can I remove Google App integration? Yes, you can remove Google App integration. Click on remove integration link to remove integration. summary: SAML Integration - Workforce
body: Single sign on SAML Integration - Workforce SAML Integration - Workforce SAML, is a standardized way to tell external applications and services that a user is who they say they are. SAML makes single sign-on (SSO) technology possible by providing a way to authenticate a user once and then communicate that authentication to multiple applications. SAML will authenticate the employee and the employee will be able to log into the portal and take the survey deployed for him. This will also add the employee in the employee roster, if the employee is not present. It is an XML-based open standard data format for exchanging authentication and authorization data between parties, in particular, between an identity provider and a service provider. And you will have to enter the issuer. Login >> Workforce >> Employee roster >> Integrations Click on connect for Single Sign On, enter the issuer ID for SAML and click on save. Can I change the issuer ID once the SAML integration is established? Yes, you can change the issuer id or remove the integration. Click on change issuer id or remove integration. You can use End point URL post authentication to take survey. Note: Email address field is compulsory for authentication and take survey License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? summary: This article will help users how to integrate with calabrio to import and sync their data directly to the employee roaster.
body: Calabrio Integration This article will help users how to integrate with calabrio to import and sync their data directly to the employee roaster. Calabrio Integration What is Calabrio? Calabrio is the customer experience intelligence company that empowers organizations to enrich human interactions. The scalability of our cloud platform allows for quick deployment of remote work models and gives our customers precise control over operating costs and customer satisfaction levels. Our AI-driven analytics tools make it easy for contact centers to uncover customer sentiment and share compelling insights with other parts of the organization. How to integrate Workforce with Calabrio? Please follow the steps below to connect your Workforce to Calabrio: Go to:Login » Workforce » Manage Employee Roster » Integration » Calabrio Click on Add/Update to connect the account. Add Calabrio account credentials and connection URL. Click on the Save button. After creating a connection you can check the details on the screen. You will need to map the survey to your Calabrio. Select theCustom Field for mapping. You can set up a daily sync from the toggle - for automated sync every day. Click on Sync Custom Fields to save the settings. Once saved, the logs will be displayed as per each sync. You can check log details from the tooltip. On clicking thetooltip, you can check the pop-up with log details. Click on the Sync Employees button. Log of employee sync updated. In the pop-up you can check as per location, it is synced with the employees. Go to the Employees, the roster is updated as per the sync. ToRemove Integration, click on the button. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.
