summary: Multiple choice- Select Many
body: Multiple choice- Select Many Multiple choice- Select Many Multiple choice- Select Many The multiple-choice select-many question type lets respondents choose multiple answers from a set of predefined options. Click to download video To add a multiple choice (Select Many) question to your survey, follow these steps: Login >> Workforce >> Select a survey >>Click Edit >> Then go to Workspace Click on the “Add Question” button. Under basic question type, click on Multiple Choice » “Select Many” A multiple-choice question allowing multiple sections will appear in your survey. Click '+ Option' to add answer choices. Add 'Other': This option lets you include 'Other' as an answer choice. When selected, respondents can enter their own answer. Add N/A: This option lets you include 'NA' as an answer choice Bulk Edit: This option allows you to edit multiple answer choices at once. You can copy and paste a list of answer options directly into the field. You can also enable the “Other” and “Not Applicable Option” to include it as an answer choice. Click on “Save” to proceed further. What types of validation can I apply to multiple choice “Select Many” questions? Validation: By default, validation is turned off when you add questions to a survey. This means respondents can skip questions and still complete the survey. To ensure respondents answer specific questions, enable validation and choose from the drop-down options: 'Force Response' or 'Request Response.' Force Response: Makes a question mandatory. Respondents must answer the question to complete the survey. Request Response: Prompts respondents to answer a question before continuing or lets them skip it if they choose. Select at most/exactly/at least: You can also set validation on the number of choices a respondent must select before moving to the next question. For example, to limit answers to a maximum of 2 options, choose 'Select at Most' and set the value to 2. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: multiple-choice drop-down menu
body: Multiple choice-Drop Down Menu multiple-choice drop-down menu Multiple-choice drop-down menu The multiple-choice drop-down menu is a type of question where respondents can select only one answer from a list that appears when they click on the menu. This format is especially helpful when you have a long list of options, such as a list of cities in a county or state. By using a drop-down menu, the options are neatly organized and easy to scroll through, making it simpler for respondents to find and choose the right answer. This question type is commonly used in surveys to make it easier for people to select one option from many possibilities. Click to download video To add a multiple choice (Drop-down Menu) question to your survey, follow these steps: Login >> Workforce >> Select a survey >>Click Edit >> Then go to Workspace Click on the “Add Question” button. Under basic question type, click on Multiple Choice » “Drop-down” A multiple-choice drop-down question will appear in your survey. Click '+ Option' to add answer choices. summary: Ordering -Drag and drop
body: Ordering -Drag and drop Ordering -Drag and drop Ordering -Drag and drop Ordering > Drag and Drop The drag-and-drop question type allows respondents to rank items by moving them around on the screen. They can click and drag each item to place it in the order they prefer. This makes the ranking process more engaging and interactive. Additionally, you can include images as part of the answer choices, making it visually appealing and easy to understand. This type of question is useful for gathering preferences in a way that feels intuitive and user-friendly. Click to download video To Add a drag and drop Question Go to: Login » Workforce (select a survey) » Edit » Workspace. Click on the “Add Question” button. Under basic question types » Ordering, click on drag and drop question. Click '+ Option' to add answer choices. Add 'Other': This option lets you include 'Other' as an answer choice. When selected, respondents can enter their own answer. Bulk Edit: This option allows you to edit multiple answer choices at once. You can copy and paste a list of answer options directly into the field. You can also enable the “Other” and “Not Applicable Option” to include it as an answer choice. Click on “Save” to proceed further. Validation: Default Setting: When you add questions to a survey, the validation option is off by default. This means respondents can complete the survey without answering all the questions. Survey Completion: Even if respondents skip questions, the survey will still be marked as complete. Enabling Validation: Turn on the validation option to access two settings: "force response" and "request response." Force Response: Makes a question mandatory. You can also set a minimum number of required answers. Request Response: Prompts respondents with a pop-up if they skip a question, asking them to answer or continue without answering. 'Select at most/exactly/at least': You can require respondents to select a certain number of choices before moving to the next question. For example, to allow a maximum of 2 answer options, choose 'Select at most' from the dropdown and set the value to 2 How does drag and drop question work? Click the preview button Drag and drop questions let respondents move items from the left column to the right column, ranking each item in order of preference. summary: Text > Comment Box
body: Text - comment box Text > Comment Box Text - Comment Box A comment box is a section in a survey where respondents can freely write their own comments or suggestions. It allows them to share additional thoughts, provide detailed feedback, or offer ideas that aren't covered by the survey's specific questions. Click to download video To add Comment Box Question, go to: Login » Workforce » Select Survey » Edit » Workspace » Add Question Under Basic » Text » Comment Box Add and Remove Rows: You can add multiple rows by using the "+ Text Box" feature. To remove rows, click the dropdown on the right and choose the option to remove them. Validation When you add questions to a survey, the validation option is turned off by default. Without validation, respondents can complete the survey without answering all the questions. If they skip all the questions, the survey will still be marked as complete. Enabling validation gives you two options: "Force Response" and "Request Response." Force Response: Makes a question mandatory. Respondents must answer it before finishing the survey. Request Response: Shows a pop-up if a question is left unanswered, asking the respondent to answer it or continue without answering. You can also use RegEx validation for comment box questions. How does the comment box question work when filling out the survey? While taking the survey, the respondent just needs to type their answer in the text box for comment questions. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: eNPS
body: eNPS eNPS eNPS eNPS (Employee Net Promoter Score) measures employee satisfaction and loyalty by asking how likely they are to recommend the company as a place to work. Responses are scored on a scale from 0 to 10, with higher scores indicating more satisfied employees. It helps companies gauge overall employee morale. In an eNPS (Employee Net Promoter Score) survey, employees are grouped into three categories: Promoters: Employees who are highly satisfied and likely to recommend the company. Passives: Employees who are neutral. Detractors: Employees who are dissatisfied and may speak negatively about the company. eNPS calculates how likely employees are to recommend the company. The system adds up responses in three categories: Promoters, Passives, and Detractors. It then calculates the "Net Promoter Score" by subtracting the percentage of Detractors from the percentage of Promoters. Click to download video To Add Employee Net Promoter Score to the survey, go to: Login » Workforce » Select Survey » Edit » Workspace » Add Question Under Advance » Customer Satisfaction » Net Promoter Score By default, the eNPS (Employee Net Promoter Score) question uses a 0-10 rating scale. This scale helps measure employee satisfaction and loyalty. If needed, you can adjust the scale in the survey settings to better fit your survey's needs. Following Scale options are available. Scale Detractors Passive Promoters 0-10 Rating 0-6 Rating 7 or 8 Rating 9 or 10 1-10 Rating 1-6 Rating 7 or 8 Rating 9 or 10 1-7 Rating 1-5 Rating 6 Rating 7 1-5 Rating 1-3 Rating 4 Rating 5 Validation: By default, validation is off, so respondents can skip questions in the survey. If you don’t want them to skip, you can turn on validation. When you do, you'll see these options Force Response: This makes the question mandatory. Respondents must answer it to finish the survey. Request Response: This shows a pop-up if a question isn’t answered, asking the respondent to either answer it or continue without answering. How does the NPS question look to respondents in the survey? The respondents can see the NPS question as below: Here, the respondents need to click on the score that they wish to give. License This feature is available with the following license : Employee Experience Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.

summary: | SALESFORCE → QUESTIONPRO | | | | Integration | | | | Cookbook | | | | Triggering QuestionPro surveys automatically from Salesforce — built for Hewlett Packard Enterprise. | | | | Step-by-step: Named Credentials → Flow Builder → HTTP Callout → Survey Delivery | | | | QuestionPro CX | Internal Use |
body: | SALESFORCE → QUESTIONPRO | | | | Integration | | | | Cookbook | | | | Triggering QuestionPro surveys automatically from Salesforce — built for Hewlett Packard Enterprise. | | | | Step-by-step: Named Credentials → Flow Builder → HTTP Callout → Survey Delivery | | | | QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | Hewlett Packard Enterprise | SALESFORCE → QUESTIONPRO | | | | Integration | | | | Cookbook | | | | Triggering QuestionPro surveys automatically from Salesforce — built for Hewlett Packard Enterprise. | | | | Step-by-step: Named Credentials → Flow Builder → HTTP Callout → Survey Delivery | | | | QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | 1. The Client & The Problem | CLIENT | PLATFORM | | | | | Hewlett Packard Enterprise | Salesforce CRM → QuestionPro CX Surveys | | | | | Global Technology | B2B Enterprise | Triggered via Salesforce Flow + HTTP Callout | Background Hewlett Packard Enterprise manages a large global customer base across enterprise technology — infrastructure, cloud, and as-a-service products. Their customer relationships are managed in Salesforce, where support cases, sales cycles, and account interactions are tracked at scale. HPE already used QuestionPro to run CX surveys, but the two systems were disconnected. Surveys were triggered manually or by batch process, meaning feedback arrived days after the key moment — or not at all. The HPE CX team wanted to capture feedback at exactly the right moment: immediately after a support case was closed, a product delivery was confirmed, or a critical account milestone was reached. These are the moments when the experience is freshest in the customer's mind and when the feedback is most actionable. The gap was that Salesforce, where these events were recorded in real time, had no native way to dispatch a QuestionPro survey automatically when they occurred. The Specific Gaps - Survey invitations were sent manually or via scheduled batch, often days after the moment that mattered, when recall and motivation to respond had dropped significantly. - There was no way to pass Salesforce record data (case number, account ID, contact details, product information) directly into the survey as custom variables — meaning the team couldn't pre-populate the survey or tie responses back to specific CRM records. - QuestionPro did not have native integration with Salesforce for survey distribution. The core requirement When a defined event occurs in Salesforce — a case closes, a delivery is confirmed, a service milestone is hit — a QuestionPro survey invitation must be dispatched to the relevant contact automatically, within seconds, with the relevant account and case data pre-loaded. No manual steps. No batch delay. No data disconnection. What Success Looked Like A support engineer closes a case in Salesforce. Within seconds — without any manual action — the customer receives a personalised survey invitation from HPE. The survey is pre-loaded with the case number and account context. The response comes back into QuestionPr

title: Salesforce Triggering Ready to Sell
kw: salesforce flow survey no case closed event questionpro customer crm trigger response record api post delivery time custom callout real integration already invitation seconds pre back loop one middleware builder pattern feedback so within tied contact account triggered cx built salesforce smoke alarm trigger survey crm pricing rts sell benefits
summary: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) Salesforce-Triggered Surveys Real-time, event-driven survey dispatch from Salesforce into QuestionPro CX Solution type Services-led integration (Salesforce-native, no middleware) Proof point Built for Hewlett Packard Enterprise — real-time post-case CSAT across a global B2B base
body: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) Salesforce-Triggered Surveys Real-time, event-driven survey dispatch from Salesforce into QuestionPro CX Solution type Services-led integration (Salesforce-native, no middleware) Proof point Built for Hewlett Packard Enterprise — real-time post-case CSAT across a global B2B base Core platform QuestionPro CX + Transactions API + Custom Variables + Salesforce Flow Builder Released Ongoing CX integration pattern | Internal positioning | | | | Use this when a customer already lives in Salesforce and wants feedback captured at the moment of truth — case closed, deal won, delivery confirmed — not days later in a batch. Position it as a direct, real-time bridge that needs no middleware and no third-party connector, built entirely from tools both teams already own. | QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | Version 1.0 1. What problem does this solve? Most CRM-driven survey programs fire on a batch or a manual trigger, so the invitation reaches the customer days after the experience — when recall has faded and response rates have dropped. The events worth measuring are recorded in Salesforce in real time, but Salesforce has no native way to dispatch a QuestionPro survey the instant they happen. Salesforce-Triggered Surveys close that gap. A defined Salesforce event automatically dispatches a personalised QuestionPro invitation within seconds, with the relevant CRM data pre-loaded and the response tied back to the originating record. 2. What is the solution and how does it work? What it does. A Salesforce Record-Triggered Flow fires on a defined event (e.g. Case Status → Closed), assembles the contact and record data, and makes an HTTP callout to the QuestionPro Transactions API. QuestionPro imports the contact and dispatches the survey immediately — carrying case number, account ID and any other fields as custom variables for pre-population, reporting and export. How it fits the product. The entire integration is built inside Salesforce Flow Builder and the QuestionPro API. No middleware, no third-party connector, no scheduled batch. Responses land in QuestionPro tied to the CRM record, ready for dashboards, filters and closed-loop workflows. Core components Component Role in the solution Named Credential Securely stores the QuestionPro API base URL in Salesforce so it is never hardcoded and easy to switch environments. Salesforce Flow Record-Triggered Flow that fires on the defined event and orchestrates data assembly and the callout. Assignment Element Populates the request body dynamically from record fields — contact details, case number, account ID. HTTP Callout Action Makes the POST to the QuestionPro Transactions API with the API key header. QuestionPro Transactions API Imports the contact and dispatches the survey invitation in real time. Custom Variables in QP Carry CRM context into the survey for pre-population and into the raw data export. Core flow Salesforce event fires → Flow assembles data → HTTP callout to QP API → Contact impo

summary: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) AI Router Real-time text intelligence for open-ended survey feedback Solution type Platform capability + services-led AI prompt/configuration implementation Primary use case Convert open-ended comments into structured sentiment, themes and summaries Core platform QuestionPro CX +
body: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) AI Router Real-time text intelligence for open-ended survey feedback Solution type Platform capability + services-led AI prompt/configuration implementation Primary use case Convert open-ended comments into structured sentiment, themes and summaries Core platform QuestionPro CX + Surveys + AI Router + Webhooks + Custom Variables + Dashboards Released Ongoing CX platform capability Internal positioning: Use this when a customer wants to unlock the value of open-ended comments without moving analysis into a separate text analytics tool. Position AI Router as the embedded intelligence layer that converts raw qualitative feedback into dashboard-ready fields inside QuestionPro CX. QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | Version 1.0 | May 2026 1. What problem does this feature solve? Open-ended survey responses contain rich insight, but they are unstructured, inconsistent and difficult to analyze at scale. Most organizations either review comments manually or push the data into separate analytics tools that do not integrate cleanly with core CX dashboards. AI Router solves this by automatically converting qualitative responses into structured, actionable data - including sentiment, themes and summaries - directly within the survey and respondent dataset. 2. What is the feature and how does it work? What it does AI Router processes open-ended responses in real time and transforms them into structured CX intelligence. It can extract sentiment, identify key themes, classify topics and generate respondent-level executive summaries mapped into predefined fields. How it fits into the existing product AI Router is fully embedded within QuestionPro CX surveys. Responses are sent to AI Router through a webhook or router call, and the returned JSON output is stored as custom variables within the same survey dataset. This makes the data immediately usable in dashboards, filters, reporting views, workflows and raw data exports. Core architecture Component Role in the solution Survey open-ended questions Capture the raw qualitative feedback from customers, employees or respondents. Webhook / AI Router call Sends each open text response or combined respondent payload to AI Router in real time. Prompt and codebook Defines the classification rules, accepted themes, sentiment scale and summary format. Structured JSON output Returns normalized fields such as sentiment, theme, subtopic and executive summary. Custom variables Store AI-generated values in the same respondent record as the original response data. CX dashboards and exports Use router outputs as filters, widget dimensions, reporting fields and exportable columns. Core flow Stage How it works Response capture Open-ended responses are collected within the survey experience. AI processing Responses are sent to AI Router through a webhook or router configuration. body: | AI ROUTER | | | | Sartorius | | | | From Employee Survey to Real-Time AI Intelligence | | | | QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | Sartorius | AI ROUTER | | | | Sartorius | | | | From Employee Survey to Real-Time AI Intelligence | | | | QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | 1. The Client & The Problem | CLIENT | PLATFORM | | | | | Sartorius AG | QuestionPro Employee Experience Surveys | | | | | Germany | Life Sciences & Biotech | with XA Dashboard visualisation | Background Sartorius AG is a leading international partner for the biopharmaceutical industry and research institutions. With over 14,000 employees globally, they run structured employee experience (EX) surveys through QuestionPro to measure engagement, satisfaction, and organisational health. Results are visualised using QuestionPro's XA dashboards, which the HR and People Analytics teams use for ongoing monitoring and leadership reporting. The Specific Problem Their EX survey includes 5 open-ended comment box questions designed to capture qualitative feedback across key dimensions of the employee experience: Q1 What does Sartorius do well that makes you proud to work here? Q2 What would make Sartorius a better place to work? Q3 How would you describe the quality of communication from your direct manager? Q4 What barriers, if any, prevent you from doing your best work? Q5 Is there anything else you would like to share about your experience at Sartorius? The insight team needed to do four things with these responses that were not possible out of the box: - Show the sentiment (Positive, Neutral, or Negative) for each of the 5 comments as a widget inside the existing XA dashboard — one widget per question. - Show the primary topic associated with each comment (e.g. Leadership Communication, Career Development, Workload) — also as a dashboard widget and as a filterable field. - Generate a respondent-level executive summary — a concise paragraph synthesising all 5 open-ended answers from a single submission, so HR managers could read a summary rather than 5 raw comments per employee. - Make all of the above available in the raw data export, so the structured AI output sits alongside standard survey responses in the same file. Why Existing Tools Could Not Solve This The natural first thought was to use QuestionPro's BI module. However, BI creates an entirely separate dashboard environment. The data it produces cannot interact with or filter the existing XA dashboard — they operate independently. This meant: - The HR team would have had to maintain and switch between two separate dashboards. - Filters applied in the XA dashboard (by department, location, tenure, etc.) would not carry over to BI-generated insights. - Sentiment and topic data would not appear in the raw data export for the XA survey — they would live only inside BI. - Leadership reports built on the XA dashboard would have no access to the AI-generated fields. The core constraint The intelligence had to live inside the survey itself — as

summary: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) Dynamic TV Guide Engine Searchable dataset selection inside QuestionPro surveys Solution type Services-led reusable implementation pattern Proof point Built for TRP Research Ireland media/audience measurement workflow Core platform QuestionPro Survey + QuestionPro Middleware Data
body: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) Dynamic TV Guide Engine Searchable dataset selection inside QuestionPro surveys Solution type Services-led reusable implementation pattern Proof point Built for TRP Research Ireland media/audience measurement workflow Core platform QuestionPro Survey + QuestionPro Middleware Dataset API + Custom Variables + Advanced JavaScript Internal positioning: Use this when a customer needs a searchable, guide-like survey experience for a large and changing catalogue of programmes, products, locations, accounts, sessions or other reference data. QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | Version 1.0 | May 2026 1. What problem does this solution solve? (1-2 sentences) Customers with large or frequently changing catalogues cannot rely on long static answer lists without hurting respondent experience, data quality and completion rates. The Dynamic TV Guide Engine solves this by turning a standard QuestionPro survey page into a searchable selection experience, where respondents find the right item, add it to a personal list and then answer follow-up questions about exactly what they selected. 2. What is the solution and how does it work? What it does Dynamic TV Guide Engine is a services-led implementation pattern that converts QuestionPro survey questions into a searchable guide-style interface. Respondents can search by title, episode title, synopsis or description, add items to a My Programmes or My Podcasts basket, remove items, and continue to downstream questions that reuse their selections. How it fits into the existing product The solution sits on top of QuestionPro Survey, QuestionPro Middleware Datasets, custom variables, piping and advanced JavaScript. Middleware stores the uploaded catalogue as a versioned dataset and exposes it through a Dataset API endpoint. In production, a secure proxy can sit between the survey and Middleware so the production API key is handled server-side. Core components Component Role in the solution QuestionPro Survey UI Hosts the respondent-facing search fields, result list, selected basket, validation and downstream questions. Middleware Dataset Stores the searchable programme/catalogue data from CSV or Excel uploads and exposes the dataset endpoint. Secure proxy pattern Recommended for production so the browser does not expose the Middleware API key. Search basket Lets respondents add, remove and de-duplicate selected programmes or catalogue items. Custom variables Stores selected values into controlled ranges such as custom101-custom125, custom126-custom150 and custom151-custom175. CID / internal ID tagging Preserves ContentID or another internal identifier in the stored value while stripping it from visible text. Pre-fill and cleanup scripts Restores selected values when respondents navigate back and removes technical tags from the respondent-facing UI. When/how a customer would use it Customers use this when they need respondents to choose from a large dataset, but also need those choices to drive survey logi

The Client & The Problem | CLIENT | PLATFORM | | | | | TRP Research Ireland | QuestionPro Survey - Research Edition | | | | | Media Research | Audience Measurement | Advanced JavaScript · CSV dataset · External API · Custom variable mapping | Background TRP Research works in the media research space, providing media expertise, primary data, content monitoring, metadata management and reporting services to help media companies understand audiences, viewing behaviour and content consumption. For this project, the requirement was to build a weekly media survey that could capture what respondents watched, listened to and talked about across programmes, streaming platforms and podcasts — at scale, every week, without degrading the respondent experience. The survey needed to ask respondents which programmes they had watched in the last week, whether they watched other series or movies, which programmes they discussed, how they felt about selected programmes, how those conversations happened and which podcasts they listened to. The key requirement was not simply to ask these questions — it was to let the respondent search for content, select it, build a personal list and then answer follow-up questions about exactly those selected programmes, with the selections feeding downstream logic, loops, and exports. The Specific Problem The client wanted the survey to behave like a TV guide or streaming-service search page — not a form. A respondent should be able to search for a programme by title or synopsis, click a result, add it to a personal list, and then answer questions about those exact programmes. This created five distinct technical challenges: | | Large programme universe | | | | | | The survey referenced a large dataset of programmes — with static placeholders up to Programme 118 and further dynamic search sections — making a standard multi-select list impractical and unusable. | | | Respondent recall challenge | | | | | | People remember a partial title, an episode detail or a synopsis — not the exact programme name. The survey needed to support fuzzy search across title, episode title and synopsis. | | | Multi-step downstream logic | | | | | | Selections had to feed later questions including ratings, discussion tracking and loop questions such as 'how many times did you talk about this programme?' — requiring clean, reusable values at every downstream step. | | | Hidden identifier tracking | | | | | | Every selected programme n

body: CORRECTION FILE Original question: SITUATION: I want to integrate my survey with PowerBI. how can I so that? Submitted: 2026-06-26T14:36:24.325Z CORRECT INFORMATION: Solution QuestionPro TechOps team has developed a connector that can be used by the QuestionPro Enterprise users to pull their survey responses to their Power BI dashboard. **Note: QuestionPro TechOps team is also working on developing an app on the Microsoft Power BI platform which will help users to pull their survey responses to the Power BI platform with less effort. Steps to implement Add the custom connector to your Power BI application on your Desktop: Copy the QuestionPro connector from the following link and paste it into the following directory Directory: C:\Users\User\Documents\Power BI Desktop\custom connectors Connector link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1p0vDslA4bHLrIWlhFQSAlLRdliM36Xnk?usp=sharing Grant Permission to the connector: To allow third-party connectors to work in Power BI we need to make some changes to the desktop application. Go to: File -> Option and Settings -> Options -> Security -> Enable any extension to load Screenshot: https://www.screencast.com/t/GIbECkXZ Launch your Power BI desktop application Create a new file and click on Get data -> More and search for QuestionPro Screenshot: https://www.screencast.com/t/lhtK6xpc Enter your access id and API key from QuestionPro You can append the survey id to the following URL to get your access id. https://www.questionpro.com/a/power_bi?surveyID={{Survey_Id}} You can get your QuestionPro API key from the Integration tab in your account Access id Screenshot: https://www.screencast.com/t/X1kqcIUHTKOM API key Screenshot: https://www.screencast.com/t/zhLqL6EnhfD https://www.screencast.com/t/jcMlgpPDkm Pull data from QuestionPro Click on Connect and pull data from QuestionPro and make some transformations and click on load. https://www.screencast.com/t/ZhCLsTRid Publishing workbook to Power BI service (www.powerbi.com) Once data is pulled from QuestionPro using a custom connector, the workbook can be published to the Power BI service platform and can be shared with user’s and groups https://www.screencast.com/t/YvRepNvKj1

body: CORRECTION SUBMITTED: 2026-06-25T09:52:34.680Z QUESTION: SITUATION: Research a competitive discplacement WRONG ANSWER: CAPABILITIES: - Merge responses from multiple QuestionPro surveys into one master survey using **Merge Data 2.0**. - Get an **aggregated response view** after merging. - Benefit from **auto-mapping** of questions with similar structure during merge. - Build **BI dashboards based on one or more surveys**. - Create dashboards from scratch, add **multiple tabs**, use **different chart types**, and compare data. - In XA, create a dashboard with **+ New Dashboard**; in BI, the flow is **Login » Creat CORRECT ANSWER / MISSING CONTEXT: Survey Stacks is a data management feature that empowers users to seamlessly integrate data from multiple surveys into a single, cohesive dataset. This centralized repository of information facilitates streamlined reporting, in-depth analysis, and the generation of actionable insights. By merging data from various surveys, Survey Stacks eliminates the need for manual data consolidation and enables a holistic view of respondent feedback and behavior. This feature is particularly valuable for clients who conduct multiple surveys over time, across different departments, or for varying target audiences. With Survey Stacks, users can identify trends, compare results, and track changes in respondent sentiment over time. This comprehensive understanding of data can inform strategic decision-making, drive process improvements, and enhance overall organizational performance.

summary: The Dashboard Chef CUSTOM CX DASHBOARD WIDGETS Cookbook Turning QuestionPro's native CX dashboards into interactive, insight-driven experiences — built for Qatar Rail and Miral Destinations. Step-by-step: Source Widget → Custom HTML Widget → Filter Sync → AI Insights QuestionPro CX │ Internal Use 1.
body: The Dashboard Chef CUSTOM CX DASHBOARD WIDGETS Cookbook Turning QuestionPro's native CX dashboards into interactive, insight-driven experiences — built for Qatar Rail and Miral Destinations. Step-by-step: Source Widget → Custom HTML Widget → Filter Sync → AI Insights QuestionPro CX │ Internal Use 1. The Client & The Problem Background Qatar Rail and Miral Destinations both run active CX programmes on QuestionPro, collecting post-experience feedback across customer touchpoints. Their teams log into the QuestionPro dashboard regularly to review scores, track trends, and share insights across the organisation. The platform gives them the data — but not in a form that works for every audience. Executives need a single number and a clear signal. CX managers need trends and segment breakdowns. Analysts need to drill into distributions and drivers. The out-of-the-box QuestionPro dashboard serves all of them with the same layout: raw gauges, static bar charts, and no interactivity. Filters update the numbers, but they don't explain what changed or what to do about it. The Specific Gaps Dashboard widgets were designed for generic use, not the specific survey structure or KPI definitions each organisation cared about. Non-analyst stakeholders — executives and operations leads — saw charts, not decisions. There was no plain-English signal explaining what the data meant. No interactivity: a manager could not drill into a segment, compare periods, or pivot between views without leaving the dashboard entirely. Source widgets that existed only to feed data showed as blank white boxes on screen, making the dashboard look unfinished. After filters were applied, the dashboard updated silently with no indication of what changed or whether the numbers were meaningfully different. What Success Looked Like A CX manager opens the dashboard and immediately sees: the current CSAT score with a plain-English status (On track / Near benchmark), a CX Health Score combining CSAT, effort, and resolution data, and an anomaly banner if anything has moved significantly. She applies a filter for a specific support channel. Every widget updates simultaneously. She clicks a year bar in the effort tracker and the quarterly breakdown expands inline. She clicks the AI Insights widget and reads three prioritised actions generated from the live data. The entire experience happens without leaving the dashboard or opening a spreadsheet. 2. The Solution: Custom JavaScript Widgets on QuestionPro CX The solution uses QuestionPro's built-in Custom Widget (HTML widget) to inject JavaScript directly into dashboard tiles. Each custom widget reads live data from the native QuestionPro source widget sitting on the same dashboard, renders a bespoke UI, and re-renders automatically whenever a dashboard filter is applied. No external platform. No separate tool. No data exports. Everything runs inside the QuestionPro dashboard the teams already use. 3. Prerequisites QuestionPro Requirements Source Widg

body: READY-TO-SELL (RTS) The Dashboard Chef Custom CX Dashboard Widgets Turning QuestionPro's native CX dashboards into interactive, insight-driven experiences for every audience in the organisation. QuestionPro CX | Internal Use | Version 1.0 1. What problem does this solve? QuestionPro CX gives teams the data. The Dashboard Chef gives them the signal. Out-of-the-box QuestionPro dashboards show gauges, bars, and numbers — but they don't explain what the numbers mean, what changed after a filter was applied, or what to do next. For organisations with a mixed audience — executives who want one number, managers who want trends, analysts who want distributions — a single static layout serves none of them well. Custom CX Dashboard Widgets replace the native QuestionPro tiles with interactive, audience-aware visualisations that read the same live data, respond to the same filters, and surface plain-English insights alongside the charts — including AI-generated action items from the QuestionPro AI Router. 2. What is the solution and how does it work? What it does. JavaScript widgets injected into QuestionPro's Custom HTML Widget tile read live data from native QuestionPro source widgets on the same dashboard, render a bespoke UI, and re-render automatically when any dashboard filter is applied. No page reload. No data export. No separate tool. How it fits the product. The entire solution runs inside the QuestionPro CX dashboard the customer already uses. Custom widgets sit on top of native QuestionPro widgets, reading from them and hiding them. The native widgets keep doing their job — receiving data from QuestionPro's backend on every filter change — while the custom widgets handle the visual layer. Core components Core flow 3. Key benefits Every audience gets what they need from the same dashboard: executives get a CX Health Score and plain-English status; managers get interactive trends and segment drill-downs; analysts get distributions, year-on-year comparisons, and raw breakdowns. Filters update every widget simultaneously — and the AI Insights widget re-generates its recommendations based on the filtered view automatically. The dashboard looks finished. Native source widgets are hidden; users see only the custom UI. No blank white boxes, no duplicate charts. Built entirely inside QuestionPro. No external platforms, no middleware, no additional licences beyond what the customer already has. The widget pattern is reusable. Once the architecture is understood, any new widget for any QuestionPro source widget type can be generated using an LLM prompt template in minutes. 4. Who is it for? Roles that care: CX directors and VoC programme leads, operations and service managers, data analysts and CX specialists, executive sponsors who receive dashboard links. Workflows it improves: monthly CX review meetings, real-time operational monitoring, post-filter segment analysis, executive reporting, and board-level CX storytelling.
